Comment: Trải nghiệm cực kì tệ. Đầu tiên là không có tờ map nào hướng dẫn, thứ 2 là rất ít nhân viên, hầu như không có nhân viên nào ở dọc đường đi để hỗ trợ khách. Công viên nước thì không có chỗ để khách lấy phao, phải đứng chờ khách khác xuống đưa. Khi đến chỗ thấy nhân viên đang xếp phao, mình đên xin thì nhân viên không cho, bắt mình đợi khách nào trượt xuống rồi đưa. Tối đi coi tatashow thì nhân viên rất ít và không biết quản lý khách kêu khách ngồi xuống để ko ảnh hưởng khách sau. Có phát loa nhưng phát loa bằng tiếng Việt thì khách nước ngoài người ta ko hiểu, nhân viên cũng không thèm nhắc. Kết quả là các khách đứng sau không thấy gì cả, tệ nhất là các em bé nhỏ không xem được luôn. Tóm lại là quá tệ!!!!

5 sao trên google review toàn seeding Chất lượng tệ xong đi xoá review của khách, post lại để KH khác ko bị mất tiền như t.Rất nhiều trò chơi bảo trì, còn ít trò để chơi, máy móc thiết bị khu trò chơi trong nhà cũng rất chán, cũ, hoàn toàn không xứng với số tiền bỏ ra. Khu chơi nước nghỉ cũng sớm hơn trước rất nhiều . 5h30 nghỉ thì 5h đã không cho mình xếp hàng chơi vì còn khách chờ ( khách chờ dài đúng rồi, vì có bao nhiêu trò chơi được đâu)

1. Quét nhân trắc cho bé nhỏ sai TẬN 2 LẦN, làm con bé khóc, đi chơi cũng mất vui. Buổi trưa hướng dẫn viên đã làm việc với nhân viên để fix, thời gian fix rất lâu, trưa nắng mà nhân viên thái độ cực kì tệ. Chiều đi vào chơi công viên nước thì quét được nhưng tối vào coi nhạc nước và tata show lại bị tiếp, đổi thừa do khách sạn. Nhưng đây là bị lần 2, mà vẫn không chịu nhận lỗi là sao? Trể mất nửa show nhạc nước mà tối đó cũng không vui gì. Thái độ nhân viên nam lúc đó cũng xấc xược nốt.2. Chỉ có nhân viên ở khu công viên nước là dễ thương thôi. Còn nhiêu ở mấy khu đi tìm thỏ, câu chuyện tata thái độ cũng như mẹ khách nốt. Tủ đồ hết chỗ, nhờ nhân viên để tủ khác thì bị lườm liếc, nói trống không. Cứ nghĩ khách là học sinh cấp 3 nên muốn nói gì nói, người ta lễ phép lịch sự với mình mà mình thái độ vậy đó hả?3. Khách du lịch nước ngoài chen hàng cực kì nhiều nhưng nhân viên không có nhắc nhở.Nên chỉnh đốn lại cách xử lí khi quét nhân trắc vì tốn rất nhiều thời gian của khách, mình sai thì mình nhận lỗi chứ không có cái kiểu mà đổ lỗi cho khách sạn này kia. Thanks! Và đương nhiên đừng hòng tôi quay lại. Có đi thì chỉ đi vinpearl nam Hội An thôi
Chúng tôi lên cáp lúc 15h20, Buồng cáp số 74 có rất nhiều mùi thuốc lá trong khi các bạn cấm hút thuốc lá trên cáp, đã có chuyện gì xảy ra trước đó ?
No rental bikes? Đi bộ xa kiểu này nghĩ tới là muốn mệt huống hồ gì nghĩ tới chuyện ăn chơi, các khu du lịch nổi tiếng trên thế giới đều có dịch vụ xe đạp điện hoặc scooter.
Thanh toán cáp treo 2 chiều nhưng 18h00 khi chiều về nhân viên viện đủ lý do , thoái thác không cho khách lên . Ko biết vì lí do gì mà buổi chiều ko muốn cho khách lên cáp treo về bờ

Cách vận hành và thái độ của nhân viên càng ngày càng tệ,trời mua tầm tã bao nhiêu con người già trẻ xếp hàng dầm mưa để lên vòng quay mặt trời nhưng,cách xắp xếp của nhân viên quá tệ khiến nhiều người dù đã phải dầm mưa nhưng bức xúc bỏ cuộc quay về.1 không có mái che khu vực xếp hàng lên vòng quay, nhưng dù khách có xếp hàng dầm mưa thù nhân viên cũng không cho lên khu vực có mái che để che mưa.2,không biết cách xắp xếp,khi trời mưa có rất nhiều khách  không xuống và chỉ ngồi có 2 người hoạc có những người đi theo nhóm chỉ 2 khách 3 người nhưng vẫn không xắp cho  các khách lẻ để đi chung ,dù một cabin dư chỗ để ngồi được 6 -_8 người.Dù là bỏ ra số tiền không rẻ lưu trú tại khách sạn Vinpearl Nha Trang resot và sử dụng các dịch vụ tại đây,khách hàng bỏ tiền ra để sử dụng dịch vụ nhưng cảm giác thái độ của nhân viên giống y như xin cho bố thí.không biết quy trình và quy định của các cấp lãnh đạo vinpearl hiện tại như thế nào mà từ lễ tân check in trước khi lên cáp treo qua đảo thái độ phục vụ cũng rát khó chịu với khách hàng.dù biết là mùa hè cao điểm khách đông nhưng làm ngành dịch vụ không thể có thái độ lồi lõm như vậy được,cảm giác mất vui và bực bội khi đến vinpearl Nha Trang lần nàyTôi là khách lưu trú tại vinpearl Nha Trang ngày 22/7/2024 và sử dụng trò chơi vòng quay Mặt Trời lúc 18h cùng ngày.

Lại thêm một lần trải nghiệm thất vọng của Vin. Mình nhận phòng 3735 Vin Wonder Phú Quốc ngày 14/7 lại hỏng điều hoà. Lúc mình nhận là 16g mà bên Vin vẫn chưa khắc phục xong. Có rất nhiều phương án giải quyết nhưng Vin đã không giải quyết để mình nhận phòng 1 cách thoải mái nhất.

Nay ngày 12/7/2024. Trải nghiệm quá tệ. Nguyên khu rộng lớn như vậy mà k có nổi dịch vụ thuê xe đạp điện cho khách du lịch, xe đạp điện như vậy đi sẽ tự do thoải hơn xe bus dịch vụ của vinwonders. Điểm này còn thua Sunwork. Đi bộ mệt đến nổi chẳng còn sức mà chơi những trò khác nữa. Chưa kể đã có nhiều ý kiến về việc đi bộ vậy rồi mà k thấy lãnh đạo của vinwonders khắc phục.

cách quản lý tại Vinwonder quá tệ, ngày 15/6/2024 cty chúng tôi đã có một trải nghiệm kinh hoàng khi phải ch5u cảnh chen lấn suốt 4 tiếng đồng hổ đợi tàu để về đất liền. Đến rạnh sáng hôm sau mới thoát khỏi kiếp nạn tưởng như bị đè bẹp tắt thở tại chỗ vì lượng người quá đông cáp treo không hoạt động, tất cả đổ dồn về tàu mà tiếc thay cho Vin một tập đoàn lớn mà cách quản lý quá tệ khi sự cố xảy ra không có một lời thông báo, giải quyết sao cho mọi người xếp hàng ngay thẳng để mọi người hoang mang chen lấn lẫn nhau. Có rất nhiều người già, trẻ em thiếu nữ phải ngất xỉu, kêu cứu,.Đang trách nhất là bên Vin ko có ai ra quản lý khâu tổ chức xếp hàng cho mọi người được lần lượt về đất liển bằng tàu. Không xứng đáng 1 sao.  Do vậy rất mong mọi người lưu ý khi chọn đi ra Đảo vào những dịp cao điểm.

My family lets our nearly 3-year-old child play, and most of the children's games require height. There is a merry-go-round for children that requires a height of over 90cm. In the instruction sheet given, it is written that parents can be under 90cm, but when they arrive, they must be over 90cm to ride. In fact, the slow-motion game where parents can sit with their children but still have to be strict about every inch, parents will never want to let their children play in this area again. Everything may be fine, but even having an unsatisfactory experience will lead to the whole experience being the same. The female employee explained in the same way as to ignore the customer, just said no and then turned away without saying much. Attitude like disdain.I can't help but be disappointed after waiting an hour for my child to play and receiving such an attitude in return

We went with our 6 yr old who has been on huge water slides in Bali and Australia, but in Nha Trang the height to go on all of the decent water slides was 130cm, and our daughter was slightly too short so couldn’t go on them. Disappointment was an understatement. The park is so huge that you can’t get round it in one day, and it is so hot in July.

Firstly, if your children are under 1.3m, don't bother going. 4 rides only for the shorter ones.If you are getting a taxi or Grab then be careful they don't stop earlier at a dodgy ticket office on the way. A girl rushes put and tries to sell u tickets. Fortunately I could see it was a scam and told the driver to go or I would get angry.The park is accessible by boat only. The cable car is out of operation, probably forever. Why, I don't know but guess its because of an accident.The boat takes 7 minutes.The park has more areas than just the water park. I am used to large American style water parks so maybe I might sound a little harsh.The facilities are okay, changing area, showers, lockers etc. You will need to pay a deposit for the locker.The rides are carried except for young ones. Teenagers will like it for sure. The parkour course is really cool if you have the guts to try it. Harnesses are provided but you still would need to be the adventurous type to have a go.Staff are really nice and smile wave and help for people and children is superb.Would I go again? No, not at all.

We visited on a heavily discounted ticket which included 1N2D and 3 meals. The good: the lodging and meals were excellent, apart from somewhat thin walls in the room - we could hear passion on one side and crying children on the other. We were supposed to have villa accommodation with the ticket but ordered 10 days before our visit and were given a room in the Discovery section. Meals were grandiose buffet style and were easily the best part of the weekend.The bad: the park is aged and overall terrible. It is sad to know that the typical Vietnamese will pay roughly a month salary for parents and 2 children to visit as we did. In Vietnam, creativity is largely absent and the idea of entertainment is still a foreign concept; typically it equates to whatever gaming zone exists at the largest shopping mall of a town or city. Vinpearl is merely a larger version of that with tired painted castles with no accessible interiors and an overall feel of corporate dystopia.For any foreigner familiar with 1st, 2nd or 3rd tier carnival or theme parks in your own country, lower your expectations significantly. Any Americans familiar with Disney, Six Flags, Cedar Point or even Wisconsin Dells, should take a hard pass on visiting, and only stick to the accommodation and beaches on the island which are quite nice.Expecting closures in the park, we were explicit in asking about what would be open before we purchased - we were assured everything had reopened post-lockdown. We phoned to check again the day before we left because of rainy weather and again were told "all open". Lying is one of the most consistent character traits here however, and on arrival we were first greeted by car-less gondolas and fallen cables despite sales people pushing the longest cable car in Asia when we purchased. Disappointments continued on the island with 80% of the park shuttered. Note, language here is key - nothing is 'closed' but everything is 'under maintenance' with no actual maintenance occurring. Rides were closed, but not so interesting and at best are of the variety of spinning midway rides circa 1981.The Aquarium was thankfully open, and features an extensive underwater tube walkway under an overloaded tank, but the displays and building are also dank and depressing. The only other area open was the zoo, which was disgusting and would certainly be grounds for closing the entire operation if it were in the West. Asia suffers from the biblical sense of Dominion, with no consideration for responsible stewardship of flora or fauna.The final irritation are the frequent stairs throughout the park - zero consideration is given for elderly, disabled or families with strollers. For example, 3 flights of escalators as the main arteries to travel the park end in additional stairs. The zoo walkways were tortuously uneven with a stroller and again, inexplicably, stairs are **frequently** placed in the middle of paths. Entering or exiting the Aquarium is by 2 flights of steep, slippery stairs - never with ramps or alternate entries. Benches and rest areas are infrequent. Drinking water access is also infrequent and only via overpriced vending machines. Hot, crowded, high-season would be a total nightmare. Park design is frankly anti-visitor. Avoid.

We visited in December and before going checked on the website to see if there were comments on rides being closed due to Covid. We didn’t see any and also when we bought our tickets there was nothing to say things would be closed. So  we paid the full price of 880,000 vnd per person. However on arriving things were very different. Only 3 rides were open in the first area and then in the water park only a few slides were operating. Also be aware that very few of the food outlets Are open. When we have visited other parks in Vietnam during Covid we have been informed before buying tickets that not all rides were working and were given discounts. Unfortunately, that was not the case today. I’m afraid we won’t be coming back.

No atmosphere, poor food, rides suited better to smaller children. The only ride that we were going to go on was the zip line but the cue was massive. Water park old. Beach was lovely. We spent the whole day on the beach, had one ride each and then went home. For £30 each wouldn’t recommend.

For 1, there is not enough staff. When the park is full of Chinese staff cutting in line, I wish there was some more staff around to stop it from happening. We were next in line for a coal ride when one women called her 20 person family in front of us. Staff didn’t even notice. There were people walking around with cigarettes and staff didn’t care. Someone vomited on the ride before us and the two men working spent 25 minutes looking for a towel and water to clean it. Then did it so slowly. Shouldn’t they be prepared for this situation? Other places I’ve experienced them clean it in 2-3 minutes max. Also there was still vomit on the chair after they cleaned it. The workers in the park were VERY unapproachable. They didn’t speak English, and would just ignore you or laugh at you and shrug if I asked them a question. The park itself is amazing, but the waiting line for the cable car, rude staff, low quality lotteria (our food was cold and order wrong, but the staff ignored us when we spoke to them in english to complain), and the incompetence just didn’t make up for it. I didn’t see many Vietnamese people in the park at all. It was all Russians, Chinese and Korean tourists. So why don’t they expect staff to speak English? If you want a GOOD theme park in Vietnam just go to Bana Hills.

1 hour in the line to the cable car. No, buying the ticket online will not save you any time or money. While price is similar, it takes them more time to locate your e-ticket in the system than to sell you in spot. 30-60 minutes waiting for every more or less worthy attraction. The famous Zip Line is available for 10 visitors per hour (they are making a lottery)The line for the bob car is about 2 hours. Plenty of pushing rude Chinese. On the way back, expect to wait for another hour for the cable car. They are confiscating all food and drinks at the entrance. So expect to buy all on spot. Shiny it’s looks from the shore, on spot, much sad picture. I wouldn’t come back again even for free.

Get your tickets at the counter in VinPearl Land instead of buying online from other 3rd party websites.  Recommended to take the speedboat in and take the cable car out.You are not allow to bring your own water, they will check your bag.  Make sure you block the area that you queue, otherwise, some China Tourist will just cut your queues.  Very bad, no proper queuing system.Be prepared to queue under the hot sun for at least 1-2 hours for those normal rides and min 3 hours for Alpine Coaster rite.The wet park closes at 6pm.

Me and my boyfriend visited Vinpearl on our travels round Vietnam. It was a good day out, the water park is great and the 4d cinema was cool. The rides are average, nothing amazing as they are mostly for kids. But the cable car and views are amazing. However Like every other place in Vietnam, the animal welfare standards are very poor and frankly spoilt my time there! The enclosures are tiny, species are mixed and there’s no shelter or place for them to avoid the crowds of people!! The aquarium was good apart from the turtle enclosure which was absolutely tiny and had tortoise and turtle in!!! The owners must be really stupid to not realise that these are to very different animals, you do not  put a tortoise in a water enclose with only a few rock to climb on! Such a bad quality of life for them. There was about 30 turtle and tortoise crammed into a tiny space and you could tell how distressed they were😣 overall it’s a cool place and a lot of money has been spent and made!  such a shame not on the important things like the animals !!!!!

There is a variety of attractions to make a good day out for people of all ages. We particularly enjoyed the gardens and the ferris wheel ride. There was a long queue for the cable car in the morning so we caught the boat which was fast and fun; in the afternoon we were able to take the cable car back and enjoy the splendid views of the water and town. One unpleasant experience was the compulsory search of our bags for food and drink, which are forbidden in order to maximize the profits of the restaurants on the island. We witnessed bags of food and bottles of water being brusquely confiscated from families who came prepared with snacks. This process was intrusive and without a legitimate purpose. Unsurprisingly, the food on the island is overpriced and the meals we had were of poor quality.

My family and I came here on February 17th because we have heard so much about the vinpearl amusement park in Nha Trang. So here we thought maybe it would be the same, hence the name VINPEARL!!  but boy oh boy I was wrong, it was a great disappointment. Half of the rides aren’t even as powerful nor nearly as fun as the foreign amusement park. I had fair share great rides such as those of Carowinds in North Carolina, Wonderland of Canada and Universal studio of Florida. As stated by some of comments before, there are NO proper lines to wait..people just randomly skipped us and fitted themselves infront of us wherever they like. Okay fine! I’ll let that line skipping part slide because the wait was not that long. We then decided to head to the water park after we went through most of the ride because like half of the rides are under maintaince. At the water park, my poor 12 years old cousin was not allowed to be in like 90% of the water rides because they deem him too tall for the kiddie slides yet too little for the adult water rides. We were approached by workers telling us that we can’t ride this water slide nor that water slide. Especially the water slide that looks like the funnel with a 4-people floater. You need exactly 4 people to ride the funnel looking water slide, but we only have 3 people. Yet in America, in Canada, in Korea and in vinpearl NHA TRANG even if there are 2 people we can still ride the funnel water slide. You pay so much to go into the park just so people telling you CAN NOT ride anything, and just to go sight seeing. You’ll be disappointed!!

Very poor service, sometimes don’t even have a logic in it. Starting from cable car - waiting for about 30-40 mins in both ways = about 1 hour totally ! 8 people for one cable car and it’s ok if they are slim.Going to electric mountain car in this queue of people have to wait around 2-3 hours (of your life under the sun) for 5-7 mins of ride. And if you don’t want to wait you can pay extra 150k which is affecting on the main queue, that means if people’s decide to pay it will increase the time of the usual queue of people. In my opinion it’s just a time killer in this island. Small variety of attractions, I would say it’s for kids or under 15 years old. Poor service on the water attractions, only ONE lifeguard working for 3 slides like a robot and it’s really affecting on the queue (it’s really slow)... and lifeguards really aggressive sometimes.Different colors of the slides it’s hard sometimes to find a way or any instructions where to take these buns, I saw many confused tourists... all the slides working and closing in different time (why they not mentioned it on the map).It’s hot outside no places to seat or hide from the sun, if you want to drink some water: pay. You have to pay for everything on this island and prices higher +100% or 200% from regular prices for example for a  small bottle of water 15k and in regular store (not in this island) you can buy a big one for 10k. I’m a tourist here, but why they cheat me that badly. Water should be free, it’s not a joke. Finally I spent about 8 hours in this place and I can say its not worth it, service is not even close to other parks in other countries (like in North America). Really poor service... everything doesn’t look new. They don’t even have a background music everywhere to create some thematic-happy mood.. you just walking from point “A” to point “B” to wait in different queue and pay double price for everything.

Our teenage grandson was desperate to go. The cable car over was a novelty but didn’t make up for the disappointment when we got there. The food was below average and a third of the menu was not available. The rides were somewhat timid with nothing to really challenge you. I think the place would be ok for smaller children but there is a 1.3 metre height restriction on many rides. Not a fan of seeing large animals being kept in relatively small compounds. Like a lot of places in Vietnam not particularly friendly to people with mobility problems or mobility aids. The Sky Wheel was probably the highlight of the day.

Came here during a recent trip to see what all the fuss was about. The toboggan was fun however we had a slow coach in front of us who just stopped their cart every few seconds to take selfies, which kind of ruined the experience for us who were behind and could not pick up any real speed. Very annoying! The main issue we have with this place is the animals do not look in good condition. The bears were pacing up and down which is a sign of distress. The lions were tiny and looked like they needed food. The emus has plucked all their feathers out. The spaces that the animals had to live in were not adequate and at times we couldn’t even see any source of water for them to drink!Come here for the rides if you want, but give the zoo a miss - we wished we hadn’t been.

Vinpearl Land has turned into a time & money wasting spot. Except the dolphin show, there’s nothing interesting about the park. Looking at the ticket price, the value in return is minimal. The park is not maintained well & we found most of the water rides very dirty. People spitting in the water and no one to monitor. It was quite disgusting!Along with the tickets, we were given the day’s schedule of the park. We went on Christmas eve and there was a Santa parade supposed to happen near the Ferris wheel. We stayed there for more than 30 minutes waiting for the parade but seems it was cancelled or wrongly put in the schedule. Worst thing is there’s no one available in the information desk or any staff visible around to answer your questions. The support staff at the bouncy beach was quite RUDE. The biggest disappointment was the locker and shower area in water park. Around 5PM we came back to get shower, but the locker staff said they ran out of towels (Seriously!!? The park wasn’t crowded so wondering what would happen on a busy day) I felt it’s just a way for the staff to force people buy new towels from their souvenir shops. Well, shocking part is there’s no hot water and soap in the shower area. Back in 2012, this park was the best attraction in Nha Trang with good service and even utilities like hair dryer too in shower area. Now I can say – AVOID this place.

Vinpearl Land, nothing special here, and nothing for small children, under 1.1M.  Although they charge nearly  It will cost you $80 for 2 adults $30 if the child is 1 meter tall and free if under 1m.  However to ride the vast majority of rides children must be 1.1 -1.3 meters tall.  I truly don't see how so many rate this place as good or excellent.  What a let down. Stay away is my best advice.

Was not that great. Sections of the park were still under construction. We waited in line for the 1hr to ride a rollercoaster that was quite average went fir a couple minutes. Food was not really good. Had to line up for a food card that didn’t work so had to line up again and prove i paid for it initially and then reload money on it. Food tasted like it was a frozen meal. Ferris wheel was the best part because of the view. All in all a 2/5 star is being generous. Go if u want over priced entertainment and hr long waits in lines.

This amusement park used to be compact, fun to visit, but now it has grown out of proportion, turning into a Frankenstein island littered with gigantic, bizarre buildings. The park is very popular with the locals and chinese tourists.

We had a terrible start when we got there the first 5 rides which are ones she could do anywhere else she was too short.We did the little ones but it was not good for us older people.The best part was the gondola ride over and back but definitely not worth the money charged.

Pretty much anything worth going on has height restrictions of 1.5m for almost all water slides and 1.3-1.5 for the other mechanical rides. If these rides were actually scary or had high Gs I would understand but all are very tame. If on their website or the park map this information  was stated, we would not have had to waste our time tracking from ride to ride checking height restrictions. Left with my daughter in tears, I was angry. On the plus side our youngest was free. However if your children fall into the dreaded 1m to 1.3m range I would reconsider the cost of going.

My husband and I started off the day having a great time at the water park which had a reasonable variety of rides. We then went on the Alpine coaster and some other rides. The Alpine coaster was a great new ride but there needs to be more warning provided to others to not stop in the middle - we had people in front of us stop halfway down on the fast section to take photos - this was a very dangerous thing to do!!! Unfortunately we ended our day on a sad note by visiting the animals. There was a great variety of animals but they were all very sad in the approximately 35 degree heat. There was hardly any shade and foilage for the animals and they had limited room to move about. The bears were the worst - one bear was pacing in a circle and another bear was standing on the spot shaking its head from side to side repetitively and they both looked like they were having psychotic breakdowns. Most people I saw looking at the animals looked just as sad as I did. I don't understand why such a new park didn't plan and build a park that would look after the animals better. For a place that is meant to be designed to be surrounded by beautiful nature, it's a stark contrast to the conditions that have been designed for the animals to live in.

We spent the whole day there with my friend (we’re French) and we’re pretty disappointed:- the fun park is very small and not fun for anyone else than kids (far from Disneyland, Europapark,...)- food is terrible and there’s no choice (stick to local food) - cable car was under maintenance so we got the speedboat instead which was actually faster and well organized - the “village” is actually a ghost town - the water park is alright, fun slides but could be better organized - the zoo was heartbreaking +- the view was amazing - the beach is really nice, thin white sand and a few fun activities for an extra cost like flyboard,.. - the area is quite small which allows you to walk around it easily - the dolphin show is very nice and is the light show (make sure to ask the staff the show times) I wouldn’t go again nor recommend it except to families with kids.

We read about the cable car trip over the ocean to Vin Pearl and made a special effort to get there. It was not working and from the accounts of others seldom is. It was a wasted expense of long taxi rides. Advice is get the resort to check its operating.

Ticket was $880,000 Dong ($52Aus). Cable car is currently 'under maintenance' so we had to take a complimentary 10min boat ride to Vinpearl Land instead. As compensation we were given a fast food voucher however, the line up was crazy long so we ended up eating else where. Lots of rides were under maintenance which was disappointing but if you include the water park rides there should be enough to keep you occupied for as least half the day. Most of the water slides require you to be 1.2-1.5meters tall so some younger kids may miss out on the more adventurous rides. Would recommend the Ferris wheel, great opportunity to relax and take nice photos of the theme park. Overall, worth a visit but don't your hopes up with wanting to go on a particular ride as it may not be available on the day.
Exhorborant entrance fee, limited rides, odd opening and closing tmes for rides. long waits on some rides. Most of the place was closed or closed for renovation.  Better things to do in Nha Trang

The only good thing about this place was the cable car trip over. Dolphin show awful ...it's like a building site all round it. Good prices were high and good basic. If you have children they will love it.

Had high expectations of a great day out, as usual the queue was long but moved freely The cable car ride was greatGot to the island and cracked the first roller coaster and thought it was going to be a day to remember In short The roller coaster rides were good especially the luge ride The water park was hit and miss, meaning that the rides were in use depending if there was an attendant present at the time A huge area of retail which was for the majority was closed Not a lot of food options, there are more options to buy diamond jewellery at an amusement park

Expensive and overrated for what you get and what is actually working on the day.None of the staff look happy they are all miseragle.Restaurants served basic amusement park food.There is absolutely no comparison to Disneyland at this place.Save your money and try and go to Disneyland.Have been once now won't go back,nothing interesting.

Very bad service at lotteria restaurant, hamburger was cold and very bad services, quality very bad if you ask for beverage with not ice you receive half glass of liquid very disappointed overall services of this place

Overall amusement park was good but the animal enclosures were bad to say the least. Not only did some animals look distressed and uncomfortable but the enclosures were very small and bare (particularly the lions, tigers and hyenas). The amount of money that would currently be going into constructions around the park could seriously be put towards a better life for the animals. Massive thumbs down to this area of the amusement park.

the weather is a big problem.. if it's cold and windy - many facilities are closed.. we came today with a lot of expectations because of the comments, but unfortunately many slides and facilities that we planned to do were closed. we shouldn't come  if the weather is not sunny. the park is very big and has many attractions if you arrive in a good day. we had a bad luck and it was disappointing

We have visited many amusement parks and we accept that there are usually high restrictions . However what we did not expect is that not only were many rides limited to 1.3 meters in hight to ride but so were all the Water slides ; this resulted in our 7 year old been unable to ride any of the slides and a number of the rides . Having had to purchase a child’s ticket for him this was very annoying . I was unable to find this restriction in any easy to access place so if your taking small kids not great for them . The park does check bags for outside food and drink so be careful if your taking it in . It was still an ok day but I would not be going back.

Coming here for a second time it is great to see the newly added Ferris Wheel and Bio Domes are a fantastic addition to the park. The Japanese garden and pagoda are worth visiting up there and ride 71 the Alpine luge is worth the queue.But then there is the catering or lack of it. The choices are limited and having to line up for 75 minutes for a menu where 8 of the 10 options were not available at 1.30pm in Loteria is just not acceptable for what is billed as Vietnam’s leading amusement park. The icecream shop that ran out of ice creams by 11am was just another example of badly getting it wrong. Getting something to eat when spending 8 hours at a park should not be an exasperating time consuming and tAsteless experience. Sadly this is the second time visiting the park for the same bad food experience and they clearly do not read the feedback comments to make any changes. And this badly let’s down what is otherwise a fun day out.

Too remote from the real Vietnam.  Stay home and go to own local amusement park.  Prices high as not near any local shopping centre.  Breakfast very crowded as one country strongly promotes as a destination for their people, their arrogance totally ruined our holiday

We visited Vinpearl land mid November - on the other side of the island you would be mistaken in thinking this is going to be the best theme park on earth - bright lights at night, a massive Ferris wheel and even a zoo! All of the advertisements for vinpearl land would have you thinking that this will be a huge day of fun with so much to see and do. Our first impression of vinpearl land is where you stand in a queue for half an hour waiting for a gondola with 100 other people to take you across the water to this magical land. We were lucky enough to have had a good taxi driver who stopped on the side of the road at a milk bar sort of place to get us our tickets otherwise we would have been waiting in another queue for that also. The cost for 2 tickets was 1.6 million which gives you access to the whole park, and a return gondola ride.Upon arrival at the park there are lots of pretty castles and the park had an abandoned fairy tale like ambience to it. As we walked around we noticed a lot of abandoned places where food outlets used to be and many of the rides closed. We went on 3 rides before deciding to give up and go to the water park. The water park wasn't really much better, the slides old and leaking and much of them closed. We did notice that some opened up later in the day but that meant other rides closing so we can only assume that perhaps they do not have enough staff to operate all of the rides. When it came time to hunt for some lunch we were severely let down. There were 2 places open in the water park, a 'BBQ' open bar and a Diner type establishment with 9 meals on offer, none of which looked appetising in the slightest. We ended up going for the potato swirl on a stick and decided we would just eat once we left. We did visit the aquarium which was very loud and crowded and not really very impressive. it did seem that the fish were looked after and had enough space to live. The zoo also seemed to be looking after its animals which was nice to see. The zoo had some interesting animals and the exhibits looked to be clean and had some stimulation for the animals.Overall vinpearl theme park seems very much like where old rides from around the world go to die and you shouldn't expect much from this place. It is an experience more than anything and makes you realise how lucky some other countries are.

Vinpearl land amusement park has the potential to be a really good experince however at this moment in time the only people who can get the most out of it are those who are bale bodied, if you have any mobility issues then you are going to struggle here. Just to clarify I am a wheel chair user.Once you have purchased your tickets, prior to accessing the cable car you come across your 1st issue. The only way to get to the cable car is by 2 sets of stairs, they actually have no lift to get you to the upper floor. I was able to struggle my way up and then the real fun began.One of the staff pushed my wheelchair through the cable car doors (the cars themselves keep moving at a slow rate) however she stopped with only half the chair in the pod and as the car was still moving, it pushed me out of the pod and then as my chair was effectively pinned between the doors it pushed me out of my chair. Thankfully I fell away from the drop and the staff were able to retrieve my chair before and damage could be done to it.My wife then took over and we were in and away and I have to say the view from the cable car is superb (even better at night as the pylons that support the cable car light up like mini Eifel towers) the journey itself takes around 12 minutes and it was worth it however when you get to the other side you still have the stair issue (though there was only a few)  Once in the park you see a range of amusements (all free with your day pass) and they could be considered a touch basic. You can choose to either go up the hill towards the animal enclosures or go down to the waterpark area. Sadly the only way to get down is by steps or escalators so if you have any mobility issues you will find yourself staying in the higher bit of the park which is not so bad however at nearly $50 per person you would expect to get some better value.On the higher levels there are plenty of food/drink stops and while the hill is steep I was, with some family assistance able to get up there. The view is superb, you overlook the bay back to the mainland and it is truly stunning. At this point my family went off to see the water park while I went around the animal enclosures which seemed relatively spacious and tidy. There is a good range of African and Indian animals such as lions, hyena’s tigers and a range of monkeys too. Most cages are viewable from a chair though certain pens such as the camels and ostriches were hard to see as there were steps involved.From there I went passed the extremely impressive big wheel (unfortunately too not disabled friendly) and up to Blooming Hill which is also impressive yet the domes that doubtless contain fascinating species of plant are also not accessible by wheelchairs.I won’t lie, I enjoyed my time though it was restricted by accessibility (so much so I went again during my stay in Nha Trang) my wife and child also enjoyed their time though they did complain that the waterpark which advertises that it’s open till 6pm effectively closes at 5.20.Also a lot of the buildings down near the water parks are empty though apparently they are quite new so who knows what it will be like when they are open.The water and light show looked amazing from the videos my wife got so I would recommend seeing them if you can.The height restriction for children’s tickets is somewhat suspect as they are charged as adults after a certain height (140cm I believe) and yet can’t go on some rides even if they have paid full price.The return journey on the cable car was just as much fun though we did get a lot of help both getting on to the cable car level on the island and getting my chair back down to the ground floor once we were back on the mainland.In conclusion I would say this, the park is not Disneyland, it’s not Universal Studios and it’s not even Sun City. If you build such a resort, on a hill make it accessible to all. It has the potential to be so much more. The biggest issue would be, that it needs to be brought in to the 21st century and the staff while pleasant enough are extremely limited in both their language skills and their attitude.It is a lot of a money to go with a family and I would advise anyone to seriously consider this though as I mentioned before, I did go back a 2nd time during my stay (would have been nice if someone offered me a free return visit after I was tossed out of my chair)

High entrance fee. Terrible service and navigation. Relative dirty everywhere. Dark and dirty looker rooms and showers! Nobody speaks English. Food expensive and little choice. Only one plus - no waves at the beach. Water sports prices unbelievable high. It's the best Park in Vietnam but one of the worstest Parks in the world. After all should admitted that  the Ferris wheel and Zoo is very nice!

Only went as our daughter had joined us for a few days and we all wanted to do the cable car. Cost 800k VD for the combined cost of cable car and park - so about £27 each. Therefore decided we would go for a wander round- only went on one ride - which was surprisingly good. However, when we went to look at other rides or shows they were either closed or only ran on the hour which was ridiculous as the shows were only 15mins long. There was a small zoo with varied animals - white rhino, Bengal tigers, lions as such but there pens were extremely small so while we enjoyed seeing them, we came away feeling guilty as they were doing a lot of pacing etc. On the whole i definitely wouldn’t recommend this even for families with small children as while the park looked clean and pleasant there was just really not enough to do.

Good if you have no kids...We have a 3 year old and he could do nothing,,,,,, If you ever go here make sure there are no chinese holidays on as they are  the rudest people on earth, The one thing we could do was the ferris wheel and just as we were going to get on t broke downThe  zoo was the one highlight

We went to Vinpearl purely to take the cable car.  Tickets quite expensive 800,000VND but if you utilise the theme park rides and beach then a good day out.We visited the Aquarium and noted the tanks were very overcrowded but it was still worthwhile viewing. Enjoyed the cable car ride and good filler for an overcast day!

I went in line onto a small roller coaster but the line was pretty long. I also noticed that it was wide and there had only been on cart taking people, usually there would be 2 carts. The line had nothing to make it straight and people kept cutting through the lines. There were practically no fans so I was soaked with sweat by the time I got to the ride. People also stand way to close to your back.

We went to visit vinpearl land (18th of July 2017) in order to experience some fun through the roller coasters and water park. On our arrival, we were definitely disappointed by the type of roller coasters but also the queue at interesting spots (min 1h30  to 3h waiting time just for a single ride... which reflects the bad and inefficient organisation of the park)! If you experienced other parks (Movieworld, dream world, Disneyland, Europapark, etc.), please strongly reduce your expectations!!! Nevertheless, the water park is pretty fun and refreshing. However, you first need to fight for getting a floater to make the rides.Moreover, the price of the entrance fee is very high (VDN 800'000/pax) for such a low entertainment quality. And food quality and price are on the European level but with low quality standards.Eventually, I would not recommend Vinpearl land.

This place is full of trash, old and dirty! If you had tried any other water park in the world, then this will be really big disappointment! Be aware of those water slide tubes, you might get cut while sliding down, because those tubes are too old already, and not even well maintained!The height limit here is too high, most of the games are 1.2 meters and above so if you're with kids they can mostly play at Kids Area only, kind of super boring! Definitely no going back!

This is a public place so crowded.  The guests are rude and simply take your beach chairs the minute you leave it to go swimming. When you told them that your bagpack and towel are on the chair, they laugh and replied PUBLIC and refused to get up. Call me difficult but I expect basic civility

My husband and I visited with my 5 and 7 year old. As they are under 110cm, they missed out on lots and lots of rides and water slides that had a 130cm height restriction. Yet Vinpearl were very happy to take the full child fee. Very disappointing! In saying that, the cable car to and from the park was lovely and is worth doing. The wave pool in the little kids area was also very fun. A couple of notes:1. Don't take snacks. No outside food allowed and they remove it from you and then you get it back at the end. 2. We bought our tickets from a drive by shop before getting there. They thought my daughter was under 100cm so she was free. Once we got through the queue and we're ready to board the cable car, we were stopped and she was measured. She was 3 cm over and so we had to pay the full price. 3. Bring your own towels
A lot of chinese and so crowded. Look like in china town. Not recommend for people who have young child like us

Went early in morning to waterslides, nothing appeared to be working as per the displayed times.Waited for 20 minutes no staff no rides.Staff speak very little English & really have no interest to help.Very dissapointing.

We visited Vinpearl land quite excited and left very deflated. The park itself I do think is good value as your one off charge of 800000VND gets you a return on cable car and entrance to park which is a theme park, water park, aquarium, zoo and some shows. But overall it's just not good enough. The theme park is really poor the rides are extremely tame compared to theme parks back in the UK. And the water park even though is a little better still doesnt hit the mark. We enjoyed two or three slides out of the lot, and the lazy river isn't lazy as you don't move unless you swim yourself. We didn't visit any shows or the aquarium so I cannot comment. One thing that was a laugh though, was the floating obstacle course on the sea. We had a good laugh on that for a while. I did notice a lot of construction on going so maybe there's a lot more to come from Vinpearl but at the minute it's just a bit "meh". We only ate at one restaurant but food was reasonably priced for a theme park and there seemed to be lots of choices.
Spent about 1 and 1/2 hours there. Not nice no shade so very hot.  No shops to buy anything just restaurants and few rides so good with kids. Will be a great day out when finished in July.

You may like it if you come with active kids or love water game. That's all.The zoo and aquarium are so poor (zoo has not been completely finished). All the rest is nothing.Sad to say but you should lie on the beach instead of paying time and money to go here. And it will take at least 45mins to queue up.

Wow talk about bank rolling the construction by opening it to the public. An enormous crane made its way through the crowd up to the hill. The Change room facilities were crowded and stank of urine. The water park was fun for the kids but the water quality dubious. There were a lot of rides closed which didn't matter to us they looked lame anyway. Best part the gondola ride over was the hi light. Our 5 year old son bought a gift from the many cigarette ash trays scattered around the place, they out numbered garbage bins 10/1. There's a 4 d theatre that has 2 shows a day, WTF with the times clashing with other stuff. If you still want to go take water and avoid the heat of the day it cools down about 4:30. It was SO hot and little shade that wasn't crowded. Disney its not so don't expect too much and you won't be disappointed.

The reviews on here I do not feel give the true perception of what Vinpearl land is actually like. But then again everyone has different standards. I've seen people commenting here and putting this Park in into Disney's league. They clearly haven't visited Disney correctly. The park itself has a lot of potential. However, it should not be open. The park is under major construction and it's a building site. They have built the frames and decorated all the facades on the front, however the insides of the building haven't even been started and in actual fact they are probably a solid 5 years from even considering being open. From what I can gather, it was originally just a water park and they've made a decent sum and now want to improve it. That's fine, but no one tells you it's a building site until you get there yourself and see the scaffolding, empty shell buildings and sand everywhere. Either way close the park, build it quicker and more efficiently rather than wasting people's time and money. But I guess the ticket sales are driving this project now. Water park can be described as OKAY. But not worth 650k dong. Alpine coaster is good and can be romantic in the evening with its gorgeous views. Remainder of the rides lack a thrill as they're very basic and now out of date. Splash bay is good fun and a mini version of total wipeout. Worth a visit if staying at vinpearl or visiting the park. Food, where to begin. Staff are useless, getting paid to sit and play games on their phone. Don't offer any advice, don't speak any other languages so any tourist that goes there ends up with overpriced tasteless sausages or a Magic Potato which I can ensure is not magic. They cook the potato stick, let it sit there on the side not covered up and then dust it in cheese. So by the time the customer gets its, it's a cold, powdered cheese stale magic potato. Not worth 50000vdk. Water show at the end was very good to be fair. You are sat in a huge amphitheater with about 300 other people watching coloured water being sprayed. But was a good showing. Trying to replicate what Dubai Mall have done. 4D ocean cinema at the end of the day would be good for kids. Quite funny watching Nicole the fish getting attacked by the Crab gangman. We did enjoy the day as you do everything you can to make the most of it. But just don't believe the hype like we did as you will be disappointed. The park will be good in about 10 years when finished. But right now it should be closed.

Visited 4 years ago and had a good time. This time there were long queues and litter. The beach area which was a nice relaxing place was untidy with litter just left. Lots of building work around. Wished we had given it a miss.

So we planned to swing over by cable car and have a family day at Vinpearl . . On the approach with the cable car it already unfold that there is plenty construction ongoing, and true out the visit the construction noise and dust was very apparent. Once in, we visited the aquarium' (that seems a bit outdated) and then headed to the water park. Once in the water park we learned that our son only "qualified" for one slide; too bad. So we headed for the beach, which was nice but with insufficient beach chairs. In the early afternoon we went to see the dolphin show, which didn't really impress at all. One trained dolphin and one "young" one who appears to be "in training". Well at least the kids liked it.On the way back we went to enjoy all the ferry rides; just again to find out that junior was to small; while I found out that you should not go there if you're above 1,90 m; as some rides are aa bit "tight".Overall, we might have another attempt in a few years once we could enjoy all the rides, especially water park.But at this time, I don't see the value for money to go there if your child is below the required height / age.

This place is too popular with Russians and Mainland Chinese which makes for an interesting mix of crowds. Often we get pushed and shoved around. This park is not a Disney although they play Disney songs. The best ride to go for first is the Alpine coaster. Do this ride and you won't regret it. Wait too late and the queue is 2 hours or so.Dogem cars surprisingly didn't have much of a queue. So we enjoyed that. One day is definitely enough here. Food options are satisfactory and watch out for a bus load of tourists from other countries who push and shove through queues. Whilst I say satisfactory we didn't find the food that great. However at approx 100,00 -150,000VND average per meal per person it's cheaper than eating at the Vinpearl hotel. Bring cash, don't rely on credit card. The shopping street is catered to Russian tourists and things not very practical to genuine amusement park buffs. Aquarium can be done in 30 mins. Whilst children enjoy it, it's not a Disney. Water park was better. Too pricey for what you get.
We thought that it should be en extremely park. Nice park. That it. Good for children. The only thing that was good, is the cable car, and the aquarium era
Only went for the cable car ride. Park itself was very average with poor advice as to what events were on. Dolphin area closed for renovation.
The highlight here is the cable car ride over. For couples it's a waste of time and 700k dong each. Expect lines, dated rides, grubby smorgasbords and a mish mash of people pushing to get around.

Vinpearl land looked exciting for a visit, so we decided to take our two children aged 6 and 9 whilst staying in Nha Trang.We were a little dissapointed, as the childrens entry fee ( based on height of 1m-1.4m, not on age) was only marginally cheaper than the adults entry fee, despite the fact that our 6 year old was upset at not being allowed on any of the rides due to her height (roughly just under 1.2m!!). We found that all the kids rides and water slides were boring for her, yet the ( conservative nature of the) amusement park would not let her go on any decent fun rides or water slides. To add to this, after rougly an hour and twenty minutes waiting for a turn on the alpine coaster, we were stuck behind a very slow person who wouldn't go any faster than walking pace!!There was also a great deal of construction work going on, and many of the attractions weren't open.We would not return to Vinpearl Land on a future visit to Nha Trang.

If you enjoy waiting the better part of 2 hrs to get in via cable car, this is the place for you! Small amusement park, expensive food, good sedate rides for smaller children, decent waterpark (when open), although the signs and operating times are not the same.
My girlfriend warned me that the cable car ride over is better than the park. Having visited today she was proved to be correct. As always

I guess it's up to you to make your own choice as if you're used to American amusement parks, you'll be disappointed. It's small, cute, and not very high tech. We went there only because I wanted to do the cable car ride and it included admission, so we checked it out. We did the mine ride roller coaster which was amusing. Not scary. Their most popular attraction is the alpine slide down the mountain. A small line warned of a 2 hr wait, so we didn't do it. It seems they don't have efficiency down pat there and it takes WAY too long for them to move people up and down the mountain. The other rides are on par with local fair rides.

If you are expecting a good amusement park then you will be disappointed with Vinpearl. We went purely to go on the cable car, which is included in the admission price of 650,00VND, and we did not expect much from the park. We did however enjoy the aquarium although we had to jostle against the swarms of Chinese tourists to get a view of some of the exhibits.

After spending a few days in Danang and experiencing Asia Park and Ba Na Hills Amusement parks I came to Vinpearl Land with high expectations. Especially after reading/hearing it been described as Vietnams Disney Land.Compared to Asia Park which charges 200,000 and Ba Na Hills which charges 550,000, Vinpearl charges a whopping 650,000. For a couple we spent over $100 Australian to simply arrive/get into this park so I was expecting a lot.We arrived on a sunny 29 degree day and after waiting in line for over an hour with a bunch of rude Russians whom had forgotten what a shower was we finally made it onto the cable cars. I wasn't really impressed by the cable journey as Ba Na Hills simply blows it out of the water.We arrived in the park and to my shock several of the main rides were closed due to maintenance! Eg the Alpine Coaster.We then walked over to the water park section and not even half the water park was open again in the name of maintenance! The aquarium was a saving grace though. Large, beautiful and many species of reptiles and fish to see.Vinpearl should have offered discounts today and informed patrons at the gate they were paying to not ride on rides but watch them get fixed by staff. Vinpearl should also offer refunds to everyone whom attended today to as charging 650,000 a ticket to experience a amusement park under going maintenance is an utter disgrace! Criminal!Head to Danang! Don't waste your time or money here! Head to Asia Park and or Ba Na Hills. Cheaper and more enjoyable!!
Too much money for nothing special.If you are from Europe it's no reason to pay and go there.tThe best place there is the beach that is very nice,and the waterpark was ok.

The cable car ride was great, definitely the high light of Vinpearl Land. Once you are on the island it was a little disappointing. The amusement park had good rides with minimal waiting times, except for the big attraction which had lines waiting for over an hour. The water park looked a little under maintained, most of the water slides were leaking from pipes or joins & one even had gaffa tape holding a pipe in the right place. The lazy river was the highlight of the water park. The wave pool looked very old with cracks in the flooring & was dirty with many leaves & sticks. When we entered the park we were informed that the cable cars would not be running between 5:15-6:30 that evening. This was fine & we timed our departure to leave before this. We arrived at the cable cars to leave at 4:55 pm & the cable cars were already shut off to people. This was annoying as we had cut short our trip specially to get the cable car back before it was shut. Overall I wouldn't go back. It was a fun day but out dated & a little dirty/ old & the cable car mess up was very annoying.

Great concept, lots for the kids and adults alike to do. PLenty of choice. Unfortunately we had a bad Experience With some of the entertainers. THree singers? people one of whom was so angry, yelling and screaming, it was very upsetting for the kids.

This park is not as good as you think. When we were there, the water park wasn't working, but nobody inform guests in advanced. So we payed full price for half a park. When we asked about it, they said that only at noon they put a sign on and if we got there in the morning, they could't know that it will be like this. Weird, because they gave everybody that day, including us, vouchers for food, to compensate for not running the waterpark (Oh, and when we came back on the cable car we looked for the signs - there weren't any anywhere).We tried to enjoy whatever else the park is offering, but this place is like a very old amusment park and some of the rides didn't work. Won't recommend it.

Entry fees were about $ 30 a person and still we discovered that some of the facilities are suitable for very young children and other facilities will be open "soon" as the dolphins pool.  Water Sports that could be suited for adults were not included. We decided not to pay the 690000 VD = 31+$

I personally feel that it wasn't that amusing after all. You pay 650,000vnd at the ticketing station and take a cable car/boat(if peak season) over to the island. Cable car ride was nice as you can see the view of nha trang. Arrived at the place and it was a turn off. It wasn't worth the money. Not thrilling enough! At the water park, you can rent a locker for $1 usd and keep your stuff in it, but the place was so crowded. Systems need to be change. You need to carry your own float up a stairs to play games. I felt like I was doing a lot of exercise carrying those float. Would rather spend the money for island tour.

I should have taken note of previous review. We paid $78AUD to got to this theme park and half the park/rides were closed due to maintenance. I think they should have offered half price tickets during this time as I felt it was a total rip! We left park feeling very disappointed only after an hour or so.  Good cable ride by expensive if only going for that!

I booked online via the park's website but once arriving at ticket office, I had to wait for 30 minutes without any explanation or excuse while other visitors only needed 5 minutes to buy tickets. Very unprofessional! Some parts of the park were closed for renovation. The best thing of Vinpearl I think was the water park, other places were quite boring to us.

came here with an open mind but after queuing for what felt like years at the cable car on the mainland even at 8.30 in the morning was such a buzz killer, even though we knew it would happen it still sucked.  the cable car was good fun though, probably the highlight.  once we reached Vinpearl we noticed that many rides/attractions had been closed for renovation.  the water park was probably the highlight but even then, one of the workers was racist towards us and wouldn't allow us to put a raft on the raft elevator and told us we would physically have to carry it up... (I'm talking like the huge 8 person round raft) although he was letting all the Asians put their rafts on the elevator but said that we couldn't. so we left the raft because it's way too heavy for 2 people to push up the staircase.  anyway as expected, beach was packed, food areas were packed and people were rude everywhere, just didn't like this place at all. was glad to leave.
The cable car was enjoyable but the water park itself was a bit chaotic and really lacked organisation. Locker room and toilet were also pretty dirty.

Other similar Vietnamese theme parks are better.  Hanoi water park is waaaaay better than this.  Also Bana Hills out of Danang is quite a lot better than this.  The rides are generally slower and lamer here.  The rollercoaster is slow and boring. And so on..
The rides are more for children and the water park was closed.  Prices are reasonable for an amusement park.  Food staff was quite rude.

Vinpearl was a different experience than expected. Most of the park is closed til 1/6-2016. We still paid full price but got lunch vouchers. Everyone got vouchers so the lunch cafe's were crowded and took us an our to pick up our horrible cafeteria lunch.The rides got closed around two o'clock because of an electrical problem so didn't get to try that many rides. Love the concept though, if only it was made good.

Have been before so was surprised to see the redevelopment that has been done and still going on! Good place for kids of all ages inc the adult versions. Rides and attractions are free in with the admission. Only way to get there unfortunately is by cable car as they have stopped using the ferries except for staff. This can be a pretty scary experience for some especially out over the sea when the breeze catches the car. I am used to the Tung Chung HK cable car where the cars have many safety features including hydraulic dampers. This is just a car sat on a single cable! My only criticism of this place is a lack of attractions where you can get away from the stifling heat and humidity. The Aquarium was stifling as was the amusement arcade for kids(as doors are left open). On the whole worth a visit ,places to eat for every taste. One again though the fast food places and Vietnamese food places were open air OK with a sea breeze but still air!!

Upon arrival in the morning we were informed that the water park is being rebuilt until june 2016. Well, we could have walked away but the kids had looked forward to this visit. So we entered anyway. Information on the website should be mandatory, but I guess they did foresee that most people act like us and enter anyway. If we had known that the water park was closed, we wouldn't have visited vinpearl.The cable car ride was nice. Beautiful views.The shopping street was nothing for us, we just walked through.The aquarium was ok, maybe half an hour of entertainment. Mediocre.The kids spent some time in the smaller rides, they were OK for maybe an hour and a half. After that we visited the indoor playground for a short time.The 3d photo gallery is a joke.The Alpine coaster was indeed cool, but the waiting line was so long even though the park was almost empty.We saw a 15 minute 4d movie, quite OK.And then we had lunch.And the rest of the shows were so far away in time (4 hours) and we could not spend any time doing new stuff. We had tried all that there was. The kids did some reruns to the previous small rides and then we returned to the mainland about three PM.So for us with kids age seven and five, this theme park has very little to offer - not even a show.

Don't go here until they finish it. The only good bit is the aquarium. The rides are rubbish, the food from yummy land is disgraceful and inedible. We were given food vouchers because the water park was closed, but still felt it was a waste of money. We were told we could go to the beach. You can't. They should have closed the park completely until the work was done.

Hi i recently visited Nha Trang April 2016 and was looking forward to visiting Vinpearl ,i wanted to ride the cable car over the sea and visit the water park but i called Vinpearl to ask if everything was open and was told that currently the Waterpark is closed ............

We knew that Vinpearl Land was under renovation but was still keen to visit - but for the cost 600000 VND per person I wouldn't bother until the renovations are complete. The cable car ride was great in that it gave you a great view over Nha Trang, but the rest of the park was very tired- you can see why they are renovating! We each received a 150,000 VND voucher per person to spend on food and drink but the food offerings weren't very appealing. It looks like once all the renos are complete it will be a great fun park, but I'd leave it till then.
We had entry as part of Island hotel package but many attractions are closed now. Check before you go.

This is supposed of the best Vietnam theme park. We all know the water park is closed but most of the normal rides were closed for maintenance too.The free arcade centre was poorly maintained. Most of the controllers were faulty so you really can't play the games properly.The aquarium was pretty good and as it has a very long underwater glass tunnel with plenty of sharks and stingrays to see.  The food joints were ok and luckily we had coupons for it.I think the whole experience was ruined by mainland Chinese tourists. It looked like there was 90% of them and rest were Vietnamese, Russians and Koreans. The problem with mainlanders is that they love spitting, smoking, shouting, and pushing in the queues.Luckily the 2 seater coaster was seriously fun, scary and dangerous. Queues to the all rides were short.So 'been there, done it and NEVER coming back'!

Went back for the 2nd time with my wife, April 2016.  Had a blast the first time but it was mostly for the beach.  This visit access to the beach was blocked.  I don't know when it will reopen and I was a little miffed that no one bothered to tell us when we bought our tickets.  The cable car ride is still nice, but expect little else than that and a great aquarium.  We were not interested in the dog and pony carnival rides and the food sucks but I do think it is almost free as your ticket earns maybe 300,000 VND worth of coupons toward the swill.

I visited vinpearl 7 years ago and sadly its no better. I think they are pumping money into there resort facilities instead of their amusement park.We discovered that the waterpark and dolphin show were shut and because of this we received a 150K dong food and beverage voucher to be used at vinpearl.On arrival we soon realized that numerous other rides were also closed "for maintenance". I'm talking more than half of the other attractions. Even elevators were closed so be prepared for additional walking.What rides were open had long queues. So prepare yourself for long wait times.Even getting food and drink wasn't an easy task as everybody is doing the same with there voucher. Here's a suggestion to vinpearl management...open up some more rides and that'll reduce the wait times in the queues...simple.I think the park is the cash cow for the resort....lift your game vinpearl.
We did not end up visiting, the waterpark is closed for a month, you still have to pay fill price but they offer you a voucher. We decided it wasn't worth it.

Ok so this place costs 600,000 VND to enter, which is a lot for travellers on a shoestring budget but anyhow it sounded quite fun/different to what we'd been up to so far and came recommended by our hostel so we went along. The water park was definitely the highlight, although the one in Ho Chi Minh is better (and much cheaper). The video games area was fun and all free which ate away at some time but on a whole the amusement park is quite basic. There are only a handful of rides which are not great. The main reason I wanted to come on here and review this place is to say that the small Vietnamese restaurant within the park was awful and the customer service pretty disgraceful! We both ordered chicken & rice, pretty basic meal in Vietnam, what can possibly go wrong? The plates came out with warm-cold rice and a half cooked chicken leg, literally looked like scraps that should have been thrown away. We both had 2 mouthfuls before convincing each other this may not be a smart idea to eat and called the waitresses over. We tried to explain that the chicken wasn't cooked properly and the plate was cold but none of them spoke very good English. We instantly asked for the bill and gave them money for the drinks and not the food. The waitresses were all stood round us demanding the rest of the money for the food (which we hadn't eaten because it was not cooked properly) after a few minutes of trying to explain why we weren't going to pay, we eventually gave up and handed over the money...not what I would have done in England but in Vietnam I didn't want to cause a scene/any trouble. If you do go to Vinpearl do NOT go to the Vietnamese restaurant at the bottom of the stairs within the park, 99% sure it was called Nha Hang. Trying to put this behind us, we head to the main 'ride' where you go up the mountain. We were in the static line for about 40 mins before we gave up and left the park all together after feeling pretty disheartened after the restaurant. If you're travelling Vietnam, arrive at Nha Trang and think this place is a good idea...I honestly wouldn't waste the money.

Our family of five visited Nha Trang and decided to go for this amusement park. The most expected destinations will be the waterworld and dolphin show. However, both of these areas had been closed for renovation until end May. The park had offered F&B voucher of VND150k per person to make up for this.However, this is a huge disappointment because we would not choose to go here in the first place if knowing this beforehand. There is no announcement on the park's website and no one from the sales agent or ticket office informed us of this.The park offers so-so games with no real interesting attractions. The new cable car trip from the top of the hill can be interesting. However, there was a long queue since this is the only hot spot in the park. In general, we had a disappointed experience and would never visit this place again.

Visited here today with my 3 friends all aged 20"s wasn't really the best experience, the rides are very dated would say they are more for children. Only a hand full of rides available, the water park is shut for renovation maybe if it was open it would of been a lot more fun.We eat from one of the food restraunts on the park, the food was appalling.  Chips were re-heated and stale, the chicken burger was processed and also very stale. The staff were also very rude who served the food. The best part of the day was the free games in the amusement arcade.

went to check it out today, 28th Feb 2016. Waterpark is closed for maintenance for a long time, about 1 month I think from today, so bad luck if you were going there for water slides. Taxi also stopped just before the park and a merchant tried to sell us tickets there, don't fall for that. So we didn't go and wasted some time.

The view of from the cable car is quite lovely, but the line to get on it was absolutely horrendous took at least an hour standing waiting. Rides at the park had enormous lines. We waited over an hour to ride down the alpine coaster and you weren't even able to go down at a constant speed having to regularly stop to immobility because there were too many people going down at once causing blockages. 3 minute descent took at least 10 minutes at very slow speed. Took the fun out of it completely Water slides are fun and there are heaps to choose from but not enough blow up toys available to ride down some of the slides.Overall crowd was pushy and a large majority of people cared more about selfies than they did about having a good day at the parkThe aquarium is mayhem. Would be nice if there was a continual one direction flow to people walking but they're everywhere and care more about selfies with fish than being in peoples walking path. If you're willing to wait in lines it is a good family day trip but be prepared or you'll be shocked

Vinpearl failed the high expectation of my family.  Many of the locals we met highly recommended Vinpearl.  However, we were  really disappointed at several places.First, the staff were fine when people cut in line, which happened every few seconds.  For example, during the lunch time, we needed to tell other customers to stop cutting in line.  Only between the restaurant took the order of our previous customer and we told them our orders, four group of people tried to cut in lines.  Another example was when we waited to get in the 4D theater, a teenager girl pushed me back so she and her friends could go first.  Still, the staff just stood there and watched.Second, signage and directions were not friendly to foreigners enough.  Besides, in their tour map, the park was divided into several areas with different colors, but the signage did not follow the map.Last but not least, we were especially disappointed by a lady at the 2nd ticket booth to the Truc Lam Pagoda.  The map said we could go to the pagoda by buying transfer ticket there.  However, throughout the park, there was no timetable!  There was no written instructions for the tourists to follow!  We went to the ticket booth at 12 o'clock.  The lady said we needed to wait until 13.  We sat beside and waited for one hour.  At 13, we came to the lady again, and she said the car was not coming and told us to "try waiting for another hour."  During the 1 hour we sat beside, she just sat there and she did not share the important information with us as she received the news that the car was not coming!!We were really disappointed by the staff!  Much to our regret, we put Vinpearl in our vacation plan to be the perfect ending.

Worth only if you travel with small KIDS, otherwise we felt surrounded by a lot of KITCH!Didn't like their amusment machines, expansive restaurant for Nha Trang, not very fresh seafood, maybe due of loe season ;))) Another spot, they had only chemical powder coffee, not beans coffee.Only diving and beaches otside the city worth in Nha Trang. We found for our 1st day Angel Dive with French manager, and expat instructors. Too many shops and tour agencies for Russians around. It seems they don't care about others, bad English spoken well, just look for rude fat russian wallets. No interesting 1 day tours, I heard other europeans rafting or villages tours are very weak.

If you've been to amazing water parks such as Atlantis in Dubai then this is disappointing. If you've not been to many water parks you may love it. The rides are not at all scary. The scariest part of the park is walking around because the tiles are sooo slippery! Whoever designed that made a big mistake. The steps up to the rides are all metal so tough on the feet. However, it's what you make of it and we had a fun day. The lazy pool was relaxing, the area wasn't busy (probably because it was a rainy day but that doesn't matter as you're getting wet anyway!) so we relaxed on a lounger (no cushions) for a short time and we walked around. The walk around was the best part as we found some beautiful spots. Don't miss the amusement arcade (called exotica, under a sculpture of a topless women which was a bit strange!) as most of the games are free and it was a bit of fun even though I'm not usually into arcade games.The cable car over to the island is spectacular and a little unnerving! Worth doing! We bought our tickets from the hotel at 600k and that covered cable car and whole park. The taxi to the cable car was cheap and quick. At the end of the day we walked back into town and (about 30/45 mins) through the local areas, sampling some roadside food and seeing things you wouldn't see otherwise. That was the best part of the day! Overview: go but don't have high expectations!

You know the feeling when you land in a place where it seems nice initially then things just not quite right ... That's Vinpearl Land. Tickets are 600K for adults and kids below 140cm are discounted. Otherwise they pay full price. Note most of the rides require a minimum height of 130cm. So the sweet spot are for kids between 130-140cm.First after queuing for half an hour, we were told the cable car was not working. Off to the ferry then. I'm sure it was over capacity, some nervous Chinese tourists began putting on life jackets.Upon arrival more than half the rides were not operational or they only worked on a half hour schedule then told to return in 30 mins. Maybe they did not have enough staff.The Underwater world was surprisingly good. Great example of tropical fish and some reptiles. The Mermaid show was a hit with young kids.Unfortunately the water park was a let down. You can rent lockers and towels but the system is chaotic. Throw in tourists from certain countries who don't queue and it is almost comical. They ran out of towels after lunch so my kids were shivering after their swim. No idea when they will replenish them either, basically the message was you should have got here early in the morning. There really isn't a change room. Just shower stalls and toilets with perpetually wet and dirty floors. You will have to stand, hold onto your things and balance everything so nothing gets wet or dirty. The young kids play area with fountains and mini slides look Fun. Some of the larger slides were not operational. Food and drinks outlet only accept cash. So you either carry cash ie. Risk theft or make the long walk back to you lockers near the entrance. They should implement a barcode wrist band where you can load so money and use that the whole day.Maybe it just was not our day. I think if you set your expectations low you will probably enjoy yourselves. So unless you have a spare day in Nha Trang or kids that love waterparks, I would give it a miss.

There were no workers or even signs to tell you anything. Rides are weak. Water park is confusing. No way to know what the water slide requires before you climb to the top of the stairs. So you get a stern no and a hand in your face from the guy at the top. Them have to go back down and look around for any sign of a tube and carry it long distances back the the stairs and up again. Beach was ok.

We didn't have huge expectations for Vinpearl as we have been to amusement parks such as Disneyland, Dreamworld/Movieworld etc. However we kept an open mind and paid the 600,000vnd entry. A few of the rides were ok but the service and food was terrible. There was no organization or customer service. The 4D show was corny and awkward. Maybe kids would like it. The aquarium was a positive. Really cool animals in there. If you have nothing better to do maybe go, but don't expect much.

We went with another family, primarily for the water park to cool off.  It was fun though extremely cheesy.  The water slides were actually a bit dangerous for "western sized" people.  It's a fun day and a nice ride over the sea to and from the park.  Most of the rides are pretty low on the excitement factor - they seem to reduce speeds so as not to scare people, so compared to established parks like Universal or North American amusement parks, this place is pretty tame.
A little tired looking but this was made up with the friendly Vietnamese staff who were always friendly.....eye opener!!!!!Got lost in the 80's it did.... but the ride across the water was fun!!!
