We are staying at the Vinpearl resort (which is amazing, great staff) we have visited the amusement park twice now, once for the park itself, second time for the water park inside. BOTH times we were greated at the main entrance with INCREDIBLY rude staff and security. They were extremly unhelpful and beyond rude. The security guard even became threatening to my sister, getting all up in her physical space and yelling at her when we wanted to pay with our credit cards, their lack of English didn't help either. we ended up having to get the staff at the hotel to help us out. The park itself, once we had finally gotten in, left much to be desired, a lot of construction (I'm guessing it's low season at the moment) the rides are basic and few and far between. The water park was worth a look for a few hours, if you can get your hands on the floating rings required for most slides (if they're open).Not to say it isnt all worth a look if you're willing to pay $40AUD (BRING CASH)
This attraction is expensive by Vietnamese standards but not very interesting. The Nha Trang city beach is nicer and free.

The ride with the cable car is beautiful, but it was very hot and humid on the way back. I took me about 30 minutes to do 3 rides and in water park, and there was almost no line! The slides are located very far from each other for no good reason. It's difficult to walk barefoot there as the floor is slippery and it hurts your feet to climb the metal stairs up to the slides. Some slides are only open in specific hours. There's 1 life guard for 3 slides located on 2 different floors. He can't handle the storm of 3 people coming so he always closes 2 of the slides and you need to wait. The park is huge, and I guess little children would find other things to do, but as a waterpark, it deserves the 2 stars.

Unfortunately we were incredible disappointed, you're charge to go the the amusement park and if you want to use the rest of the island you are then charged the same amount again. Resulting in quite an expensive day. but if this is a place you do want to visit, book a night at the resort. You'll end up saving yourself money and have access to the whole island and the park. The park is probably best for children, no real rides for the adults, food wasn't great. All and all quite a let down.
SOOO HOTThis place would be great for teenagers or a young family but don't waste your money for anyone older

Just returned home from vinpearl having rode limited waterslides due to the poor management which allowed tourists (specifically Asian) to retain their rubber rings for the duration of the day as there were no life guards at the swimming pools at the end of the slides which is not only a safety concern but meant that the rings were not shared amongst everybody to allow everybody to ride the slides. This wouldn't have been as much of an annoyance if the people were using the rubber rings but they were mostly lying by the side of their sun loungers and we were met with strong objection when we asked if we could use them. When we eventually got some rubber rings we were not permitted to leave certain areas with them however their seemed to be different rules for other tourists. When we were waiting at the bottom of the slide for a rubber rings it soon became apparent that the Asian tourists were reluctant to share their rubber rings with other nationalities. This ruined our day as we were keen to ride the waterslides however were unable to do so due to the poor management and ignorant people. Not only was the poor management visible in the distribution of the rubber rings but also in the fact that my children were pushed in front of by adults while in the queue for rides and food which was noticed by the staff yet noting was done. And to top it all off everywhere in the water park smelt of urine. The only good feature of the park was the beach however it is just as good as the beaches on the mainland and frankly I wouldn't waste your time or money.

Well we expected a "plastic wonderland" just like in any other amusement park all around the world, but what we go was worse than a cheap copy of Disneyland.Only one ride was attractive but you had to queue for more than an hour, as the line up system has been totally ineffective. No staff was able to answer simple english questions like Is this the "Alpine Coaster"? If you are used to european or north american amusement parks DO NOT GO THERE. The best "ride" is the cable car, which is not very spectacular coming from Europe. One thing to add, I have been shocked by the amount of shops selling cheap knick-knack ... I expected something different in Vietnam.All in all a big disappointment but only the tip of the VINPEARL Iceberg. Worst thing about the park is the "We love green" propaganda!! The "Deli Land" river is full of cans and trash can be found all over the park  - THIS IS NOT GREEN AT ALL!! Most "attractions" can not be reached by wheelchairs!!

We went to Vinpearl for our young children. Children under 1 metre free entry so although my youngest one got in for free there was not much activities for him (1m height restrictions for rides eg bumper cars). Entrance fee include cable car and majority of rides (you have to pay extra for JetSki etc in the beach area). Long queues to get into rides - after more than an hour for the "roller coaster" where you control the speed yourself, we decided to leave and do some other activity. We were late 1 min to the dolphin show - they lock the gates and it was the last show so we missed out SO DO NOT BE LATE. We enjoyed the underwater world though that was our highlight. If you want to find a tube to use for the waterslides in Waterworld - good luck! Pick up any you see lying around not in use. Once you get hold of one don't relinquish it. And getting the cable car back to the main island you might get annoyed at the locals trying to push their way in front of the queue!

As a theme park it was disappointing. Very small and not many rides of interest for older kids. Video game centre games were free (as you already paid entrance fee) and there was an aquarium. We missed the dolphin show, but heard from one of the trainers that for around $50 you can swim with the dolphins. Food was standard fast food variety. The majority of the people visiting were Vietnamese and Russians and they seemed to enjoy it...so maybe it wasnt aimed at us...maybe we are a little spoiled with better parks in the west.

If you take the time and spend the money to visit Nha Trang, Vinpearl is like a … why go?!! Personally, we did not venture to VN to check out an expensive amusement park. We were much better off exploring old Nha Trang, had a whole lot more fun and got to feel the local vibe … NOT possible@ Vinpearl. The cable car ride over to the island was OK, the best part of it. My advice: do not bother with VP unless you have that total mall mentality and are terrified of genuinely smiling folks wearing conical hats ...

We went to Vinpearl today. Cable car was great. 45 mins to line up to get on it then another 10 once inside the building and that was with a pre paid ticket. Once across we decided to try the luge type ride, an hour wait to get on that one. Our 5 year old son was 110cm so too short for most of the rides except the tiny baby ones in which he had no interest and his ticket was still 500k dong and ours 600k dong. Even the waterpark he was too short for the big slides. We took him on 3 anyway, no one questioned it. Line up for lockers, line up for food. Too many lines for me. Didn't bother lining for the dolphin show. We stayed 5 hours which was enough for 1 luge ride, something to eat, a walk through the marine aquarium twice and some time in the kiddy pool and a couple of slides.The good bitsAll seemed quite well maintainedCable car was greatUnderwater marine aquarium was excellentLuge ride was great fun and fantastic view, had there not been a huge queue we would have gone again.Many trees for shade, mostly mango trees with plenty of fruit which looked amazingA nice place but way too many people.

Sorry, but was not overly impressed. Though the location is ver nice I found the lack of good food to be very disappointing; not much to choose from and what was available was largely unedible. Enjoyed the waterpark but does not take long to do everything. I do not think I will go back.

A lot of open spaces a really empty feeling. Has a tendency to have a few things shut... Water park was okay, apart from that the only other thing really was bumper cars and an arcade with masses of arcade games (all free games and made up for the lack of anything else). Terrible food, take you own if you can! Desolate... More of a resort island than a theme park. Women at the coffee place next to the first beach entrance, as you walk in through the water park are the rudest in Asia... Go anywhere in else in the park to get a refreshment!!!

We had 2 days in Nha Trang, and the only reason we went here is because we were with local family that really wanted to.  Otherwise me and the wife would've preferred to just stay in the city and walk around.  Half a day is probably enough time, and I think we should've done afternoon-evening instead.We stayed mostly in amusement park section.  Rides are small and mostly for younger children.  Although, the locals seem to find them extremely exhilarating.  We probably had the most fun inside the arcade, where all games are free!We walked into the waterpark, but didn't have our swimsuits, so didn't go in.  It did look like it would've been more fun.  The aquarium was good.Food - save your stomach if you can.  Overpriced and tasted horrible!  Try to eat before or after you come.

Arrived at cable car station for ride to theme park. No power (Blackout for 4 hours) so 1 hour wait and we were placed on a "ferry"to take the water journey to the theme park. Not really an issue except for the 1 hour wait. Arrive OK 11am with the hour delay most of the rides were just starting. Do the water park first if you can because the wind comes up in the afternoon and its a bit cold to swim or do any of the rides. Note that most of the water park rides only operate at a time schedule during the day eg: hour on and 2 hours off and so on. Most of the other non water rides operate all day.The absolute worst part of the day was the trip home. The cable car was not working on the way over because of the power outage that day, which while extremely inconvenient was unavoidable on the day we went.  The cable car was due to restart at 1pm which it did. Unfortunately they had a big cruise ship in port that was leaving that same day and they needed to close the cable car from 4pm to allow the ship to leave. They did not advise anyone of this situation and on arriving at the cable car at 4.15 for our trip home we were advised that it was closed for the next 4 hours to allow the ship to leave. This would have been great information at the start of the day, during the day or just before it closed, but NOT HAPPY JAN at 4.15 when they have just closed it for the next 4 hours. No communication to any of the guests of this situation and many people stuck in the same situation in getting the smelly old ferry back to the other side AGAIN. Why did they not tell guests that this was going to happen after we missed out on the first ride on the cable car, to miss out on the return ride as well. For those reading this the cable car is the best part of the day.

If you like a theme park that hasn't been updated since the 1960's, then Vinpearl is for you.Not much action, a few ok rides in the 'theme park' and the waterslides were downright dangerous. No lifeguards, and I scratched the hell out of my back on one ride. Another time we saw two girls run into the sliders previous as the guy at the top obviously didn't check if the first two were out of the water.Good luck trying to get a water tube from the Russians. They seem to get there at 6am and hang on to them for dear life.. There's no such thing as 'you're next, here you go'.. It's all "mine, not yours" - but that's the fault of the Vinpearl for not having lifeguards or anyone monitoring the park.The aquarium is pretty cool though.All in all, I wouldn't go back again.PS - don't go on Monday's, as the Dolphin show is not open.

we paid 500000 dong for the entry after receiving a map of the park and all its rides...when we entered we found out that half of the rides on the map was not there, still being set up,  or under maintenance.  one of the water slides was so poorly made that it hurt our backs. if you are a small kid there is more stuff to do. for us however this place was overpriced and we felt somewhat cheated.

The highlight was the cable car ride.  Then it went direful from there. Very little in terms of organisation,  guides, interesting things for grownups. Food is decidedly average. The whole sad experience finished off with incessant and unavoidable Christmas music blaring from huge speakers every 20m.Christmas is over by 3 Weeks...!

The cable car was nice. If false advertisement is an artform, vinpearl is the Picasso. They promised a rollercoaster that goes upside down But there was no such thing. Also there was a lot of constructionwork going on. It looks good on paper But its Not worth the visit. Also the animals Are being held in small cages and they look really sad.

Nha Trang was not my favourite of stops in Vietnam. To be honest I would say bypass it altogether, but if you do find yourself there with little to do, a trip to Vinpearl kills times. The only thing that perked my interest was the cable car ride over to the island and back. Pretty cool. After that, don't expect a whole lot. The food is awful, the water park was good craic because there were a few of us and the rides are so so.Basically it is a way to kill time in what I found to be an undesirable stop off point.C

Don't visit if you've been to Disney,  universal,  six flags or a large carnival as you will be disappointed.  Cable car over provides some awesome views but once your are on island you are trapped in amusement park.  Food was horrible.  I will typically eat anything but felt ill after eating half a burger, which was served cold along with cold fries.

Nothing to do with children as most of kids water attraction were on work.Don t need to talk about the noise!!Beach is small and dirty, the beach showers didn't work.No elevators nor ramps for prams and people with disabilities.Not worth for now.Tickets: 550.000dong/person

Half the slides are still being built, the fairground I seen better ones in fun fairs back home. The dolphin and seal show just seemed cruel to the animals especially when you see the tiny cages they live. The beach is cramped and small. The only saving grace was the cable cars to the island does have great views.

It is really a great place to bring the kids, but aside from the shelves of bourbon behind the kiosk counters, it doesn't seem like adult amusement has been taken into account; just lots of basic kiddy rides. The cable car is absolutely magical though - it takes about 17 minutes to get from the mainland to Vinpearl land, and is beautiful both day and night. It luckily isn't so busy that you are shut up in a cable car with strangers; it caters to individual families per car.The island itself mainly caters to Russian people, and it gets very old quickly, because unless you are Russian, Korean or Vietnamese, you can't understand a darn thing anyone is saying. We went to watch the dolphin show at Vinpearl Water Park (most attractions under construction - only 4 main slides available) and the entire things was conducted in Russian or Korean (with lots of "everybody clap NOW!" screamed in high inflected voices for the English speakers among the audience). We had no idea what was going on, and were left feeling disappointed.

Monkeys and dogs in small cages then led out into a little arena to perform for the bunch of idiots that are stood there clapping and taking photos. Not cool. Also saw the handler pinching and hitting one of the monkeys. The animals seemed nervous of them.

We visited vinpearl and was very disappointed. Half the water park is closed due to construction (which should of been mentioned to us prior to going) the beach was disgusting with floating rubbish everywhere. The view from the beach is nice expect when you look to your right you have construction being done on the mountain. The cable car ride is good but looking down into the bay you get a taste of the floating rubbish. Will not return here.

After reading a few reviews we were quietly disappointed. Firstly Kevin P says food average so bring your own. We get to gate with big signs saying food not allowed so all was confiscated, thanks Kev !  Cable car ride gave an awesome view yet stinking hot. Half the land under construction, yet still pay full price....what the ! Slides were old and rickety, had to wait for slides as only one of the two were open at once due to one bloke manning both. Same as the park rides. Beer was hot, dolphin show was great ! Roller coaster crap as you are able to apply your own breaks hence had to go slow/wait behind the less adventurous...If able to have a clear run down I reckon it would be great. This joint will be great once complete, when ever that will be.

Be warned there is construction working happening throughout the amusement park. We went solely for the water park, which had five slides shut (just about half I think), and a lot of areas shut off with construction barriers. The aquarium is also under renovation. There is a large iguana isn very small cage and there are also a dozen or so turtles in a small, very poorly constructed tank. This is our second visit in the last two years. However, the construction and shameful conditions of some of the animals this time definitely ruined our day. At the moment I don't think it is worth paying the hefty (for Vietnam) entrance fee. We are also hoping the cages some of the animals are kept in are just temporary. The 'cheaper' ferry ride across to the island was unavailable with no explanation; resulting in us having to pay the extra for the cable car. The amusement park also seems to have become a very, very popular place for the Russian tourist.
Kids from 1m must pay 450.000 VND but cannot enjoy most of the attractions. From 1,3m OK, but not a great thing.

Vinpearl has great unrealized potential. However, money must be spent to make it a great attraction. The whole place looks tired and in need of an upgrade. They should have a look at the great theme parks in nearby countries and try to emulate them. Children will no doubt love it, but we felt there was little there for us. If you don't like water slides, there are a few large carnival type rides and a nice aquarium. It is inexpensive, but the park itself lacks value. Even the avenue of shops is overpriced. Best thing about it is the cable car you must use for access. It is reportedly the longest cable car setup in the world (they claim) and the views are quite spectacular, almost worth the price of admission in itself. You are in trouble if you are disabled, as there are lots of stairs and no ramps. Maybe OK for a family with young children.

First i will state that if you are in Nha trang for more than a few days then come to vinpearl as their is not much else to do in nha trang apart from the beach; especially if you do not go out of town or up the coast. However Vinpearl was heavily disappointing. Firstly all of the rides close every 45 - hour, as their are only about 30 people that work at the whole attraction for thousands of tourists (exaggerated but you get the point). The amusement park is very small with only one decent ride. The water park, while amusing, would close each water slide every second hour meaning that the water slides was heavily overcrowded. this was very frustrating especially due their was enough demand for all the slides to be open, they seemed to be closed due to lack of staff or other reasons i do not know about. While i understand this is vietnam... for the amount they charge in comparison to the water park in Saigon, it is far more expensive. Secondly and most frustratingly was how everything was shut every second hour, which was very frustrating. Good for a day but yeah, i feel as if my expectations were too high and thus made it seem possibly worse than it really was.

VinPearl has the potential to be a great theme park. This being said, the staff here and the way it is managed, makes it a place I would not recommend and would definitely not return to. The rides here were quite good and well maintained. However, Russian and Vietnamese are pretty much the only languages spoken at the dolphin show and many other parts of the park. Do you realise how many english speaking travellers there still are? I found this pretty naive of the management to assume we all speak Vietnamese or Russian. I also found most of the staff were very rude and treated you with disrespect. Especially the lady at the Alpine ride, in the photo booth after the ride. Raising your voice at a tourist never goes well mate, not when I hadn't done anything. Just cause you have a bad day, dont ever take it out on customers. The amount of locals here also makes it quite disappointing and frustrating. They push in everywhere, thinking they obviously have some right to be ahead of tourists all the time. Yeah, not really my cup of tea. Theme parks in Indonesia were a lot better than this. Nothing really special about this placeCheers anyway VinPearl
Maybe in the middle of summer this place would be awesome but who knows.500,000 dong to get across via cable car and full access to park is reasonable value I guess?Almost worth a look. Almost.
I honestly feel this is a great park for the locals but quite disappointing if you are looking for Disneyland in Vietnam. Food village good value if nothing else

Vinpearl did have some good aspects. The cable car ride over was fun and had a great view. We went on a Saturday in December so it was reasonably busy however each of the rides were only open for half an hour at a time. The rides were very dated and not great. The biggest turn of for me was the fact that they had an animal circus. If you don't enjoy seeing innocent animals being treated very poorly then I'd suggest you don't go to Vinpearl. Had I of known that there was an animal circus then I certainly would never support such a company. Many of the people visiting were extremely rude. There were very few sun beds by the beach and if you walked up to one that was completely empty and absolutely nobody close by then a group of tourist would start yelling at you in Russian. They were very intimidating. There should be way more sun beds and it would be nice if they had cushioning rather then just wood! The water park was similar to the amusement park and we were disappointed to find that the lazy river was shut and looked like it had been for a very long time. Not worth the visit and would never go back!

Most random experience of a "theme park" I've had. To be fair the best thing about this place is the alpine roller coaster gives you good views of the islands surrounding. Possibly a good day out if you have small children to keep them amused. Water park was below average and the slides only opened at certain times aswell as the theme park. And as the title says it's a Russian paradise but the full of nha trang is pretty much.

Really liked only alpine road. No transport inside park, no lifts in waterpark make you tired very soon. Decorated with tateless sculptures. Water attractions are really dangerous, i have got more or less thraumatical experience with everyone

We came here, expect something extra-ordinary. But no, what the give us in the very annoying heat is a not beautiful place, unnatural and bad service. Pay around more than 10$ each but none of us can satisfy with this.The nice one is the aquarium. The rest is normal and underexpectation, alot.Ah, one more thing, the cable to here is beautifull too.

We read the reviews but felt we still need to try it for ourselves.  The aquarium was great and the toboggan ride too but that is where it ends.  It's like walking into an 80's time warp with bad music.There are quite a few stairs so it you are challenged with mobility, don't go.

The water park is fun but the other attractions very disappointing. The dolphin show was only on once early in the morning, the 4 D show was a waste of time and even our 12 year old declared it was the most pathetic thing he had ever seen! Most of the free amusement machines didnt actually work and there was a monkey and dog show where the poor animals were tied up so tightly to chains that they could not lie down in their cages with horrible nylon outfits on to add to their misery in the heat. It was utter animal cruelty and tragic to watch.There is only one main restaurant which was packed so we ate the fast food which was utterly disgusting- despite advice against doing so our kids ordered the pizza which was tasteless and bright orange and the the hot dog which was beyond disgusting. The cable car ride was nice though and the children (and us as waterpark fans) enjoyed the day out but mainly because of the water park which has a very good selection of adrenalin slides, many on 1-4 man rings/tubes/rafts which justify the entrance fee

If you go to the waterpark don't expect to be blown away. We made the mistake of going during Vietnam school holidays and on a saturday. The best part was the cable car ride over. You basically have to fight for a tube which you need to go down most of the slides with, people hang onto them like they are gold (even though they are meant to be left at the bottom). We probably wouldn't bother eating any of the food from the 'fast food' places which are everywhere. If you are very pale and wearing a swimsuit, prepare to be ogled and feel violated while young local boys start taking photos of you like you are a zoo animal. All in all we feel like we wasted half a day at this place, it was average at best. Maybe good if you are looking to entertain kids, but as a couple we became sick of the place pretty quickly.

heavily disappointed! not somewhere i'd go ever again. the ride controllers were non existant, we rode on 3 rides as everything else was closed. The lazy river pool was ok, but pool was dirty and in need of desperate cleaning of park grounds. I'm sure it would have been better in summer.
Getting old, you will have completed all in 1 hr. The water park is a little more fun, but also getting old. Very clean

This place is definitely showing its age now.  We traveled here in jan, 2013 after being told it was a "must do" whilst in Vietnam.  It's a nice family activity I guess, a place for the kids more than the adults but seeing as though we don't have children it probably wasn't worth the money. Having said that, the cable car ride provides a great opportunity to see the area from above and once on the island, the toboggan ride was good fun, even for the big kids:) If you're on a budget, I'd give it a miss. If you have a bit of time to spare and don't mind spending the money you could pay it a visit. I won't be going back though.

I think for Vietnamese to expensive. First of all the cablecars : they are from good quality and quite new, but no air circulation.  You sit in the cablecar both ways for about 20 minutes baking in the sun even with only 32 degrees.  Why they don't invest in a simple fan I don't understand.  No enough windows which can be opened. After arriving it is beautifull shaped, but very old fashioned for Westerners.  They have this kind of amusement parks with much better and more fun to do in almost every country in the world. What I really didn't like was all the overpriced shopping-area which you have to pass before you arrive at anything interesting.  Very long distances and to many stairs, so its absolute not suitable for elderly or small children.  The Vinpearl Underwater World is very good and worth a visit.  They made this fun to do on two levels.  The Waterpark is something else, they only open at 10 am, which is to late.  All attractions are fun, but again old fashioned. Be carefull the maintenance is not what it should be & the park is not responsable.  Of course another bumper, not everything is included in the very high price for Vietnamese understanding.  You have to pay extra for some fun entertainment.  Last they have to many staff running around doing absolute nothing.  They could save some money and reduce the ticket price if they have less staff.  Conclusion : A poor copy of Western versions, overpriced, too long and difficult distances between the attractions, useless & overpriced shopping area.
a rip off fun park with a tired looking ride.Most rides are closed off in non peak period.Do not go there and expected Disney land type of fun

The entry fee is cheap (£15/~$25) that should have been a sign that the park itself wasn't going to be great. The water park is downright dangerous in places with very few lifeguards on duty. We saw a number of people getting hurt including one in our group who ended up with a nasty concussion.A lot of attractions seem to be closed for a fair proportion of the day.Food and drink is a little overpriced but still significantly cheaper than home.On the day we visited they were not running the boat service, just the cable car which wasn't ideal.

The cable car trip to the island is great and sets your expectations of the park way beyond where they should be. The fairground rides are very ordinary (when open) and are only for bigger kids over 1.4m tall.Most of the water slides are also aimed at older kids over 1.4m tall.. Our 7 year got quite upset when he discovered that he couldn't go on most of them.You can't take any food to the island and the food on offer is absolutely shocking. Our 'Pho' was made with instant / super noodles and the kids burgers were disgusting.Oh, and the place is chock full of Russians.To be frank, I can't think of anything very good to report.

Cable car ride to the part was great, but after that things we not quite what we expected. On the day we visited, very few of the rides were operating, and in the water park most slides were closed as well. The wave pool was fun for the kids and they really enjoyed jumping in it. We ate fast food which was incredibly poor quality (probably should have had something else, but the kids insisted..)The location is very nice, but somehow it is now run that well. A pity.
There are only few atractions and they are oldschool. You can not bring your own food and drinks to the island.

First the minuses...Animal cruelty show. Staff unfriendly and unhelpful. Only fast food available and no vegetarian food. The waterpark is dangerous. I stubbed my toe on a broken drain. My friend smacked her head in the wave pool very badly.  When seeking medical assistance, the 'dr' had no idea what we were saying and just handed over three mystery pills. The lockers costed money and were behind the counter, rather then being a desposit. Also, once you had handed all your belongings in, you couldn't revisit your stuff throughout the day unless you paid more money. Pluses ...the cable car over there is worth the ride, but bear in mind if you do get injured it will take you a good 15 mins to get across back to land, and then a further 10 mins to get to the hospital. The aquarium was very good, I can't fault that. Could be a fun day if you want to risk it.

We were very concerned about the lack of safety. There were not lifeguards at all slides! And some of the slides were dangerous and worn out - we saw quite a few persons got bruises and cuts and hit the heads on the slides. The lifeguards didn't seem to know/be interested in safety. If you go there be careful. Would not recommend this place. Poor food also.

I wasn't expecting a lot from this amusement park, but it really did end up still disappointing me. The cable car ride to Vinpearl was pretty interesting, but the fun kind of ended there. There was a long line to just get into the park that took over 15 minutes to get through. I enjoy bigger thrill rides, and there unfortunately were none that existed. The rides were a step up from carnival fare, which isn't a major problem, but you may be disappointed if you are looking for more. Furthermore, several rides were shut down. On top of that, most of the rides looked fairly dilapidated.After perusing the rides, we went into the aquarium. The aquarium was actually pretty decent and better than I expected. It didn't hold my attention for very long, but I think it could have been very enjoyable for younger children. Finally, we visited the water park. This was the worst part of the park. The place was mobbed and very disorganized. We wanted to try some slides, so we notice that everyone is walking around with their tubes. We went to go find tubes, and could not find where you pick them up. My wife finally asked a kid where he got his tube, and he told her that you just have to find one anywhere and pick it up. Soon, we found out that their is no organization to this, and you have to wait for the rare chance that someone drops their tube and you fetch it before the five other people running after it. I wasn't going to play that game, so we just decided to relax on the small beach nearby for a while before leaving.All-in-all, if I had a family with younger children, this may have been 3 or 4 stars. Then again, I would imagine that my children would be very disappointed that they couldn't enjoy the water park. For around $25 a pop, this was very poor value, so I would only recommend this to anyone looking to blow half a day or so.

First, they open your bag and take away your food and drinks, that you have to buy them in the expensive park. Many attractions are closed, some are in bad and dangerous condition. My girlfriend hit her head in one of the water slides and I cut my foot in one of them. We saw some other people who hit their heads too. Staff is pretty unfriendly. Locker costs extra...

That's the only reason we went, it is the longest cable car ride over water in the world.If you have children with you it's ok.  We are great fans of Aquatica  and Universal in Orlando and this certainly does not compare although the publicity leads you to think it does.

The amusement park was great, but spoilt by the poor quality of food and service.  We ordered our lunch which took 3/4 hour to arrive the chicken in lemongrass and chilli was so hard and chewy it tasted like it had been reheated ten times, the seafood fried rice was just bearable if you are hungry.  We were very disappointed as we have heard heard so much of the name Vin pearl and Been traveling through Vietnam for the last month eating out every day and this I'd by far the worst meals we have had. Very disappointing.

Liked the Amusement park...   Not so much the food poisoning that came that night. Yes I am 100 percent sure that it was from the chicken burger ( the only thing my partner didn't eat that day, he is fine)Overall Park - Fun and amusingFood - Horrible. Pity you can't even bring any in. Bad formThis review is a low score due my overall experience....

We stayed 1 night at this resort. We were very dissapointed. Not all of the waterslides were operating and when you did climb up the very hot stairs you were met by a grumpy staff member who told you it was closed. The all inclusive meal package was a waste of time. The banquet dinner was like a fight to get to and when you did get to the food it was all cold. The range of food was good but I was not prepared to eat cold cooked chicken or pork (Dangerous!) The place is full of Russians who are pushy, rude and never smile. I could not wait to leave the place and regretted actually staying on the island. The room was clean and comfortable and the staff on the mainland reception very helpful and friendly
everything was closed. no tubes availaible, staff absent...

The place is very beautiful. I have enjoyed walking and making pictures. The park is located in the Vinpearl island. You can reach this island by cable car. The view is fantastic. I have made a lot of beautiful pictures. Then I've enjoyed a lot just sitting and watching the sea. Unfortunately, there are still things to work on it. For example, food village.I was starving and wanted to have a meal. I found restaurant named food village. There were tables outside with great sea view. But some workers were making some repair work with a lot of noise. When I explained to waiter that I can't have a meal in such noisy place, she instead of stopping their work has suggested me to have a meal inside. Anyway as far as I was starving I went inside to second floor and had meal there. Then when I wanted to buy some water (0,5 L water without gas) at small cafeteria number 13 it took 15 minutes to explain them what I want. And finally they suggested me to go to another shop  with fast food.The last thing, I had to much sun that day and did feel bad. I went to medical service. And there were nobody. It was closed. I've tried twice. Thanks God, I found and bought cold water. It made me feel better.I think that there should be done more efforts on teaching English for stuff and they should care a lot about medical services. It's very important in such sunny place.

My wife and I went to Vinpearl on our way Through Nha Trang and were disappointed as the rides were rather lame and half of them weren't even open. The water also looked and smelt dirty and over-chlorinated.
we went there on a Tuesday and most of the facilities for kids were closed, the restaurants as well, we only could see the fish aquarium - the rest slept.
Many of the rides were closed and there was very little available in terms of food-drink.

We went over on a very windy day.   The highlight of the place was the cable car ride over and back.   Not many people there, some rides not operating.   Food at the restaurants there (the one we ate at anyway) was very ordinary,couldn't even get a cup of tea, even the rice paper rolls seemed to be a bit of a problem.We only went over because we had crap weather in the town and seeing as we had never been over on the previous two visits to nah trang we gave it a go.   They are trying hard but it just doesn't, work for me.   The water slides are activated on a timed roster system.   On a good day they might be fun.   ,t

We (two 24 year olds) visited Vinpearl on a gorgeously sunny, hot day in the wet season. Taking the cable car to get there is fun and gives good views. I found the price steep, especially for Vietnam and so the park was relatively deserted with only a handful of hopeful holidaymakers there. The water park is what we were most interested in: it had some good rides such as the Tsunami (steep u-shaped ride) and standard flumes that you ride on inflatables and a forward-facing matt ride. It also had some that battered the back somewhat! The rides felt unmaintained and there were only a couple of attendants. Rides are open for set time periods and quite spread out so it can feel as though "everything" is closed. We got half way up one tower to find the flumes had just been closed and the attendant moved to another tower to open other rides with the queue following. From the 2nd tower he couldn't see when it was safe to go because he was on the top floor and 2 flumes were on the floor below. Many of my issues with the place are minor and we had fun but it took hard work. Someone had the brilliant idea of putting metal grating for the flume towers which was quite painful to walk on so that the fun of the flume may not be worth the climb! Likewise the lazy river had some odd material that would have been very uncomfortable to bash into. For a complete contrast go to Dam Sen waterpark in Saigon; very well maintained, only 100,000 dong ($5 / £3.50), just as many rides, lots of staff, locals instead of just for tourists (but not too full on weekdays) .Perhaps we should have spent more time in the amusement half with the free arcade and couple of rides. The looked fun, but it was late in the day and the man manning the rollercoaster/pirate boat loved to close slightly early when he saw people queuing/running towards the ride as these were open for half an hour at a time. So in conclusion, it may be fun and worth a visit but I have been to better, cheaper waterparks in both the UK and Vietnam!

If you don't mind paying the 320,000 Dong for a cable-car ride then fine. Hugley disappointed with the state of the place considering the relatively high entrance cost. It's all a bit dated and past its 'sell by' date. Needs massive investment. Food rubbish and overpriced.Sorry.

Went on some rides and waterslides, but found the place very bland compared to other theme parks and water parks. Besides for some very overpriced food, there was only some fast food (really not good). The beach is really fake. Dont waste your time or money.

I write in part of the children's pool:The condition of the pool is three, the mud, the bottom is patched.Moreover, after staying in the children's pool a couple of hours later they discovered that all the clothes had turned orange. And that color is not washed away. I don't know what kind of chemistry is added to this pool, but it's definitely not worth taking a kid there.

The park is very nice for the older ones. However, children under 1m20/1m10 are left behind. The water park made my 6-year-old daughter very envious all day but almost all the activities were reserved at most 1m30 or even 1m40, very frustrating pity.For rides, the same thing, the few attractions for children <1m10/1m20 are rare and too baby for middle-aged children.Too bad that no information is well specified at the entrance.For lunch we ate at Yummi world garden. Not great, the meat was very very fat.The zoo is nice, so is the Ferris wheel

I came for the first time in September 2024. Back this time for a day. I was disappointed with the 2025 tour, because while there were fewer visitors the Waiting time at the main attractions was very long, I even had to give up doing Alpine Coaster. Vinwonders Parks should be guided by the large international parks that welcome thousands of visitors in accordance with safety rules. It remains a pleasant park.  Objectively to spend a full day there you have to go to the water park.

I like the faceID entrance system and the option to buy a ticket with a discount if you stay in one of the island’s hotels, the park is huge and great to walk around. Navigation is challenging for the first time, maps around will help. WiFi worked well. The number of people is linked to the season, weather, and public holidays. On a cloudy Sunday, you can expect fewer people than on a sunny Monday. Prices for drinks and treats are relatively low (much lower than in hotels nearby). Dislikes: In August 2025 the half of the dry park was closed for renovation. The biggest frustration is linked to the aqua park. To get a key from a locker and a towel you have to stand in a queue for approximately 20-30 minutes. And they accept only cash deposits to get both without the ATM around. Second sad news - there is no area with the sun-beds near a big pool to chill, no areas with grass, nothing. Only benches. A limited number of sun-beds are available at the beach for an extra charge.I kindly ask the management to automate/digitize the lockers/towels service as you did with the faceID entrance to the park.

You want to get tired of walking around the rambling area from ride to ride? Wait in line for an hour? Constantly seeing the disgruntled faces of the staff? Here you go, then. The area is beautiful, lots of photo locations. That's it. And if you suddenly weigh even one kilogram over 100, you won't be allowed on a water park slide. My spouse is over 190, weighs 103 kg and it turns out it is a ban on coasters 🤦🏼‍’”️. What's the logic? Neither the park manager nor the staff on the coaster could clearly explain! You can't do that. Kids under 140 centimetres have nothing to do there either. They don't. The staff on the slides are in principle rude to visitors.  There's a lot of people, the Vietnamese are queuing, there's a lot of locations, but they're kind of rambling. The water park closes at 5:30 p.m., and interestingly enough, a lot of people had just arrived. At the checkout they were quietly accepting payment for storage, towels and other things, deposit for towels, without warning that the park is now closing 🫢.  If I knew what was coming, I'd have had a better day on the beach by my hotel. And you, if you've been to similar parks in other countries before, you certainly don't have anything to catch. Don't waste time and money.
Heat, queues, oceanarium down. Lived in a hotel on the island, so went. And if you drive on purpose, there is nothing to do there.

I was so excited for VinWonders but it is an absolute waste of time. The queues are up to 3 hours long, the under water world is closed, no submarine, you need to pay extra for the zip line and alpine coaster and they sell out each day. There really isn’t that much to do for older kids. I wouldn’t come again.

Drove with his family at 22.05 in Winpearl, Nha Trang, as a result of only negative impressions.I'll start with the fact that exactly a year ago I was in Winpearl on Phu Quoc, I had an incredible experience, I really enjoyed it, there were few people, everything new. But the amusement park in the natchang upset for several reasons:(I'll start with the water park)1. Children under 130 cm. absolutely not allowed anywhere. do not even count on any slide if you or your child is below 130 cm, which is very strange, since the entrance ticket costs not little money even for children.2. Very old coaster. When you roll, your back hurts a lot, because of the joints. I got my back. I've discouraged skating.3. Some coasters were closed, just not working.4. The variety of slides is very small, there are no tall and interesting slides.(Attractions)1. There are only 3 types of attractions that are included in the ticket price. I can't say much about them, just like the slide was boring. Only cart rides on the mountain were enjoyed because of the beautiful view.2. There's a very long line for these same carts, but I'll write about that later.(Park and Zoo)1. Most common small park and zoo. The animals are well kept, it was interesting to watch them.(Organization, queues and other)1. It really didn't like that there wasn't a single sign in the park. There's a navigation problem.2. There were big queues, both for the entrance to the island and for the slides and other rides, but think it depends on the time of day and season.3. It was confusing that the food voucher was supposed to work everywhere, but one diner refused to accept it.4. The service was very good, the good-natured park staff smiled and prompted questions(Conclusion ; Outcome) I wouldn't recommend this amusement park, but if you don't look at the rides and the water slides, you can go for the zoo and the park, but the cost to me doesn't justify it. For me, he only gave me a negative impression, since my youngest daughter, who is 5'8", was not allowed on any of the slides in the water park. I also tore my back off one of the slides.Water park needs renovation :)

It was okay, but if your child is under 80cm, don’t go.They are not allowed on anything, not even the Ferris wheel. It was disappointing that we had to stand and wait while we took it in turns to go on simply things with the older children.Also much is closed  and not working.

Driving as a family. Fourth time. (last time with a gap of 6 years)It's gotten considerably worse.Some of the rides don't work (there aren't many of them already), so some of them just don't work and others are gone.Changed the place of arrival (previously, the exit was immediately at the amusement), now you have to wander and find a way to climb to them. Maps are not informative, they are hard to navigate.But shops and cafes were stuffed on every corner.The Ferris wheel didn't work until about 2 p.m., I take it it it's not on for long.Oceanarium under reconstruction until spring 2026.Subjectively, cafe prices have gone down.The overall impression is negative.

We had a great day until we entered the waterpark, firstly we paid for a locker, used it to get changed, but when we went to get on any of the rides we were told we are too old. You see you need to be under 50 to go on the water slides. The funny thing was that if you can’t swim they give you a life jacket for the waterpark. I can swim very well and I am very fit, but refused entry. Just because you are over 50 does not mean you’re ready to hop in the grave. Come on respect your elders.

The water park is generally not bad, although the working hours of the slides vary, a lot has been closed. The amusement park was disappointing, I don't see any point in going to it at all. Such attractions can be found in any seedy little town.  On the zipline, the queue at 3 o'clock at the same time the administration has decided to make extra money from it and charges VND 200,000 per person not to sit in line but to arrive at the scheduled time. Free of charge, you're assigned a time too, but you can't go anywhere and it's suggested you spend all day in line. Absurd and some sort of divorce with money from the park and extremely strange treatment of customers. Navigation through the park is disgusting, everywhere you need to specify and ask because the signs are completely unclear what and where is. Of the good cool botanical garden and evening show, liked also in the oceanarium fish feeding, starting at 17:00.

Bought a cable car ticket +given a food voucher 100.000 ,said you can eat anywhere where the sign is hung.But encountered that many places simply refuse to take vouchers ,although they have a sign (that they are participating in this promotion).Attached is a photo of these places.

If you are heading to VinWonders with children there are a few things you should know - particularly in regard to the water world area. There is nothing for the kids under 130cm to do, regardless of whether or not they are good swimmers. We are an Aussie family, grown up waterskiing and at the beach, and the kids were not allowed to do any of the slides or rides in this area. They couldn't even do the wave pool with us, without wearing an oversized life jacket - which in itself is a safety hazard.  They were only allowed to use a small 'water play area' with some mediocre slides. Our children are 8 and 10. Great swimmers. But unfortunately they were told they were 'babies' and not allowed to go on anything. As a contrast - they could do the majority of the rides in adventure world and fairly land, which was great for them. Albeit I would question what was the greater safety hazard... The park is a great experience nonetheless, but caution if you were expected To chew up time at the waterpark and your kids are under 130cm.

Nice to have seen, mega big and something for everyone I think, but what a terrible staff at some slides and attractions.There was often a push by ‘we think’ the locals because they could communicate with the staff and the staff here do not speak English. At one of the eateries everything was gone except 1 dish and the guests next to us could order everything 😅 Unfortunately, we both got sick of the high chlorine content and the dirty water in this park. We thought the 'amusement park' was okay, toboggan run very cool experience! If you do go, buy water shoes because you have to take off your slippers ‘almost’ everywhere, we were glad we had them!

Due to the heat we could not enjoy the trip, it was impossible to move from place to place and look for shows, and so on. May be it’s easy with bigger kids.The lines were very long and kids were just getting more tired and so did we. A bit chaotic system for getting the lockers, towels, after that delivering again- lines, lines, lines. We just did couple of swimming pools and king’s garden, and decided to go back to the hotel.

The park is very overloaded, half of the rides are not working. You'll have to wait in line for about an hour for popular slides like the sleigh ride or the steam train ride. At the same time, there is a queue in the sun and without fans. There are many slides in the water park, but they are all approximately the same and only 20 percent are open at the same time.

If you want to swim on the beach, then this is definitely not the place for you, it is small, the water is dirty, like the rest of the sea. It seemed that the beach was aimed at children, it looked shallow, but no, the entrance is not gentle, but does not go down very smoothly. Beach rating 1 out of 5.Water rides, like everywhere else, I’m not a fan, I haven’t ridden. The park is large, there are generally not many people, despite it being Saturday, there are a lot of beautiful plants, which is a plus, and there are a lot of benches everywhere. To leave clothes in a locker you need to pay separately, for a regular box 30,000 VND. But I think since you charge a considerable amount for a ticket, lockers should be free.

Went on Sunday, March 3, played indoor games, went out to play 3 3D games, three staff members were very enthusiastic, guided how to play and wear glasses so that players felt comfortable, friendly and responsible.However, the racing game next door has 4 racing screen seats, but the last one is broken and can still play with 3 people. The employee is quite rude, not very friendly, and does not give instructions. devoted. (March 3, around 9:45 a.m. to 10:15 a.m.)Specifically, when I just sat down to play, I didn't know what to do. Why didn't she just jump in and tell me to put my foot on the brake? Then when the car stopped, she told me to press the button and said. Click and ask me to click again. It's obvious that I'll just do it, but my attitude and voice are so annoying. I'm not the manager of this game but I know everything. But it's not like I was proactive, just before I sat in and struggled to figure out why, I raised my voice and sat down, or the car stopped and I was struggling, or just pressed the button, just waiting for me to get confused and then just click :)While playing those 3D games, the other girls guided me through each step, wearing glasses and a safety belt or giving me a gun, just light and fun things, and asked me over and over again that I should wear my glasses. Are you comfortable? I'm happy to say hello. There's a lady in the 3D room who shoots guns. Even though she's quiet, when she gives me the gun, she says I'll take this gun, very gentle.Actually, when I left that game, I lingered and waited for my boyfriend who was still playing, but I felt extremely uncomfortable. I paid to go out, it wasn't free. I didn't bother or ask for help from that employee. I remained silent and waited for my boyfriend to come out and asked, "Did you see this employee?" My boyfriend said "not enthusiastic at all" I said "I thought someone forced me to go to work, it's not to make up for the girls in 3D games who are happy and enthusiastic and even say hello like that".This employee's words were very normal, but her attitude was what made me unhappy and wanted to get out of there immediately.

A lot of rides closed or have under 50 age restrictions. Being an active dad I now feel like I have 1 foot in the grave. Tata was a good show, water lights show was good. I guess the age restrictions killed it for me it was not clear but I was stopped on the waterslides 50years old and over 90kg. And or confusion. I’m 57 turned that day., 82kg , 181cm tall but stop from going on the rides. I guess once you turn 50 years old you must lay down and die because your not aloud to participate anymore. Thank you vinwonders/ vinpearl.

Kind of a cool experience but overall still a ghost town amusement park in Asia. Everything but the waterpark was closed even when we got there at 11:30 in the morning. That was anpity because I wanted to hit the amusement park rides. The waterpark while being the biggest in Vietnam is still underwhelming size wise. However the slides were pretty cool with some thrilling lights, twists and turns.The staff at the waterpark were well equipped to deal with medical situations as my friend got immensely motion sick from the slides as it turns out amusement parks are not his thing. I was fine since I love them. They accommodated him in the infirmary and nursed him back to health but we knew we had to call it a day.Keen to see how transport to the Vinwonders island will go after the cable car is finished! Until then you gotta jump on that boat ride but it’s only 7 minutes ride and very digestible.

We went to vinwonders expecting the rides to be open however most of the fun rides like flying cinema was closed as well as most rides. We went to the beach which was open however there was about 6 chairs. We did the water boat wonder ride and the zip line the only ones opened. Had I known there was so few rides and they were closed, we would not have come. The had the zoo and aquarium opened. Do not bother to come unless you want to see only animals.  The beach was nice.  It’s barely worth 3⭐️

Visited the park on Christmas Day with our 7 and 3 yo daughters. The boat ride across the bay was fun as was the safari and bird show. We felt disappointed that our 3 yo could not go on many of the rides despite the information on the map saying 90cm minimum height. We were told that the information on the map was out of date and the app was correct. I question both why it was changed in the first place and why the map has not been updated? Just not in line with a theme park aiming to be up there with other major tourist attractions in the region. I understand and respect why these restrictions are put in place but they are over restrictive in many cases and prevent children riding on the attractions for which they are designed for. For example, the 1m minimum height restriction on the mini playground was simply ridiculous. This extends to the water park where the minimum height for many of the slides is 135cm: your kid needs to be at least 9 years old to enjoy even the most basic of water slides. Our day could have been much better had the park provided consistent information.

Attractions for adults are so-so, but a very cool water park, a nice zoo.I gave it a 3 because we bought more expensive tickets with food vouchers.The amount should have been 400,000.Firstly, no one writes/explains where the only cafe is where you can spend it.Secondly, the food in this Monta cafe is disgusting.Thirdly, we were told that we had a voucher for 100,000. That is, in the end we paid extra for it the amount for which we could have a delicious meal in the city.And they ate terrible food.

They could do with hiring out electric scooters to get around! It’s far too hot to walk. We didn’t get any further than the water park.They definitely need some sort of rail or transport from the different theme parks.We will probably give it another try in January when we come back.
The shows are amazing Most of the rides were shut so not really fun and language is a problem which made me miss a lot on the activities

The boats used to ferry people from the mainland while they decide whether to fix the cable car are fairly quick and efficient but only run every half hour so time it carefully.The first boat leaves at 8am, putting you at the attractions by 8:20 but many don't open until 9am or even later!If you want to do the main zipline, be there at 8:45 for the 9am run as they only take 10 people every half hour and you cannot book your place, so if you arrive any later you'll be sitting twiddling your thumbs for up to 90 min before your turn.The rides in Adventure Land are disappointing, not nearly as good and fewer than a quarter as many as those at Vinwonders Phu Quoc.  Also rides do not operate all day, many with "windows" and closing for an hour or more for lunch.By contrast, the water park area is much more extensive but a lot of rides are similar and tame, with only a handful interesting.  Also, this area is very difficult to find your way around and to know if you need, what type and where to get tubes.  Some map boards distributed through the area would have been very helpful.  Most of the water obstacle course was not in the water :(.  More staff at the top of some towers would also go a long way to improving the experience as it takes a long time for one person to manage four different slides.The entire park is spread over a huge area and getting around requires a lot of walking, especially to the ferris wheel, which only opens at 2pm on weekdays (10am on weekends).The gardens are very nice, varied and interesting, and the zoo animals seem well-maintained even though some of their enclosures are on the small side.  The aquarium is small but well-stocked with a good underwater tunnel.  We did not make it to see the seals/dolphins.The sound, water and light show at 7pm in the amphitheatre is fantastic.We were advised at the ticket office without asking which rides were closed.  The submarine was not running.

Decided to do a re-visit of, as its now called, VinWonders. Last came here in 2010, so quite a few years have gone by.A fair number of rides are open but it is obvious some are still either under construction or having the finishing touches done to them.One thing the management of this park could do is go visit theme parks in Florida. One thing they have in abundance are fan powered water misters, usually near queues for rides. Stops the puntets muttering, its getting hot. And where are the free water top up? We bailed out and left early as it got way too hot in the sun.  As for checking for food and drinks in bags on entry. What planet is this company on? Certainly doesn't get me recommending as a place to visit.Oh, and you have to take the boat. The cable car system doesn't have any cables on it. And the submarine, well that has still to be seen. It plastered all over the park (on the escalators etc) but calling the number didn't do much for me either. This theme park is probably best visited late afternoon or on a cloudy day.

Only 2 rides open, 4 water slides. Outside inflatable water park extremely outdated. Many kiosk for water or snack are closed and restaurants, and souvenirs. The animals, particularly the big cats are in dreadful tiny enclosures. One tiger was pacing back and forth on the same spot, it was so sad to see. We did overall have a good day, the sea lion show was fun, the dolphin show however was average and I wonder if like the animals have small enclosures

The gardens were stunning, the rides good and the water park very fun however some things we planned to do were shut eg. The blow up water course thing in the sea and the 4D cinema which was irritating and the zoo ... vile is all I can say the conditions those animals were in was shocking, they had barley any space in their enclosure, some were alone such as the tigers and the bears were dangerously overweight!! Much of the large animals were pacing up and down from boredom and had depression written all over their face. Furthermore many of the locals were harassing the animals such as banging on the glass and hissing, throwing in flyers in their enclosure thinking its funny that the animals are enclosed. It’s unnatural and disappointing that more people don’t care for this. ITS ANIMAL CRUELTY !!!

We went mid week.  The line for the cable car to the island was super long, luckily we figured out we could get there by boat.  There is no staff telling anyone this information.  It is a typical amusement park with long rides for the best attractions.  The worst part was on the water park side, the lines for the food places was extremely long.  We had to feed the kids something so we took turns standing on line for an hour to receive standard pizza, hamburger, and fries.  The water rides - there were no long lines which was great, but there were no tubes available to ride on the rides that required them.  You had to scavenge for a tube when someone gave up theirs as many people were going back on the line with the tube they just used.  I saw a few staff members blowing up tubes.  This confused me.  The lines were only maybe 15 people long.  I have been to many water parks where plenty of tubes were stacked and ready to go to enjoy the rides.  The lazy river is literally just that.  There is barely a current to help you float.  Overall, the concept is there, but the mismanagement of this place is pretty evident.
People can spend a whole day here. Food and water are not allowed on to the island - bags are checked. Water and food can be bought on the island.

Was great to not have to que for any rides as it was pretty much empty. Rides were on the small size but still fun for the little ones. Food was an absoloute rip off (yet to go to a theme park that isn't)The highlight is the Gondola ride over.

Everything was so fantastic. We really enjoy every single one of the locations. But the staff worked like they were forced to do the job. The staff at ticketing counter (the chubby girl with bangs) had no manner at all. She didn't even ask anything .she was only standing and looked at her friend working. And when I asked her, what is the difference between two prices. Cause I saw two price on the poster and I think the more expensive one included the show.She just said "No no no" "No discount!" And rolled her eyes. Can you guess how would I feel. 🙄 I didn't wanted the discount. I just wanted to know the difference! After I bought the tickets she didn't explain anything she only gave me the tickets not even said thank you. I had to find the guide on the internet by myself.but other staff inside the islands were okay they helpful and friendly.

We had a great time but disappointed that a few things we wanted to do were closed for renovations. Also be careful to read operating times for rides as some (especially the zip line) only open for short windows of time and could mean you miss out - like we did!

Cable ride over was good, food and drinks on the island is rubbish, service non existent and ride wait time was over an hour. Very sparse area with not a lot going on. The animals however where amazing and made up for the day. A lot of potential once they finish all construction etc
1.) People smoke everywhere which the place is mostly for the kid. They should have a smoking area.2.) The information to use the voucher for food after pay cash more than 100,000VND must be clearer.

Out of the all the amusement parks which i have visited across the world, this may not appear on my list. However, they do have some decent games which is good for day outing.They do not have water filling station and try to make money ruthlessly by selling water bottles at an expensive price. Make sure, you carry enough water as the place is super hot & humid.  They do have some decent eateries.

We love theme parks amd were looking forward to this one.Disappointed that water was confiscated at the cable car entrance,  giving the only option to buy 500ml bottles inside ... with no refilling station.A lot of rides were closed and over 2 hour wait for alpine coaster. The rides we did get on were great and less than half hour queue each ... 4 rides in total.Aquarium a nice area  for a cool off. Water park needs more floating tubes and bins. A lot of rubbish left lying around.The Ferris wheel had amazing views on Nha Trang.Not wheel chairs or walking stick friendly. Overall an average day and the park needs some serious improvement.

Lots of rides open only in the afternoon - about 2pm. Ziplining was closed!!!! at sunset time, very limited variety of rides. Tickets included a voucher for food and drinks at various locations - minimum spending of 200k, with 100k (half) free. WTF? Cheap tactic to get more revenue. In addition, one of the coconut we bought was spoilt! Clearly it was kept for too long apparently. The view at night was great though, and the other good thing was not needing to queue for most rides - it was the low season.
Nothing much exciting except for the alpine ride.  Have to wait for enough pax for each rides to start. Some rides are boring. Pretty scenery.

It's a nice place but spoiled by the number of selfish chinese people who seem to think it's OK to push to the front of a queue.  I know it's cultural but please, if you want to run a theme park you have to put people in the queues to stop this happening.The queue for the alpine chase was about an hour long (actually all the ride queues were an hour long) but my ride was totally ruined by a sloooooow Chinese lady in front of me.  It's a ride, get on with it!Food was expensive and you are not allowed to take any with you so, sorry Vinpearl, you get one of my average votes.

We visited here for a day after we had been to Bana Hills whilst staying in Hoi An in March. Sadly Vinpearl is not as good as Bana Hills which we enjoyed.  The Wavepool was quite good but the food and drinks were expensive.

There was quite a bit to do at the park, and some rides for everyone. The animal enclosures (especially the African animals) were very small and not great conditions which was a bit sad to see. There were good places to eat something and the night time light show was good. We appreciated being able to come in and out of the park and changed our clothes in our resort before the night time show.The cable car and Ferris wheel were good, as were the gardens.We didn’t use the aquatic park but it looked a lot of fun and teenagers would love it.Definitely not a worlds best attraction, but ok to spend a day whilst in Nha Trang.Quite a lot of walking and some inclines-  not for everyone, especially when it is hot.Good bathrooms throughout park.

The lines for the gondola were 2 hours or more. Took a boat over and back and only had to wait 45 minutes or so.Long lines for some of the more popular rides. Not bad though for the small kids attractions. I guess it depends on when you go, and what you're interested in. Water park had lots of choices. Lazy river was a bit of a let down. Too narrow and dirty. The inflatable island was a blast. Be prepared for a good workout! They don't allow you to bring any food or water over to the island. Prices at Vinpearl for food and drinks weren't too bad though.

Me and my boyfriend went full of expectations and really looking forward to a fun time. We had our minds set for a long wait for the cable cars due to all the comments about that, wich was right. I think we waited for about two hours. When we arrived we got in line for the Alpine Coaster. After standing there for 30 minutes without the line moving much, we discovered a sign saying the wait was over 3 hours long... Since we didn’t want to waste 3 hours of our day at Vinpearl Land, we got in line for the Zipline in stead. When we arrived to the counter for the Zipline we got to pick between a bunch of tickets where you could win a ticket to ride within a time set. My boyfriend didn’t win, but I did, so we assumed that I got a free ticket but he had to pay. But no... you could not pay for a ticket. The sign that said 150.000 dng for a ticket was valid for the Alpine Coast ride and if you didn’t get lucky enough to pick a ticket, you couldn’t go.. In that case you had to wait for another hour to try again... There was a guy who won a ticket but didn’t want it due to none of his friends winning, so he gave it to my boyfriend so that we could go together. But the personell would not allow that unless he was our familymember. The whole thing got us pissed off and in a bad mood. I did not have the fun experience doing it alone. And my poor boyfriend had to wait for a really long time while I and all the other people who won got ready. Other than that we had a great time in the zoo and in the waterpark! Unfortunately a lot of the animals were lying still in the shadow or inside so we didn’t get to see much of them during daytime, so we went back after dark but couldn’t see much then either. But what we did get to do, was feed and pet the giraffes! In the waterpark you have to get your own floating mattress to ride the slides where that is required, but when we arrived all of them were taken and we didn’t get to try all of the slides.. In stead of the personell handling the mattresses after each turn in the slide, people took the mattresses with them around the park and barely let them go so that others could use them. It was simply idiotic. Also the asians queuing is horrible. Many of them don’t get in the back of the line, they just mingle/squeeze in at the front. And they don’t understand it if you let them know there’s a line. The rollercoasters are little fun if you’re used to big ones, but it looked like the whole park is a great place for kids!

Nice place and worth to visit, especially with kind. There sre something for everyone, place is clean big but very noisy.Only problem, you can not get any pached lunch to island. Even small snacks for the baby and child were taking off before entering. They were checking every bag. They want to maximize own profit, every food and drink have to buy from their shops. I see this poor, especially travelling with small children.

Well worth the visit for the experience and at 30 English pounds it’s quite reasonable when everything inside the park is then free. Apart from obvious commodities like food and drink. However be very prepared to queue for absolutely everything in the park, lines for hours on end for almost anything worth doing. A side note is that the queues aren’t controlled very well and you’ll get a lot of people shoving and pushing to the front.

Ok when I went to this long time ago it was good. Now you need to be on the line at 7.30 with tickets already purchased. If not it takes about two hours to get there by the sky train. The lines are so long after 0800 you think they would put more boats on the water to get people across.

Went to counter, told to come back an hour later. Returned and queued to be shown a deck of cards, my boyfriend picked one which said something along the lines of "hard luck, better luck next time", mine gave me an allocated time. We were extremely confused and disappointed. We presumed we could just pay. There was another couple Infront of us who also only got one ticket. As we wanted to go on a double one together, I tried to give my ticket to the other couple so they could both go but still wasn't allowed do that. I don't understand why you can't just pay to go on! Well, you still do have to pay but only if your 'lucky' enough. So disappointing.Very tough to see the little space the animals had to roam around.Cable cart was amazing. Great views.Botanical garden nice.

The park is big but quite average as compared to other bigger theme park such as Universal Studio or Everland in Korea. Took the boat in the morning as there is super long queue for the cable car and took the cable car back at night.

I had read a number of reviews prior to going which helped when our taxi driver pulled over on the side of the road for people to approach our taxi to sell its tickets.  We tried quite forcibly to say no but the taxi driver would not go until we bought the tickets and yes they were the same price but we would not have paid the little extra to get the cable car over as the line for this was way too long and it was much quicker to catch the boat and then we caught the cable car back at the end of our time.  Once there we went straight to the Alpine ride as suggested in other posts as this is really the only exciting land ride.  It was about 9am and already the line was just past the 1hr waiting time mark but I insisted that reviews had suggested it didn't get shorter.  My husband and 1 child went off to scout out the other rides and by the time they came back we had moved along in line to where it is sign posted 1hr wait but they told me that the seems of the line was more than 3hrs long now so we decided to stay.  In this time someone fainted and it took a very long time to get an attendant to come and help and no sign of anyone with medical experience turned up.  As time ticked by more people left the line feeling the effects of heat exhaustion.  By the time we got to the front it had been 2.5hrs wait so those signings are incorrect if only 1 track is working and so we hoped it was worth all this time of our expensive day out and we ended up stuck behind 2 very slow people.  You can control your own speed which means longer waits and slower, less exhilarating ride. Sooo not worth the wait or the money for something that is super supposed to be their best ride.  Water park was good but too expensive for just a water park.  Would agree with others about the horrible policy of no outside drinking water to be brought in.

We wanted just to ride the cable car but were told we have to take the entry to the park also. There were no people at all queuing for the cable car across, around 10.00 am on a weekday in the low season. The island actually seemed busier as we were leaving mid/late afternoon (I guess to avoid the heat of the day).The park is OK, a few rides that adults can enjoy, nothing to the scale of crazy rides in US theme parks but OK. There is a zipline we forgot to try. It didn't look tremendously fast. Younger kids should have a good time in this park though (not on the zipline, obviously....). Some OK shopping for souvenirs and clothes. We didn't eat here, I wasn't impressed by the look of the restaurant I saw. You cannot bring food or drink onto the island (unless pre-arranged for medical or religious purposes). I am very much against animals forced into captivity, so this is my big problem with Vinpearl. I would visit, say, Disneyworld USA's Magic Kingdom once a year, but I would not bother to come back to Vinpearl (they are still expanding it though).

First of the line for the cable car was horrendous but after waiting 30 minutes we took the boat which was fun.  The problem was then our bags were search only for food and water.  The only reason for this is to maximise the profit on the island,  families having snacks and water taken is disgusting while the food on the island was awful and overpriced.  The island attractions were good and very enjoyable with some improvements it will be great ie if people are waiting in lines make sure the rides are full when starting them.  Took the cable car back and that was amazing, great views across the bay to nan trang.  Improve the quality of food and organisation and DONT steal food and drink from people, you charge enough for the tickets without having to rip people for poor food.

Its ok but really limited in rides but they are in the process of expanding I got the full day ticket which is about 100 dollars you can get half day for 50 but most restaurants close early and long waits for rides

Many of the rides (about 1/3) were not open. We went with a 1,6 and 10 year old. Most the rides are 1.4 meter height requirements with some flexibility but not much. Not very many roller coasters, 3?, but the water park was finally nd we really enjoyed it. The food isn’t very pricey. Overall fun and you get the money’s worth. Is it amazing, no. If you’ve been to elitches, six flags, Disney it won’t really compare. If you’re looking for a fun day that isn’t too expensive you’ll have fun.

The theme park is a little tacky to me. We took the cable car to Vinpearl Land which was a pleasant ride with panoramic view of Nha Trang.
