Being at the top of the attraction list of Nha Trang, there are a lot of Chinese and Korean tourists which makes the park feel very crowded. The aquarium and zoo both look and feel very run down which actually made me feel sorry for the animals. Nevertheless, I enjoyed my time at the water park as there are a lot of slides and water sport activities on offer. I spent around 5 hours at the park and most of my time at the water park going through all the slides. There is also a beach with sun beds where you can chill when you get tired of the slides.  I also spent a lot of time laying on a floatie at the lazy river. Overall, I would recommend you to go to the park if you have nothing better to do. However, if you are going as a family with kids, then you should definitely visit this place as I'm sure kids would find the park fascinating.

The rides here are fun, there is a lot of variety. If you enjoy theme park rides you’ll likely enjoy it. The gondola ride over is stunning. And that’s about where the good parts end - ANIMAL CRUELTY & SUFFERING: like many parts of Asia this park has decided to capitalize on animal suffering. Wild animals are wild and do not enjoy life in confinement for visitor entertainment and photos. Management keeps replying to messages here that they’ll “pass on feedback but the animals are happy”. If you compare your zoos against any international standards it would fail. And no, wild animals do not like being trapped and kept in unnatural habitats. I hope you understand that you will lose visitors based on this. Not sure how you sleep at night when animals without a voice are involuntarily confined at your park.- LIMITED FOOD CHOICES & FORCED WASTE: I have a food allergy (am Celiac) and am vegetarian. Very very limited options and the restaurants close at all kinds of odd hours even whilst the park is open. Around 7PM I had the option of French fries at Loteria and French fries at German Garden. - CARELESS ENVIRONMENTAL WASTE: in an effort to make profit the park prevents you from bringing any water or your own food (terrible for people like me with legitimate food allergies). There are exactly ZERO water fountains and all guests are forced to buy disposable plastic water bottles which the rest of the world is now realizing is rapidly killing our planet.- WEIRD CLOSING TIMES: the rides are each only open to certain times - 5PM, 6PM, 7:45PM. Make sure you memorize or constantly consult the schedule (which is not listed on the pamphlet, only at the park entrance). Very strange since the park is open til 9PM?Overall the rides are awesome but it’s sickening that visitors money has to go towards mass environmental waste and animal cruelty.

I didn't want to give 4 stars because there are so many things wrong with this place: - The Aquarium and zoo: no matter what people say about making sure the animals are comfortable, they are not. Animals shouldn't be kept in small spaces for our enjoyment. My only hope is that visitor numbers will decrease as people become more aware of animal cruelty and Vinpearl will do the right thing and remove them from their park. - Shade: there is so much open space in this park and in the summer temperatures it's unbearable! More cover (natural or artificial) would make the experience so much more pleasant! - Water: you can't bring your own water and there is no where to refill your bottles. They say this is to protect the island. Just look down as you cross on the gondola and look at all the plastic. If they really wanted to protect the island they would provide refill points for water. So far we have - animal cruelty, customer welfare and environmental impact. So the fact I've given it 3 stars shows you just how good the rest is. No doubt I'll get a reply saying "rest assured we've passed these comments on..." That's great but I can see whoever you pass them on to doesn't care as long as the park keeps making money.The good: the water park is excellent, the sea obstacle course is brilliant, adventure land is every kids dream (free arcade games! Although many need to be fixed), the alpine coaster is a real thrill and even the ride over is beautiful. You can see the park is going through some major upgrades at the moment and I hope they can make some changes that not only improve their bottom line but set a good example to society.

Good parts: The waterpark is fantastic. We spent hours there and if it was only the water park we would give it 5 stars. There's loads of slides, pools and a lazy river. There is access to the beach where there is an inflatable obstacle course and many water activities you can pay for (eg jetskiing, parasailing etc). You have to pay 20,000vnd for a locker and then 100,000vnd for a deposit which you get back when you return the key.There is a small range of "rollercoasters" which were fun. We went during low season so queues were minimal.The cable car over the water to get there and back was nice and had lovely views of the park.There are a variety of shows on throughout the day in different areas (mermaid show, dolphin show, water and lights show) but we didn't see any of these.The bad:The price of the ticket is very expensive (880,000vnd) and we didn't feel Vinpearl land justified this much.There is NO vegetarian food. We went to every restaurant and stall in the place and the only thing we could find was plain rice or chips. After reading other TripAdvisor reviews we saw apparently the Coral Buffet does some Vegetarian food but it cost 350,000vnd ($15 USD/£11.50GBP) to eat there and after paying the extortionate entry fee to the park. You are not allowed to bring any food or water in to the park and they do search and remove any food before you enter the cable car.They wait to fill the rides before you take off amusements. We waited over 5 minutes until the ride filled up for two of them.We were depressed after going to the aquarium. Too many fish, turtles or snakes crammed in tiny spaces and unable to move freely about. There is a zoo but we didn't even bother going there after seeing the aquarium.Overall the theme park isn't great but the water park is good. It was a fun day out overall and we probably would recommend it if you had a day to spare in Nha Trang.

If you’ve been to Garden by the bay, Aquarium , Sentosa and Underwater world in Singapore, you definitely can skip Vinpearl land Nha Trang as Vinpearl is a imitation of those places combined but not nearly as good. Cable car queue is always long in the morning so you can take the canoe instead.

We went to Vinpearl Land Nha Trang twice in the week we were there.  Both times entering the park after 4pm with the discounted tickets.  The first time was not by design but since we purchased the wrong tickets we had to wait an hour before they would let us on the cable cars.  They would not let us pay the difference in price and get in the park!There are very few attractions for children under the age of 5 or 6.  Most of the rides in the amusement park and waterpark require a height of 1.3m and the staff will not budge even if you are 1cm from the line on the wall.  We also find the staff not very friendly, which is probably the result of a combination of language and rudeness.  Food options are horrible, but they don't allow you to bring your own food in.  They actually search your bags and make you take out any food or drink, even water, before entering the park.The zoo (which is not advertised on the website) has many animals but the conditions in which the animals live are far from ideal.  The enclosures are way too small for large animals like tigers, lions and bears.  It was depressing to watch.Our kids had a lot of fun both times we went but I think Vinpearl Land has a lot of improvements to do before it can become an international standard amusement park.

Pros:1. Big themepark with wide variety of activities to do. Waterpark is a highlight. We went there on monday and not much queue for the rides. Cons:1. Very limited food available. While outside food/ drink are not allowed, more food options should be provided in the island. We went for lunch at the fast food restaurant at 11+am and there was only fried chicken and lime juice left. Since we went there early and no huge crowd of people there, my own assumption is that they did not even prepare other foods as stated in the menu. It is definitely not a healthy meal/ proper meal for young kids/ infants. Worst thing is when we stayed up to 7pm, most of the shops were closed and only left snacks for dinner. 2. Long waiting time for cable car rides. It took us near 1 hr to queue for the cable car. 3. Not wheelchair/ pram friendly. A lot of staircase and escalators. No way to push pram in the park. Full day ticket is better option as waterpark closes early and only few rides left in the evening. And literally only snack and some drink from vending machine in the evening.
There are not much shade its very hot.  Only the cable car was exciting the rest is pretty much boring.

The place is huge and has lots of facilities but lack shade and it is packed with tourists and can be hectic.The queuing for he cable care ride and ferry can improve as there is always a long wait. The roads leading to attractions are mostly unshaded making it very difficulty under a hot day. Make sure you have proper protection and stay hydrated. Lunch is stressful as there are not enough restaurants or they are closed and you have to queue and fight for a table which is dirty and has lots of junk on it. There seems no place for a relaxing lunch with reasonably decent food. Music is too loud in some areas.That said, the place is huge and has lots of spaces. The ticket include lots of attractions - we like visiting the animals though we hope some should deserve a larger dwelling given vin pearl is so huge. We love feeding the giraffe.The mermaid show is interesting, cable car ride is fascinating, indoor flower dome is refreshing. The alpine coaster is thrilling.

The place is big and it's situated on a mountain side so we had to use the escalators to get up and down the place. Not sure how wheelchair people could access this place. We travelled with a baby in a stroller and we had to carry the stroller to get up and down the escalators and steps. The underwater display has steep steps with no lift access at all. There were no designated smoking areas so people were smoking everywhere. We had people smoking next to our sleeping babies in outdoor restaurant areas. I would have expected to see designated smoking areas for a kids park like this.

The place look amazing. Price too high for what you get.We arrived at the park and paid 880000 dong for one ticket including the cable car. We arrived at the park at 12:00pm when the place opened at 8:30am.Park area:The first facility we were on(a small roller coaster) failed to stop and so we found ourselves doing 2 turns in a row .. (not a bad thing, but ...) when the operator has to press the emergency button which stops the train operation, it shows poor maintenance.There were a few other non-active facilities in the area, and that's pretty disappointing.Water Park:Many slides were not active when we arrived, some of the main slides was active.In conclusion: when you pay such a high price you expect a high level of maintenance and that all facilities will be active.Tip:Call the park before you buy a ticket to ask if there are inactive facilities, and then decide if its worth it.One more thing:I have to say that a week ago we were at 'Vinpearl land Hoi an' and it was amazing, the price was also convenient and all the facilities were active.ENJOY

I mainly went to go on cable car ride over which was ok. Once inside very similar to any other amusement park around the world, about the same prices too. Would be ok for kids I guess. I just had a quick walk around was all clean etc. looks like they are expanding the place. Is 880 vnd flat rate including cable car ride and no you can not just pay for cable car only. First ride over is at 8am rides open at 8:30am. If your actually gonna go over with kids etc get there very early as it gets busy very quickly.
Not enough staff everywhere, that’s why people stuck in lines(((  especially waiting for electric car and even not enough fans there. Sad

We went for the cheaper choice from 4-9pm. Since the rides are right as soon as you get off the cable car we did those first and then went to th aquarium. We hoped to go to the Zoo after that but it was closed at 6pm, disappointing! Then we were so hungry so went to eat but everything had closed at 7pm!! Everything!! This would’ve been ok if we had known about these closing times because then we would’ve saved the rides for the end but NO ONE had told us. For the amount of money you pay, expensive for something done in Vietnam, we were dissappointed. Now that you know of all the closing times, it is worth the visit.

The park is good fun if you have younger children as the rides are catered to their level. Titan peak was a fun run however it took over 2 hours to line up as the second track was not on operation and two goes on the ride took us over 4 hours of the day away. Mine adventure was fun for the kids and the water park was good fun but the park is lacking and nothing like Euro Disney.
this is an ageing facility.Great if it is included i your package however only worth a day or so to tick a box

We popped in for the afternoon and rode only one ride after another we wanted to go had broken down. After 45 minutes we gave up. The sky wheel was amazing. Would have preferred to ride on the cable car alone but hey.

Family of 5, kids 10, 8 & 7. Good day out, moderately quiet, approx wait of 20 min for popular rides. Waterpark was good, spent most of the time there as bloody hot. Locker and change rooms fairly manic as expected. 3 decent rides the kids enjoyed, many of the basic ones not running. Didn’t catch any of the shows, hard to work out when and where they were on. All in all, fun day out would recommend.

I went to Vinpearl in the afternoon - tickets can be purchased to use from 4-9pm for 450,000 VND per person. The staff are really strict and will literally not let you on the cable car 1 second before 4pmThe lights at night are very beautiful There’s lots of gardens and large grassed areas. Wear good shoes as there’s lots of walking involved.What made me sad where all of the animals in such confined spaces. It is true what you read on the other reviews. There were 2 large hippos in a small pond area. I don’t understand why there are such massive gardens and large grassed areas and the caged areas are tiny. I feel that the animals would have such a poor quality of life. There is a nice aquarium with a variety of sea life.There are areas to swim and small ‘happy snack’ stands to get drinks and ice cream etcTaronga Zoo and the theme parks in Australia are superior to Vinpearl

A lot to do and see. All in all a fun but exhausting day. The fast food  burger joint is crappy, stay away  😅A lot of water sports available at the beach. The beach is very nice and clean. In the aquapark there is no soap in showers so take your own. No dressing rooms as the locker room is unisex. Changed to a bathing suit in the small toilet as the shower booths are all wet inside. So I recommend to have it underneath ready before you go.

Mild n average. Good for young familys, not for adventurous ones. Good range of activities otherwise food outlets plenty of toilets, aquarium prob highlight, waterpark good to cool off too.Ideal for 4-14 year olds. Or 1st time theme parkers.

Well... that was a kind of a expiriance. We expected a very nice place like western parks like Europapark, Disneyland, Alpamare, etc. But instead it was a very loud park with probably 90% chinese guests. But let’s start at the beginning... First, it was bevore 9 in the morning, we had to wait in a row for about 1 hour to get on the cablecar to the park. Then we where surprised that there was only a few very short rollercoasters. The zoo was very sad. The animals where in to small cages. Most of them with only one small place where they can hide from the sun. The waterpark was nice with a lot of activities. This was the big plus! Only the looker area was to small and much overcrowded at the waterpark.

Visited Vin Pearl Land in August with and 8,10 and 15 year old. Rides are ok and queues not too bad. 8 year old wasn’t able to go on most though. Food is dreadful! One restaurant ran out of everything and food is poor quality and not much choice.

Went together with my boyfriend. It was a nice day. Driving jetski was propably the best thing. You can do so many things in there. There is an amusement park, waterpark, zoo. Like a mini disneyland and even adult people like us enjoyed time in there. Negative thing was that it was overcrowded, chinese and russian people mostly. They were rude and cut in line, it was frustrating. Restaurant was dirty and trash everywhere. But all in all it was a nice day and worth money even thought we had to line up too much.

Theme park is very average. We queued an hour and a half for the mountain rollercoaster. Wasn’t worth that. Apart from that it’s a fine day out. Food is very poor in the park and I would recommend eating a lot in advance then just snacking. The water park and inflatables was fun! Cable car ride over and views from the island make it worth doing.

Great value but the waterpark is a shambles! My kids enjoyed their day here but when it came to the waterpark they were too small (1.2m) to go on any of the bigger water slides or even the inflatable park. Not too sure why there is a height limit, this is a very tame waterpark. Saw numerous tourists getting saved by lifeguards, my son who is 4 is a very capable swimmer and would have been less risk than some of these clowns who constantly put the lifeguards to work.

We went on monday, and surprisingly it's still super crowded. We arrived at the ticket counter around 1030am and saw the queue line for cable car, and give up the cable car ride immediately, and went to the speedboat (no queue at all). There are pro and cons in this theme park.Pro: - Variety of coaster rides and park which include animal land, blooming hills, water park, indoor games arcade etc - There is no queue at all for the Ferris Wheel after lunch hour.Cons: - This park had disappointed small children. There are many rides that small children cant ride at all. Similar rides in other themepark had allowed small children in, provided accompany by the adults. - We queue for more than 30 minutes at the line, and when it's our turn, the staff only tell us our small children cant take the ride. Management should hired more staff to check and control the queue sequence from the start of queue line! - Not Stroller friendly. There is escalator, but no lift for stroller and wheelchair access.- Some of the vending machine for drinks malfunction, and only accept certain notes and no change.- Lotteria run out of certain foods at 1pm. -  There is only one dolphin show on monday at 330pm. When we reach there at 330pm, it was already full, and we're not allowed to go in anymore.Anyway, this is still a great park if you're adult without any small children. But, make sure you plan the time and play area accordingly.

Today we visited Vinpearl island. The water park and the zoo were very nice.  The fast food restaurant was horrible. Much food sold out. Only large Pepsi instead of medium could be chosen. Hot coffee is difficult to find. Why?The staff in the park is not friendly and many of them do not speak English. The aquarium was nice, but in our opinion too many fishes per squared meter!

I eventually found the gondola station after riding about for 30 minutes, in my opinion more signage is needed for tourists to find the park entry.  After arriving I purchased the two way gondola entry ticket after lining up in the entry line first.... more English signage is needed to instruct guests.  The ride to the park was nice with beautiful views of the coastline.  Luckily, It was quite quiet the day I visited vinpearl land, I rode the downhill sled first it was good, then the 4D movie experience, it was more child oriented, I fell asleep but what I did see was very good effects.  Then to an expensive coffee and ice cream in one of the many food stores.  Lastly for me and I am glad I went was the eye, the view was spectacular!!  I will attach some photos, but not for the person which is afraid of hights obviously 😜
The small amount of rides holds the park back, when more rides are built will be a lot better, cable car going to and from was good

Not really worth a visit if you have experienced other theme parks. The water park is ok, but smells of disinfectant all round the park. The changing facilities are terrible, a really bad smell that makes you want to vomit and all 95 degrees!!Food available is all fried, if you like fried sausage and Pepsi, you will be well catered for.Only good ride is the new Alpine Roller Coaster, but be prepared to queue for 2 Hours in 95 degrees.In summary a 2* theme park that needs to offer more, just like       Vin Pearl Resort itself which is definitely NOT 5* as published.

After waiting in line for an hour and a half ( yes we got there early) we finally managed to grab a cable car to the island. But first we got scammed by the Grab driver. We told him we wanted to buy tickets at the cable car office but he INSISTED they had none and took us to his “mate” who had them. Fair enough we only paid maybe $3 extra but it’s the whole scam mentality that annoyed us. Anyway, we finally got to the island. Everything is run down, sad and makes you feel dejected. The animals have captivity boredom and pace, head shake and tremor. It’s so sad. The rides are just okay I guess. The concession stalls all had no bottled water by lunchtime and it was 35’C so stinking hot. It’s got a nice outlook and I guess you’ll go anyway, but I think you’ll also be disappointed like we were!
Needs a bit of work in the presentation and service areas. A lot of things not open or still being built.The wheel and gardens are great but food areas not good.

Cable car still under maintenance during my visit. Such a waste that I did not get to try. Anyway, with the price 880k VND per person, it is supposed to include return cable car ride. Since the cable car is under maintenance, we were transferred to Vinpearl by speedboat. We were also given a 100k VND worth of meal voucher, which were sufficient to feed us for the whole day. Queue was not long, perhaps it was a weekday. It was a huge place. We spent our time from 9am(Vietnam time) to 530pm, and we have yet to explore the whole place. We did not have time for animal parks and all the entertainment shows. Basically we did not spent anything else, even the arcade games are free to play. As far as I know, the only thing that has to pay separately is the parasailing at the beach. Overall, I would say it worth the day. The buildings can be more beautiful and well maintained. Not suitable for elderly, as the price will not worth if you're not playing any rides and water park.

It was a nice ride over on the cable car. Very spread out so be prepared for lots of walking. Waterpark was crazy busy so didn’t even bother. Limited rides- some in construction so maybe it’s better now. Only decent thing was the toboggan which had to line up for 1 hr and 15mins to have it ruined by someone going so slow in front of us. Needs more rides, food stalls and activities

Basically is you have experienced good US or EU parks then this is a poor relation. Overall we had a decent day but will list pro’s & con’sPros:Good size site, doesn’t feel too busyArcade games etc all included in priceBeach area & inflatable Waterpark great, clean, good for swimmingWent parasailing, pretty expensive at 1.2m Dong but good experience Speedboats prompt & fastCons:Lots of rides closed in Waterpark for maintenance Alpine ride also closed, I did contact vinpearl prior to arrival and was told it’s openCable Cars closed - I was informed of this before bookingStaff didn’t seem too happy to see you, no greets etcLazy river has virtually zero water flow, so if u are lazy, don’t go on it as you have to swim most of it:)In summary, average park, also feel it needs a “signature” coaster as the ones they have are like kids ones

I found it hard to describe this place. There is lots to do on the island, water park, amusement Park etc. it’s just kinda weird? Massive wide open streets with castle and medieval themes but Hardly any people walking around. Looked like it could be a scene from a post apocalyptic movie. Half the rides in the waterpark were not in use. I’m not saying don’t go I’m just saying it is definitely on a different vibe to other parks I’ve been too.

The park is far cry from what we expected and it's not worth the price : 880k vnd /pax includind the car cable round ticket.The main ride was out of order, the other was the mine train, not spectacular, 2 or 3 other upside-down attractions, merry-go-round in the central area, it's nothing to write home about!The warter area was way more disappointing, 6 almost same water slides (some smaller or bigger) ending up in a small paddling pool.The beach was crowded by groups of chinese tourists who are rude, talk loud and push, they took every sigle sunbed, it was really unpleasant.No proper place to eat either, Loteria and other burgers or vietnamese fried meat, even if we don't eat that crap, we didn't have the choice as we were super hungry...The busy sulky staff was unpleasant and didn't respect the line, so we had to struggle to place an order, and finally after 45 mn of a messy line, there were no more beef burger nor french fries! Great! who's next?The zoo is poor, except the aviary for those who love birds and yet, 2 lions, 2 tigers, not much to see.I didn't see security staff which is a shame in a place crowded of people. Once is enough and as far as i remember, 10 years ago, Dam Sen Park in HCM was funnier  ...

If not for the hot weather, it'll probably be on par with everland in Korea but no where close to Disneyland standards.. My 2.5 year old boy good food posioning with constant vomitting and diahorrea throughout the night after having meals at Lotteria... Lousy service and tainted food... Please avoid

It is a big place, and was not busy on the day we visited. We bought tickets at our hotel, which helped bypass the ticket seller the taxi driver took us to buy tickets enroute. However there was not a big queue to buy tickets to get on the cable car. There was still a half hour wait to get on the cable car, and remember this was not a busy day and we were arriving around 9.00am. Cable car ride is scenic as many people have mentioned. A bit hot, as I don't think they are air-conditioned.Food: as expected, low quality, and a bit overpriced. However compared to western theme parks the food is not too pricy. Just don't expect good food. We visited the Dolphin cafe which also seems to be also named the Russian Restaurant which was better than Yummy Land which had large queues for fast food. Splash bay: probably one of the highlights for our teenage boys. You have to wear a life vest, and it is great fun. Just don't look to closely at the algae growing on the sides of the floats.Beach: push and shove and you may be lucky to get a chair to sit on!Water park: OK, lots of slides closed. As others have mentioned there seems to be an ado hoc system of opening some and closing others without warning. Perhaps due to lower numbers they reduce staff? Not enough floating tubes to go around, so people were waiting for them at the end of the water slides. Unfortunately some visitors did not understand the need to pass their tube on to people waiting and there was limited oversight or organisation. Slides that were open did not have a rating system so you just observed and tried them out. Other water parks I have been to have signs letting you know how fast or difficult the slide is before you enter. One of the problems in the water park area is that the attendants spoke limited other languages and there are visitors from many different countries so communication was not always great. Most of the time this was not a problem, but a few times a noticed it was an issue when things are not going to plan. Hardly can blame the local staff as they would need to learn English, Russian, and Mandarin at least!There is a huge indoor arcade game area which was empty when we visited. All included in your entry, but with so much else to do we did not encourage our children to visit this attraction. Ferris wheel looked like there would have been fantastic views but was not operating from lunchtime. I am unsure why.Extreme launcher was out of action for maintenance as mentioned on the website, but looked like good fun.Luckily the queues for outdoor thrill rides were relatively short. I would imagine on a weekend or peak times these could be very long. Some rides in the late afternoon when people has left had no queues at all. These rides were enjoyable to us and our teenage children. Overall the park was an enjoyable experience. I went with some intrepidation after reading the reviews. Just don't have high expectations. Don't expect Disneyland, WaterBom (Bali), or the organisation of a western theme park and you will be fine. We took a couple of bottles of drinking water with us. They do inspect you bags as you enter but did not seem bothered by some water. The park has potential. I think if management employed people with experience in running a theme park they could improve their offering. There are lockers by the water park. Quite reasonable at 10,000 Dong each, with a 100,000 deposit. They give you a key on a wrist strap. Make sure you keep the receipt to get your deposit returned.Our tickets cost 880,000 Dong which included the cable car each way. However interestingly there was no other option at the end of the day when the queue for the cable car was long I asked and the motor boats I have read about were not on offer on this day.My wife visited the Zoo and bird aviary which she found enjoyable.

We spent the day with our boys 7 and 11 and we had a ball. There is plenty to do and the effort that has gone into this place is quite incredible. It is a real shame that the same amount of effort has not gone into animal welfare. The areas for the animals are way to small and are very sad to see and really lets down the park and there is no excuse for this when so much money has been spent elsewhere . I have read many reviews and seen replies saying that they take the welfare of their animals seriously. Please do not reply with this response as it is clearly not correct as you would never put animals in this situation if this was true. The attached picture has 2 tigers in it!!

Worth the 800k dong entry but not a park I’d return to.The GoodIt wasn’t very busy so little queueing.The tunnel in the aquarium The big wheelThe water & light showSome of the water slidesThe BadThe changing rooms/ lockers. Compete shambles and nowhere to get changed. A voyeurs wet dream.Lotteria burger bar. No burgers available. Just horrible chicken. 30 mins from getting In queue to sitting down.Hygiene in Lotteria - see photos. Place should be closed down.Chinese people. Loud & lack spacial awareness in queues.Food offering in general. Light years behind most theme parks. Poor quality & lack of choice.Broke my bum on the kamikaze slide. Left bum cheek is painful.The Ugly70% of reptiles/ fish in aquarium kept in spaces too small. Lion/ tigers/ hyenas/ rhino enclosures. Disgustingly small. Poor animals. PETA / WWF should visit here.

Me and my girlfriend visited the park last week and had a great time in the waterpark. The slides are great fun, the beach is lovely and the water assault course is a great laugh. We then walked around the gardens at the top of the hill which were beautiful, then had a go on some of the little rides, but then sadly after going into the zoo our ideas of this place changed.White Tigers are being kept in a tiny environment. I have a bigger back garden  than these animals have as their entire home! It was such a shame to see, they were pacing up and down the cages, clearly anxious with something. We then went on to see bears in a tiny enclosure, one of which was swaying side to side which is a clear sign of distress. White Lions too in a tiny space.The park is massive, there is so much free space there but they have built tiny enclosures not fit for the animals. I genuinely don't understand why they're that small! Tigers roam for miles and miles everyday but here they have a patch of land no wider than 10 metres!Overall, a great water park, great small rides, lovely scenery, but hideous living conditions for the animals kept in their Zoo!

It’s worth the visit here. There is quite a lot to do but it’s quite dated. If you go don’t have high expectations, that’s the best advice I can give. The Waterpark is average but the single person rail ride gives you a great view across to the main land.

Though some of the things where not working on the day we arrived, the park was a great experience. The zoo part is realy beautiful and the water slides where fun. The thing we hated was the huge lines to buy food in the restaurant where hundreds of people are waiting in line with only ond cashier. This is unacceptable. If you are in na trang do go there but know you cant bring food from home and lines for food is an hour long.

Spent about half a day here. Very expensive and takes a long time to get in. The theme park itself wasn’t very good, no rides that we really enjoyed. I wouldn’t go to the zoo if you care about animals, it all seemed rather cruel! The water park was fun but rides kept shutting. On the inflatable obstacle course in the ocean a member of staff vomited into the sea, we got off that rather quickly after. Don’t think we’d go back

The entry ticket cost 800k for an adult (or child over 1.4m), which includes return cable car ride to the Vinpearl island, waterpark, zoo, all the shows - basically everything except food & drinks and anything you want to buy at the shops.The 10min cable car ride was the best part of the day! We really enjoyed the experience and the views.Everything else was mediocre... The waterpark is good for children, but slides were not really interesting for adults. The lazy river that's not a river, but a long pool - doesn't have a single water feature or a stream. Some of the water slides didn't even work. The waterpark closes at 6pm.Nice zoo and aquarium, but the bird show 2.30pm and dolphin show 3.30pm need improving a lot! They were boring, tricks - not impressive, animals didn't listen... The dolphin show was the worst I've seen in my life.The food kiosks dotted around the island - all have the same stuff, not much variety. There is a Vietnamese, Russian restaurants and burger place. The burger place at 1pm had half of the menu unavailable...they didn't have any oven cooked chicken, no beef burgers, no tornados (milkshakes), no nice lemonade drinks...it was very disappointing considering the park was half empty!We expected Vinpearl to be a lot more busy with a lot more entertainment going on. We think we didn't get our money's worth.

The park is fun,4 of us in our 20s went, the rides are for children in early teens I would say and it’s okay The best part is the water park and the inflatable blow up on the water which made our dayHalf the slides where closed and the ones that were open had big queues because there wasn’t enough staff so they had to keep closing one to let people down another so there was a long wait tooThe food was a good price but not many options for fussy eaters nothing was plain it was a strange combo like seafood pizza We were given half a cup of drink because we asked for no ice so they said we can’t have a full cup then and literally gave us half The staff were all so miserable 9/10 though there was the odd member clapping and waving goodbye on the rides which was funnyThe ride where you go down hill in a small car thing was really really good was so much fun as you got control and it goes much faster than you can prepare yourself for I would say I’m glad it was the equivalent to £25 because if I paid more I would have been very annoyed but for the money it’s not too bad it’s just more for children than it portrays The cable car is also beautiful and fun
it was a funny day to visit Vinpearl. It is a bit expensive for what you get. I liked the change after all the touristic activity a lazy thing you could do everywhere in the world.

OK--It's nothing like Disneyland or Six Flags so we understand that !! It has too few rides. The dolphin show was nothing like Seaworld--it was more like a joke and lasted very short(~10-15mins) w/ just very few repeated (2-3) basic acts from Dolphins. Nothing special. We were actually disappointed w/ the show. Food was horrible and no selections. Only one fast food restaurant opens for each area so it's a drag for you to walk so far upstairs or up the hills to find another food vendor--it was torture because it was built similar to Universal Studios which has different rides at different high hill level.side note: there are many Russians and Chinese and nothing against them except it was annoying one middle aged Chinese man cut in front of me (woman) in line while waiting to purchase food at the fast food restaurant. What a jerk! You would think as a MAN he had his manner and dignity not to cut in front of a woman.
Great day out. Water park , gardens and rides good. Disappointed with the zoo as not enough room for the big cats. Ferris wheel not working.

We bough our tickets from an agent so we didn't have to queue for those but we lined up for almost an hour for the cable car to Vin pearl.Once on Vinpearl  the lining up continues. Most of the popular rides like the toboggan and Wild West railway have 2 hour waits! We didn't ride them as we didn't want to line up that long.The botanical gardens and zoo are interesting and worth seeing. The waterpark is popular with lots of slides and not too much waiting. The beach area is lovely and sandy with an obstacle course on the water which was a highlight for our teenagers and young adults.Food is quite average on the island.The park is made up of lots of areas and some areas are a little 'scruffy'. Lack of signage on buildings/shops also make it difficult to find food.All in all, we had a good day but its certainly no Disneyland.
Expect to queue for the cable car and the popular rides. The water park is ok, worth a visit if you are stuck for something to do and have a spare VND 800,000 p/p.

Day out in Vinpearland was reasonably priced The ticket includes cable car to and from the island.There were only 2 rides on the theme park that were really for adults. Get to the alpine toboggan early because the queue can be over 2 hours wait for a 4 min rideMost of the other rides are suitable for children. The water park is where most of the action is - lots of selection for slides but things are only open every few hours and they close rides for certain parts of the day.

The facilities in the park in developing process. It's like a theme park included water park, under water world and garden with some animals. The fees for all the entrance included when paying for cable car. Besides cable car, there are boats services to the island. Resorts and villas can be found in the island. The water park is big, the garden for the animals is big too. There are only few restaurant in the island, it may cause inconvenience for some travelers.
Visited 30 December. The park is fantastic, 2 hour wait to get a burger. Hint: take the boat across to avoid the large queue for the chairlift and be prepared for a 1.5 hour wait for the ride home.

Me and my partner came here for a quick vacation. My partner has been to the Vinpearl resort in Nha Trang and thought this would be great too. The place looked awesome, just like any other theme park but it was so quiet. It was like the theme park wasn’t open at all. We pretty much had the place to ourselves. Plus side - the rides were all open and there weren’t any lines! Down side - food sucks. They have the same food at every stall and restaurant so there’s no variety. They did have a pretty cool water show at 7:00pm. Should see this if you go. The Water park - it’s just like any other water park (very similar to wet n wild in Australia), but they wouldn’t let you go on the 4 person waterslides if you didn’t have 4 people. With the lack of people at the theme park, me and my partner weren’t able to find another pair to go on these rides so we stuck to the individual rides. Overall, maybe if there were more people the atmosphere would have brought the place to life but it literally felt like we walked into a deserted land. There weren’t even many workers around so we were literally there by ourselves.

Firstly I bought our tickets online the night before and received an email to say I had been charged double (3.2 million dong for 2 tickets) and that I would be refunded 1.6 million. I have no idea why I was overcharged and will be watching to make sure the rest goes back into my account. Secondly, when we arrived there were no instructions to say what to do or where to queue. It was chaos. We joined the queue that everyone was scrambling for and assumed it was the one for the cable car. After a while I saw people with cards in their hands and figured we probably had to go to the ticket booth and swap our online tickets for cards. Tip: don’t bother buying tickets online, it’s more hassle than it’s worth. The cable car queue is right in front of the ticket booth. The queue for the speed boat is to the right of the cable car queue and was much smaller. Don’t get stuck in the cable car queue if you are wanting the speed boat! We arrived at 9.30 and queued for an hour for the cable car. It was pretty windy but the cable car was good. They search your bags on the way in for food and drinks which I think is ridiculous and intrusive. The park itself has a lot of maintenance/building work going on and it felt a bit deserted in places. We were disappointed that the sky wheel was closed as we had particularly wanted to go on that and it was our main reason for visiting. There were limited places to eat too as most were only selling drinks. We ate in the coral buffet restaurant which was ok as we were starving. Certainly nothing to shout about. The only ‘street food’ stalls we saw were huts selling sausages on sticks, popcorn and drinks. The 4D cinema and the extreme launcher were highlights but it’s a lot of money just for those.Maybe it was the wrong time of year but we left feeling deflated. We would have enjoyed it more if the weather was better perhaps and everything was open. The worst part for us was the swarms of tourists pushing and shoving in the queues for the cable car and the speed boat (the cable car closed half way through the day so we had to get a speedboat back). It could be improved by having more people on hand to show people where to go and stop people pushing and running to get to things.

My children are looking forward to go to Vinpearl Land Amusement Park.But the Amusement ride area are kind of sad and run down,nothing interesting.The aquarium even worst,the employs escalator is not working at all ! Life guards at waterpark are kind of rude ! Again, not many of them can speak English and not friendly at all. This park should be a cheerful,happy,jolly and child friendly to all the visitors !

Visited Vin Pearl Land with our young baby, so couldn't actually go on any rides - but most of them were suited to young children. The land itself was cool to see, and we spent some time at the beach that forms part of the water park. We didn't have a good restaurant experience, the one we went to that was close to the water park had lots of pigeons in it :S and was pretty dirty. Menu was also not very appealing.The cable car to and from VP Land was the best part of the experience. Also, great views of Nha Trang from VP Land. The zoo was pretty cool; the animals all looked to be treated well.

The cable car ride here was the highlight as nearly all the attractions here are designed towards children and personally I didn't really enjoy our day here, to make things worse alot of the water attractions were under either maintenance or construction..

We went here for the waterpark, but it wasn't as big as we thought (not very many slides), so you probably wouldn't want a whole day here unless you have young children who can enjoy the rest of the park, which has a few extra kiddy rides available. There is a nice beach to relax on while the young ones enjoy the slides. It looks great on paper but we were a little underwhelmed I think...probably best for those with young children only.

Pretty Average. On the day we went there was not enough life gaurds organising the water slides so there was always a long wait. Dont go on a day where bad weather is expected as we got told the park would close at 2pm. Price is ok $45 Australian. Better for kids not for thrill seakers

Visited there today, enjoyed what we could but lots of the park closed including eating places so very poor food selection and most things sold out  unless you wanted buffet or restaurant style meal. We still had to pay full price $800 000 dong to get in even thought lots part of park closed. We enjoy water slides and water activity and aquarium, cable car ride was good experience also.  If the whole park was open probably worth the money but not worth what we paid today.

We stayed on the island just to go here, that was a huge mistake.First we missed out on the cable car ride.It meant a golf buggy to the gate instead.The park was pretty quiet during our visit.Thought the entrance fee was very expensive and was seriously disappointed that hotel guests are given no discount or perks.This should be addressed.The park is pretty big, didn't think there were any WOW rides, but we had a good day. The evenings however are a different story, there is nowhere to eat! The restaurants all stay open - but stop serving food?I have a theory this is to push hotel guests back to the horrific, and overpriced, hotel buffets.This really made it a miserable place to visit at night.The rides all close at different times, frankly this is confusing and forces queues at the later opening rides. Just keep them all open or close the park earlier?We didn't get to the water park during our visit.Thought the zoo cages were really small for the animals. We seen some shows that were pretty good.You can easily spend a whole day here but the food situation on this island knocks stars off for me. People need food choices avaliable throughout their visit.Still feel for what it is that the entry fee was on the high side.Some perks as a hotel guest (like disney do) would have made this all so much better.

We went across to Vin Pearl land the chairlift was great giving amazing views across the water to Township of Nha Trang. The complex is still under construction in some areas, other parts looks quite tired and doesn’t have a feel of a Disney land, it’s quite expensive by Vietnam standards about $48 A per person, the food options were also quite limited.

Beautiful island with an amusement park. I give them credit for trying but being from Orange County (the land of Disney and Knott's) it's not a fair comparison. Vinpearl Land has some nice rides and attractions but is operated quite inefficiently and lacks the panache of western amusement parks. Spread out over many acres, there is potential and current construction suggests bigger and better things are coming when we visited in August, 2017.Ride lines were slow and a work shift ends at 4PM, so you'll see literally hundreds of park staff leave for the day as you're walking around. Food options also close early, forcing you to find outside food options after 8PM. The best part of Vinpearl land is taking the cable car back to Nha Trang.

The whole day was a mixture of good and bad experiences. The first major downfall was the price. It was 800,000vnd per person, which is unreasonable if you aren't planning on staying the whole day. The cable car to the mountain was one of the best parts, with some great photo opportunities and an amazing view. Upon arrival to the island, there was a lack of atmosphere, as the place had barely any people and was very quiet. Most of the rides were very underwhelming and small. Although we didn't get the adrenaline rush we were looking for, there were barely any lines anywhere, so we could go on the rides as much as we wanted. A big highlight was the ride that went up the hill, then you were able to control your speed on the way back down. That was lots of fun for the whole family, and had some incredible views in the afternoon and at night. Unfortunately that was the only ride with a longer line. In terms of food, I would not recommend eating at the Lotteria fast food chain. Inside, it was PACKED with pigeons that were flying everywhere trying to get a bit of your food. We went to eat at midday and they only had two options of burgers. When the burger arrived, it was cold, bland, and utterly disgusting. One part that we all really enjoyed was the water park. The slides were quite big, had short lines and were loads of fun. There was also an inflatable obstacle course on the water that was so different and hilarious! Swimming in the ocean was a great escape from the heat. Overall, it could be a good time if you are more interested in water parks rather than roller coasters. Either way, we don't really think it's worth the money.
For what it is, it’s a cool spot! Probably one of the best theme parks in Vietnam. The dolphin show was on point and some of the rides were exhilarating.

Nice aquarium and zoo! But we came here for the water park. When you go to a waterpark in Europe you can just go in the tubes without the help of a lifeguard. In Vinpearl there has to be a lifeguard at every slide, the problem was that there weren't enough lifeguards so we had to wait and wait and wait...some slides where closed because there was no lifeguard available which left us somewhat disappointed...

Went to Vinpearl land yesterday. Large sections of the park seem to be abandoned or closed. The person that designed the map that you get when you get off the cable cars, and the signs around the park obviously had a good sense of humour because neither the map or signs seem to bear any resemblance to what is on the ground.Our 6 year old was under 1.2m so he couldn't do many of the rides, yet they were still happy to charge full price for his admission.Once you figure out how to find something to do it's not that bad, but there are better ways to spend your $$ in Nha Trang.
Good for families with lots of attractions. We found it could do with a little TLC as it is starting to look tired. Loved the cable car  ride.

We had the amusement park included in our stay at the Vinpearl Golf resort. Glad we didnt pay extra as my children were too small for nearly everything. The height restrictions were 130cm for most rides even water slides.  My 5 year old was very disappointed he could not go much and my 2 year old was not even allowed one the Carousel with me..... that said the shows and zoo were fantastic and the new blooming gardens were gorgeous. Still a great place to visit if staying in island but doesn't compare to a theme park in Australia.

We had the luxury of staying at one of the hotels on the island which included daily entry into the park so we managed to avoid a lot of the cable car queues and had free run at the attractions last thing at night which was great.We had read a lot of negative reviews before we went, mainly from westerners (like myself) about how poor quality it was, unclean, small rides etc. It is not Disneyland or the Magic Mountain and doesn't pretend to be.Take it for what it is and you will have a great time  here. Yeah the queues can be long, show me a theme park in the high season where that doesn't happen and i will show you a bad theme park. No the rights aren't the biggest you can find but they are still a lot of fun.The water slides were great although my daughter had a pair of flip flops and a hat taken which was poor form. The inflatable course was heaps of fun but the large amount of jellies / stingers in the water was a bit off putting, as was the blood all over the inflatables from someones cut which the lifeguards didn't seem to be bothered about.
cảnh đẹp, cá nhiều loại, mát mẻ, nhân viên dễ thương và nhiệt tình, good job
Mua này mưa nhiều nên nhiều trò chơi bị hạn chế, công viên nước thú vị, có khu sở thú và vườn hoa. Đồ ăn tạm ổn, ra khu habour nhiều lựa chọn hơn. Có thể đi ra vô nhiều lần. Đi bộ hơi nhiều

Cáp treo dỡ nên chỉ được đi tàu.Đóng cửa khá nhiều, không đáng so với tiền vé bỏ ra! Việc đóng cửa và dỡ bỏ để tu sửa thì mình nghĩ Vin nên giảm giá vé cho thích hợp, mua vé 750k mà ko chơi được gì mấy thấy phí tiền lắm!Thuỷ cung hơi cũ và ko được đẹp lắm, kính mờ và xước nhiều, mấy bé cá lớn lớn và mấy bé bò sát rất có khiếu tạo dáng, thấy máy ảnh là nhìn thẳng ống kính luôn.Vườn thú thì mình đi buổi trưa, chắc đang giờ nghỉ nên mấy bé thú lười lắm Do dịch vắng người nên mặc dù là khu vui chơi nhưng ko bầu không khí khá ảm đạm

Điểm vui chơi lý tưởng tại Nha Trang không thể bỏ qua, vòng xoay và khu vườn là nơi đáng để tham quan và trải nghiệm. Chỉ tiết là cáp treo đã bị gỡ đi để bảo trì, Công viên nước cũng không hoạt động. Còn lại thì xứng đáng trong tầm tiền.

Sau khi dịch Covid 19 cơ bản được kiểm soát tại Nha Trang, tôi có dịp ghé thăm và vui chơi ở Vinwonders, đó là một trải nghiệm vô cùng thú vị trong dịp cuối tháng 10. Điều đáng tiếc là không được ngắm thành phố từ tuyến cáp treo xuyên biển và không thể tham gia các trò chơi dưới nước, nhưng không vì thế mà mất đi trải nghiệm đáng nhớ. Dạo quanh vườn hoa, tản bộ trong vườn thú, vườn chim, rụng tim với các trò cảm giác mạnh, zipline, sâu lắng cùng show nhạc nước. Chất lượng dịch vụ Vinwonders mang lại không có gì phải phàn nàn, nhưng sẽ còn tốt hơn nếu các kiosk bổ sung thêm các phương thức thanh toán không tiền mặt. Nhân viên rất nhiệt tình từ khâu tiếp đón, bán vé đến hướng dẫn viên trò chơi. Vinwonders đã mang lại cho tôi một ngày giải trí tuyệt vời.

Xin chào mọi ngừoi. Tôi và gia đình đã có dịp trải nghiệm tại Vinwonders Nha Trang vào ngày 9/4 vừa qua. Không gian vui chơi vô cùng rộng lớn, hiện đại, phù hợp cho tất cả các thành viên trong gia đình. Có rất nhiều khu chơi mà trẻ nhỏ rất thích như vườn chim, cung điện hải vương, đu quay, xe đụng vv. Vinwonders cũng rất quan tâm đến ngừoi cao tuổi khi bố trí rất nhiều chỗ nghỉ chân, toilet. Và có rất nhiều cho chơi cảm giác mạnh phù hợp với các bạn trẻ tuổi. Đồ ăn của vinwonders cũng khá ngon, giá cả cũng vừa phải. Phải nói là các bạn nhân viên ở Vinwonders rất tử tế và niềm nở. Họ luôn tận tình hướng dẫn cho khách tham quan. Ngày hôm đó tôi có đánh rơi 1 chiếc ví tại khu nhạc nước, và đc bạn Lê Đình Cương thuộc bộ phận an ninh khu vực nhặt đc và trao trả lại. Qua đây tôi xin chân thành cám ơn Vinwonders Nha Trang, đặc biệt là bạn Cương đã nhặt được và phối hợp cùng bộ phận an ninh để trao lại chiếc ví cùng toàn bộ tài sản bên trong.À mọi ngừoi nhớ xem Tatashow nhé. Đây là nột show cực kì hoành tráng. Các bạn nhỏ sẽ rất là mê đấy ^^.

Ở đây cũng không có gì đặc sắc lắm. Chắc do mình đến ngày mưa nên chẳng chơi được gì. Mấy trò ở đây thì hầu hết mọi nơi đều có. Mọi người đến đây nhớ mang theo 1 chai nước 500ml đi nhé, nước ở đây mắc lắm.

Công viên nước là tốt , nhưng bên khu vui chơi cảm giác mạnh là khá chán, quá ích trò chơi và đa số những đều cũ lạc hậu từ 10 năm trước. Hy vọng Vingroup cải tạo lại khu cv trò chơi này vì đây là nơi nhiều khách du lịch của Nha Trang, cứ như hiện tại ko thay đổi thì sẻ không ai muốn quay lại.

Đẹp, rộng rãi, nhưng chờ xếp hàng rất lâu. Nhưng đi cap treo rất thú vị. Các trò chơi đa dạng nhưng sắp xếp nhiều khu và các khu xa nhau quá mà k có xe trung chuyển nên đi rất mệt. Và đợi chơi trò chơi xếp hàng cực lâu. Chưa sắp xếp các chỗ nghỉ mát cho khách muốn nghỉ ngơi vào buổi trưa để khách nghỉ sau những khi tản bộ mệt mỏi

Khu vui chơi giải trí bậc nhất VN nằm trên hòn đảo được nối liền bởi tuyến cáp treo vượt biển dài nhất thế giới. Đến đây, bạn được tự mình trải nghiệm các trò chơi rất hấp dẫn : xe trượt núi dài, đầy cảm giác mạnh từ đỉnh núi xuống, đường trượt zipline sở hữu 3 kỉ lục của VN,.. hay vườn thú nhiều loài quí hiếm, show cá heo đầy thú vị, tât show rực rỡ sắc màu,...Chúng tôi đã có những giây phút rất sảng khoái tại đây sau những ngày làm việc mệt nhọc. Sẽ còn ghé lại và giới thiệu cho nhiều người nữa.

Mình đi vào những ngày cuối tháng 5 nên là lượng khách chưa phải quá đông hay phải xếp hàng chờ đi cáp hoặc bất cứ nơi nào. Thủy cung là nơi khá đẹp, tuy nhiên các bồn bể cả nên vệ sinh mặt kính bên trong vì khi nhìn cá khá mờ. Bể chính nếu được Vin hãy thêm những dòng cá có sắc màu nhiều hơn sẽ khiến cho các bạn bé con đỡ hoảng sợ!!!Mình có lên thăm quan khu Vườn hoa thì chỉ có 1 vườn hoa đồng tiền trong lồng kính là được xử lý thông gió bằng qạt, vào đó rất mát. Tuy nhiên sang các khu lồng kính khác lại không được như thế nên không thể vào thăm quan do nó thực sự bí và nóng --> nên xem xét lại.Màn trình diễn cá heo + hải cẩu rất vui và xuất sắc! (14h)Trình diễn nàng tiên cá, nên thay đổi cốt truyện của người hướng dẫn viên khi thuyết trình lúc các nàng tiên múa vì chưa đủ hấp dẫn để níu chân du khách đứng lại xem, mọi người nán lại đa phần ở Thủy cung mát hơn các nơi khác do có điều hòa. (15h10)Do lúc mình đi nhiều công trình cung điện đang tu sửa nên cũng không tham quan được hết và mình chỉ sang đó chơi từ sáng đến chiều không ở lại tối nên không xem được màn trình diễn nước ánh sáng.Cuối cùng thì đây là 1 hành trình cũng khá thú vị để có thể lựa chọn tham quan nếu như ghé Nha Trang. Giá vé thì sẽ khá cao nếu như chỉ sang đó ngắm cảnh như 2 vợ chồng mình (do mình có bầu nên không tham gia chơi bất kì trò chơi hay vào khu công viên nước). Cũng có thể Vinpearl nên nghiên cứu có 2 loại vé 1 là chỉ để tham quan và 2 là full options!!!Chúc mọi người sẽ có được hành trình thú vị trong Vinpear!!!

đẹp, đáng đi khi tới nha trang. nên đi gia đình, đi một mình hơi buồn. Khách khá đông nhưng không khí bên đảo tốt. Nhân viên khá thân thiện, chi phí hơi cao so với giá trị. Đi một lần để biết chứ không quay lại làm gì.

Nếu đã từng đi Bà Nà Hill ở Đà Nẵng thì Vinpearl Nha Trang mình thấy khá bình thường. Cáp treo đi khá ngắn, trò chơi và công viên nước không quá đặc sắc, giống công viên nước Hồ Tây.Được cái Thủy cung đẹp, trò Zipline nhìn thú vị nhưng đông quá không chơi được dù đi vào tháng 10.
Tôi thích dịch vụ ở nơi này,,không khí yên tĩnh,cách phục vụ chu đáo rất nhiệt tình ,tôi nghĩ các bạn nên đến đây để trải nghiệm thử một lần cho biết ,

Diện tích quá rộng, nắng cực nóng nên di chuyển qua lại các điểm khá mệt. Phù hợp cho các bạn trẻ thích trò chơi cảm giác mạnh, đồ ăn vs nước uống bắt buộc mua trên đảo nên giá khá cao, buffet trung bình ko có sự lựa chọn cho nước ép chỉ có nước lọc

Với gia đình có trẻ nhỏ thì đây là nơi thăm quan là chính chứ không có nhiều chỗ chơi cho bé. Được xây dựng trên địa hình dốc nên phải đi bộ và thang cuốn. Các bố mẹ mang địu sẽ tiện hơn là dùng xe đẩy, mang ô che nắng cho con vì không nhiều cây xanh.
Bạn nào thích chơi cảm giác mạnh sẽ rất thích ở đây!.Tuy nhiên vẫn có những trò nhẹ hơn cho các bạn còn lại. Bạn nào có bé nhỏ cũng có khu vui chơi trong nhà riêng.
Tất cả mọi thứ đều đẹp. Tuy nhiên lượng khách du lịch tới quá đông nên việc sử dụng các trò chơi đều phải chờ lâu.

Thú vị, nhiều trò chơi, dịch vụ tốt, có thể vui chơi cả ngày, tuy nhiên địa điểm ăn uống ít và đắt, khách Trung Quốc chiếm 60%, 20% khách Nga, còn lại là Việt Nam và những người bạn khác, cảm giác như đang đi du lịch Trung Quốc

Cùng hệ thống của Vinpearl nên cũng tựa như VinpearlLand của Phú Quốc mình đã đi. Mình đều thích vì các công trình xây dựng theo hướng cổ tích, mình và các bé nhỏ nhà mình rất yêu thích . Có nhiều trò chơi thú vị, nhiều backgroud để chụp ảnh sống ảo ^^

Đi Nha Trang ai cũng phải den Vinpearl mot lan, ma thuong đã đến rồi thì sẽ ko quay lại.Giá cao, dịch vụ đắt đỏ, phù hợp với khách TQ nhieu hơn la Việt Nam!.Khu nay thì công nhận quy mô và có đầu tư,Khach nên đi tư 10h sang, đi 1 vòng, ăn trưa, vao xem phim... roi ra chơi các tro chơi cam giac manh, tắm cong vien nước, ăn tối, xem nhac nước, rồi ra ve.Từ Trung tâm Tran Phu, nen don tuyen xe bus số 4, chạy thăng xuong bến Vinpearl lun, tiết kiem dc nhieu tien di chuyeb

Thật sự là một nơi vui chơi tuyệt vời.từ vườn thú.các loại hoa.cây cảnh.khu vui chơi trong nhà thật sự mình không thể nào quên được.cảm ơn Vinpearlland Nha Trang đã để lại cho mình kí ức rất đẹp.rất nhiều khu fast food.kết nhất là với một khu rộng như vậy thì WC ở đây rất nhiều và sạch sẽ mình kết cái điểm này ở đây.nhân viên chào đón rất thân thiện.Really like here!!
khu trò chơi rất nhiều . view rất đẹp món ăn lại ngon . có điều khách du lịch trung quốc đông là tôi cảm thấy khó chịu

Wow, những bạn biểu diễn ở show chim rất dễ thương, những chú chim tuyệt đẹp khó có thể nhìn thấy trong tự nhiên. Show cá heo rất hấp dẫn, bé nhà mình hào hứng hết nấc khi được bơi cùng cá heo. Tuy nhiên mình và bé nhà mình không vui lắm khi thăm vườn thú và nhà chim, nói thật, nuôi nhốt cũng phải tạo môi trường gần gũi thiên nhiên. qua ít cây cối, chủ yếu là những tảng cây bê tông, bê tông và bê tông khắp nơi. Vườn chim nên có thêm những cây to tỏa bóng, và vườn thú cũng vậy.Nói chung, vui rất vui! Nhưng hơi buồn cho các bạn chim nhỏ bé và những bạn sư tử, hổ gấu....!

Khu vui chơi hiện đại, rộng lớn rất nhiều trò chơi, cảnh rất đẹp tận 90 điểm check in lận. Lang thang cả ngày chơi ko hết. Mình đã có thêm rất nhiều bức ảnh đẹp. Giá vé trọn gói 800/người, sẽ tuyệt vời hơn nếu như giá vé thấp hơn.
Nhiều trò chơi, phong cảnh đẹp. Giá vé đã bao gồm trò chơi. Đi cáp treo ngắm cảnh biển rất thích. Chè và buffet ok. Các gian hàng ăn khác tạm.

1 ngày không thể đi hết được Vinpearl land vì quá rộng lớn, phong phú nhiều trò chơi, điểm thăm quan. Vinpearl có thêm khu vườn thú, vườn chim, khu nhà hoa, nhà sương rồng khá thú vị. 1 địa điểm nhất định phải đi khi đến Nha Trang. Tuy nhiên phải xếp hàng khá lâu khi đi cáp treo, và cực kỳ đông người Trung Quốc nên ồn ào vô cùng!

Không gian thoáng đãng, cảnh đẹp, trò chơi phong phú và đa dạng phù hợp cho mọi lứa tuổi. Các trò chơi được chơi miễn phí không giới hạn số lần, chỉ mua vé 1 lần và được trải nghiệm hết mọi dịch vụ vui chơi. Phí ăn uống chấp nhận được đối với 1 khu du lịch hiện đại, văn minh. Các trò chơi mới liên tục được bổ sung, tăng sự trải nghiệm sau mỗi năm ghé thăm.

Trong 2 ngày ở Nha Trang, nhà mình dành trọn 1 ngày chơi ở Vinpearl Land, một điểm mà rất nhiều trang mạng đều đề xuất là không thể bỏ qua khi tới nơi đây.Song, rút kinh nghiệm, nếu đã có kế hoạch đi Vinpearl, các bạn nhớ khởi hành từ sớm, bởi như nhà mình, đến Vinpearl cũng đã gần trưa, mọi người chỉ kịp chơi 2 trò mạo hiểm (Mine Adventure và Pirate Ship), sau đó, đa phần các trò chơi đều nghỉ giữa hiệp tầm 30'. Hai trò này gần tuyến cáp treo nên thuận đường đi lại và cũng rất hấp dẫn. Bước chân xuống mà ai nấy vẫn nói cười hỉ hả. Có một điểm thú vị là ngay cả các chú thú trong Vườn quý vương (King's Garden) của đảo hình như cũng chỉ làm việc trong giờ hành chính, nên mặc khách ngó nghiêng, chỉ trỏ, bọn chúng chỉ nằm ườn lười biếng trong bóng mát. Sau khi làm 1 vòng sở thú, cả nhà tiếp tục tham quan Cung điện hải vương (Underwater World). Bọn trẻ rất thích thú khi được đi qua đường hầm dưới nước, ngắm lũ cá mập lừ lừ bơi lượn bên trên. Nhưng chẳng gì bằng vụ chúng được vẫy vùng thật sự trong nước nên Công viên nước (Water Park) vẫn là điểm ưa thích nhất của tụi nhóc. Một trò nữa cũng được mê tít là nhà phao, thiết kế theo kiểu Sasuke - Thử thách không giới hạn Việt Nam.Trước khi trở về, cả nhà vẫn còn ham hố chơi nốt trong Lâu đài ánh sáng ngay dưới khu cáp. Quả là 1 ngày vận động mỏi rã rời nhưng VUI.Ngoài điểm vui chơi kể trên, mọi người khi du lịch Nha Trang cũng có thể đi thêm tắm bùn, thăm Tháp Bà Ponagar hay Dốc Lết... còn nhà mình đã đi từ những dịp trước nên tạm biệt Nha Trang thôi!

Nơi này thật sự rất thích hợp để vui chơi cùng bạn bè, đi càng nhiều càng vui. Nhiều trò giải trí đa dạng, thích nhất là đi cáp treo qua biển và công viên nước. Cảm giác nhìn biển từ trên cao, trong một cabin đang chuyển động thực sự rất thích thú. Càng vào bên trong, Vinpearl càng khiến bạn ngạc nhiên bởi sự rộng lớn, đẹp đẽ và sự đa dạng của các loại hình giải trí. Trong nhà cũng có, ngoài trời cũng có, trò chơi nhẹ nhàng cũng có mà cảm giác mạnh cũng đầy luôn. Mình là một đứa rất nhát gan nhưng cũng bị ghiền mấy trò trượt nước trong công viên nước ở đây rồi. Mình sẽ cho nơi này 5* nếu mình không gặp anh nhân viên đeo kính cận không thân thiện kia. Là nhân viên trực khu vực trò chơi, mình nghĩ anh nên sắp xếp để người nào tới trước thì sẽ được chơi trước, chứ mấy anh thanh niên mạnh tay mạnh chân cứ đi giành phao với con gái thì bọn mình giành sao lại được. Mình nói anh nhân viên rồi mà ảnh không hề có chút phản ứng gì ><. Hơi đáng tiếc.
Những trò chơi ở Vinpearl thì vui phải biết rồi nhưng quả thật trò nào cũng đông ơi là đông đi chen chúc rất mệt nhưng cũng phải thôi.

Mọi thứ đều ổn trừ vụ booking rồi mà nhận thẻ lâu kinh khủng.  Tốt nhất là đến mua bằng tìên mặt cho rồi,  với nữa là vé booking chỉ có giá trị trong vòng ngày còn vé mua trực tíêp thì đc sử dụng trong vòng 90 ngày,  lại còn đc tích đỉêm. Nói chung vụ booking thấy chán quá.
Đây là khu vui chơi với nhiều trò chơi hấp dẫn nhưng khi làm thủ tục xếp hàng lên cáp thì rất lâu và mệt vì trời nóng và khách du lịch thì rất đông

Thích nhất là đi cáp treo qua Vinpear, biển xanh nắng vàng, tôi đã thăm thủy cung và choáng ngợp trước nhiều loài cá lớn của đại dương cũng như những loài cá nhỏ nhiều màu sắc được nuôi tại đây. Sẽ quay lại
Khu vui chơi rộng, nhiều trò chơi cho trẻ em, thanh niên... không thích hợp lắm đối với người lớn tuổi
Nổi tiếng với đánh giá là khu vui chơi tuyệt vời nhất cho bất cứ ai đến Nha Trang, gia đình tôi rất thích nơi này. Bạn nên thử 1 lần khi đến Nha Trang
Vào nha trang mình rất thích đi vinpear này, có rất nhiều trò chơi cảm giác mạnh, chương trình nhạc nước thay đổi nhiều, thuỷ cung đẹp

Khu này đang trong được xây dựng cải tạo để bớt lỗi thời so với xu hướng giải trí trong khu vực. Khách China quá đông nên chen lấn xếp hàng mất time nhiều. Buffet cho bữa trưa không đa dạng.Việc đi lại còn bất tiện
Rộng rãi,  không gian thoáng,  còn nhiều địa điểm chưa ghé,  có cơ hội sẽ quay lại.Công viên nước với những trò chơi hấp dẫn.
bên đây có rất nhiều trò chơi, đặc biệt là những trò lạ như trượt núi, trò xoay xoay đưa người lên cao nữa, rất tuyệt!!!

Khu giải trí có bờ biển đẹp, công viên nước rất lớn. Khu trò chơi ngoài trời rất nhiều trò chơi, còn khu trong nhà có nhiều máy không hoạt động. Thủy cung đẹp, nhạc nước hay. Tuy nhiên đi nhiều lần thì thấy cũng không có nhiều đổi mới. Thức ăn mắc và không ngon. Đi cáp treo rất thích vì nhìn toàn cảnh biển từ trên cao thật đẹp.

Nếu ai đến Nha trang nên cố gắng vào Vinpearl . Giá vào cổng 600k nhưng xứng đáng với 2 chiều đi cáp treo 3,3km  ngắm vịnh Nha Trang, vào chơi các trò chơi từ sáng đến chiều miễn phí . Ấn tượng nhất là trò Trượt núi : phải xếp hàng hàng giờ mới đến lượt đi khoảng 5 phút nhưng thích thú và lại có tấm hình chụp tự động. các trò chơi trượt nước, trượt thác cũng tốt cho người thích cảm giác mạnh . Nhà nào có trẻ em cho chơi dòng sông lười cũng đáng . Bãi biển ở đảo cũng rất sạch và nước trong vắt . Thủy cung thì có nàng tiên cá ( người thật ) bơi cùng các loài cá biển rất đẹp.
Có nhiều trò chơi vui, cảm giác mạnh. Cảnh quan đẹp, Được chụp hình và vui đùa thỏa thích tại hồ bơi

Gia dinh minh co tre em nen khi sang Vinpearl choi phai mang theo thuc an cung nhu sua cho be.Nhan vien nen thong cam khi thay gia dinh co tre em ,khong nen xet net qua nhu vay!
