bảo vệ sẽ kiểm tra túi xách, bắt gửi lại đồ ăn và nước uống (họ muốn phải mua ở khu vui chơi bên đảo) vì vậy hơi bất tiện cho những nhóm muốn vui chơi cả ngày. Đây đủ các trò chơi cảm giác mạnh, bãi biển sạch đẹp. chi phí mua vé hợp lý cho cả ngày vui chơi tại đây

Nếu ở khu khách sạn thì sang khu vui chơi khá tiện bằng xe điện. Nếu từ Thành phố có thể đi cáp treo hoặc xuồng, phà sang khu vui chơi. Lưu ý là bảo vệ sẽ kiểm tra túi xách, bắt gửi lại đồ ăn và nước uống (họ muốn phải mua ở khu vui chơi bên đảo). Vào ngày cuối tuần, nếu đến bến cáp treo vào buổi chiều, chắc chắn sẽ phải xếp hàng 1-2 giờ. Nên bắt đầu đi từ khoảng 2h từ thành phố, khi sang đảo, vào ngay khu Thủy cung xem lịch biểu diễn của nàng tiên cá để không bỏ lỡ, chơi các trò điện tử trong nhà rồi sang khu công viên nước (họ đóng cửa lúc 6 giờ chiều). Bãi biển nhân tạo bên khu công viên nước có cát cực mịn, hướng nhìn thành phố. Buối tối có 2 ca biểu diễn nhạc nước. Cuối cùng, bạn sẽ phải xếp hàng dài và chen nhau lên cáp treo để về thành phố. Nói chung, đến Nha Trang thì nên sang đây vui chơi. Nhưng cũng không nên đi nhiều lần, vì nơi đây càng ngày càng cũ, xấu. Nên để lại ấn tượng tốt như lần đầu.

Tôi có dịp đến Nha Trang và sang công viên Vinpearl để chơi, Vào công viên nước thỏa sức vùng vẫy  ,không chỉ đám mình trong dòng nước trong mát của dòng sông lười mà còn thả mình trượt theo các máng trượt không muốn rời . Xuống biển trong làn nước trong xanh và êm đềm thỏa sức mà bơi không muốn rời .
nói chung đay là khu vui choi ,giải trí rất sang trọng và ,đẹp.chỉ có điều duy nhất đó là phải đi hơi xa  và rất đông người
Với số tiền bạn bỏ ra thật xứng đáng với những khu vui chơi tại đó.Nhưng giá dịch vụ ăn, uống hơi đắt
Khu vui chơi đông nên chúng tôi phải xếp hàng nhiều, mất thời gian. Nên có nhiều trò chơi hơn. Khu vui chơi nên cập nhật các trò chơi mới.

We travelled across on the cable car which was the first experience of our visit. Some nice views as you travel across.  We only paid for the harbourside and not the amusement park.  The site was very quiet post a typhoon An interesting collection of buildings etc. We walked there from town taking in the beach and a couple of other sites. We did however get a taxi back as we were there longer than we expected.

We heard about the cable car and VinWonder Resort from some Australian people we met in our hotel. We were surprised that there wasn’t more information about it on ‘Things to do in Nha Trang’ on TripAdvisor. The receptionist at our hotel, The Novotel, was extremely helpful and booked a package for us, which consisted of the cable car ride, both ways including entrance to VinWonder (which is a resort accessed from VinPearl Harbour)  and an evening Buffett meal. As we are both over 60, we got the whole package for 600,000 VND (£16-80 each) We got to the VinPearl cable car station by taxi. As we had booked our tickets online, we were able to go straight through to the cable car. If you haven’t booked, there is a booking office at the entrance. The cable car crossing was great, if a little breezy. The cable car probably holds about 10 people, but they loading people in very small and large groups, depending on if they knew each other. Once we arrived at VinPearl Harbour, there were these lovely Disneyland type buildings and shops, but we were surprised at the lack of information boards and booths. We really struggled to suss out where to go and what the suggested route should be. There is a sign board with a map outside the cable car entrance, but it’s basically in Vietnamese and there was no mention in English of any buffet venue. We ambled about after going left from the cable car entrance and came to a plaza and to our right, the entrance to VinWonder (The theme park & gardens) We didn’t realise that there was a separate area for the theme park. So basically, I now realise you can buy a cable car ticket which gives you access ONLY to the Disney type village at the harbour.  You have to buy another ticket if you want access to VinWonder, which is the theme park with fun fair rides, shops, restaurants, gardens etc. We were to find out later, that our buffet restaurant was actually within the VinWonder theme park. Due to the lack of information, we thought it was outside and left the theme park. Once we found out the buffet was actually inside the theme park, we had to struggle together back in again! All this could have been avoided if they had information booths to help visitors. A word of warning, the park is huge and involves a lot of walking, which when it’s hot, it can be challenging. There is a shuttle bus, but it finishes at 5pm. When we arrived, around 4pm, it was fairly quiet. I did hear that it can get very busy in the mornings. We hiked up to the Kings Garden area and decided to have ride on the Ferris Wheel, before going any further. The wheel takes about half an hour to do a full rotation. We then went to find our restaurant for the buffet, which I’ve already explained, it wasn’t straightforward. We got there eventually and the food was okay. It seems if you get there early (it opens at 5:30pm) there is a little more choice. We left the park after the meal and headed back to our hotel. The whole experience could have been even more enjoyable if there was more information for foreign tourists. A beautiful resort, but it could be so much more.

Enjoyed Holidays with parents and baby. Delicious, comfortable. No chic and no scoop. It's tastefully done. For people. During the rest part of the beach was in the "construction" and tractors drove around the beaches, leaving a stink and dirt.

Actually, this is a great place. VERY big. You probably need 2-3 days more to see/do everything. A little expensive entry (950k with the cable car t / r) and if you only ride the cable car you have to buy upgrade with 850 even though you have paid 200k already. There were not many people when we were there so the queues were not lp None but we only had time for the bathing in one day. There are restrictions on swimming though. You should preferably be lver 150cm, but under 50 years old and ubder 100kg heavy. Otherwise, you'll be denied most things. This is why mpnga gives it bad ratings.

I liked the Ferris wheel, the wonderful zoo (feeding the capybaras separately and on the clock, we went to the session for 15.00), the giraffes, the pink flamingos. Visited the butterfly garden, the rose garden. Compact aquazone, husband in size (height and weight) was not allowed anywhere( Very few rides in general are exactly rides, merry-go-rounds, roller coasters in the style of Indiana Jones (well, quite children's). There's an area for babies with carousels. I liked the film with the effect of flying in the castle, I strongly advise you to go there!The 7:00 p.m. music-water show is normal. But the show at the castle 19:30 to 20:10 is a total delight! It was so natural for the villains to play there, even I got the creeps!Dog, we bought all-day discounted hotel tickets on the island. There is an option to buy at the park checkout after 4:00 p.m. with a discount. But in fact, it's not a good offer. A lot of areas are open till 5:30, it doesn't make sense.

Everything is great, the weather is good, the mood is good, the desire to rest to the fullest is what this park has managed to get. Rolled up, bathed enough. You can forget the sun cream, or you can burn.

Nice park, we liked it. Beautiful zoo, well-kept animals. Giraffes were fed for extra money. Liked the birds just as much, but there aren't many in variety. The entertainment is interesting, especially if you come here with a kid, I think the kids will really enjoy this place. Didn't like the food here, not very nice cafes.

We were in the park with our husband and their children, ages 9 and 3. The younger one didn't even go on the kids' harmless rides. She's 5'5", so she had nothing to do in the park. Although I certainly wanted to ride. She was very upset. We were only able to ride a few locations because of the long lines. You had to wait 30 to 40 minutes everywhere! And the attraction itself is designed for 3-4 minutes. The ZIP line didn't have time to buy tickets. Didn't find it immediately, because for the guests of the Vinperl Hotel, the entrance to the park is quite different. Keep that in mind! Loved the zoo and the Ferris wheel!The water park was not ridden either, because for our children due to height the entrance to the slide was closed. The minimum growth limit is above 133cm. Therefore, for us the cost of the park was not justified. Maybe we'll go back to the park when the kids are older so we can fully enjoy all the rides. P.s. the evening laser show is above all praise! You must watch it!

Vinpearl Nha Trang is a large and impressive park, but one day is definitely not enough to see everything. The park offers a variety of activities and attractions, but it’s best suited for those over 140cm tall, as there aren’t many options for smaller children.We had some issues with the dining experience. At the Dolphin BBQ Grill, there was a lot of chaos, with loud arguments over table reservations and food orders that didn’t arrive. It was quite frustrating and took away from the overall experience.Despite these setbacks, we had an enjoyable day. Be prepared for a lot of walking, but the park itself is beautiful and offers plenty to see and do. Just plan for more than one day if you want to experience it all!
Was a cold day. More suited for families on a warmer day. Ride's were fun. Boat ride over was fun. Give it three and a half star's.... mostly for the weather
The ride with the cable car is very nice with good views.Upstairs it is quite nice and interesting, but we were not in the amusement park..

Pros:1. 1050kVND/ticket/pax for Cable car back and forth unlimited rides within one day from the city cross the sea to Vinpearl on Hon Tre island, included all their games (Zipline, Alpine coaster, the Sky wheel,..) is a fair price comparing to Zipline ticket of Kong Forest in Hon Ba mountain 1000kVND/ticket/pax/ 1hour ride shorter Zipline.2. Vinwonder park is inside the Vinpearl area, to walk all the surrounding Vinpearl and inside Vinwonder park, my watch counts about 25k steps that day. Good for exercise and sightseeing all their gardens, ocean view, and of course taking a lot of amazing pictures.3. The Light-Water-Fire show at 6:45pm is a good show to wait for. Come early 40 minutes before the start to reserve center view was worthy of my time.Cons:1. If your target is Zipline, Alpine coaster, ... games, be prepared that other people think the same. The waiting queue for Alpine coaster is 1.5 hours when we arrive at 10am. The Zipline is even worse, out of ticket at 11am. Arrive earlier like 8-9am might work for these games.2. The food in restaurants in Vinpearl is average quality. Not good taste comparing to the food in Nha Trang city.

Fornøyelsesparken funker helt fint for par både med og uten barn! Det var stor variasjon på om det var kø til de ulike aktivitetene eller ikke. Hele parken er utrolig vakkert designet! Men de ulike delene har også veldig ulik kvalitet!!!!Alpine Coaster and Skywheel 9/10Harry and the lost magicant og Tata world river Adventure 10/10!!!!!! They were beyond cool and so well designed! (The story sucked, but who cares when everything else is mesmerising!)The zoo and aquarium on the other hand was so depressing. The animals have tiny areas to move, they looked stressed or depressed... and even the plants in the terrarium was fake. 1/10The gardens were very beautiful, but the domes were small and should have more information about the different plants 6/10The shows were very cool! The musical water show was a favourite 9/10 (the music is too loud, but the design on the water was so impressive!)The big finale Tata show was sooo impressive in regards to the big figures, scenography and costumes! Again - the story was weak, and the dancing was not very engaging (most of the actors looked bored). There were some cool effects as fire breathing and acrobatics, but the lights were often directed towards the audience and not the artists, so you couldn't see things too well.... There were also long pauses with nothing happening. I think this is a cool show for kids and people with a special interest in costumes and scenography. Make sure to get a good place early, cause you won't see much if you end up in the back 6/10.Cable cars 🚡 10/10. So smooth and gives a great view!Understanding what kind of ticket you need (or more importantly DON'T NEED), and understanding the coupons and offers, is ridiculously complicated. 2/10

We went on an overcast December day which turned very wet after 17.00. There is discounted ticket for over 60s when you buy your ticket before crossing over on the cable car. As others have commented it’s a big park with lots to do and see. We spent the morning in the water park - there are a lot of slides BUT you must be under 50 years of age, even to get on the lazy river ride -  simply ridiculous. The only way to get round this is to say you are 49!  There are lockers at the water park and it’s beside a beach too. Key recommendations - it was pretty quiet when we went but I hate to think what it might be like in their peak season. Go and do the Alpine ride early on - it’s great but we had to queue late afternoon for at least 30 minutes. Do the zip line first thing - we missed out on it as it has very limited capacity and by the time we went at 4.00pm they were not taking any more bookings. Great fountain and light show around 18.45 - only lasts 20 minutes but really impressive.  Aquarium is very good too. Lots of restaurants around the place.Overall a great day out but crazy policy not being allowed on water slides if you are over 50 - never come across this anywhere else in  the world !!!!!!!

I was overwhelmed coming here. The grab driver without notice stopped on the side of the road to have some guy trying to sell me a ticket. It felt dodgy so I refused. Was it legit? I have no idea but was not going to risk it. At the ticketing booth I had no idea what ticket I even needed. As soon as I got to the island via the cable cars (inside the building behind the ticketing booth and Cable cars are included in the ticket) my stress started to dissipate. The ticket booth gave me a map that initially I couldn’t navigate however I got the hang of it. Initially I walked into fairy land, figure it was for kids and left. I then went into the aquarium which is relatively small but was great. Next was the waterpark which was awesome but I was solo so it was slightly difficult. You can pay for a locker and a towel (towel is like a foot towel size in your bathroom) and u get your deposit back when you return at the end of your session. You put all ur gear in the locker (including the towel as you don’t need it till the end) and ur digital locker key is a wrist band. Here I recommend bringing water shoes as without shoes the ground gets hot! Also you can not wear glasses on the rides  as you can not wear anything loose so mine got shoved into my bathing suit. This was all fun however I found it difficult to find the entrances to some rides. There was complimentary popcorn st the entrance that I grabbed as I left and it was yum! There are numerous food stalls inside too.the waterpark and the kinds garden are the two areas that close the earliest (5.30pm). So the next area I went to was the kings garden which is effectively a mini zoo. It was lovely and at the giraffe zone u can buy food and feed the giraffes. This was a great experience. From here I went to do the zip line which was fantastic fun and I am actually quite scared of show rides! Apparently earlier the line was crazy busy and people waited an hour and a half. I was fortunately on within 5 minutes. You can buy photos from the photo booth as momentos. Next I went to adventure world where all show rides are. Half were closed. The mine rail coaster was fun. As part of the island entrance fee you get a $200k food voucher to use at selected stores. I used mine at the end of the evening toward dinner. I had planned to use it coral restaurant  buffet  but that was closed and it is only open at lunch time. Would I go again - absolutely. There are many service signs to let you know where you are within the park, staying at a hotel resort within the park would be great if you had kids. You wouldn’t see them all day especially teens that are taller than 140cm and can rides without their parents.

The park on the island is a super idea. Can be reached by rope, can be reached by boat (like our rockets). Probably for those who relax in hotels on the island, having such a park is really a holiday decoration - an amusement park is never too much at hand 😛 Strictly speaking in the park Things to do are water park, zipline, sled and rides. The rest are recreational areas. The water park is the most entertaining place. Slides for different tastes and temperaments. There's a lazy river. The coasters were all available, probably because of Monday. It's bad that the river is separate and the slide is on its own. On the slides with cylinders you have to carry these same cylinders from the landing place to the descent yourself. Sometimes it's a considerable distance and it takes a lot of time to walk and drag. Be prepared for this. Zipline is cool. The first thing we did was go straight at him and hardly expected.  The slide you're coming down from, you're sledding up, all clear. I liked it unequivocally. They'll take pictures at the finish line. The price is reasonable. The sleigh feels like the most sought-after attraction. The line was 45 minutes. The climb is long, the descent much faster. The downside is that the person in front is very close - in our case, the girl and she drove very slowly, stopped often, had to follow her. The carousel rides are not good: Ferris wheel, chain. It's nothing special. They didn't go to the zoo. In the parks they did, cool, beautiful. Yes, the ticket price includes a lunch voucher. Find out where you can examine it simply - in cafes there are signs, what kind of vouchers are served. And so, of course, the prices are 2-3 times higher than in Nha Trang. Worth a visit, absolutely.

Definitely need to visit if in Nha Trang or somewhere near :) Immediately moody as soon as get on the funicular :) Fancy views, all groomed. The park is huge and 2 days can definitely be devoted to this idea. In general for adults in terms of rides there is nothing to do there, all mainly for children. If you've been to Disney Land or a less normal amusement park, you'll be bored here. Almost everything is for kids, the area is huge, but mostly it's views and locations for photos. But walking in the open air heat is such a pleasure, believe me, as beautiful as it is. And so all the fun is down :) to the right of the entrance 3d stuff, zipline and carriage up the mountain to the inscription Vinpearl well and the zoo where the coolest is the giraffes whose can be fed and photographed. You might not even waste time on the rest of it. Because AQUA PARK UNTIL 18.00 !!! Storage for things until 5.30 pm !! And after spending time on a sweaty walk in the park you will not have time for the main fun :))) and in the water park is really cool and emotions. The oceanarium there is 2 sharks 1 ray and 3 large fish will take you 10-15 minutes this, for ticking can come in. In the same square on the way to the water park there will be a castle in it 3D show cool where you fly around the world through the big screen too great. Well, the Ferris wheel certainly needs to ride as the views are magical:) so if you have 1 day of everything, do not splash out on the park, study the program in advance, there is both a map, etc. on the site and even it is possible to buy a ticket online if there is a visa card not to wait in line. We unknowingly climbed through the heat of the park and the toko eventually came to the water park for two hours of everything. And stayed for the evening fountain show and the main theatrical dance show with the dragon in the evening. Be sure to sit in the front rows, you will see people sitting in the square in rows that already know what and where and how :) then you will get high and shoot a cool video and photo and can even pull you out of the crowd and involve you in this performance. So until 19.00 do not go anywhere )) in the evening easily and quickly everyone leave by cable car beyond the queues no, but August is not the season of people in general few.

Visited at 7:30pm after having seafood dinner nearby and took the cable car here , it’s beautiful at night with the neon lights . Had a stroll here around the shops and saw dancing performances, fireworks at vinwonders , had ice cream and drinks at Mixue . There’s KFC here and other restaurants , shopping , massage and etc
Very beautiful, a lot of interesting places, the plants in the garden were absolutely amazing, a big plus for the workers, everything was clean and tidy

It's a necessary visit but needs to be more clearly explained how to get there and back from Nha Trang and the various types of tickets that can be purchased and where from. You do need a full day here as there is much to see/do with the cable car ride across and the ferris wheel at dusk being highlights.

When I first arrived at Pearl Island, I felt I didn’t know where to go. After an hour, I slowly found the roller coaster and the Ferris wheel. Water park, flamingos, giraffes, rhinos, sika deer. There are also tigers and lions. etc. Overall it's worth a visit. It would be a shame to come to Nha Trang without visiting Pearl Island.

We liked - The cable car crossing - 3.2 km log and high over the water to the island - cheap return tickets if not going into the theme park We went over on a Monday - it was very quiet. The day before - Sunday was so busy we turned around, would have taken hours to just purchase tickets.Lots of shops / eating places / night market etc Avoid weekends - extremely busy, we chose a Monday to visit and it was not crowdedIts not Disney World but it is a fun place to go and experience.

We had a great day the water park is fantastic - it’s a bit difficult to navigate the maps are confusing and little signage couldn’t find any information desks -also don’t buy the happy combo ticket you just pay 100000 extra to get a 100000 coupon that no one wants even the designated restaurants - but otherwise a fun day.

The theme park is massive with lots of walking required; & strangely basically deserted so no queuing for anything (unlike Disneyland, Universal studios, Epcot centre & theme parks on the Gold Coast) The street performers were entertaining  but therHugee wasn't a sign up to tell you where & when;  there's a Starbucks & a couple of small convenient stores onsite (I found this a cheaper option than buying drinks/alcohol/snacks from the resorts)We spent 3 days here & I think that's what's needed to enjoy it; I found the World Garden amazing not at all what I expected; and if visiting with children you can easily spend a whole day at the water park....have fun 😀

Since the cable car to the island is under maintenance, everyone will now take the shuttle boat at the pier on the other side. Next to the gate is a water park with a zip line. There are many Korean tourists in the park. Next to it is a children's version of the mobile game, which is hard to play. If it’s too exciting for older friends, you can also try it. There are also restaurants in each area, but they are mainly outdoor. Going up is the adult version of the mobile games, greenhouse garden, and extra-large Ferris wheel. The park is open until evening and there will be a parade. Activity.

We rode 10 rides, including the roller coaster, the Ferris wheel, and the water slides. We swam in the wave pool for 1 hour, and we played in the lazy river for 30 minutes. We saw a dolphin show at 12:00 PM and a magic show at 3:00 PM.But my favorite part of the day was the zipline. I had always wanted to try a zipline, and VinWonders NhaTrang had the longest and highest zipline in Vietnam.I waited in line for 2 hours for the zipline, but it was worth it! The feeling of soaring through the air was amazing.After the zipline, my legs were a bit sore, but I was still full of energy. I knew that I would never forget this day.I would definitely recommend VinWonders Nha Trang to anyone looking for a fun and exciting day out.

We had a very good time at Vinwonders. First, my son went on his first roller coaster ride of his life with his dad. He said it was a lot of fun even though there was a long line up. I was expecting him to be scared but he came out with a big happy face. Second, we went to the zoo and we saw a couple of adorable animals. My younger one fed the giraffes while my elder one found the experience a bit boring and he was grumbling and mumbling. It would help if there is a safari ride on a buggy or something under such heat for young children to see the animals. It would also be more helpful if there are food for the little ones to feed the giraffes. Children were picking out leaves from nearby plants to feed to the giraffes. Wasn't sure if the plants are safe for them to chew on... It was too hot for them to walk and see the camels and tigers though I was really hoping they would see them. After lunch, we went to the ultimate water park which brought down the heat. It was fun with many different levels of slides, though they are only allowed for children from 130 cm and above and a fun obstacle course was for 150 cm and above.

Very enjoyable experience. They have a incredibly fun water park. Also Ferry wheel amazing.Lots of activities for family and children.Very hot during daytime so people get tired and can’t enjoy much.Tips: Recommend to visit after 3pm with lower price ticket and the weather is cooler.

VinWonders is fantastic value for money with lots of fun varied activities. We arrived early, looking around the theme park in the morning and heading to the water park around 12 which meant it was quiet and barley any ques. The water slides were great and some very fast (Cosmic Vortex). The only down side is that there was very little to no food for vegetarians. This was especially annoying when it came to the food voucher that we were bought alongside our ticket as there was no vegetarian food.

Vin wonders was incredible value for money. We could not see the whole park in one day. It was very very hot. We went to the water park for the morning and it was much quieter than when we returned in the afternoon. No lines for rides in the water park. Unsure of wait times for the bigger dry rides as we didn’t really do many because of the heat. No lines for the smaller rides. The inflatable aqua park is very hot and burns your feet! Recommend taking water shoes. We were not prepared. Our 7 year old missed out on many water rides because they have a 130cm limit for most. She was allowed on the aqua park though and they do have splash parks as well as a smaller waterpark section with small water slides. The gardens are beautiful and a welcome relief from the crowds in the theme / water park rides. We visited the zoo at the very end of the day so some animals were away but it looked like a nice area.

We had a great day at VinWonders. It was very hot so we spent most of the day at the waterpark. Fantastic rides, no queues which was brilliant. The only problem is that the footpaths/pavement were burning hot and when you went on a ride there was nowhere to leave shoes without burning your feet. You could either take them to the start of the ride but then you had to collect them afterwards which meant absolute pain! Maybe more umbrellas around paving area is required?Otherwise, we didn’t see the whole park - it’s too big for one day but I’d suggest the waterpark and rollercoaster rides as the best option for a family with teens.  Heaps of food options and not overly priced at all.Locker hire really reasonable too.Would definitely recommend as a day trip when staying in or near Nha Trang.

Good for a touristy family thing.  Like a Disneyland.  Zoo, rides, beach with alot of big water attractions.  Little shops and places to eat.  Only accessible by water taxi that they run.  Something to do off the beaten path.  I am an old guy so I would only go back to show someone else.

We had a great time at VinWonders! To be honest we weren’t expecting much but we’re totally surprised with park quality and offerings. Definitely recommended.The waterpark was terrific fun, so many different water slides and entertainment areas. Well staffed by pool attendants the water slides. Note that most of the slides recommend you be under 50 years of age and under 90kgs. Not sure if you could rent towels but maybe bring them just in case you need them. Underwater world - tanks were all clean full of very healthy looking fish and sea creatures. The animal enclosures we saw were clean and tidy with engagement activities for the animals but I am no zoo expert. The bird show was cute but I was uncomfortable with toucans and magnificent parrots being trained to perform. Alpine coaster and Ferris Wheel were great fun for the teenagersThe price of food and drink was exceptionally reasonable - we paid 280k VND for lunch for 4 people, and water was only 15k VND per bottle. Accessibility is poor for disabled guests - no visible ramps or lifts. We didn’t get a map on our way into the park and then could not get one once inside, making navigation around the massive area quite the challenge.You could definitely spend the whole day at the park if you take time to enjoy all the attractions. Even post-Covid with the park still operating at less than 100% (maybe at about 70%) it’s a fun family day out.
Really fun water park with lots of different slides and also a really nice beach with recliners to relax in the sun! Would recommend
Fun and various Many activities in different areas are available, but it's a bit tired to move around without any intramural vehicles like shuttle bus or something

Variety of activities to play. Good price, if you also stay in Vinpearl Hotel, it will be even cheaper. A bit hot. And i was not able to try zipline as too many ppl wanted to do so. Will be back another time. Totally worth the visit.

Because of the virus situation there were maybe 10% of the usual numbers with a few of the rides and attractions closed. So we had the place to ourselves. We enjoyed the wheel so much we went on for a second time later in the day only to choose a pod with faulty air con. That was unpleasant to say the least.Like other reviews have pointed out the animals are kept in enclosures way too small for them and some of the animals look as though they have gone insane.Overall a great place but 4 stars because of the animal enclosures.

Cable car ride was great - we had a car to ourselves.  Not really into rides but we did them all and the big wheel is great - again car to ourselves.  The beach is stunning and it really is a good day out.  There were no Chinese so no queuing I can imagine it’s not quite as nice normally.

While this is family oriented we had a good time here as a middle aged couple also.  My wife cannot do the theme park rides so we skipped that but the water park and animal park were very vice.  In addition the huge wheel and garden enclosure was also very nicely done.  Looks like more work going on here for new attractions.  Some rides closed when we visited due to low foot traffic (no Chinese due to virus).

The day was fantastic with so much to see and do ,we needed more time.We got there 10.30am and left around 6.30pm.The only downside was the animals enclosure. They were kept clean had plenty of food etc but too small for some. The birds had a great huge enclosure but the dolphins are on their own in individual small enclosures. These animals are never on their own in the wild.! I’m no expert but the animals all seemed lonely and sad. I just know in Australia that would not be aloud. I would have preferred to see no animals at all than like that.
Very good place and i can take my happy time from there,nice,the view is beautiful,I like it!...and the island verry verry beautiful

Have now been twice in the last 5 years. They have added loads more in that time and looks as though they have no plans to slow.Water park and theme parks are both decent as is the zoo, aquarium and Ferris wheel. Plenty to do and see for smaller kids too, almost like a small Disney.Entry price isn’t cheap but once there the food and drink prices are reasonable. HUGE lines both going out to the island and coming back but it’s to be expected I guess. Found certain staff to be very rude but again others were perfectly reasonable. Very commercial but in a great setting and definitely worth doing if you can spare it.

Hello all,If you really want to get the most out of Vinpearl land you probably require two days, we got there around ten and got straight into it did not go on all ride but covered most of the place, as i said to much to see and do.The zoo the gardens the water park the rides the list goes on overall a good day i am starting to get a bit old to be going on the water slides and all the extreme rides but i gave it one last shot, worth doing, the cable car to the island was also good.
The only downside was the zoo, small encloseures for big predators, did not like that very much i must say.

Here you can find quite many things: a zoo, attractions of various kinds (including an exciting "zip line" which you have to pay extra for), beach, water sports. The ride on the cable car to get there and back is nice. The view over the bay is fantastic from the car as well as from many viewpoints on the island.The dolphin show (twice a day, the p.m. time is 14.00 - check the timetable) was quite nice.

Went to buy the entrance ticket from an authorised retailer through the suggestion of my Grab driver. Paid 880,000 VND for one. Lucky to have bought them then since the queue to get the tickets were long. However, the queue for getting on the cable car to get to the park took about an hour. About 90% of the tourists over here were Chinese. The cable car ride was great. Really nice view, but expect to share the cable car with others. The park was really nice. It includes a waterpark. Had a great experience overall. Loved going to the Zoo and flower areas. There were also shows/performances you can go to which was really amazing. However, the 4D attraction was really boring. It’s more for young kids. Other than that, the park was fun. Snack stalls are all over the place. There are restaurant to eat at but there’s not really much options in the park. Overall, it was a great experience.

We've been coming here for years and I can honestly say that its only getting! They have added so many rides and things to do. They've also built new resorts on the island along with beautiful villas. Prices are fairly reasonable but some of the rides have really long lines, like 2 hour per ride :(  love the cable car transport over the water which is a ride all by itself. TIP: Go early, leave late and have all the fun you deserve! :)

This park is good for family with kids, there are ride , animals , water park. But for myself , half day would be enough, I like the good view from cable car but it need a long queue if you are leaving around 6pm .

Lots of things to do here it’s basically a water park, theme park, and Zoo in one.A few things to note: Get there very early at around 8:00 and leave early around 4:00, you will miss the massive queues.If the water park is busy the queue for the changing rooms is pretty big so I would suggest buying the locker key outside the changing rooms and then go to one of the toilets near by and change then you will save time.As a westerner I can say that you don’t get many westerners here, a few Russian but that’s about it. Some of the animals look healthy and others not so much.Throughout my travels through Southeast Asia I can say the most unwelcoming and ignorant people (not just to westerners but Vietnamese and Thai) sadly tend to be the Chinese and there are lots of Chinese people here. me and girlfriend were custom to a lot of unhappy looks. Just ignore them and understand it’s extremely common.Hope you enjoy

We spent yesterday at Vinpearl Land and had a wonderful time. It has a waterpark, zoo, and theme park. The waterpark was relatively simple and can’t compare to places like Waterbom in Bali it was still adequate to cool off and included an inflatable obstacle course. The zoo was great to have a quick walk through. The theme park was again fairly simple compared to places like Disneyland or Universal. Think of more of a local show at home. I’ve read reviews about that food being expensive, but I thought compared to other theme parks I’ve visited it was cheap. Avoid the Coral buffet though. Very average and food was cold. Overall it was great for a family with young children. Not sure I’d go if I didn’t have kids though.

Started off with para sailing. People in charged were helpful and friendly and fun.My teens had an awesome time.Went on to do some other rides. Not much queue.Wanted to try the zip line but we got there right when they closed the queue. That was at around 5pm. Utterly disappointed but understandable that they had to close the queue in order to finish by closing time.Snacks were expensive, just like any amusement parks.The most painful part was the queue for the cable car back to town. Took us 1.5hours to get into the cable car. View was amazing though. Will consider returning again.
Lots to doDisneyland , beach , waterpark and zoo rolled into oneMake a day of it and take your bathing suit.

Recommend to be at the cable car station by 830am, to avoid human traffic at the station.As you reach, ride the rides at the entrance, before heading to the water park. The rides are not that extreme but there is a minimum height requirement for all rides. There are also rides for those below the minimum height requirement. At the water park, you could get a locker and rent towels too, in which you need to pay a deposit as well. Very convenient as you can open the locker as many times as you want. The rides at the water park were great, but some rides you have to have 4 people or 2 people. The beach is great too, as the current is not strong, compared to the beach at Nha Trang.The safari is pretty good as well, but those are the animals you could see in any zoos in Asia. I was looking forward to the dolphin show but apparently it has been under maintenance since September. When I asked the staff, they said the dolphins are sick.I suggest to also wear comfy shoes as your feet will hurt after the whole day. Walked about 30,000 steps that day, I think.To also leave to the cable car either by 430pm or 7pm, to avoid human traffic.
A well thought of amusement park set out in an island which is to be accessed through a cable car being the longest over the sea in the world.Had a fun filled day but staff need to be geared up.

It was a pretty fun theme park actually with quite a lot to do. View was amazing, rides are so so...hard to compare to other theme parks in other countries. It would probably be better once all construction are done but staff really needs training esp. Many can't explain what they offer or answer foreign tourists question

Arriving in Na Trang on the cruise ship Westerdam. We noticed the cable car stretching across the harbour. To ride on the cable car involves admission to the fun park. We rode on the alpine luge, roller chair, Ferris Wheel and roller coaster. There was a water park and bird sanctuaries I have not been to an amusement park for decades, we had fun.Well worth the visit
Staff are nice.The decoration is beautiful especially the light.Building is beautiful.Things need improve:Fast food (The skin of fried chicken too thick.)Fun park closed too early.

If you enjoy getting wet, this is the place for you.What is remarkable about holidaying in Vietnam is that the prices are constant, no surcharging, no rip offs, everything is excellent value for the money.

Visited Vinpearl Land on 29th October 2019. It is quite a large park with lots of different activities. It is currently off peak season so it wasn’t to busy which is a plus, but also meant not all rides were being operated. It doesn’t have the same amount of thrill rides that you would see on the Gold Coast theme parks. But it is much bigger and includes a large water park. There’s a large amount of construction going on at the bottom of the hill so it will be interesting to see how that looks when it’s complete. All in all, it was a great day out and if I come back to Nha Trang again, it will being on my list of things to do.

The place was located on a separate island which was nice. We spent here from the morning until around 7pm. There were a lot of people came here in the weekend. Many games to play and good for kids. Price was a bit expensive for locals.

There are a few amusement rides geared towards being kids friendly. Shows were okay but had some cancelled maybe due to rain (dolphin & seal show). I would recommend going early as you will see more and the #4 bus going and return ends around 6. The cable car ride is quite nice, not as bumpy as the ride in ba na hills. Overall an enjoyable day outing at the Vinpearl land.

This is quiet a fun waterpark. There are a lot of rides. On the day that we visited there were no queues at all. Be aware that some rides require four riders and there are height requirements for most of the major rides, which means that kids can't go on. The staff is pleasant and friendly but very few spoke English well. Refreshements in the park are expensive and there are no good dining options. There is a free shuttle bus from the south of the island so no need to get an expensive taxi.The kids had a ball!PS avoid the cringy "5D" movie - totally not worth it and the cinema is a bit gross.

Stay at Vinpearl Nha Trang Resort plus unlimited entrance to the park. Park was nice but weather was so hot. No lift for stroller. There are many non smoking sign but smokers are everywhere too. Food and drink is not so expensive. Lotteria is the best here. Nice bubble house for little kids. Too long que for alpine coaster. Dolphin show was close. More review please visit FB : 2littlebosses :
This place is a good place to spend the day with family. Many types of Water rides with pools. The whole Island is for Family amusement. A great place to cool down from the heat.

For full day of adventure go to vinpearl island, not cheap but definitely a must while in Nha Trang area, no long lines, everything is working like swiss watch, cheap food and beverage, great waterworld area with amazing slides and water sports, kids loved it, both my teenagers and my 9 years, so it's a mix for all ages

Not a bad day out the cable car wait in the morning was about an hour and the alpine roller coaster was about the same but worth it best ride in parkWater slides easy access and wave pool greatFood bit ordinary but you don’t have a choiceThe vouchers they give you are hard to use so many restrictions on where and how much u spend we didn’t use ours poor add onOverall teens enjoyed rides and was pleasant day just be prepared to wait in line in the heat

It's hard to find any information on this park online in English, as their own official website is in Vietnamese only. Try to purchase your tickets before you go, as this will save you at least 1 queue. You can catch a Grab taxi to the Cable Car station from city centre for about 90k VND.We arrived at the Cable Car around 8.20am, and only had to wait a couple of minutes to get on, but we've heard it can get really busy. Before we got on we received a 100k VND voucher each, as we understood was as a compensation for some ride closures. More on this later. Please note that you're not allowed to bring any food or drinks (not even water) into the park.When we arrived at the island we straight went to the Alpine Coaster, where there was already a 40 minute wait, but after us quickly became a 2 hour wait. This is a great ride, with some fantastic views, so worth the wait. Did a few more rides at Titan Peak that were fun, and waiting lines were ok.As it was quite a hot day, we then decided to go to the Bubbleland Waterpark. You can rent a locker here 20k VND (+100k VND bond) and towels 30k VND (+150k VND bond).There are some really great slides here, from super fast and scary to very family friendly. Where this park could improve is to have some more staff available to control the use of tubes and rafts. A lot of people will grab some tubes, and claim these for the day, creating a shortage of tubes, which is completely unnecessary.A special mention for the lifeguards that work here, as they do earn their money here, as a lot of visitors that can't swim visit this park, and will drown at the bottom of the slides in 1 meter of water if they wouldn't be there, this makes a very interesting show, if you want to have a little break.So around 5.30pm we started to have a look around for some dinner, and use these vouchers, only to find that all the places where you could use these vouchers had already run out of the popular food choices or were already closing?!? So with hardly any food and a 90 minute queue to get back on the cable car you feel a bit stuck. In the end we managed to get 2 some sort of burger combos at the Lotteria Restaurant in King's Garden, before they also closed their kitchen. This restaurant does have some great views!! Not sure how you are supposed to stay in this park until 9pm if they haven't got any food?We got back at the Cable Car around 7.15pm, and had to wait approx 45 minutes to get back on. Lot of taxis waiting back at the mainland, or order a Grab to get back to your hotel.Overall we had a great day, but feel that the park could be even better. Maybe send some staff to one of the Disney parks to see how you can streamline stuff a bit better?
Whole day in this huge amusement park, nice and fun... But weather are too Hot, please bring sunblock and umbrella!!!

The place is huge with a lot to see. These rides seem faster than ones in North America. The lines can get long and exiting the island can be hard during closing. They started offering free boat rides back because of the long line on the tram.
It is a good theme park for everyone but our visit was spoiled by unruly visitors who think they own the place. We cut short our visit due to the unbearably hot weather.

저는 디스커버리1 빌라에서 숙박을 했지만 외부인들도 이용할수 있다는게 좋은것 같아요. 다양한 놀이기구와, 먹을거리, 놀거리가 많아서 좋았고, 낮에는 오락실(어드벤처 월드)을 들르거나, 4D 상영관에 가서 놀았고, 밤에는 야경을 볼수 있는 Evolution ride, 자이로드롭, 롤러코스터를 탑승했습니다! 야경이 너무 좋았고, 직원 분들이 웃으며 맞이 해주셨습니다!I stayed at Discovery 1 Villa, but outsiders can use it too.I liked it because there were many rides, food, and fun things. During the day, I rode swing carousel and went to Advantage World or 4D theater. At night, I rode the Evolution ride. I rode a nice night view, a roller coaster, and the staff greeted me with a smile. It was so good.

Decided to book tickets as we had a 6 year old who was getting fed up of temples and waterfalls.  Instead of heading there for it opening we decided to hang back and hopefully beat the crowds we had read about, it worked.  We pulled up in a taxi just before 12 and we straight into a gondola without any waiting...!!!On arriving you land in the ‘rollercoaster’ part, first one we queued up for we couldn’t get on as Oscar is just under 130cm and there was NO budging on any of this.  Please keep this in mind if you are thinking you may be able to sneak them on, they measure.  Same for be water park too, a lot he was too small for but we still had a couple of hours in the pools and smaller slides (much to his disgust).Food and drinks seemed reasonably priced.There is a parade as the sunsets which was ok but it wasn’t Disney.After a few for goes on the rides we decided to head back around 7pm - big queue for the return gondolas but it does keep moving, just be patient.One thing we didn’t get to go on was the Alpine Adventure, we checked a few times throughout the day and the queue never got below a 2 hour wait and as we’d already done this in Wales it wasn’t a massive disappointment.We payed around £85 for the 3 of us and it was worth it, just would have been better if the boy had the extra cm for the ‘big boy’ rides.Def worth a visit if you’re in Nha Trang.
Generally this place worth a visit. You could just spend a day here. Win alot of tourist hence it is super crowded.

Bought the tickets from klook.com, have to get the physical ticket from the cable car terminal. The place is crowded with China tourists. The lines to the cable cars (both ways) were terrible, long and extremely hot. There is no proper or strict queuing system. The China tourists are always cutting queue when they saw their friends in front of the line, especially in the return way. The rides are fun especially the Alpine coaster. Try to go in the evening when the sun is setting, still long queue but the heat is more bearable than in the afternoon, also you get to see the Vinpearl Land lights light up too when it's about your turn after queuing for about 1hr. Dolphin show was good and entertaining. 4D cinema is more for kids, nothing impressive for adults. There are varieties of flowers and animals at the Blooming Hill. You can escape from the hot sun at the flowers dome. The Underwater World is good but the aquarium glasses are dirty and you can't see the fishes clearly. The cable car back to city is so crowded as everyone is trying to catch the cable car before it closes at 9pm. The park management tried to arrange speedboat for visitors by announcing via the broadcasting system, but couldn't be heard clearly where is the exact location to take the ride, so we decided to go to the cable car terminal and ask around. Unfortunately the staffs near the terminal can't understand or speak English and just asking us to queue up. As mentioned earlier, there is no proper queuing system, people trying to cut queue every single chance they have. Hope the management will look into this seriously.
Nice about 15 minute way to cebalcar  and good management inside have a too much fun place and beautiful views

We were there from 1pm-8pm and didn’t have enough time to do everything. The water park and slides were brilliant and any of the amusements in the theme parks that we did were great too. The only down falls were the locker rooms being too packed, and the shower rooms being dirty. Unfortunately for us the island had a power cut while we were at the very top of the Ferris wheel - not a nice experience sitting in the pitch dark for over 5 mins. If I was going again I’d book a VIP or priority pass for the return cable car, we queued for about 25 mins to go back. The way to VPL was perfect and they kept our large cases for us.

After getting off to such a bad start our day turned out to be so amazing!! We booked our tickets on Klook and we went to redeem our tickets at the ticket booth (where we were told to go by the staff), we queued for 30 mins, then when we got to the front the lady told us that we couldn’t get the ticket there and to go to the boat station to get the speedboat across to the park! We then went to the boat pier and we were advised we needed a ticket to get on the boat and our Klook voucher wasn’t enough so we advised we had queued up and they told us to go there! They then sent us back to go and queue back up, which I refused and they said we couldn’t board the speedboat without a ticket and we couldn’t get a ticket at the booth so we didn’t know where to go from here. We  ended up going to a different pier and paying to go over to the island on a different boat as we just wanted to get to the park! We eventually got to the park entrance on the island and the lady at the ticket booth was lovely - we showed her our Klook voucher and she printed our tickets and we went straight into the park. After that the park was absolutely amazing and I would 100% recommend it to anyone who is coming to Nha Trang!! There is so much to do inside the park and everything is included in the admission fee which is good - apart from food and drink of course. Such a fantastic day just don’t book tickets through Klook as there is no benefit as you still have to queue at the ticket booth to redeem them!
but it was great place to visit with family and friend. highly recommend to stay in vinpearl resort.

We took the cable car to Vin Pearl the cues are long but moved quite quickly. The island itself has something for everyone plenty of rides places to eat shops kiddies area, water parks & relaxing on the beachThe cues to leave are also very long but well organised & quite fast moving.Great for families as there is something for everyone

Getting there by cable car is exhausting, the line is around 1-2 hours waiting line in the morning and around 1 hour when coming back.Very nice botanical garden, okay aquarium but not a lot of animal in the zoo.Water park also look very nice. (I preferred to stay dry so I just walked pass them.)The dolphin show is not so good compared to other place.The rides is look quite new and in a good condition. The line in each ride is not too long to wait.Now (2019), some areas are still under construction. So, not all the things in the map is available.You may need at least half a day for this place as it is very big.

The place is so big. I suggest you spend 2 days in Vinpearl so you would see all attractions. I was not able to swim there, though, as I needed to go back to the city early. The show with the dolphins and seals was awesome! I will come back here soon!

Our day at vinpearl land started off with crowds and plenty of heat and waiting around in line. The cable car being one of the longest in the world offered lovely views. There was a lot of construction going on when we arrived which was not great to view. Unsure how long they have to completion. This was apparently a quieter time and arriving early in the day was preferable to avoid more people. Some rides and attractions were not operating during the day so we missed out on live shows due to this. A lot of the rides were very tame but still many were vomiting everywhere before lunchtime. This held up things on a few rides and discouraged others contemplating the attraction. If you don’t want snack and fried food and prefer something healthier like me then bring something with you. The beach was pretty and very populated with families. Water rides were slow paced and leisurely but busy. Tip -pay extra to cut the line for the downhill luge and walk straight on. This was in all a fairly good experience but don’t expect thrilling rides galore. Drink plenty of water and wear sun protection and a cap.

in overall, the trip was not very special, perhaps, because i went to Ba na hills in Da Nang before so i am not very impressed in the cable car in Nha Trang ( short, small...)and when we go to the island, i can say they have a successful investment to there but it is too too far from this area to anothes.it is very hot as well.

Family with teens loved it for 10 hours.   Though not as grand or  thrilling as American parks,  we loved the variety of rides, slides, beach, gondala, boat, and arcade ( we intentionally skipped the zoo, aquarium, and shows)Tip 1:  no need to buy tickets in advance ( especially not from taxi drivers who push you to) because everybody else apparently does.  Swamped entrance at opening time but nobody really in ticket line.Tip 2:  if you want to ride their signature alpine slide, get there at or before 8:30 opening to get on first speedboat.  Skip the huge line at cable car and ride it back later.  Run! Directly to the alpine slide.  Only 4 riders get on this thing each minute so even if you are the hundredth person there it is a 25 minute wait that quickly grows to 2 hours.  Whole family wished we hadn't waited the hour that we did for the ho-hum ride.Tip 3 Water park is so fun in morning before crowds descend, but later in day is a bit aggravating.  And the rides are empty midday, but bearable cool if you are still sporting your wet suitTip 4 ( for Vinpearl)  Demand the same quality in restaurant as in park. The Lotteria was covered in litter swirling in wind with ketchup smeared tables and overflowing trash cans. And as other reviews say, out of many items by noon.
Was a good day out though a bit expensive,the cable car ride out to the island is spectacular and we'll worth doing.
Nice if you have kids I'm sure they would love it. My partner and I loved the gardens & the cable car. The zoo section was nice also. Would recommend going.

Really good place for one time visit in nha trang. The price is a bit expensive as the park doesn't have that much worth rides to try. We have reached there around 8.30 in the morning, rush start even at that time. The cable car is really good and quite long where you get a awesome view of nha trang city and the sea. I suggestion to start with alpine coaster otherwise you may stuck in a long q, it steal about 1 hour. You have option for fast track but again its expensive. Water rides are really good compare to the dry ones. There are different shows but only in specific time, check with the show times else you might miss ! Unfortunately we miss the dolphins show its around 2 pm. The food was ok noy that really bad.no idea why dont they allow food and water from outside as they are also using plastic only.what i really like the beach, it is stunning.

Reached by a fantastic cable car ride over the bay, Vinpearl has something for everyone. We went for the beautiful gardens and biospheres but managed to sneak in a ride on the big wheel too. There are lots of rides for families too. When we went to get food at a kiosk the attendant ushered us into the shade and somehow made us understand she would bring our food to us as soon as it was cooked. Typical of the friendly , helpful people we encounted throughout our Vietnamese adventure.We were on a cruise stopover so concerned about time but could easily have spent more than the six hours we had. If you are visiting on a cruise I advise a taxi to the cable car station as you will be pesterd all the way there otherwise and it is worth saving the time.

Nice afternoon and very funny. Especially roller coaster and water park. Changing location a bit messy to put on swimwear but still ok. Keep enough money to buy water and come before 9h30 at the cable car if you dont want to queue 2h...

There was a huge lineup waiting for the skyrail which would have taken 1 to 2hrs so we took the boat over, no line up and fast then take the skyrail back.. once there it's huge  lots of walking so be prepared, the waterpark was fun spent most of the time there because it was extremely hot.. there's a zoo and really beautiful gardens display. Need a few days to see it all properly but well worth the visit.

Overall is good in term of:Environment and staff-The place is clean and well organised (but too hot), not much place for rest-The toilet (or called WC), have a lot, no worry -All the staff that I met is goodFood and beverage-Not allowed to bring any food and drink inside (have stall sell food and beverage inside)BUT: I really disappointed that one of the restaurant (selling fast food), a lot of food already sold out, only left two choices (if not mistake), instead menu have more than 10 choices.Price-My self think it’s worth the price (as inside have a lot of thing to play, such as theme park, water park and wildlife park)

We are a young couple who decided to go to VinPearl for the day after reading lots of reviews, and had a really fun time. We arrived at the cable car at 1pm on a Monday afternoon and there was no line at all, lovely ride across the water. The park wasn't very busy which was great - we didn't have to wait for more than 5 minutes for any of the rides we went on.As everyone says, the water park is the best, and has lots of different slide options. We also enjoyed the dolphin show and the Mine Adventure roller coaster. We also took the ferris wheel at sunset which was beautiful. We did not eat at any of the restaurants since we had read such bad reviews about the food, but we did find a lot of cheap beers at different vending machines and snack stalls - ranging from 15000 to 50000 VND depending on what you got. Very happy about that!We left the park at 7pm and there was a big line for the cable car, but they moved through the line very fast so we were probably only waiting for about 15 mins.It was a fun day out so I would definitely recommend!

It’s a must go if you travel to Nha Trang. The cable car is something I can’t see in anywhere else. They said it’s the longest all over the world. Price is reasonable. Some popular facility may take 30min wait.

Look, it’s not Disney land but it’s great value for money. I’ve read a lot of negative reviews and to be honest they’re harsh. If you have a young family it’s not bad at all. The queues are small and the rides on a whole are family friendly. Big recommendation is to go to the water park first off as it is the one attraction that does get busy.   Staff are great and very pleasant. Language is a bit of an issue but they try their best.  The light show was good too.

We went to Vinpearl to have a wonderful time with family & friends. We went early & there are a lot of people already. I was impressed in how they move people from the city to the island. It was fast. You have 2 choices when going to Vinpearl; either by cable car or by speedboat. The speedboat is faster where it will take you just 7-8 minutes. Bringing in food is not allowed unfortunately. Options is not that much if you want to eat decent meal. We went to Lotteria for lunch & it was toxic. We even encountered bad mannered & selfish tourist as they snatched our seats. Anyway,  there are a lot of features inside the theme park. Dolphin & Sea Lion Shows, Water Fountain Show and more. Above the park there’s a zoo that features animals that are very beautiful that came from Africa that you can only see in the television. The kids enjoyed seeing the animals in real life. One thing you must try is the big Ferris Wheel where you can view the while theme park above. Culminating your stay will be spending some time in the water park. Lots if options but I tell you, you cannot go to all the rides just the whole day.

The reviews seem rather mixed but this is a pretty incredible place.  For a very reasonable entry fee you get: the world's longest over water cable car ride (especially impressive at night), theme park rides, aquarium, zoo (complete with an impressive collection of animals), waterpark, dolphin show, huge ferris wheel, floating water park and much more.A lot of work has gone into making this park look beautiful and it really shows.  The castles are very pretty and I like the way it is set into a hill and has different levels that you can access by escalator.It could benefit from a few improved thrill rides, most are pretty tame, whoever there is a decent selection and the kids loved everything they went on.I have little doubt that based on the levels of construction here, in a few years time this will be a truly world class theme park.My only real complaint is with the food.  It is absolutely appalling.  There are countless reviews pointing this out and it doesn't seem like park management is doing much to address this.  We visited Lotteria on two seperate days.  The food quality was average but the service was appalling both times.  They have a confusing system for collecting your food (everyone seems to be "number 1").  There is a huge scrum of people pushing forward at the collection point with no real system for figuring out when your food is ready.  My order was missing items and my friends order was completely missed and they had to wait 20 minutes before re-ordering.So much work needed to get better quality food outlets that can function quickly and efficiently.Overall I would still highly recommend this to anyone travelling to the area.  Whether you have kids or not, it is well worth the price of admission.Tip: stay late as the place looks amazing at night.  The fountain show is spectacular and the ride back across the ocean is breathtaking.

Hey there, spent the day at Vin Pearl land with the wife & figured I’d update the information on here as I found it a little difficult to understand what is out there. Getting there & cost. Ticket sales start @ 8am & the park opens & 8.30am. *height restriction of 1.3m on most arcade rides*Would suggest getting in early as the crowd starts about 9am when the tourists buses start arriving. You can either book through here at$125aud p/p or do as we did & catch a cab to the cable car point for about ($6/8aud) & buy your tickets there for $880000 vnd ($55aud) p/p. Just tell any of the local cab drivers & they will get you there. Don’t forget to Allow for the return cab fair also. Your cable car fee allows for a there & back cable car ride, no drinks allowed on the trip there, but we were not checked on the return trip. I’ll let you make your own assumptions on that one. 🤗Also allows for all activity’s at the Park, with the exception of the powered water sports on the beach. Would suggest that you make the 4D cinema a must. A little childish but the effects are incredible & the aquarium is pretty cool too. We were a bit disappointed that there wasn’t a std swimming pool to just chill in, but that is only a minor thing really. We found the food & beverages to be a little more costly than on the mainland but that is to be expected. Water is $15000vndSoft drinks are $25000vndBeer is from $40000vndWould recommend the dolphin restaurant located at the far end of the water slide area, this was clean & not over crowded or noisy. It allowed a view of the beach & the staff & food there was amazing. Can recommend the burgers & pizzas if you’ve had your fill of noodles 🤪. I hope this helps you out with any questions you might have had & have a great day out. We did.

We arnt really theme park people but the kids wanted to try it out because it's all you can see from Nah Trang. We took a grab to get to cable car line. Cable car was about a 20 min wait. Be warned. They searched your bags and take water off you. I am really disappointed in this and the park should rethink this policy. It's not a good start to the day. Any way cable car is fun and a great view. Park is huge so be prepared to walk heaps. Fun rides throughout, water Park was great, beach was ok but was full of little jellyfish which did sting while doing the floating course. All shows get packed so arrive early. You can hire a locker for 20k for the day which was great. Food options were average if looking for a quick bite. There's a convience store, burgers, german and local food etc and lots a little drink and ice cream stalls so you can get through the day. Trying to get dinner was hard. Some places were closed and others had run out of food so we brought hot chips to get by and dinner when we got back to our hotel. We left about 8pm for cable car and the lines were terrible so be prepared. See last pic. We did the toboggan ride at night and it was great. Cost was 4.2 million dong for the day for a family of five.

Our family of five had an amazing day here. Kids aged 9,12,13 enjoyed everything, despite the heat. Tip - keep your shoes with you at the water park, as the concrete is extremely hot to walk on between rides. It's safe to leave shoes at the bottom of rides because there are next to no other people there. We ate at the burger place that starts with L, apparently it's a chain restaurant. It was bad. Other than that, a Hoi An must-do for families.

We visited this place in March 2019.  We were on a cruise and stopped at the Nha Trang port ,which is maybe a mile down the road. The cruise line charged more than double the cost, than doing this all on your own for about $40 US.  From the port we walked towards to tram which was about a 15-20 minute walk.  Once at the team we paid our fare and then rode across the bay to Vin Pearl.  What shocked me was the amount of garage in the ocean.  That really upset me.  However, the resort itself was beautiful.  The Tram ride took about 12 min or so to cross.  Once on the property, all attractions were included in the tram fee!  There was a zoo with many animals which did have somewhat small enclosures as mentioned here, however it appears the animals are well cared for and appeared to be healthy.  There was also a botanical gardens, water park, amusement rides including roller coasters, aquarium, and a beautiful beach with floatable course, and kayak and other rentals.  There are many restaurants also.  My kids were teens and really enjoyed this place.   On our return to our ship, instead of walking we hired a taxi for $3 US (for all 4 of us).

We had a fantastic time here! Loads of activities to chose from! My only 2 suggestion would be: - to take better care of the animals under your supervision. We were at the aquarium with a open section with live turtles, with an obvious sign not to touch them, yet people still picked them up for photos!! Pls have some staff to supervise the safety of your live animals! They are under your care & should be treated as such. - Yummyland is very under staffed with a total of 5 people serving close to 200 - 300 guests. Take better care of your team & hired enough staff so they’re not overworked. I felt extremely sorry for them.

You can’t come to Nha Trang and not go to Vin Pearl. Costing 880,000 per adult, including the cable car ride - great day out.Lack of food options is the biggest downfall. More on that later. Closes at 9pm if you have the stamina to stay all day.Queues build early - so arrive as early as you can. Alpine roller coaster was unique and fun. Great views. You can control your speed on the way down. Put that first on your list. Go there on arrival - as queues can be very long. Dolphin show fantastic also. Arrive 10 mins before. Has a great variety of things to do.Kids rides, more thrilling rides, animals, flower garden, bird aviary, wave pool, water slides, beach play with inflatables.  Lockers for valuables, cheap, easy to use. Main downside :Food - really bad. Lotteria was the only place to buy food... crap food, but better than nothing. But ran out of chicken burgers by 12.30And nothing left but Pepsi to drink there.No other proper food options - other than Buffet lunch (which I can’t comment on cos we didn’t try it) HEAPS of ‘snack shops’ around the place - with water and popcorn, ice cream. Pretty cheap - but when you need proper food- sweet popcorn doesn’t quite cut it. Not allowed to bring your own food or drink.My tip : sneak in some muesli bars or small snacks wrapped up your towels if u have kids.Food issues aside - we had a great day out, lots of things packed into one place. Definitely recommend it. Side note : we used Grab (Uber) to get to cable cars. Don’t bother with formal transfers. Easy and cheap

I was dubious.  I arrived early and spent the whole day . I was surprised. It was great. Ths highlight for .e was the botanical gardens . Great respite from the heat and very beautiful.Recommend for the whole family

Arrived here at about 1pm. Started off with the waterpark. (Do the waterpark first, as it closes earlier). Ticket was 880,000 vnd per person. (This included the cable car over and back to the island). Myself and my friends thought this was a bit expensive. waterpark was quite good and probably worth the money. However themepark was not great. Not many rides and mostly for small children. The place is big, so between the themepark and the waterpark we didn't have any time to see any shows. There is a very nice beach you can relax on too. Not many food options available. Overall, I would recommend if it was in your price range or if you had kids.
clean environment and no busy. huge amusement park but need more attraction. staff also need to be trained more with a good customer service skill.

We visited with a group of family the place is a Vietnamese version of Disneyland along with a water park.The best part is getting to the park as for kids it's exciting to be on a cable car or via speedboat.It is pretty big and recommend to break it down over a couple of days to visit. Rides were good. Water park was clean the place has many restaurants although only drawback is that the place is not baby friendly as there are no parent room/change room for babies this was a surprise seeing how much the Vietnamese people love babies. Overall a great day out.

This is a great amusement park, we didn't use the huge water park but it looked like you could spend all day there and it is fantastic. The amusement park doesn't have a lot of rides for adults but lots for younger kids. Also we enjoyed the several flower enclosures and the views from the top of the park. The cable care ride across is fantastic, with views back to Nha Trang, it would be amazing at night. Excellent value as you get the cable car ride over and back, and every ride and attraction is free when there, so for a family it would be very good value. You could spend a couple days there. It is a must see if you are going to Nha Trang.

It is not many point playing game but good qualitative point. Their water park make me disapointed because of having same game with Dam Sen water park in HCM city. However, it is better than Ba Na Hills. It is worth!!!

Considering there isn’t too much to do in Nha trang this was perfect for killing a day. We arrived early to avoid crowds. Make sure that you purchase tickets beforehand including a return cable car ticket. Although there was already a line it moved quite quickly and they were really efficient at getting everyone onto the cable cars. The rides were alright although a few were shut for maintenance. The gardens were beautiful and the animals would be a great attraction for young children. The water park was also great, and the lockers were super cheap and easy to acquire. Great view of nha trang too! The only annoying thing we found was the line for lunch!! We went to Lotteria around 3 and waited at least 50 minutes for average fast food of which they had sold out of most options. All in all it was a good day out for the price.

We went there by GrabBike in the morning, it was a 20 minutes ride from out hostel. Bought ticket in advance from Klook App with discount code so its cheaper than retail price. Luckily it wasn't that crowded in the morning to queue for cable car ride. We arrived around 830am and the theme park was quite empty , the staff still operate the facility for us to ride even though it's just me and my friend. The merry-go-round was the longest queue, we queued for 1hours+ for it. Other than that all were not that packed. Water park however was quite packed. Be prepared for China tourists to cut your queue. By that time water park was closing , shower room and locker is super crowded , so we decided to chill at the beach until the crowd was lesser. We leave around 730pm and super long queue for the return cable car ride but the queue kept moving so it took around 40mins for us to get inside the cable car. Not much food varieties , packed with people and hard to get a seat. Overall all the games were not that extreme, more kid-friendly , so far there's only game that impressed me.

Были семьёй в феврале 2026 года. Ребёнок остался в восторге от всего парка. В целом, очень много локаций - и аттракционы, и зоопарк, и аквапарк. Такого зоопарка разнообразием птиц вы не встретите нигде в мире. В том числе порадовал шоу птиц также, когда были в зоопарке смогли очень близко пообщаться с павлинами. Сам парк аттракционов не очень большой, но все аттракционы интересные. Отдельно хочу рассказать историю которая произошла со мной на американской горках - пока мы катались, из моего кармана выпал iPhone. Сотрудники парка сбились с ног, но всё-таки нашли его на вторые сутки. Ngan из службы поддержки сообщила нам о нахолке на следующее утро. Ребята действительно искали мой телефон весь предыдущий день, хотя я смирился с его потерей. Спасибо вам огромное! 😍 В общем, нам очень понравилось в парке. Его точно не обойти за один день - очень много локаций и зоопарк, и парк аттракционов, и дендрарий, и океанариум, и аквапарк. Искренне советую закладывать сразу несколько дней на пребывание в этом замечательном месте!---Gia đình tôi đã đến thăm vào tháng 2 năm 2026. Con tôi vô cùng yêu thích toàn bộ công viên. Nhìn chung, có rất nhiều khu vực khác nhau – các trò chơi, sở thú và công viên nước. Bạn sẽ không tìm thấy một sở thú chim đa dạng như vậy ở bất cứ nơi nào khác trên thế giới. Tôi đặc biệt ấn tượng với chương trình biểu diễn chim, và khi ở sở thú, chúng tôi đã có cơ hội được đến gần và tương tác trực tiếp với những con công. Công viên giải trí không lớn lắm, nhưng tất cả các trò chơi đều rất hấp dẫn. Tôi cũng muốn chia sẻ một câu chuyện về điều đã xảy ra với tôi trên tàu lượn siêu tốc: khi đang đi, chiếc iPhone của tôi bị rơi ra khỏi túi. Nhân viên công viên hoàn toàn bối rối, nhưng cuối cùng họ đã tìm thấy nó vào ngày hôm sau. Ngan từ bộ phận chăm sóc khách hàng đã thông báo cho chúng tôi về việc tìm thấy vào sáng hôm sau. Các chàng trai đã dành cả ngày hôm trước để tìm kiếm điện thoại của tôi, mặc dù tôi đã chấp nhận việc mất nó. Cảm ơn các bạn rất nhiều! 😍 Nhìn chung, chúng tôi thực sự rất thích công viên này. Chắc chắn là không thể tham quan hết mọi thứ trong một ngày – có rất nhiều địa điểm để ghé thăm, bao gồm sở thú, công viên giải trí, vườn thực vật, thủy cung và công viên nước. Tôi thực sự khuyên bạn nên lên kế hoạch vài ngày để khám phá nơi tuyệt vời này!
