I also spoke Vietnamese to the staff in the hopes it would generate a positive response but to no fail. Also bought flip flops thay turned out to be too small, they wouldn't exchange or refund which is fair enough, but could have been a saving grace in me not writing this poor review.
Got food poisoning at the buffet, absolutely awful. Other than a reallyfun theme park. The water ride the space hole is very painful would avoid it

hair found in the burger, the environment is open and windy for the food served to be blown away. staff are not approachable and it gives this attraction a bad reputation. this should be reviewed as I would not recommend anyone to eat the food at this venue

Main attraction in Vinpearl Amusement Park is NOT AVAILABLE for everybody. After waiting for 30 min we found out that there will be a lottery to choose 10 lucky persons! No information about this stupid rule before!

The overall experience at Vinpearl Land is ok, but the voucher that you are given is useless and they do not allow you to bring water or snacks. You can only use the voucher at certain shops and it’s when you spend 200,000 dong or more on certain items. I tried using it multiple times and couldn’t. They were always out of popcorn and chips and this couldn’t be used for cotton candy. I ended up letting my kids play with them and threw four of them away as I couldn’t use them. (I was at the counter with other patrons who were equally frustrated with the voucher)The overall food options were extremely limited and only junk food. As I mentioned in the aforementioned paragraph, they were out of most items at the Happy Snack, Yummy Land, and other supposedly quick service shops form when we arrived at 10:40am until when we left just after 6pm. The Lotteria restaurant was where we ate, but we couldn’t use the voucher and it was fried chicken or a hamburger. Given the abundance of fruit across the water in Nha Trang, there were no fruit or vegetable options available.Sadly, the dolphin show was cancelled due to renovations and the line for the Alpine coaster averaged 2 1/2 hours.Could have been an amazing experience but hungry kids with limited food options = not a great time!BTW - bring lots of cash as credit cards are rarely accepted.

All watter atractions are available for people more than 1.3 meters. No one told us. We bought tickets for 3.5mio, however yonger son has only 1.2 meters. Thus we were not allowed to go for all watter attractions. Restaurants not good. Staff spoke to us in russian language. Big queues everywhere. Not possible to take own watter to the island. The main goal is to make us to spent lots of money here. Better to stay in Nha Trang at the beach.

I came to Vinpearl for my 22nd birthday hoping to make some awesome memories. But it was so horrible we went home early and ended up wasting so much money on our ticket. The regular park had only a couple of rides and the workers were very slow and inefficient as they need to be train much better at how to be more efficient. The water park was the worst part yet! We witness adults and kids falling off their tubes going down a slide! One kid bit his lip and his parents were pissed but couldn’t communicate. The slides were simply not safe and didn’t have enough water. The park is so tiny to be charging people so much $!

I don’t know where to start to be honest. Very understaffed as rides take forever to finish and start again. Half of the rides were closed. The water park is very dangerous as the slides are the most ratchet things I’ve ever witnessed. One kid cut his lip and other people fell off several times. Other slides had not enough water and a 7 year old got stuck half way through. This all happened in 5 minutes of us sitting on a bench and observing. The animals are underfed and look miserable. Half of the park is under construction. And it was expensive. Wouldn’t do it again.

Our first dissapointment started when we got to the Gondola. 1.75 MILLION dong just to get there. They also had no dolphin/seal shows (not that I would have watched as I hate that stuff, it's cruel) so we were given 2x 100k dong vouchers as compensation. Boy oh boy was compensation the wrong word. We went to buy 'lunch' at the park and was told we could only use 1 of the vouchers, and we had to spend over 200k dong JUST to use one, as if the ticket price wasn't expensive enough. The food was like we were eating lumps of concrete, infact concrete would have been more satisfying, we fed the food to the wild cats by the eating area instead. I was literally waiting for my stomach to start churning which it did after a bit. The big light up ferris wheel we had been eying up from Tran Phu beach was not in operation (the part we were really looking forward to) and there were only a handful of other rides to choose from. We gave up on the actual park and decided to hit the water park. That was the best part. Not the slides though as only about 1/3 were actually in operation, the staff were also rude. We climbed to the top of some of the slide towers only to have the staff let people push in front of us, without explaining to us why. After a couple of hours of using the few select slides etc, we went back to the park to wait in line for the jungle car ride - was really overrated.Everything about this park screams tourist trap, money hungry management, underpaid and undertrained staff and not to mention, horrible overpriced food. And the animals such as Lions, Tigers, Rhino, Monkeys etc were kept in HORRIBLE conditions and they all looked so sad and distressed as their enclosures were SO small.Please do not use this park, it treats its animals horribly, it treats its customers horribly and I assume it treats its staff horribly.DO NOT USE VIN PEARL LAND. YOU WILL REGRET IT 100%.To prove my point I guarantee you if this review gets any reply, no compensation will be offered, not that anything they offer would compensate the day of our holiday lost.From 2 VERY DISSAPOINTED customers.

Me and my girlfriend was looking forward to going to Vinpearl in Nha Trang in September to spend quality time with each other travelling and exploring. However, we was disappointed by the experience with misleading advertising and marketing. We was both looking forward to going on the zip line together as it was one of the main attractions there. But was only informed once we was inside that there was a lottery and draw to get the opportunity to go on the zip line so we was not guaranteed to go on the zip line. To add to my frustration and anger, we had to wait in line for 20 mins to get the tickets for the zip line by the time we got to the front of the line there were no more tickets. This was all around 4-5pm.

Today I went to Vinpearl with my families. When we took the cable car back to the central, the staff assigned my bro to another cable car. I told him we were together but he replied me stupid. So in the end my bro forced to take a separate cable car. But my cable car still left one seat. I won’t visit this place AGAIN!

We were a group of four friends keen to go zip lining so we paid all the money happily and waited the booth. Then we were told we had to pick a card to see what time we could go Zip lining. Three of us selected 12 noon and the four person got a better luck next time card. There was no queue for this ride photo attached and the staff didn’t care at all we were told to come back in a hour to try again!!! VERY POOR CUSTOMER SERVICE.  There were no signs advising you had to try your luck to see when you got a ride. We wasted our money going to Vinpearl Land Would not recommend.

The trip to Vinpearl Land in Nha Trang was a very negative experience and one that I wish I never had!  The cable car ride over and back was pleasant enough and the view of the bay was beautiful. However, upon arriving, unless you are taking children to some kind of third rate "Disney" experience, save yourself some time and stay on the mainland or go to other islands.  You are essentially a prisoner in the Vinpearl resort, along with thousands of other people.  There is a beach with a very small restricted swimming area, so full of people it is worse than swimming in a small public pool.  You cannot go outside of this demarcated swimming area, notwithstanding the island is huge, unless you want to pay for water sports, also within Vinpearl Land and right next door to the swimming area.  The point is that you are NOT ALLOWED OUTSIDE OF THE PARK. There are hotels,where one might like to have a nice lunch, other beautiful beaches, paths to walk etc etc., but SORRY, you cannot leave Vinpearl Land JAIL. Horrible day!

We came with our 4 kids. There were only a few facilities which were intresting, and most of them had a very long line, so we gave up.For our young doughter there were even less things to do - some were not working and most of them were not suitable for her hight (she is 5 years old, and they reqwested 130cm hight). We went back after short time couse it was hot and boring.A wast of time and money !

We waited in que to buy a ticket to the zip. Come in front and we had to pic a card. I didnt get a ticket but mye girlfriend did. So i offered me to pay for one, but we could't. So that ment that i had to sis for a long time alone and let my girlfriend travel up there alone and experience it whitout med. So stupid! And right behind us there was a group of 3, and only one of them got a ticket. So he gave it to me. And when i showed them the ticket they just said no and could not explain them self. So bad english here! Just plain stupid. Just destroyed the whole trip here. Whriting this at the moment im waiting for my girl to zip down. 45min and still counting... What a waste of time!

We heard many people talked about this Vinpearl Land NhaTrang amusement park, so we decided to check it out. Ticket was around 40 USD or 880,000 VND which was not that bad. We got our tickets at the hotel hoping to avoid a long line to buy tickets when getting there. However, it was a huge disappointment when we were here. There was no where that we could find any signs to show us which lines to get on to either buying tickets or getting on the cable cars. The worst thing is that it was so crowded with a lot of Chinese tourists who didn’t speak English, so it did not help for us to ask questions. We couldn’t be able to even find any staffs to ask for directions. Sadly, I and my 4 family members just left without even trying to go to the island as we already imagined how bad it would be, to get in line and to fight with other people who have no sense of what getting in line meant. Sorry to say so but it was so true.I definitely don’t recommend  this place.

I am extremely frustrated at the way my friend and I were treated from the moment we stepped foot at the Vinpearl land. There are no sign posts anywhere when you first arrive to get your tickets which causes confusion and frustration for everyone. As we booked our tickets online to avoid queues (which I would not recommend because it seems like the staff have never seen this before), we asked a security guard where to go and he still told us to go to the ticket office. When we got there the man said no it’s fine we have our online tickets and to go ahead and queue for the cable car. When we arrived at the cable car queue there was hundreds of people pushing and shoving in a possibly 2 hour long queue. We left the queue and headed towards the speed boat. When we got to the top of the queue we were told no and to go back to the ticket office because our online ticket was not enough. We were sent back and forth until someone finally said that the tickets were ok. This happened again when we arrived at Vinpearl land. Our experience was completely spoiled by now your staff treated us. While in Vinpearl land people stared, pointed and took photos of us without our consent. The tourists at the park were extremely rude, even recording us when we were trying to have fun in the water park.I did not support the zoo, aquarium and dolphin shows and I am horrified in this day and age this park would keep animals captive in horrible conditions. I have read the replies to other review stating that they are cared for etc but how when they are stressed, made to live in tiny enclosures and forced to do shows everyday for the entertainment of humans? This is extremely disappointed and unnecessary. I will not be recommending this park as we walked away feeling disappointed, embarrassed and harassed from the people in the park.

Second Vinpearl land iv visited since being in Vietnam..  And I should have learned the first time...  When researching it seemed there was an option between the cable car or boat to get there...  Only to be told that the boats weren't running..  Lerfect for someone that hates heights but had promised to take my kids there.  Even linning up to get into the cable cars we were pushed around by Chinese..  Even grown adults pushing my 5 and 7 year olds out of the way. We end up being separated in the cars there. The line ups for the rides were ridiculous and once again grown adults pushing and shoving like there lives depended on it.  Rides targeted to either toddlers or adults..  My kids sit at 120cm and 115cms and couldn't really go on much...  3 rides... They were even denied jumping on the trampolines as that required them to be 130cms??  Don't know why it's safer to jump on a trampoline if your taller...  Alpine ride looked great.... Although we went waiting the 2hrs it had up on the gate...  Overall another waste of time and money. Super disappointed

This place isn’t friendly for Muslims and when I brought food with me they didn’t let me in until I don’t bring the food with me but I brought that food with me bc I’m vegan and there’s no vegan food inside the restaurant the animals was in a really bad environment there cages was so small

Went to Vinpearl for a fun day out. Unfortunately this place is completely not as expected. The botanical gardens are lovely and the Toboggan “alpine” ride was really fun. However, the zoo and aquarium was absolutely awful! I was shocked to see the small enclosures and depressed looking animals. I have attached pictures below to show you the full enclosures captured in one photograph. It took me no more than 10 minutes to walk around and take the images which I only stomached so that I could expose this place. I’m so disappointed, the white lion was alone, malnourished and miserable. There was animals crammed into the same enclosure too. Vinpearl has so much to offer but there is no need for a small zoo that takes little interest in the welfare of the animals that are caged there. Not to mention the food was awful at “yummy food” in the water park area. Cold chips, flat cola and no vegetarian options! Aquarium was too small too and the poor reptiles were cramped into tiny small enclosures!! Again, pictures are attached below- I could not believe that two bearded dragons were in the same small vivarium.  As an animal behaviour student and an owner of reptiles/amphibians I was disgusted at their lack of space. Many bird species were also given little flying room in a merged walk though area compared to their natural habitat size.

This park is not friendly for Muslim, they wouldn't let us enter with food even though we already explain many times to them we can't eat the food inside, they took our food just like that... They would rather let us starve to follow their rules... Even before entry, our mood already ruined.

Worst experience ever!!! The park was fine but the restaurant service ruined the whole trip. Lunch via tour booking at 1.00pm June 8, 2019; my 14-months twins were tired so we came ealier at 12.30pm. Staff wouldn’t let us in and told us to go outside rudely as they said we would mess up while they were cleaning the tables??? (our advance booked tables anyway);  After they let us sit at the corner we ordered a bowl of rice for our hungry babies but they refused saying the kitchen was still cooking WHILE WE OBSERVED they brought large bowl of rice to other tables. Can’t imagine how they treated little babies. The staff were very hostile even towards babies and it looked like they wanted to throw us away, not welcoming at all. I am disappointed as it happened at Vin land. I used to trust Vin service. Terible restaurant service, we won’t be back again. We strongly dont recommend for anyone with babies!!!

The reason I’m giving this a terrible instead of average is the state of the zoo. I’m disgusted by the small cages that consisted of a little umbrella for shade and not much else. Certainly no where for them to get a little privacy from the public who were extremely disrespectful, tapping on windows and yelling at them. It was just a joke that the owners obviously have a lot of land but throw these animals in tiny cages where they have no way to exercise or entertain themselves. The white lion in particular looked extremely malnourished, I actually thought he was dead at first (I've attached a pic to show how skinny he is). Other tiny cages had thrown in several species together and despite the information sheet stating that one type of deer lived near the river there was no water for them to bathe in. We had only come here for the water park but I’m disgusted I paid into somewhere that treats their animals with such little consideration of their needs when the owners of the park obviously have the means to give them a better life. Additionally, they only have two rollercoasters which are ok and we did enjoy the water park which was good. But we couldn’t use the beach/swim in the sea there because of jellyfish and the lifeguards were very strict about not going in the water.

For those who are going Vinpearl, the Alpine coaster was fun and some rides were mediocre. Just to be aware if you have to be careful with your own personal belongings as my friend gold bracelet was almost snatched by the lifeguard. It was the tornado water ride. The lifeguard was supposed to bring my friend out of the water but instead, he pushed her down and try to snatch her gold bracelet. We didn't fight with them because we knew they will be helping their own people. BE EXTRA CAREFUL WHEN YOU ARE THERE, the people they treat you very rudely as well
Online says you can take only water , they tuck ours off us but let the Chinese and Vietnamese keep there’s :(

The actual experience with the fairground rides, food, water park, beach etc were very good. The cable car over is fun to get, the rides are pretty fun, there’s a whole arcade where all the games are free which was pretty cool, cool shops scattered around, okay food, the obstacle course on the sea was fantastic, great views...Now you’re probably wondering why now I’ve given this place 1 star. This is really simply because of my upset with the zoo, aquarium and dolphin & seal show. First of all, in both the zoo and aquarium none of the animals have half enough space that they need. I would show a picture but I was too upset at looking at them! It was honestly just so sad to see. Zoos are bad anyway as animals need to be in the wild, did you know most if not all animals only live half their life expectancy or less by being kept in captivity? It’s just not right. Please only support sanctuaries that take in rescued animals who aren’t able to survive in the wild or who are being planned on being set free after getting back on their feet. The animals themselves just looked terribly sad. They deserve a better life. The person who opened up Vinpearl is supposedly a billionaire, maybe he should invest his riches into a MUCH bigger space for the animals. Better yet, not have any of the zoo or aquarium or show and fill it with more rides. Second of all, the dolphin and seal show. Please do not support this by going. These animals also go by the rule of living half or less of their life expectancy by being kept in captivity. They are also very mistreated because they should be hunting live fish like in the wild because that’s natural, except they are being fed dead fish. Moreover, they are controlled by the food they are given. How else do you think they learnt all the tricks? These animals are not pets, they should not be so vigorously trained to perform in front of us. They belong in the sea where they have so much area to swim freely and do what they want and feed on prey. They are forced to do what the trainers want them to do despite being full of food because the food is purely the thing controlling them. It’s almost like slavery.These sort of things need to stop and they need to stop NOW. Paying money for tickets or even just turning up means that you’re supporting mistreatment of animals that belong to be happy in the wild and that is something terrible to support. Please spread word so people are more aware of this. It seems many people are very uneducated on this matter due to their happy and laughing faces looking down on the miserable animals before them.

The worst treatment of animals I have ever seen. How can anybody write a good review for this place!? Not knowing what we were about to walk into we could not believe the extent of the types of animals they were keeping (Lions, Tigers, Rhinos, Hippos, Hyenas) and also in such horrific, tiny enclosures. This was the first and only part of the park we went to, feeling horrendous that we had even given these people our money. DONT GO HERE!

We booked tickets to go to Vinpearl as the reviews we read were all very good! When we got there we first went to the ‘Zoo’ and after seeing what living conditions the animals were in we turned back and left. It was horrible!!The space where the animals have to roam is extremely small. There is nowhere for the animals to move, they are all very thin and obviously malnourished! All animals looked very depressed as they paced up and down in the tiny space they had to live.Do not waste your time and give your money to a horrific place like this!

Definitely not worth a visit. The food is appalling, the rides are boring with long poorly managed queues and the animals are kept in very poor conditions. Was very sad when I left, one of my biggest regrets in Vietnam. Do not visit.

What a fabulous journey over on the marvellous cable car.But after that, it all went wrong.You queue and queue. Then people queue jump.I would have been arrested if I’d stayed any longer.Nice journey back on cable car though

I m nikmen from malaysia i visited vinpearl land nha trang on 6th dec 2018 at 11am would to highlight the some of the rude staff altitude towards chinese ppl n polite towards western ppl very racist. That's why I was not surprised was quite deserted when I was there. Franklin speaking if the Chinese ppl don't come to vinpearl land nha trang I m sure the business will go down cos in nha trang we can see they depend a lot on china customer. On 6th dec 2018 11am I arrived at the first entrance of the cable car the first staff was very polite n friendly to all customers until I reach the 2nd entrance where the staff collect our ticket that staff is very rude n i saw him scolding n shouting at the ederly chinese ppl in Vietnamese language n i feel irritated n scold him back ask him don't simply shout or scold at ppl n don't be rude n he scolded me back n show me his anger face n i scolded him back n walk away cos he can't understand english. I know some china ppl r clumsy but as a staff at the entrance u can talk politely n ppl will respond u back politely that's call customer service. This type of rude staff should ask him take care of the toilet. Frankly speaking vinpearl land is a nice n clean place to spend a day there. I have been traveling for so long n have been to so many amusement parks around the world I have never seen a parks so less customer. I have heard internet comment on rude staff altitude here until I see it than I believe. There is no information counter where customer lost information in park activities can ask for directions cos i can see some customers lost information don't know who to ask they speak to the staff they meet around the park n the staff can't communicate in english or chinese so there is a big language barrier problem. Like us we book tru klook apps n in the apps say the dolphins show is 3:30pm but we went to the dolphins show has already ended at 2:30pm n klook apps information is not accurate too n the brochure we get from the park didn't state the timing of the show so we go around ask any staff met n most can't speak english or chinese so atlast we miss all the show but we enjoy the zoo n underworld water n swimming at the sea n water slides. In sun world bana hills danang there is information counter at the park so we won't get  lost n we know what to do there. U can see bana hills get lots of  customers. I hope vinpearl land nha trang will watch out for some of their rude staff altitude n pls put up some information counter n hired staff that speak english n chinese who can communicate with illiterate customer who don't know how to check tru the timing of the show n what exciting place to visit there when ask at information counter? I believe with good altitude staff vinpearl land business will boost up. Even hoi an vinpearl land is so deserted.

Went here because of the rewievs. At first we had bad luck with the weather and almost ever rollercoaster and others close during rain. We stod in line 1,5 h for the big rollercoaster and despite rules of no smoking in line adults did it anyway. They even skiped the line several times, despite the staff saw this they did nothing. Quite annoying. We moved on to the garden and it was beautiful. After we went to see the birds. They did not have any space, so low cealing and many birds in small area. We felt bad and went outside to the aninals. This was even worse. Skinny lion, many animals in small areas. So so sad to see how bad the animals beeing treated. True human cruelty and abuse. DON’T SUPPORT THIS PARK

I would not recommend coming here. It is a waste of time and money. You cannot get from the park to the resort, there is no swimming pool unless you want to play in the wave pool. It cost 880,000 dong and certainly not worth the trip. If you are from Australia go to the Gold Coast and don’t waste your time here

Not expecting a Disney experience but this place certainly has seen better days. The entrance is the classic shop lined promenade that leads to a castle, of sorts. Contained in that cake is a bar. I recommend a drink at this stage. Dodgem cars are in a tired fashion, one was on its side being repaired while we drove about it, so much for safety. The aquarium is over stocked with marine life to the point you feel sad for the them.The water park, has a good collection of slides but the leaks in the front attraction make me question the structural integrity of the equipment.Stay clear of the raft ride with the purple pipe, my wife and I got stuck half way down in the bowl part of the ride. Thankfully one of the attendants rescued within 10 minutes.Half a day is more than enough to experience everything that the park has to offer.

To many people. No awailable locker (all day) to place our belongings... therefore no waterpark for us. Long lines everywhere... absolutely everywhere.It took us 1,5 hour to get to Vinpearl with the cablecar... just waitin in line with two children. All in all a big failure :-( Waisted one precious day of our holiday - and money.
It’s nothing special if you went already to Disneyland. Not that much stuff to do. I think it’s for family with kids around age 9-13

The whole zoo part should be shut down, absolute cruelty. Animals are kept in small restraining “enclosures”. Highly recommend not to give your money to this place. Really upsetting don’t bring ur kids their either. Really confronting and sad

Maybe we just timed everything badly, all day, or maybe every day here is the same. However, I feel as if I did nothing but queue only to be disappointed by the rides.The queuing started on the mainland, trying to cross over to the island on the cable car. Eventually, we discovered that we can take the boat at no extra charge, saving at least another hour of queuing.Once inside the park, more queuing for upto 90 mins per ride. Gave up after 3 rides and headed for the water park, which was a better experience.Upon trying to leave at around 6pm, headed for the cable car only to see another huge queue. Decided to take the boat only to discover that it wasn’t running so had to return to the cable car and queue for another hour, just to leave!I am sure that many people will have a great day here, they will enjoy the rides and love the water park. I enjoyed the Alpine run and had great fun on the inflatable playground in the water park but my abiding memory will be queuing and the disappointment of the quality of rides.

This was by far the single worst experience in Vietnam. Vinpearl land is the equivalent to a local carnival, except the carnivals are better. The lines were long. It was expensive. The staff were rude. The park was difficult to navigate. The rides were lame. They conduct bag checks before you enter the park to make sure you’re not bringing outside food or drink! Food and drink on the island is overpriced. They have no express pass system like other amusement parks meaning the lines are huge. I waited near 2 hours for the alpine roller coaster and it was sadly underwhelming. If this park is the reason you’re travelling to Nha Trang I’d recommend skipping it and picking another city.

We landed up in the zoo section of the amusement park by mistake and sadly saw a few exhibits while trying to find the exit. Five lions are being kept in a tiny enclosure and they looked malnourished and thin. Four hyenas in a tiny metal cage with a concrete floor. A monkey alone in a cage and the desperate creature was facing a wall while idiot visitors hurled abuse at it to try to get it to turn around. We left this horrible resort very depressed and I wanted to cry as at least I could escape the horrors but the poor animals are there for life. Avoid this place at all costs.
It was a very hot day, not much shelter so we had to leave. We ended up trying to get a early lunch however by the time we got to 1 restaurant they had ran out of many things. This was at midday.

After bought tickets we staed in queue apprx 1 hour to cable cars and it was tuesday not weekends. After coming to island we staed again on attractions in queues everywhere. Too noisy too overcrowded. Chinese everywhere practically all off them try to go under your heads. They do not use toilets. Delfins show very poor. 5 years ago was much more better. Pls don't waste your time.

It was very upset when my family began our trip in Vinpearl Land . When I went to buy the ticket for just only me and my son , the staff in there Mr Duc he didnt check what i want , just said : "We dont sell the ticket for hotel guest in here " and forced me back to the hotel to buy with the rude attitude and talk so loud with me, after came back to the hotel , I was guided very clearly by the receptionist at the hotel about sprate ticket , if u want to buy the ticket not for whole family i need to buy in The gate of Vinpearlland . That's all i feeling before playing in there . The staff made me feel bad about this paradise park.
Expensive overated island owend by the infamous Vinpearl group..  ok if you are used to the quality of thier attractions.. just a waste of money.
Dont go to this park. Everything is down for maintenance. A waste of money. The best part of the entire park was the water park wich was open. Other than that dont go.

Got to the ticket booth only to discover the cable car was not working due to maintenance. This was not advertised on their website. Caught the boat over,the first sight is the shopping arcade which has no shops. Very minimal rides working, the main attraction ferris wheel not working. Vinpearl land looks amazing from the mainland but when you get there you are disappointed and feel you have been ripped off. Supposedly it closes at 9pm we went to one of the food stands at 7.30 to purchse water and were told sorry madam closed. Dont waste your time or money ,stay at your hotel pool.

We heading out to the island excited to check out Vinpearl. The cable car was good but the lines to get on it were long. We were very disappointed, half the rides were closed. We decided to go check out the water park, there were 12 or 13 slides and 9 of them were shut. Its not worth your money. Like a poor man's dreamworld with no big ticket rides and even worse service. We went to have lunch but that was no good as well really bad service at the chicken place and when it came out it was cold. Finally we headed to the aquarium, the glass on the tanks was dirty and hard to see through. They also crammed 30 turtles into a glass tub the size of half a standard bath, I felt sorry for them. I'd read the bad reviews in relation to the hotels on Vin pearl but didn't expect it to include the park. Just don't waste your money. Film yourself burning 50 dollars and you will have a better time trust me.

This park is absolutely awful. The only good working ride is the cable car to the island. Every ride in the park was either closed or not open at a specific time. There was hardly any staff in this place to run even the rides that they had running. Out of a whole day spent there I rode 2 rides and stood in like to one of the very few restaurants (took over an hour and it was disgusting). It was madness. Also the park was completely empty. No one was there except people crammed into these restaurants!  I would never recommend going here… spend your money somewhere else!

Today I visited Vinpearl land mainly for the water park. When I realised they had animals here I needed to check how they were being treated as I’ve realised being in Vietnam 2 weeks, animals aren’t being looked after well at all. As I started walking around their confinement’s, I noticed how bored the animals looked and how little space they had. When I carried on further it to see the lions I could not believe the lack of space an animal like that had. Some lions and tigers I noticed was pacing back and forth. These animals are meant to hunt in the wild, it’s their natural instinct. I carried on, it got worse. I saw 3 bears in their tiny space, we stood for 15 minuets watching one of the bears literally rock from side to side none stop!!! It broke my heart to see the poor animal going insane. I am mentally scarred. I saw a sign that said hyenas and I just knew what was coming again, well actually, worse. 2 of them trapped in what looked like a large bird cage. No grass, no toys, no sunlight and absolutely no space to even walked around. Do not write back to me and tell me this is acceptable or that you look after your animals because this and I know this for a fact, it’s ANIMAL CRUELTY!!! It’s an absolute disgrace. Do not dare insult me and say this is okay because I know it is not. I also noticed there was a dolphin show but could not bare to go and see it. As we stood at the very top we looked down and happened to see that it had started. To look at the tiny pool they were in and see the huge sea behind them was just killing me. Dolphins swim for miles and miles in packs, how can they do this in captivity?! I’m so heartbroken seeing this, and this isn’t even half of it!! I even saw a large lion locked up in an even smaller cage pacing, dying to get out. I’m so gutted I have given my money to this company. Compared to the wild and their natural habitat, this is like locking animals in your living room, why is that fair? This is not the life these animals should be living. Avoid this place at all costs!!

This place is an awful ugly eyesore. Bright lights and tackiness at its worst. It has ruined the once picturesque Nga Trang and unfortunately there is no escape from it dominating the view from every aspect day and night.

I dont understand how people can wright good reviews about this place? The animals are stuck in minimal cages not big enough for them to even move! This is animal abuse! and its discusting and horrifying  made us actually cry!

We had been so looking forward to visiting here but in all honesty save your money unless you like queuing and being pushed around by rude Chinese and Russians.It took nearly 2 hours to even get on the cable car and after checking with the security guard at the start of the line that the ticket on my phone was ok, we got to the top only to be told we had to go all the way back down and get the electronic ticket converted to a paper one. My daughter by this time was in tears as she was so over it already and we hadn’t even got on anything! We should have quit then.We finally made it on to the island, by which time we are hungry only to find that every place we went to had either run out of food or if they did still have food then it was chaos trying to get anywhere near the counter to order, only to be told you then had to wait 30 minutes to get your actual food! It was lunchtime, it wasn’t the end of the day, I don’t understand how you can run out of food by lunchtime? So over the course of the day we ate 3 packets of crisps and lots of beer as this was readily available and certainly made this hell hole seem more pleasurable. By the time we made it through the hoards of people to get to the water park we did the obstacle course then went to the lazy river which is rubbish by the way, then went to go on some water slides only to be told they were closing. By now we were over it, big time. Then we start the process of trying to leave...On the return trip we didn’t bother with the cable car, we went straight to the speedboat which was literally the only thing that had any organisation about it. Still though people felt the need to push and shove and queue jump. The highlight of our day was when the security guard sent some queue jumpers to the back of the line like naughty little school kids!Then we make it back to the mainland where dealing with the chaos of trying to get a taxi was the next challenge. A tip for you if you are still crazy enough to still consider going, money talks. By now we were willing to pay whatever it took to get us out of there, but in the process a rather robust Russian lady felt the need to kick my 8 year old daughter quite deliberately when we got in a taxi before she did, even though we were actually there before she was! So I guess all in all, if you like noise, dirt, run down and inadequate facilities, chaotic excuses for queues, stress, rude people and being hungry you will have a great day, but we definitely did not.

We have been on the road for a while now and decided to take our 4 year old here yesterday as he's not had a great deal of kiddie activities. I cannot express what a complete disappointment the whole experience was.Issues were as follows:Buying tickets - Very disorganised. We got the public bus there which stops at some random ticket office (why we couldnt buy tickets there I wasnt too sure) where staff just bark at you - were so very rude.On top of that, the price i paid especially for my 4 year old son was a absolute disgrace. Adults at 800000 VND, kids 1m - 1.4m 700000 VND. What they dont tell you is that unless your kid is 1.3m tall then most of rides are unavailable to them - he was so disappointed. This is just a complete rip off.Queues very lengthy and staff didnt seem to have a clue what was going on. We had to wait an hour to get on the cable car alone.We were unable to do many of the water park rides due to lack of floats available. It was very busy possibly due to it being the tail end of Tet, but we found people were just commandeering the double floats as if they were their own - we missed out on so many rides as a result. The staff barely spoke any English.We witnessed a near drowning in the childrens pool due to unattentive staff. And when the "lifeguard" did jump in he nearly wiped the kid out. The food options are extremely poor, just fast food  - what was on offer mostly had items sold out and we were forced to resort to french fries & ice cream just to keep us going. Given taking your own food is "prohibitied" (they actually do a food and beverage check of your bags on the way there) this has much room for improvement.Transportation from the resort back to town after 6pm is non existent unless on a tour bus - no information was available as to timing of last services - why they do not run shuttle services later on once public buses have stopped, I dont know - they could clean up!  In addition there were barely any taxis available, apart from the few trying to rip people off - it was just complete chaos. We ended up (as did many others) having to walk the entire way back into nha trang town (not one taxi driver was interested in picking us up). We queued up at 6pm for the cable car back and finally made it to a restaurant for dinner at 9pm utterly exhausted (and starving!!)Much improvement is required to make this into the resort they claim it to be. Its shameful. Dont be fooled by the marketing material - this place is to be avoided.We have been travelling for 6 weeks now and this is by far the worst day of our trip and the most expensive!!

This dolphins torn away from their family and home waters and housed in petting and tiny pool. They forcing them to perform cheap tricks, which are harmful and abusing.Capturing and keeping dolphins in captivity is deplorable. They are highly intelligent. They live in complex societies. But they are taken from their homes, separated from their families and confined for human ‘entertainment’.

Arrived about 1.30pm and had a few rides (where people constantly pushed past the queues), before heading to the inflatable water park for our teenagers. They witnessed an almost drowning of a woman where the lifeguard did nothing as he was too busy minding his own business. My son waved trying to get his attention but three other park guests had to pull her out. When we went to leave the park to get back to the main island it took us 3 hours, where we experienced rude Asians and Russians continually pushing in the front of the line to get on the boat to leave, as if they were more important than anyone else. Almost at least 200 people pushed in front of us. There was a lovely Russian family behind us where the wife did more crowd control than most of security staff. SHE was incredible. It was beyond appalling the behaviour of some people. We would never return after our experience.

The day out was ruined by how unorganised the park is. When trying to leave they closed the cable cars for 3 hours without telling anyone before hand which left the boats but everyone was trying to use the boats and more than 200 people cut in front of us in the line and the security just watched!? Everyone was pushing each other and it was not a suitable place for children at this time. It took 3 hours to leave vinpearl because of this. The rides arent great and also we witnessed a girl drowning and the lifeguard did nothing he just stood there while three Asian men had to jump in and pull her out. The staff were rude and not helpful. I recommend not wasting your money here

Vinpearl was great  .The que to get off lsland so out of control .3 hours to get off island .200 people pushing in security hand no control .Vinpearl needs more security . I will never go back .Avoid leaving after dark
Very tame rides. Long queues. Cable car was good. Watch out for all the Russians trying to queue jump.

The place is good and peaceful, but the worst place to be in if you're a vegan or vegetarian. NO FOOD. One of the review says Coral restaurant, but when going they said no food for Veg. I had to fill my stomach with fries only. I starved and had a proper meal only when I exited the park. Bad experience.

We arrived at the queue for the cable car at 11.40 am on a Tuesday. We are still waiting as of 12.45. Maybe the park is nice. I don't know yet. They forbid outside food and drinks... After charging 800,000 dongs per person to enter??? Come on!

Park itself was average, run down and many closed rides. Animals were in terrible conditions. The monkeys, whilst enclosure was kept in liveble conditions was small.The lions and tigers were on a tiny patch of grass surrounded by stagnant green water, they had absolutely no enrichment what so ever. They were skinny and dirty.The rhinos were kept in a larger enclosure but their grown was a mix of dirt and rock,  wry dirty and barley any shade - as were all enclosures. The birds were in a large and well kept aviary, although they did look very manky and in poor condition. Their enclosure was large, which was nice for a change, however the caging was wore outer layer with an inner layer of netting. They had chewed through the netting in many places and many birds were stuck between the two layers, unable to get out, or to food or water.The water world area looked ok, although again, animals in poor conditions and small enclosures. The tortoises were in a 1m by 2m rick pool with about 30-something tortoises. 3 Large monitor lizards in a tiny 1m by 0.5m glass box.Overall, sure, nice place to take the kids for a couple of hours, but if you have any love for animals, boycott this place.
absolutely overpriced. bad for handicapped. the zoo is a insolence, that's cruelty to animals. eat disaster. never again.

If you’re visiting the area, make sure to stop in front of K.Hoise restaurant where Lloyd manages the deck chairs. Lloyd is an absolute gentleman who genuinely cares, not just about his work, but about the dogs he rescues. He’s not looking for money, reviews, or attention. Instead, he quietly dedicates himself to rescuing lost, mistreated, or injured dogs. He works three jobs just to support his rescue efforts, rehabilitating these dogs and finding them loving homes when possible. If no one can take them, he keeps them himself.I only learned about his cause because he had a litter of puppies at the beach, and I asked him about them. If you happen to see Lloyd, please consider sparing some change. It’s not about charity for him—it’s about helping with a cause he deeply cares for, and every bit of support goes a long way.On a side note, Nha Trang itself is quite dirty and feels somewhat like a heavily tourist-focused spot, similar to Benidorm or Magaluf. It’s very crowded with Russian tourists, so much so that most signs and menus are in Russian.The beach overall is ok. Beautiful water but ruined by plastic and rubbish washing up on the shores. Other beaches half hour each side of  Nha Trang are generally private and can only be accessed if you’re a resort guest.

What was once a laid-back coastal gem with charm and a friendly, relaxed vibe has now transformed into a bustling, high-rise jungle. The beach is still beautiful, but the atmosphere has changed dramatically.The local culture feels overshadowed by the heavy influence of mass tourism—especially catering to Russian and Korean tour groups. Most restaurants, menus, and services are now tailored to those markets, with very little consideration for Western travellers. English signage is harder to come by, and the welcoming feel that once made Nha Trang special seems to have faded.Sadly, it feels more like a packaged-tour stop than the authentic seaside experience it used to offer. If you’re looking for Vietnamese charm, quiet beachside cafes, or friendly local markets, you might want to look elsewhere these days.

The beaches in nha trang have become extremely dirty. Especially the beach next hong Chong. The smell of urine can be very strong at times as a result of an open sewer discharging on to the beach. Plastic, beer cans bags and other rubbish litter the beach daily. I've seen syringes in the sand ,and very nearly stood on one ..it's disgusting..the Vietnamese piss everywhere  . And constantly leave rubbish behind when leaving the beach .it really is a shame the have no respect at all for the  Natural beauty that surround them..

Truly is a beautiful place, unfortunately people don’t treat it with the respect it deserves. How hard is it to throw your rubbish in the bin? Or take it with you. I feel like a lot of the tourists come here because it’s cheap and treat it that way. No one wants to go swimming at a beach when there’s rubbish everywhere.

Siamo stati qui 3 giorni l’acqua è sempre mossa la spiaggia pulita a differenza di altre nella zona. I servizi sono molti ma la spiaggia è mediocre. La spiaggia piú popolare del Vietnam sembra Rimini, c’è di meglio. Sicuramente non torneró

The beaches in Nha Trang Vietnam are filthy. While the city seems to make provisions to keep the adjacent park areas clean and manicured, there appears to be no concerted effort to keep the beach clear of debris, some of which is dangerous.I have personally seen broken glass and sharp disks of metal amongst the many pieces of trash laying along the waterline. Oddly, the only people who seem to be picking up the garbage are tourists like myself. The locals walk right past.Sidewalks in Nha Trang are not considered spaces for pedestrians, but rarher just places to park vehicles including many of the over one million scooters in Vietnam. Crosswalks are meaningless, leaving pedestrians to brave multitudes of inconsiderate drivers/riders racing past as they attempt to cross busy streets.Many locals spend their days sitting inside dark coffee shops smoking cigarettes while glued to their cellphones. There is very little interaction between locals and tourists, with the exception of rude glances from the men as they greasily check out the foreign women up and down.Relaxation along the beachfront is difficult due to the incessant honking of motor vehicle horns. It really never ends. In the few minutes it has taken me to write this review, I have literally heard over a hundred irritating beeps.I returned to Nha Trang with the consideration of purchasing a vacation condominium, but now I just cannot get away quick enough.

It's so much garbage everywhere.Vietnam and Nha Trang is a really beautiful country with good food, nice weather, and good Vietnamese. When looking at the scenery far away, it's stunning, but with a closer look, you will see too much garbage everywhere. I wish there was a good system for clean up so locals have their beautiful country back and the travellers/tourists and those who seek adventure came back.The beach of Nha Trang are often crowded with loud and ignorant tourists who smoke smelly sigars, talking down on other countries and yelling lout towards "everyone." It has become a place I actually don't feel safe to walk anymore because of them.These tourists destroy the good feeling of being in Viet Nam and they probably bring a bad reputation into a beautiful country with their way of behaviour. And it seems the Vietnamese people are not very happy to deal with them also.Nha Trang is worth a visit for a day or two, but then you move on.

This beach could be absolutely amazing but sadly it has been ruined by people. We walked onto the beach and quickly turned around after seeing how much litter had been left there. It made us really sad as the beauty of this place has been robbed.

Beach is littered with rubbish brought in by the sea. Most people seem to be happy to lie amongst it ( and add their cigarette butts) but we picked up the litter around our sun beds and filled a carrier bag in 5 mins.  At least we didn’t have to lie amongst it anymore. Did the same thing 3 hours later after the sea had brought more in.

I was amazed how much rubbish was left on the beach.No one cleaning up, plastic bottles, food wrapper.No signs about where to swim and where not to swim. No visible lifeguards or lifesaving equipment to be seen.The big hotels blocked out the afternoon sun meaning people move down the beach to catch the sun rays. Why they have parasols to rent is beyond me, wait long enough and you will be in the shade any way.The promenade was interesting if you did not trip over the loose or uneven paving stones you might get run over by the idiots on their bikes.Positives the water was warm and clean, although the currents where very powerful young children got hammered by the waves as they sat at the waters edge.

We have been there with children.  Beach is sandy but sand isnt white , besides that the ocean was with big waves so you cant swimming. Water was so dirty.  We planned to stay all month but left after few days

We did not realise that this was essentially a Russian enclave. Can't move along the streets without people yelling at you in Russian and shoving pieces of paper right in your face trying to sell you things. Fairly charmless and it was windy, so the beach was very tough so no swimming or water sports.
Not my favourite place in Vietnam.... i can see how it could be nice, but its so crowded and dirty its really hard to enjoy the beach
The powers in charge of this beach need to have strict guidelines.It could be so beautiful but neglected.

We visited at night time where a concert was held by a beer company so it is crowded and noisy. Some swim at night. Coarse sand made it not comfy to walk on.  The beach is not that clean. A lot of beachfront hotels making the street never dim.
Dirty, dirty, dirty...I liked the atmosphere but when you get closer to the beach, you will notice trash everywhere. Bottles, plastics are floating in the water. Cigarettes mixed with the sands.

It's a city  beach and very close to the main strip. However convenience is outweighed by the cigarette butts everywhere and the noise of traffic in the background. The beach we went to was quite steep and plastic floating in the water. I much preferred Hoi An beaches which were cleaner and more relaxing. Kids felt the same way. With the rate of urbanization, I really hope the authorities and residents do more to protect what is/was their greatest asset.

This place is tragic. The beach is dirty. Check the high tide mark if you are one of those who think otherwise. The worst thing is the way the sea is pillaged to supply the buffet restaurants and tourists. So many small fish, prawns, crayfish etc.In my Country they would all be deemed undersized, put back and allowed to grow and breed.  Soon there will be nothing left except shiny new hotels with no one in them. Sad to witness. Won’t be back!

The beach was littered with so much plastic waste...it seems no one really cares about the marine environment here and it makes me really sad and angry. It seems just as the UK is getting better about addressing the issue of single use plastics here in Vietnam they simply don't care, put a huge dampener on things...NHA Trang for me was a let down
We live at one of those bay front hotel and went down for a swim the first afternoon.  You can see the little trashes in the water and both of us got some rashes after just half an hour in it

Vietnam you have a beautiful country but rubbish and pollution in your waterways and oceans is out of control. Despite a few swims we ended up avoiding the beach because of the rubbish in the water which is a shame really. This is a constant comment by visitors to the country and something the government should address.

The part of the beach in front of the VinPearl is nice... it’s clean, the water is fairly warm, and if you’re a beach person the beach is good. But the problem is the city it’s in... I hated Nha Trang with a passion and will never go back. It’s dirty, expensive, filled with Russians (no offense to Russians, I’d love to visit Russia, but I come to Vietnam to be surrounded by the local people, ie Vietnamese people), and many restaurant menus are only in Vietnamese and Russian (tired of pointing at pictures that don’t really look like the food that’s served). The single best thing I did was leave Nha Trang to go on a 5 day tour of the central highlands despite having booked a hotel in Nha Trang for those nights. If only Nha Trang beach could be outside of Nha Trang! Otherwise, I’ll never return.

While the hotel was great, the beach and town were not much to talk about. Many Chinese and Russian tourists. Beach was fairly dirty and there is a loud road separating the hotels from the beach. There are things to do here, but I wouldn’t recommend coming for a beach.

Sadly but fortunately for us as we were not staying in Nha Trang itself, this is a very busy and looking quite overcrowded beach alogside what appears to be a not overly exciting town focussed primarily on Russian and Chinese tourists who are everywhere and unfortunately the majority seem to fall into the sadly stereotypical rude, brash and generally unconcered about anyone else category. Our advice, would be avoid and stay somewhere else!

This beach side town has been overtaken with Russians, they are everywhere and dominate the restaurants, shops and beach even the shops advertise in Russian. The town is busy and dirty and has no appeal whatsoever. The beach is pleasant but again overtaken with tourist who all just seem to pose for photos all day... there are a lot nicer places to visit in Vietnam than Nha Trang so my advice is to leave it off the itinerary. If you do decide to visit, the Evason Ana mandara is a beautiful resort that offers some peace from the madness.

Big Russian tourist area as they smoke as if there's no tomorrow, having to dodge cigarette butts all over the beach while trying to take a stroll, Russian tourists obviously lack any sense of courtesy to nature and other people trying to enjoy the beach, dissapointing.

We only used the beach directly in front of Nha Trang city centre and were very disappointed. Despite it being labelled a ‘beach town’, they do very little to actually make the beach pleasant to be at. It’s super busy and there is a very large amount of rubbish along the beach itself and between it and the road that runs behind it. The beaches in Vietnam in general are phenomenal so best to look to quieter ones away from the town or make your way to Mui Ne or Hoi An if it’s a few beach days you’re after.

Following other places we have seen in Vietnam, Nha Trang lacked character and charm.  We stayed on the 18th floor of the Green World Hotel next to two building sites.  The pool area was noisy and dusty from the demolition works being carried out opposite.  We would not re-visit this area which we likened to a Vietnamese Blackpool.

I had heard so much about the beautiful the beaches in Nha trang and I was really looking forward to relaxing on the beach.Sadly there was nothing beautiful about this beach - and I’m sure it is the tourists who are to blame. The beach is strewn with litter and alcohol bottles, it really is sad.I did brave a dip in the water. As soon as I got waist deep I felt lots of little creatures on my feet and legs. it is really off putting. It didn’t take us long to realise that it was actually schools of baby prawns. It still put me off going back in!Unfortunately this beach has been ruined by the very people wanting to enjoy it. I would not go back to the beaches here.

The out-of-control development has greatly reduced the beach frontage and the breaking waves are mostly brown due to the erosion all along the coast.  What was a beautiful deep blue bay enclosed by clear bright white sand beaches is now just a strip making a narrow picnic area and some platform for entertainment.Further out of town, toward the airport, quick sand is becoming more and more common due to the development.Sad.  The Nha Trang area is mostly huge hotels and tacky souvenir shops, all selling the same stuff.  A perfect place for those European tourist who want to burn red from the sun and spend the evenings drinking heavily to get drunk/"pissed" and yell and scream.  Yes, it is that kind of place.Leave the beaches for the surrounding countryside, take tours and you will get a hint of the old Vietnam.

It is nice enough but town is filthy garbage every where prices high chain smoking Russians every where and rude arrogant Chinese that seem to think a line up means elbow your ways through it’s a shame it has become this but it is , I cannot understand though a lot of reviews complain of big waves , it’s an ocean what do you expect there to be? I love Vietnamese but this place has lost itself
Personally didn't find this beach to be overally enjoyable. The water isn't overally nice, it cool be cleaner and is ruined by all the tourist sun beds scattered along it.

We notice the first day that Nha Trang local shop keepers and restaurants were exhibiting clear signs of what used to be called tourist fatigue. My wife and I both experienced harsh, hostile and rude attitudes towards us as they assumed we were Russian and or Chinese. A reasonable guess as those nationals were there in large volumes and as usual, many Chinese demonstrated disgusting spitting in the street, loud shouting and pushing and shoving to go anywhere. The Russians not so much but still they do not show even a modest level of decent waiting in queues, sharing footpaths or simple courtesies most Europeans do for each other. Not as rude or thuggish as seen in Phuket but bad nonetheless. My wife place a small piece of paper (stamp sized) in a box that was clearly containing rubbish on the footpath. She was shouted at by a Vietnamese woman who took offence at the liberty! Why when there was any number of people who had discarded a great deal of rubbish all around the box! My conclusion is that once all the multi billions of new resort projects are finished and more tourists arrive, Nha Trang would be a place best avoided. Not recommended. I feel sorry for the decent locals who live there and have to put up with inconsiderate tourists.

Not recommended for a beach holiday. The Beach was quite dirty, foam floating in the water together with seaweed. We have experienced far better beaches, so the only solution was the hotel swimmingpool.
The most awful beach I've ever seen! Dirty, rats running all around. If you can, skip Nha Trang and save your time for other beautiful places in Vietnam

Hi. We came to this city with lot of expectations but unfortunately we did not find the city we were looking for. 1) The city is in extreme noisy, I mean really noisy, it's full of buses, everywhere honking the horn like maniacs, all the streets are crowded. The only place where we found some peace it was on the beach. The traffic is a nightmare, I saw even trolleys with babies almost being mashed in the zebra cross, nobody cares. 2) The tourism is highly oriented to russians and chineses, it's not real Vietnam. One tip, never stayed in the building Muong Than (it's in the middle of everything). It was the worst decision ever, it's hotel until 27th floor and from 27th to 45th floor is residential (Airbnb, real state, etc), that building is always crowded! ALWAYS, you need 15' only to go up or down, before the beach you are done and kill all the experience. I am sure is the same with all these buildings, the lobbies are always full! My god...my advise skip Nha Trang, Da Nang is much more nicer, very good beach, nice people, real Vietnam. The best of Nha Trang is leaving Nha Trang...

Upon arriving at the beach the initial view is beautiful. The water is nice enough, but this is definitely not a beach I’d linger on. Trash is everywhere and the public lounge chairs provided are gross. Opt for a quick dip and then enjoy the view from further away.

Travelling through Vietnam and have been to much better places than Nha Trang, with much prettier beaches.Sand is a little gritty compared to other places and there is a lot of rubbish in the sea.The whole town is full of Russians and Chinese, as is the beach and caters mainly for these 2 cultures.I would not return to Nha Trang, given the option.

Clean beach with alot daily activities. Nice to soak yrself in . Along the beach has food and drink restaurant , can be low and high price. Depend on individual budget. Nice to walk along during evening time. Afternoon will be Abit too hot .
Lots of people and unfortunately this brings rubbish along with it. There are some nice spots along the foreshore for a drink but there are nicer and quieter beaches around the area.

We were looking forward to going to the beach at Nha Trang but were very disappointed. The sand was strewn in rubbish. There were bags, wrappers, rice sacks, even a padded chair on the beach. The sea had bits of rubbish floating in it. Granted, the view if you looked out to sea was pleasant, and the water warm, but we weren't about to lie on the beach or go for a swim.
This place is paradise... Well, nearly. If there was an initiative to rid the beach of all the rubbish and littering, this place would be four stars. If the water was cleaner and clearer, five.

I prefer to swim in the sea and then go back to the hotel, have a shower and then into the pool, sadly, I couldn't go into the sea, it looked so inviting and warm, however there was a lot of plastic rubbish, paper wood and can only describe on here as human waste, very disappointing, I even picked up about 6 plastic bottles from the beach, I do hope this is sorted out soon as it is so beautiful and I would like to go back there again

Came here with my husband, in hindsight we really regret coming to Nha Trang, after traveling extensively through Vietnam, Thialand and laos, Nha Trang was certainly not what we were expecting. The whole place is literally little Russia, including the beach. It looks like it had potential back in the day but Russian and Chinese tourism has cleary had an impact on this once pretty beach, sand is full of plastic, cigarettes butts, food and the water washes up small amounts of plastic also. I actually ended up staying on the sand. My advice, unless your Russian and want to be surrounded by other Russians, skip Nha Trang altogether and go to a nicer, cleaner, more authentic part of Vietnam.

If you like a white sandy beach that is very crowded and busy and VERY TOURISTY then go for it! Having been in beautiful hoi an where there is so much to do - this was not the place for me! really did not like it. The beach itself is pretty and makes great pictures but it's overpriced and feels like Benidorm, will not be going back!
Some parts of the beach and water are quite dirty and full of people. Would recommend to go to the southern side early in the morning if you want a quieter experience.

Nha Trang beach is a long stretch along the coast of the city. Despite this, there are crowds and crowds of people throughout the day, even at 5am. The water is not clean and there is a lot of rubbish on the sand and in the water. There are also loads of people trying to sell you things when you are trying to relax which defeats the purpose of relaxing. Not recommended to come here! There are other beaches around nha trang that are cleaner and more worth spending time at.

Honestly I was fairly disappointed with the beach. Not nha trang itself but this would be so much better if the local government employed some people to clean upmthe beach. As swimming amongst cigarette butts and plastic bags just isn't my thing

Coming from our last stop at Saigon, we heard how good the beach was at Nha trang. When we were on the esplanade it looked pretty good and inviting. However, when we walked onto the beach there was plenty of rubbish on the sand and floating debris in the see. Quite disappointing as we were looking forward to chilling on the beach before our trip to da nang
Not nice at all. Try to avoid Nah Trang if you want to chill at the beach. However nice sunrise at 5am

Nha trang reminds me of the Gold Coast in Queensland - tacky, over commercialised and full of bogans/ Chavs... I felt nha trang was a bit trashy filled with rude russian and Chinese tourists. It’s western to the poor that it defeats the purpose of going OS and is accordingly overpriced. Skip it if you can, if you are after sun and surf there are plenty of other places to go.

The beach itself is great.  Nice white sand (not the softest but nice!) and and long, wide beach.  You can rent sunbeds for as little as 35,000 dong a day.  It's perfect if you don't want to go for a relaxing swim. The waves are huge and they crash close to shore.  It's difficult to get out past them without a lot of ducking and diving. And when you think you're safe, a massive wave crashes further out and knocks you to the bottom.  Nothing about this is relaxing. Fun if you like the challenge but I felt like I had whiplash the next day! The highlight is watching people get smoked by the waves after we learned our lesson!

Nha trang feels like small russia. Menu’s in restaurant can eveb be russian, everything is catered for the russians. It is also a very big city, doesnt feel like a nice beach place. I would not recommend going here. It is very tourist, restaurants are not great and environment is likea big city
While I can see it's potential, very disappointing to see a huge amount of rubbish on the sand and in the water, a real turn off. Wouldn't return.

The beach itself is okay although nothing really spectacular. Maybe it is deemed as wonderful if you are not used to really nice beaches. What really makes it bad is that the whole place is just set up for tourists. Bad to average food at too expensive prices. Not much else there.
Not like the brochure, its off season i guess, but water is murky and dumps hard on shore.not much but russian tourists

The beach was OK but packed full of sunburnt tourists with their bellies hanging out! Defiantly has a Benidorm feel to the place, maybe worth a 1 day visit if your passing unless your into that sort of stuff I wouldn't stay longer.

Did not love, in fact, did not even like this beach.  Littered with trash, including rusty nails, so walking barefoot is not wise.  Sand is on the rough side and water was not safe for swimming two of our days there.  If you are a beach person- skip it and go to Indonesia.  So many better places to see in Vietnam, like Hue!
This beach looked lovely from our high rise hotel but on the ground level was absolutely covered in rubbish. It was gross.

Start of wet season so water not good, ok first thing in the morning, but when tide comes in washes mud for hills into water and it's very brown, great service from hotel though sun beds and towel provided as you go on beach

We stopped at Nha Trang for a beach day as the pictures in Lonely Planet book looked great. Reality = an ok beach strewn with litter, it's everywhere, there is litter on the beach and in the sea. Went for a swim and found myself swimming alongside plastic bags and rubbish, quickly got out. So sad to see the beach not being cleaned up and looked after properly. Cigarette butts everywhere.

If you are Russian or Chinese this is a home away from home experience. During the day there are about ten thousand deck chairs four and five rows deep for kilometres. They are especially designed to support full figures. Apart from workers you will only see Vietnamese before 6 am and after 4:30 pm but it really isn't a place for them. Seven years ago the water was clean and clear with lots of fish. None of that now that there are so many people. No need to speak English or Vietnamese as almost all of the beach menus are in Russian or Chinese. Getting to the beach is an exciting challenge as the traffic is unbelievable for much of the day. For awhile there were guides to help tourists get across the road but they seem to have disappeared. Possibly they died. I recommend getting to the beach about 5:30 am and swimming out about 70 metres. It is not too crowded or polluted out there at that time of day.
I guess it all depends on the area you go to, but the ones I went to were just not good and being super crowded (even during low season) meant lots of rubbish too.

The beach may be much beautiful than now in 20 years ago. With too many tourists and rubbish on the beach, not really the kind of most beautiful beach as shown in the photos.  We are too lucky have the festival which attracts more tourists by the events, and rock music and party times of the beach is good for those love parties , not really suitable for those want to enjoy the sea view and music by nature.

The beach and location is beautiful, especially if you get up early and go down to see the sunrise, BUT.....during the day & evening it is crowded with Russians. Every shop and menu is in Russian, shop signage in Russian, if you are a Westerner they will talk to you in Russian.....this location is to Russians what Bali is to Australians. The shops and street sellers only sell cigarettes and alcohol. The tours around the area are limited of interest if you have already travelled other parts of Vietnam. Will not go back and recommend you spend your time in other areas of Vietnam - in our case it was not an option as location part of a pre-arranged tour from North to South of Vietnam.

The beach itself is very picturesque as long as you don't go into the water. The silt runoff coming out of the Cai River right next to the beach tends to eventually flow into the swimming area turning the water a dirty brown colour.  If you travel up river, as I did, you will see garbage that has been dumped on the river bank from the local fishing villages, which is obviously picked up at high tide and washed out into the bay.  I received an ear infection after one swim and didn't go back into the water.  Try the sun baking, but give the swimming a miss!

Unfortunately on NhaTrang Beach there was so much rubbish and plastic in the water, particularly after the weekend. I even dodged a decomposed snake!!! Of course on a hot day the water is tempting to relieve the heat,however sadly our swimswere minimal. I guess plastics and rubbish disposal are not taken as seriously in this part of the world as we see in other countries.I still love the atmosphere of the local sellers and the passing parade,but I really wish this be beautiful stretch of beach was cleaned and care for more.

Nha Trang beach is strewn with sea junk and sunburnt people. However, it's a convenient way to escape muggy days in the city.  The exercise stations that the locals like to use are clever and the walk along the esplanade is pretty and could be romantic at night with the lighting. For a better beach experience, Try off the beaten track, cousin Doc Let Beach - a bus ride to Doc Let beach (1.5 hrs). It's less populated, more rustic and a nice way to see countryside at the same time included in your bus fare.
The beach offers a beautiful view on the islands and mountains but it's crowded and noisy. If you're looking for quiet, go further.
Bad shore break, lots of russian folk ( not a bad thing) but the beaches here are nowhere near as nice as DaNang. Very pretty at sunrise at least.

Nice beach, but massively over rated. After what I read online before going, I was expecting good things. But was disappointed as it's just an average beach. Also all the locals use it as a public toilet, just whipping it out and urinating on the beach front! Just what I want to see when I'm relaxing!
The beach is over rated. Vendors and hustlers bother you often. Many tourists in search of a cheap winter retreat.

First of all, April is definitely not the best month to visit Nha Trang. Rain, sun, rain, storm...The beach itself is not the cleaniest... There is trash floating in the water and obviously no one bothers to take care of it.Occupied by hundreds of Russian tourists littering and letting their kids scream and run around.A huge dissapointment after a couple of our Vietnamese cooworkers recommended the destination as one of the most beautiful Vietnamese beaches...
This is really upset me - there're so many trash on the beach! The locals put it just into the sea and in the middle of beautiful forest.

We normally travel elsewhere this time of year. But due to the amount of Rubbish on the beach last year, we decided to give it a miss this year. Having travelled in Vietnam before we decided to visit Nha Trang. Imagine our disappointment when we were confronted with an endless tide of plastic bags, cups, spoons, cardboard boxes and even a sunbed table washing up on the beach. What a disgrace!!Although it is sometimes cleaned up as soon as it has been done it needs doing again. Such a shame as the beach itself is not bad.

Nha Trang is a very popular beach resort with Russians who don’t know about the existence of sunscreen. Be careful please. We sat with our iced chilled coffee which was nice as a tiny girl built an impressive fort and a man tried out the various adult climbing frame or gym equipment!
We were so disappointed. We came for a beach holiday and could not swim. Sea was rough, dirty and lots of rubbish. Apparently it is supposed to be nice in February. I guess we were unlucky.
Water was quite dirty, so don't expect a nice swim. There are many places to rent a chair and get a drink.

get in there early (aka sunrise) and you might get to see nha trang at its most beautiful, great for a swim with the last of the relaxed locals. unfortunately destructive tourism has ruined this place from 8am onwards. nha trang was a one night stop, probably shouldve been less.

Beach is dirty and the waves is too strong. I found handphone covers and plastic bags when I went there for a dip. Even though some of the neighboring beaches are not perfect but generally you get lesser rubbish. Sand is not great as well. Damp. What's there to do if the main attraction of this place is not properly maintained? Taxis drivers are going to charge you 2 1/2 the normal price (normally from Novotel to Intercontinental is 20,000 Dong but they will quote 50k!) and careful when you go meter. You may get driven around!

We were there in January. This must be the worst time to visit. Just glad I wasn't staying there as the beach and the water did not look inviting at all. We are from Australia though, so we are spoilt with great beaches.

A shame to comeback to Nha Trang to find the beach worst than it was 3 years ago, Phu Quoc and Cambodia beaches much better, there seems to be an acceptance of pollution and rubbish in the sea, the local authorities need to deal with this, but with many Russian and Chinese tourist, they seem to be more busy taking their dongs than trying to keep the beach reputation intact.

This beachfront has so much potential but the huge amounts of litter and vegetation in the water is off putting. Wading in the water and having plastic pipes, packets, sticks and rope wrapping around you and knocking into you isn't great. The beach is also scattered with litter washed up from the sea. We saw cleaning teams, but they didn't quite get to cleaning everything as they passed through.

We visited Nha Trang during one of December days when the weather was a bit stormy and waves were huge. The beach was covered with trash and the sand is dirty yellow color. Overall, really bad impression. Hope it is much better doing other times, but was very glad we were not staying there...
Unfortunately they had devastating rains a few days prior and it there was literally clothes, nets, trees, and furniture in the surf. They have a lot of cleaning up to do.
It wouldn't take much to clean up but the beach is very dirty. Rubbish everywhere. Very uneven surfaces. Wouldn't return

We spent 4 days in Nha Trang & were a bit shocked at the state of the beach to be honest.The sand was covered with rubbish & the beach was particularly sad looking.
