The water is not clear but I'm not sure if that is cause of the pollution or some other naturally occurring mineral in the water ...?The Vietnamese people are generally lovely, welcoming & helpful however this is overshadowed by the Russian tourists wandering around openly staring & generally being brash & loud.We had no idea so many Russian tourists were here, which is a shame really...I'm sure not all of them are bad but we ran into a number of them & they were arrogant & rude (& none of us even spoke the same language!).We found it nicer to get away from the main tourist strips & head further into town for a more authentic experience & the number of Russian tourists noticeably diminished.I definitely wouldn't swim in the ocean but the town itself, the people & the food is worth the trip, even only for a night or two.I guess we are lucky that we speak English & the world is really geared for English speakers not Russian speakers, maybe this is why we found the Russian tourists we met so uninspiring...It's a shame that the type of tourists who visit a place have the ability to tarnish it for other tourists who are keen to experience different cultures.

Nha Trang is a big city full of Russian tourists. The beach is a long, 10-15 meters wide strip of rough sand, neighbored by beach bars, skyscrapers and a rush highway. There are some palms in pots to give you the tropical atmosphere, too. The place is crowded, everything in the vicinity is terribly overpriced for Vietnam, and everybody tries to speak Russian to you. Highest hooker per square meter ratio in Vietnam. If that's what you like, enjoy...

We were here at the end of October and the rains were just starting.  From a distance the beach looks beautiful but the closer one gets the dirtier the beach and especially the water becomes.  I would not go in the water and left.  Spoiled, no, just been to too many beautiful beaches that have clean waters and beach.
Don't even bother going to Nha Trang in November, way too wet to do anything, especially when the main attraction is the beach which is un-swimmable (?).

Yes the stretch of sand is long and unspoilt BUT you have to dice with death to cross the road to reach it through a stream of motor bikes. Also, at the time of year we went, swimming was often impossible due to the waves. I was knocked down by one and hurt my knees on the gravel-like sand. It may be different at other times of year but we would have rather gone somewhere different.

We know November isn't the right time to visit Nha Trang and its beach. But we didn't expect the beach to be this dirty! After a few days of rain & stormy weather the beach is flooded with trash & plastics, which clearly shows the pollution problem in Vietnam. We even found the skull of a dog! That was the most fascinating part of our beach visit.

Planning a trip to Vietnam, do your research before coming to Nha Trang.  We spent 4 nights here in we have young kids so we weren't the party type so maybe its different if that’s what you are after.  The beach is average, steep drop off with plastics floating in the water, we only went a couple of hours on the first day. It would have been a one rating but the kids loved Vin Pearl island .  In summary had a European party town feel about it.

Terrible place unless you want to party hard, glad we saw it for ome day but a longer stay would be a total dissapointment, it feelsl like in Bulgaria Golden sanda rather than Asia with all the russian signs and russian staff...

Ok the beach is long and sandy however .... 4 lane road is running parallel to the beach with thousands of motor bikes a minute. Everybody is beeping like mad (yes it's the local style of driving) and it becomes rather annoying within 10 minutes and within 20 minutes you want to pop your eardrums. We have visited Nha Trang at the end of October which is not the best weather as the rain flushes lot of rubbish into the water and turns the water rather brown/green. We have decided to walk up the beach and see some of the local town which was a bad idea. As soon as you walk toward the first bridge over the river you can see all the rubbish floating into the sea and the smell is just stomach turning. Lastly, tourists made the place and they are the curse at the same time. 30+ floors hotel line the beach front and after 3pm block the sun so beach is in shade. Nha Trang is full of Russian and Chinese holiday makers, cheap food and drink. If you want a relaxing holiday Chinese+non stop traffic = forget it. Save you money and go somewhere else. We gave up after 2 days.

This beach is nothing special but if you've never been to a good beach before you'd probably like it....and the Russians certainly do. Heaps of them. The larger hotels hire out sun beds with umbrellas.

Sea front is covered in rubbish - you would not want to swim in the sea at all! Saw various tourists collecting plastic bottles and much worse. Was very busy even in low season and you have to haggle for the sun beds which are expensive! Gets incredibly busy in the early evening

This beach is incredibly dirty! Among the things we encountered were: multiple dead fish, a dead rat, lots of plastic bottles and bags and some unidentifiable objects. Could be very nice once it's cleaned up though.

Long, very long, walked from one end to the other in mid summer heat. Good range of accomodation fronts the beach from the economical to very pricey but sadly the water doesn't seem to handle the constant flow of spillage from the many residents of this city . Many cafe and bar options make entertaining and people watching a bit of fun. True beachcombers will probably prefer An Bang beach or Hoi An ocean beach. This place is heavily supported by Russian party kids and Chinese families.....

We visited Nha Trang as a family . It us not a nice place and is only geared up for Russian tourisrs . No charm whatsoever left . Looking more like a rundown European beach than an Asian place . We were very happy to leave after 1 day

Spent a couple of afternoons on the northern end of Nha Trang beach.   Hired a chair, umbrella and towel for 50,000 VD plus they give you a cold water.  View quite nice but water murky and tepid.  Shoreline littered with rubbish. No matter where you go there's always loud building construction noises across the road and Russians smoking next to you. Best part is just watching all the interesting things going on around you.  Like the fishermen rolling their disc shaped dingy's to shore.

There is no way you could swim at this beach as it is so dirty. There is bags of rubbish left all along the shore and even some dead fish. It is a real pity because the beach and view is actually beautiful but is ruined by all the rubbish left in the sand and water.

Not clean , dirty and never raked overnight, wouldn't rush back to this resort for the beach , also very busy and had to walk half a mile from our hotel to find a vacant sunbed at a not too expensive price , dissappointing I would say .

A nice beach however as soon as you allow yourself to notice any rubbish, you notice it EVERYWHERE. My friend and I actually ended up resorting to picking it out the sea with our bare hands there was that much and we felt so bad - we ended up with two bin bags full before we had to make ourselves stop. A big shame as there's so many big hotels and resorts along the beach I'm sure they could do regular beach clean ups with minimal effort.

Just a beach - nothing special.  Every Australian beach is better.I personally witnessed not one but two sanitary pads float past me - one clearly used.  Why people flock to this location is beyond me.
Sea was quite dirty-@ Relatively quite-@ Nha Trang is very much focused on Russian tourists-@ Not much to do at the beach

Gross sand, very busy, not so clean. Nice to have sea food seller around and choice of few seafront lounges. The beach as the city, I won't return as there is plenty of choice that correspond to my needs elsewhere in Vietnam (Hoi An/ Danang / Mui Ne...)

There are to many chairs and umbrellas on the beech.  The sea every few days is littered with rubbish and oil .its disgusting and terrible to see. Vinpearl across the bay are bombing a beautiful island daily to expand .. very quickly nha trang is becoming saigon. ..!!

No overly impressed. Not a lot of choice of beach shacks to get a drink. Ok sand and waters. You can rent a bed. Went to public pool area. 30000 dong for a basic bed in the beach or 60000 dong for use of pool area and bed/wifi. The pool area is like a bath and dirty. Full of Russians too. Expensive for what it is. Get a better hotel with a pool area or miss nha trash  (that's not a typo!)If you are looking for a quiet spot then head either end of beach (north or South ends).lots and lots of Russians and Vietnamese people. I would totally recommend going to Da nang if you are after a decent beach  (beautiful sand and much better waters /quieter) it's completely overlooked as a place!
Don't expect clean white sand beaches and clear water.. Quite dirty with rubbish.However grounds around the beach are immaculate and there are always crews around maintaining it.
You cannot see the beach for sun beds and umbrellas, not at all like the photos on Google where all the photos show it as a quiet beach

There are a lot better beaches in Vietnam.  The sand here is pebbly and rocky, not the white sand Vietnam is so famous for.  It is on a bay so the wave action is not as good (waves break to close to shore) and the water is colder.  Probably one of worst things from our perspective was the fact that this beach was very crowded and we were not even there during the busiest time.We actually ended up taking a taxi to Cam Ranh nearby.  The sand and wave action was much better. The beach was almost empty.  The water was still a little on the cold side.  There are a lot resorts being built there but they most were not done yet when we were there.  As a result, no one was picking up trash so the only downside was that the there was some trash that had washed up on the beach.  There are two resorts open at Cam Ranh and a lot more are coming.  The airport for Nha Trang is actually at Cam Ranh so I would advise staying in Cam Ranh and just skipping Nha Trang completely.

There was rubbish everywhere and brown rough water (most likely due to weather). Ended up catching a boat to Hon Tam, I would suggest doing some research on other beaches/islands in the area if you came to Nha Trang for a beach/paradise experience.

If you are expecting a relaxing white sand beach, think again... Nha Trang is a urban beach, noisy and not very relaxing, while you are at the beach you can still hear traffic horns and motorcycles. Unfortunately not the white sand beach that they show you on pictures; actually very dangerous beach, not very safe for swimming, the whole bottom of the beach is death coral, so when you are getting in is very dangerous, my husband feet got all cut the first time he went in and we saw so many people getting hurt. I will say not worthy, go to Thailand or Philippines for great beaches, Nha Trang not so much.

Where to Start? 5 minutes at the beach and a tourist got her bag stolen by thieves. There are organized groups of female thieves at the Beach. No security or police seen really poor. The food except some places ( check my other Reviews ) is bad. Even the Vietnamese cuisine suffered from presenting Western food like Burgers Spaghetti or pizza to russian tourists. Persistent Drug dealers want to sell you everything from weed to cocain. Now you get an idea of Nha Trang. We spend our days at the Thap Ba Spa.

This beach has potential to be an absolutely amazing beach, but unfortunately it is full of rubbish! The first day we were here it wasn't quite so bad, but got worse throughout the day. On the second day, however, the entire length of the beach was just full of rubbish and the sea had filthy brown pools of water as well as all sorts of litter. It really upset me to see the lack of care and consideration for the environment, the local authorities really should start to take action to prevent this problem, as it will only get worse with time! It's a real shame as this area could be lovely if only it was taken better care of.

I don't like to see how a nice place becomes a little colony of a country, with signs mainly in that country's language. That's what happens in Nha Trang and Russia. Apart from that, dirty water with feces and dead rats along with garbage in an apparently a luxury resort nearby.
Next to busy road. Area is too commercialised. Water is brown. Seashore is not clean - there were plastics and braches of trees.
Pleasant beach but no different to travelling to Durban beach front in South Africa. Slightly calmer beaches. Fine cream sand

Really a shame how much the city of Nha Trang is allowing the beach to go to hell. The entire thing (from where one would turn inland to visit Po Nagar Temple to where one would turn inland to visit the Cable Car to Vinpearl) is plagued by huge garbage heaps. I'm truly hoping this was just the weekend I visited, and not an every-day thing, but I didn't see anyone attempting to clean up the garbage. Before I visited, the weather had been rainy, so I'm assuming the poo-colored water was runoff from the river. Did not look healthy for swimming. Really wish I'd seen some effort being made to clean up the beaches while I was there. It was truly disgusting.

We travelled to Nha Trang with high hopes of some time in the sun and beach weather. Having grown up on the Australian east coast, being spoilt with great beaches, we were pretty disappointed to arrive here. In all fairness, it rained for the 2 days we were here but we are glad we didn't have any longer booked. The lack of beach time probably could have been overlooked but we honestly got the weirdest vibe on our first walk around the place. The town is overrun with middle aged, unhappy, overweight Russians (majority of signs written in Russian) who stare at you and genuinely look miserable to be on holidays. After reviewing goggle we aren't the only ones who got this vibe. If you grew up in a smog covered, dirty city you might take this as a tropical paradise but for people who grew up on Aussie beaches this is a far cry from a tropical paradise. Come here if you like feeling out of place and surrounded by unfriendly Russians who struggle to crack a smile in return. Don't regret coming here but definitely wouldn't do it again or recommend it to anyone else.
Unfortunately i was dissapointed by Nha Trang beach. The rubbish did not make it very inviting at all. Looked better on the photos.

While many backpackers enjoy the bech, I fimd that the water quality is murky, and it is over-run with endless vendors. I prefer Long Beach, 23 km.south of Nha Trang. Tent-like beach-side restaurants have coverings that offer shade, food and drink, and places to store valuables, toilets, showers and places to change clothes. They have lounge chairs and tables too. The water is clean, and other than late-December to February when the beach vanishes under the monsoon high water, it is a fine place if you can arrange a taxi, motorcycle or, in my case, a bicycle to get you there and back.
It was sunny but it still looked grey due to all the smog. Don't really get why people rave about it. Million beggars and hawkers-can't relax

This beach is okey. But really just okey. Go to a beach in Hoi An instead! Nah Trang is totally "tourist-destroyed" and noisy, did not enjoy it at all. Did like Vinpearl however, that little island has a nicer beach too :)

Better and more beautiful beaches along the coast of vietnam. The beach is too close to the main road to make it feel relaxing! And water felt bit dirty and so did the beach, full of ciggarets and cans!

Our hotel had free umbrellas on the beach so we went down and laid on them and decided to venture into the water. Everyone had mixed opinions on here so we went in to try it out for ourselves. Rubbish floated past regulary but water was warm and fun to jump in. Went up to hotel pool to rinse off afterwards!

You may be attracted to the floating assault course but DON'T go in there! We paid, then went into the sea to realise it was absolutely disgusting! Was littered with condoms and other stuff you don't want in your face! The toilet is guarded by a jobs worth security guard who won't let you use the toilet no matter what you say to him. The only plus side is the drinks that you can get ordered to your sun bed by the sun-bed guy. They were so cheap and the man took a picture for us. Mixed reviews but un conclusion, i loved the beach but don't go in the sea!

The beach itself nice, sandy and long, but whenever we wanted to go down in the morning, lunch time or in the evening it was just too busy, people were loud and rude, lots of Russians tourists.Well, the whole town was disappointed for us, lost it's Vietnamese feeling, wanted to leave earlier than we planned to stay.

A beautiful white sandy beach but the dumped / washed up rubbish completely spoilt it. We walked about 2km's . So many tied plastic bags with un identifiable contents.....Such a shame as could be beautiful. Come on Nha Trang please get in cleared by some grateful non earners before it gets too hot....maybe we hit it on bad day....

Nha trang looks like a russian city, everywhere russian advertisements, menus in restaurants are in Russian etc etc. Nha trang schould realise there are also other nationalities in the tourists population. What bothers me the most is that i get confused for a (inpolite,rude) Russian tourist. No more Nha Trang for me !!!

A dull, fairly dirty and unpleasant place to be. It seems like the beach is the main draw of Nha Trang and it's either a clever propaganda piece or people have managed to convince and/or delude themselves and others that they quite like it here. It's clearly a place with a lot of money in it, all along the promenade are sculpted trees, statues and swanky bars. For me, it leaves the impression of low quality beach resort and a dull city built around it.

Really disappointed with the beach area and the city in general.  Felt tacky and very under kept.  The beach was not the white sandy paradise I expected.  It is so built up and a lot of rubbish lying around.  I wouldn't go back.

The beach is beautiful and the water seems clean enough to swim in however the cigarette buts and trash strewn about the beach by the Russian vacationers make it difficult to enjoy oneself. Hopefully at some point they will host beach clean ups and encourage visitors to dispose of their waste properly so all may enjoy.

We'd heard Nha Trang was the 'Gold Coast' of Vietnam. A beach lovers tourist hotspot. Tourist hotspots aren't 100% our thing however being first-timers in Vietnam we imagined a bit of a mini Phuket or Kuta and thought we'd better give it the appreciation of at least a couple of days. Unfortunately, I wish we had of left it off the itinerary. The beach is lined with high rise western hotels. There are no on-the-beach from hotels we saw, and the beach from restaurants are very western and expensive and we had to hunt around and head in town to experience real Vietnamese food. Dominated by English and Russian signage and menus, you can tell this place is lacking local flavour from the word go, however the tourism appeal is also lacking.  There is still a lot of local culture taking place around you especially of an evening, but this place lacked true Vietnamese charm and it just didn't feel authentic. On the upside, no hawkers or sellers in sight, very quiet relaxed and you feel very safe here and any local will help you out if asked.

About 10 years ago I saved up as much money as I could and decided to backpack my way around SE Asia. I was pretty naive to be honest and didn't really do my (any) homework, just bought a ticket and stepped off the pier. Now, while I know that my experiences were just that, my own, I found myself having some difficulties as I traversed China and found myself even doubting my want to continue, and then I finally made it to Vietnam...I loved it! The people, the food, the culture, it was like manna to me, especially after the hard times that I had across the border and I felt absolutely welcomed by nearly everwhere and everyone that I met. This was especially the case in Nha Trang, where I spent a wonderful New Year, surrounded by warm strangers who made my visit truly memorable. Now don't get me wrong... In my old age I know the dangers of chasing memories and the impossibility of trying to recreate something that has come and gone, but that is genuinely not what I was looking for on my re-visit to Nha Trang on my 2nd visit. I was however, really looking forwards to sampling food from some of the restaurants and cafes that I had found out the first time, such as Truc Linh (the original, not the 2nd and 3rd poorly rated versions that have now appeared), Shorty's Bar and The Rainbow Bar... But from the moment I hit the streets down to the beach I was absolutely astounded by the massive changes that this once local beach city has undergone. A lot of money has been poured into the place, and much like Mui Ne and Phan Thiet, most of it is Russian, and while I have no issue with the awesome locals here being able to create businesses and enterprises for themselves from this influx, in honesty it really pains me to see their own culture swallowed up by burger bars and Russian bistros that have completely enveloped the once thriving beach community that was here before it. Don't get me wrong, I'm not trying to be a hypocrite, I know that I am just another tourist myself, and it pains me now that for the last decade I have been telling everyone who might listen that they should see the place for themselves because it seems that such enthusiasm to see this gem has enveloped the original heart of the city. Hey ho, I guess that is what progress is all about but it has come as a hard lesson learned to this old fogey who just wanted to sip a locally made G&T and stare of into a sunset he hasn't seen for a very long time... Instead I am treated to an inordinate number of Russian themed bars and even found it a struggle to simply find a place that just sells a good old bowl of Viet Pho Bo, instead of bloody burgers and french fries.As a mopey side note for anyone who has managed my ramble this far, you'll find prices here almost triple those in HCMC if you don't do your homework and wander far away from the main areas. But it is still a pretty place if you can see past the corporately owned deckchairs and imported plants dotting the beach.

From a far the beach and water look beautiful and amazing. However when you get close you will see the garbage in the water and on the beach. It doesn't seem to bother everyone but they were also the ones smoking and throwing the cancer stick garbage into the sand and water. I would not recommend this area if you care about the environment.. The locals also have not respect for clean water or a clean beach. Will not be returning. Also if you are not Russian or speak Russian there are almost no tours to go on.
Its really not a great beach. The water isnt clear and it smelt. It was also always busy from sunrise to sunset! We were disappointed.

Could be a great spot, long sand beach with good weather but unfortunately it's rather trashed. You swim with plastic bags and leftover food that's been tossed out in the ocean. A lot of sun beds for about 40 000 dong for an entire day. The beach has been completely taken over by tourists, especially Russians but no one bothers you.

Im here just for one day and have been around the city today... Honestly if you are for a long time inside an office at home and just head down here will be an amazing place... However, if you are backpacking and have gone to Thailand or Indonesia or Malaysia, just avoid it here...Its big, so difficult to get around, food is overpriced and everywhere you read about being stolen or robbed, which makes you feel worried...Also, we took the bus from HCMC at night and the bus did not stop anytime in a restaurant or station, which means that everyone was urinating on the road, including women (local) and the foreigners for the small reason that they just didnt want to urinate in front of everyone they had to hold it for 12 hours.... The name of the company is: Viet Nhat Travel... AVOID IT....

Beach is dirty in places. Nice on a sunny day with shade from trees. Hotels have loungers for their clients. Overall, just avoid Nha Trang. The town is dirty, quality of food in restaurants is poor and overpriced and absolutely everything is geared towards the Russian tourist market. In short, if you're not Russian, you won't enjoy it here. One to skip, definitely.

Beach was pretty but the weather wasn't good when we were there. Yellow and red flags up on the beach so no swimming every other day and the other days where the flag was yellow no swimming too. The waves are far to big because of a steep falling beach. In addition there are no visitors on the beach after 13 as it faces towards East and no hence no sun an hour after noon.
This is an inner city beach, full of Russian tourists, and without any charm.  I can't imagine why anyone would choose to be here - it is the type of place I'd pay to avoid!

I didn't enjoy this beach. It was a stretch of beach about 5-10m wide. The weather didn't really help, but I like beaches with a nice bay and a decent amount of sand down to the beach. It seemed to be deck chairs and then water

Nha Trang beach is a glitzy Russian tourist filled beach that as an Australian (we are spoilt for beaches) is quite awful! In Nha Trang if you want to experience some peace and quiet and a rural atmosphere, go to Chua Sui Do temple. Be prepared for a 1 hour drive and a walk up 500 steps up a steep hill. Beautiful views and peace!! The drive into Nha Trang is lovely and Nha Tranh is a good starting point for heading North. Surprisingly Vinpearland had a great cable ride with awesome views and the alpine sled ride at sunset was superb!!

Similar to other reviews,  was expecting beautiful beaches and ocean but instead found it to be unclean and dirty looking. Ocean was not very nice (green from algea) and weather was cloudy and windy. Surround mountains are beautiful though.

To begin with I had a filling of visiting Social in Russia. Not something I intended to do.The beach itself was dirty. Partly due to high waves but mostly to rudeness and lack of culture of the visitors.Lifeguards, and I saw them only once,were  wandering around without even looking what was happening at the sea.The overall prices are lower than in Hanoi, but one could and should bargain everytime.This is a place to visit once not more and better in a good company.

I like to think that anywhere we would go in Vietnam would give me a Vietnamese experience.  Pretty basic, reasonable expectations?  We had no idea at all this once lovely beach and town has been converted to a Russian satellite. Rude, brusque and without manners they have totally overrun this resort/beach. In doing so it has lost any charm or pretence of a true Vietnamese beach resort.  Could someone please enlighten me as to how they are allowed to dominate tours, some bars, restaurants, etc. Is it that easy in Vietnam to obtain a legal work/business permit? It really is that bad to see every sign every menu every tour in Russian. Very many shops,  restaurant, tour offices and bars actually has a Russian national outside with more inside. Russian? Yes. I asked! We saw arogant attitudes towards the locals,  heard awful stories and saw bare chested youths and men slugging from beer bottles swaggering down streets, large women with shorts and tops that left little to the imagination. Not pretty! Only a poor because we had excellent interaction with the locals and walked our socks off to see beyond the beach strip. It could be a wonderful resort if the authorities didn't choose to be so focused on one ethnic group. No this dominated the visit to Nha Trang for us.So..... not for us......not recommended......and we will not return.

The water was full of trash and its located just near a busy street, so there is no peace and quiet. Still the fact that it is located in the city makes it easy to just go down and take a quick soak after a hoy and busy day in town.There are sunbeds for rent.

To be perfectly honest unless you are Russian Nha Trang is a town worth missing. You would be better off staying at another beach town in Vietnam. The nightlife is for Russians, many shops, bars etc are dodgy. It's a tourist town geared towards the Russians. Markets are overpriced compared to other cities such as Hanoi. Once you see some pergodas/waterfall there's not much to do.. VINPEARL ISLAND SHOULD BE AVOIDED.. this is a cheezy, super touristy Russian geared Theme park, that you would only like if you're a child or a Russian.If you do decide to go to nha trang, I would suggest stay for 2 nights and go see the BaHo Waterfall, eat dinner at Yen's & drink cocktails on the beach at the Sailing Club.

Nice enough beach but absolutely full of Russian tourists and you get pestered by vendors every 5 mins. The view is great but the quality of the beach itself is pretty poor. Keep an eagle eye on your possessions. An old guy of about 60 tried sneaking up on me whilst I was asleep. Luckily his footsteps woke me up. Locals everywhere up top on the grassed area are watching for an opportunity. Call me paranoid but after speaking with others in and around the hostel, it pays to be weary. Don't go down there at night time which I know is a real shame but people get robbed by knifepoint. A real shame as I was so looking forward to our time in Nah Trang. :(

We planned to stay in Nha Trang for 5 nights but left next day.Even thought the beach itself is amazing the company you have to deal with is not nice... You hear everywhere Russian speech, see Russian signs, eat Russian food and face the results of their presence here - garbage and completely Russified town. I have nothing against them, although I grow up with them. But the reason I came to Vietnam is to experience Vietnamese culture, unfortunately in Nha Trang it's not possible...

Though the views are beautiful on this beach and you can walk for ages, I would not take my running shoes off for fear that I would step on something that would ruin my vacation.  This city needs to get its act together and clean up what could be a stunning beach!!

Maybe ok if you're only planning to spend a day in nha trang the beaches are abit dirty near the waters edge but there are plenty of bars and restaurants to stay entertained.  Taxi drivers can get annoying in the area

After swimming in the sea I walked back to my hotel and saw 3 nappies, one sanitary towel, lots of paper, bags, half eaten fruit, etc etc all over the beach and in the sea. The Vietnamese should be ashamed of how they treat the environment

When I took a walk along this beach I was disgusted to see the amount of rubbish there was. There were some parts of the water that looked like a serious health hazard. Probably the worst beach I visited in 'Nam.

Nha Trang has change dramatically since my last visit 5 years ago & 3 years previous to that. I have enjoyed my stay in Nha Trang but have been disappointed by the transformation in such a short time. You come to experience the culture, food & traditions of another country not to change it to what you have at home. This is what Russian owners & operators have done to Nha Trang. Russian signage, restaurant menus even entire shop fronts. I even saw T-shirts of Vietnam but the writing on it in Russian! Could not believe it.Unfortunately this has not only happened to Nha Trang but also Mui Ne & it's not the Russian tourist but those who have been allowed to set up shop, buy business here, develope the place, exploiting this beautiful country & the lovely locals.

The water is clear, but there is a lot of plastic bags, straws, strange paper etc in the sea.We saw a dead rat on the beach, left overs from beach bbq, and lobster shells in the sand.the city does not have much to offer We went for beach relaxation, and came from Hoi An, Hoi An was much better.

Some friends seem to have had an amazing time here and maybe we were in the wrong part of town, or maybe we just have better things to compare it to (koh tao ie heaven on earth) but my experience of it was that it was very clear water but extremely dirty. I took one step in, and one step swiftly out as there were massive plastic bags floating everywhere and I'm pretty sure I saw sewage. It's a well known fact that sewage goes into the sea in Vietnam but seeing faeces is not particularly encouraging. There are some nice bars along the front but you will be hard pressed to find somewhere with aircon, even away from the beach. Another thing is that for a toilet there was a portaloo manned by a Vietnamese woman. I went to ask how much it cost (of course I couldnt pee for free!!) And I was told 5000dong, I told her I only had 1000 and was going to get the rest of it and come back and she screamed at me 'no no 5000 now' so didnt go back. Also, theres no where to post about transport so I'll have to do it here. I read someones review on here of cuc tung and have to completely disagree. Clearly she got lucky with her experience because I have had the worse experience of travelling so far (2 months). After being picked up late from our hotel in a monsoon pour down, the van stopped away from our hotel so we had to get wet to get to it. Then once we were at the bus station we were all MADE (and I say made because I asked to stay on until we could get sttraight onto the coach and was actually physicially pushed off) to get off without our bags then huddle around the door to be given them (still raining very hard) we then were stood outside the coach whilst they pissed about with where to put our bags (there were 4 people on already but the baggage holdal seemed to be full) at this point I was getting pissed off. I was soaked through, ALL of my belonging were soaked through. How my phone survived I do not know and it's lucky I had a waterproof camera. Again and again we tried to get on the coach but they would g let us. The water was around our ankles by this point , no exaggeration whatsoever. Eventually they started letting people on but insisted everyone take off their shoes and put then in plastic bags (I know this is the general rule but we were so soaked already it made no difference to the coach because we actually dripped everywhere) so yes, more time outside for our stuff to get wet. Once on board I asked for a blanket to dry off and he said later (getting angry always works and we were then all given blankets but of course with aircon and only wet clothes it was impossible to get dry) the operators were actually laughing at us. I don't ever expect to be treated that way by anyone of any nationalist especially due to where I come from. I was disgusted and I said so and moved to a bottom bunk ( if they tell you they're reserved and you have to go on the top they're talking crap, they save them from the vietnamese people ) after an argument I got my way (going on the top isn't too bad but after being treated that way I was determined to get what I wanted) I am usually a calm person who doesn't like confrontation especially as it was in front of loads of people. But as I said before, I would never ever treat anyone that way. On top of all this, people did sleep in the aisles. Fine for me, because we were near the toilet. The others had to wake people up to go through beds to get to the toilets. It also strained the aircon making it not quite cool enough. Then after multiple stops on the way ( with awful driving) we picked up some sort of cargo. When we arrived and they opened up the baggage holdal we could smell a combination of chicken and fish and obviously everyone's bags smelt of this (on top of being wet). We arrived an hour and a halfate due to all the stops (meaning longer on such a horrible journey) and meaning that one guys hotel transfer had left. It was so unavoidable that we got that soaked. Yes if it hadn't have been raining it woukdnt have happened but the operators actually wouldnt let us on at first and then took great joy in us being wet. It really disgusted me. And it's a shame because I don't think these people should be representative of the vietnamese people. It has definitely made me disagree with the supposed friendliness of Vietnamese people. It seems so far that people are only friendly when they will be getting your money. And then once they do, it's unneccessary to make such an effort. It's really such a shame. But hoi an is really lovely. Also as a side note, I haven't actually been able to look at my bag yet because the hotel I'm staying at has put it in storage until we can check in but I had a waterproof cover on the bag which was tied at the zips. The cover had been cut and the zip was open a little. Like I said, I havent checked if anything is missing but just as a word of caution make sure nothing valuable is in there.

The drive from the airport to Nha Trang beach Central has magnificent views.  But once you get here, you realise that it's just another crappy beach, covered with crappy deck chairs and umbrellas, with hawkers trying to sell you overpriced crappy wares.  The place is filled with 'New Money Russians' who strut around with their bloated bellies and melanomic tans, with an air of capitalistic entitlement.  There are few historical attractions worth seeing, if you've seen one big Buddha you've seen them all.  I spoke with numerous expats who consistently reported the same thing...many of the locals in Nha Trang are professional liars and thieves, so be warned. But if you want to drink yourself silly, maybe pick up some life-altering STI, laze in the sun until you're leather, and swim in crap, then this is the place for you.

we walked the beach several times and it was a disappointment. we have had a great stay in NhaTrang, thanks mainly to the choice of hotel. The beach was a disgrace; clean it up and remove the trash please. i dont want to see nappies and sanitary pads floating in the water.

I had heard so much about Nha Trangs beaches that what I saw shocked me. Beach by where Michelia hotel is, turned up at around 2 pm and looks great but as you approach water you see rubbish, bottles, plastic bags, coconut shells etc. Yuk. Luckily it seems that a gang of workers comes along and sweeps this up which is a relief as you can then see how pretty it really is. The only problem is that in the water you find the same rubbish. Not nice to be swimming through rubbish. Not what I expected so behaved like a bad tourist and stayed in hotel pool.

I stayed in Nha Trang 2 nights, but if I could have left before, I would have been happier....it was like a western sea-side city, too touristic, nothing genuine left. There are much better beach places in Vietnam if you are looking for a quite and non-touristic place!the beach was nice, but not excellent. at night there was a small market where tourists came to buy souvenirs from China...99% of tourists were from Russia, all shops and travel agents had advertising and messages in Viet and Russian, they didn't care a lot about the other foreign people!I wouldn't go there again....
Absolutely beautiful place but litter on the sand and in the sea made it hard to enjoy, locals and tourist a like need to be a bit more respectful of nature's gift

The beach would be very nice- beautiful views, warm water, and soft sand. However, it was completely packed with so much trash on the sand, with plastic bags and wrappers in the waves. Very disappointing :(

Pleasant enough beach but I wouldn't call it amazing. It's quiet during the day but gets very busy around sunset when you are joined by the locals. There are a couple of nice bars on the beach - Loisiane Brewhouse and Nha Trang Sailing Club. Women can cook seafood for you on the beach, you can also get a full hour massage for about $8 USD. People trawl up and down with flyers to events promising 2 for 1 drinks and happy hours. A lot of Russians visit here, there was a huge Russian dance party when I was there - ok if that's your thing.

While Nha Trang has a wonderful beach it is now so overrun by business's opened by Russian's for Russian's that it is no longer a desirable destination. Indeed, I've spoken to many Western tourist that now refuse to go to Nha Trang. The Russian's are so rude and pugnacious they can certainly ruin your trip. This is sad, because it was once so nice.

I was really excited about coming to Nha Trang... And I can say that only one night should have been enough to see it( we booked a 2 nights stay here )... This morning we went to the beach but we couldn't stay there as it was pretty windy. The water temperature is not bad and the sea was clean, the problem is that you can't come inside as the waves are braking strongly on the seashore, which makes impossible to swim. The other downside is that Russians are dominating the area, so not a re Vietnamese experience ... yesterday we even had to order for an English menu as they all think you are Russian!!!Have seen Nha Trang and I am looking forward to be back in Vietnam enjoying Ho Chi Minh city !

My wife and I spent four days in Nha Trang and were surprised to find that the entire area is dominated by Russian tourists. When I say dominate, I mean we only heard one other couple speaking english in the four days that we were in Nha Trang. Even the signs in the park are in Russian. Nha Trang weather in December was cool, overcast with very high surf. Most people could not get into the water with an 8 foot breaking wave. You also had to cross a four lane street (a six lane highway for most countries) to get to the beach. If you are looking for a beach area with peace and quiet this is not the place to go. If however, you've always wanted to visit Russia but thought it was too cold then this is the place for you!

Was there in late Oct 2013. I didn't like Nha Trang really. The city is busy and mostly filled with Russian Tourists. If you have to go there I'd recommend you grab a bike and go to Diamond Bay resort - about 20 mins south on your bike. Its private but really nice. You'll pay US$1.50 for the privilege but totally worth it. Either that go a few kilometres north and the beach is better. HOWEVER if you have just limited time and want to see Hoi An, for god sake forget Nha Trang and use he extra days at Hoi An. You wont regret it

The most litter I have encountered in the water at any beach in south east asia. There are so many high end hotels who could invest some time in maintaining the water/beach. The jetskis are dangerous as the come right up to the beach at full speed and people are simple targets to guide them to the waters-edge. Not what you expect from this holiday zone.
how can this beach one of the best beaches in Vietnam. it's really awful. too touristy dirty and not even nice sand.not worth to go!

We were here in july, we came from beautiful beaches is Hoi An to beaches full of rubbish. The view is nice but paying a premium for a lounger and beer at an extortionate rate. You can look at all the rubbish and bits of food in the surf, it turned my stomach but you may enjoy it. Go o Hoi An, beer for a reasonable price and the lounger is free

But I am from New Zealand... And really this beach is nothing to rave about.   It's ok, our hotel had umbrellas and chairs and there weren't too many hawkers , but I wouldn't rate it as a beach resort.
Skip. Hoi An is cleaner, has much much nicer beaches, better food, and less annoying tourists. If you must transit through Nha Trang heading north or south, spend as little time as possible.

We were on a shore excursion from the cruise ship. We chose the Highlights of Nha Trang. This city doesn't have much to offer. My pocket got picked. I didn't lose anything important. I hate going places where aggressive vendors swamp the group and won't let up. Well, at the temple, the adults keep pushing stuff in my face and the kids, just the right height of my cargo pants pocket, get easy access.

This beach has nice sand, the water isn't too rough, it gets great sun but the entire coast here is being ruined by garbage. Around the town of Nha Trang as well as the surrounding areas you will be swimming among all sorts of plastics and styrofoam as well as more dangerous debris. Get your act together, Nha Trang!  It's heartbreaking to see such a gorgeous area ruined in this way.
The sand was very coarse and the waves were massive and full of the coarse sand. Nothing in comparison to the lovely beaches an hour north!
I would not visit this city again. The beach is nothing great and the sellers are annoying. Good for walks though. Should be cleaner.

Perhaps it was the cloudy weather but the water didn't look all that blue. I read somewhere that it can turn brown after rain so maybe that was the problem. But the water was littered with plastic bags and had a weird oily feel to it which looked to be a longer term problem. Coming from Australia the beach was a bit of a joke.

Nha Trang is a nice town to visit, the sunshine is great, the seafood delicious, the fruit amazing, but the ocean not so pleasant with many plastic bags. It was not pleasant to swim with the plastic bags. Vietnam is lovely to visit, have to keep the plastic bags away to keep the people coming to visit the beautiful beaches.
#NAME?

A nice long white sandy beach but water looked very muddy and wavy in September and October. Lots of visitors were just lying leisurely to enjoy sun bathing rather than swimming. The city council has done an excellent job on city planning. The road from airport to the city beach was smooth and well maintained, with trees and bushes sculptured in interesting shapes and lines to make a visually appealing picture. It was enjoyable to appreciate the art of carving.
I much prefer white sand and clear water. Apparently the water can be nice when sediment is not running off the mountains during rainy season. Unfortunately, this was not my experience.
The beach is really long and there are really nice grounds all along it but there is a bit of trash and touts everywhere!

We were vey disappointed with this beach. This may be because we had spent a lot of time in Thailand where there are some amazing beaches somour standards were quite high. In general we found the beach to be quiet which is good, however the sand is very course and the water is just full of rubbish and debris. The views are pretty good, it's a shame the sun set on the opposite as well. It's worth a visit and is a nice beach but just not as good as we was hoping for.
The beach and water had a lot of trash floating around.  I think it could use a major clean up.  There is a lot of potential, but I was very disappointed.

You are a tourist. You are a target. A person could not possibly relax on this beach with the amount of determined hawkers that plague it. Literally, you could be "asleep" and they will hover above you trying to shove whatever things they are selling onto/into your face. They know no bounds. I live along the coast of Victoria, Australia so I am amongst an abundance of amazing beautiful beaches. I think I must be spoilt by this because I couldn't find Nha Trang Beach very charming at all. Ignoring the hawkers it wasn't impressive.

We were quite disappointed to see how filthy the beach was. There is a thick tide line of rubbish running the entire length of the beach, and when swimming you are constantly dodging plastic bags/ dragon fruit/ dead cuttlefish etc.We found it to look nothing like the pictures- no turquoise water or clean sand in sight.The water wasn't too clear when we visited in the afternoon, maybe because the sand was quite stirred up from the wind.If you walk along towards the cable car end of the beach you will come across the private sections where people sweep up the majority of the rubbish.We did visit in the evening though, when the sea was very calm, crystal clear, and there was very little rubbish about. Maybe early in the morning before the rubbish and sand has been stirred up by the waves and wind would be the best time to visit.
While the beach has a great view of the bay and mountains, there I a lot of crap which s constantly washed up like plastic, polystyrene and wood.
good beach, mind the constant hawkers, but better if you actually communicate with them, don't be a tourist

Even though, every once in a while you see a lady go by picking up some of the garbage on the beach, it isn't enough. It really is just a city beach, that is still quite dirty. There is garbage in the water and really doesn't look that appealing to swim.If you do swim, do your part, bring the garbage out of the water and to the top of the beach so it can be properly disposed of.

Nha Trang Beach è una delle spiagge più conosciute e frequentate nel sud del Vietnam , da una parte ha alcuni  pro , dall’altra ha qualche contro . La spiaggia è kilometrica dì sabbia bianca  fine , quando siamo stati noi a dicembre e gennaio il mare era abbastanza mosso e il cielo coperto da nuvole , tuttavia il clima era ottimo con 27/28 gradi , con venticello piacevole , per questo motivo non tutti i Beach club erano aperti e in spiaggia non c’era mai molta gente e l’ ho trovata anche pulita.Alle spalle della spiaggia c’è un bellissimo lungomare molto curato , con giardini e alberi stupendi, Palme altissime a fare da cornice come in una cartolina , monumenti , panchine , fontane  e Ristoranti sul mare , il tutto costeggia una ampia  strada principale abbastanza trafficata tra motorini e auto , bus ,alle spalle Hotel di Lusso ,Grattacieli e Mall che svettano con affaccio sulla baia . Da una parte a Nha Trang c’è tutto quello che uno può desiderare moltissimi ristoranti sia di lusso che locali , idem i negozi, banche , il mercato ,qualche attrazione da visitare e stabilimenti balneari, e sinceramente siamo stati fortunati a trovare poca spazzatura in giro , tuttavia , per chi cerca tranquillità, consiglio le spiagge di Doc Let a un ora di auto e Cam Ranh a mezz’ora circa  perché non oso immaginare nel periodo di maggior affluenza quanta gente ci sia in spiaggia. Da  fine gennaio ad agosto  stagione secca ,sono i mesi migliori per una vacanza balneare con mare con onde dolci e possibilità di raggiungere le Isolette di fronte in barca ora era impossibile per noi .Comunque scenograficamente è molto bella , e nel periodo Natale ,Capodanno la città e  il lungomare sono un tripudio di festoni, luci , concerti , eventi .
Неплохой пляж, были и на центральной его части около Лотоса и на севере, за мостом, куда можно доехать на такси или автобусе.
久々に訪れたニャチャンbeachは相変わらず人が多い…みなさんが言う様にゴミ問題は深刻な感じで、以前はもっと綺麗だった様に思う。でも、ここから見る朝日は素晴らしい
The beaches in Nha Trang were way too crowded for our liking. Vendors were constantly pestering us to buy things, which ruined the relaxing vibe.

It is a nice stretch of beach to walk along and not at all, just which is nice. The water, however, just looked dirty and not very inviting. We hired two sunbeds with towels for 100k and were left in relative peacefulness with the occasional seller.
Beautiful long stretch of white sand beach framed against tall buildings. It is beautiful no doubt, but its city setting makes me wish for something a little more remote.

Good things -- quite cheap- hotel, food, transport, packages. Good destination if you are planning for a visa hassle free and low budget trip.- great coffee - the city is natural beautiful and the air is fresh.- Ba Ho Waterfall Hiking was amazing.- Amazing Vinpearl Land, its kind'a Disney land.- Clean cityBad things -- Our hand bag (with 2 iphone 14 pro max, money and keys) was stolen from the beach which was just by my side while I was sitting on the sand. This was an unfortunate experience. The city has hardly any CCTV and I felt too low security for tourists.- Hardly found anyone speaking English or anything written in English. Police wasn't active enough to help us or talk to us in English. - Hard to get good food.- local people are not as generous as other SEA countries.- island hopping packages were boring and not as expected.- most streets has no traffic lights, so people need to cross road just with a prayer that no one hits :P- should check out the cleanliness before eating sea foods from live fish shops. Unfortunately, we all got food poisoning. - Hot & humid weather
Pretty relaxing place with good waves. But the con is that the beach is filled with trash. The beach is close to the night market which is good.

Late evening walking 🚶 on the beach. Crowds out despite late hours. Passing famous clubs, sellers for food 🥘 and snack. Facing big hotel 🏨 building on other side of road, busy roads full of motorbikes. Better to try early morning with more peaceful atmosphere
Is a beautiful beach, with Covid is nearly deserted. A lot of the amenities and shops are not open, so if you want beach, it’s great. If you want action, not so much
Nice beach, crowded, overpriced sunbeds, big wavws, not clean, possible to wash with fresh water some places

I nice beach with a mix of private hotel beds and rental spaces.  Main problem is you have to cross the busy road which could be a problem for families and elderly.  There is a way to cross roads in Vietnam which is to walk slowly and let the bikes ride around you.  This can be quite scary.  Limited availability for crossing with traffic lights but the safeest way to cross to the beach

My husband and I choose to visit Nha Trang while we were staying nearby in Cam Ranh.Nha Trang does have a lovely beach to enjoy and the landscape/city reminded me very much of Bali or Thailand. But Nha Trang has a very seedy side to it. It is rife with scams lots of pickpockets and the rickshaw/tuk-tuk drivers are dreadful to be very aware. We negotiated a price to have a 30 minute ride each this is a rickshaw designed to fit one person so two needed the price was 200 dong about $12 Aussie dollar each nothing really. Once dropped off they said  $200 USA we were only in it for less than 30 mins. I refused to pay and they started a game of yelling we won't pay and made to look like the bad people. I also told them and refused to pay that. About 5 mins I threw the money of 200 dong and walked away. Since talking to others we have been told it is common. The scammers sit out the front of the shopping mall that all hotels shuttle buses get dropped off for a day shopping and come ask you - you want a ride. Day spas in Nah Trang can be scammers too. They take you to separate rooms and while they know the women have the handbag they lay you down on the table and someone sneaks in and takes your handbag. It is planned and once your belongings are gone their gone. I have traveled extensively through Vietnam and have found Nha Trang the worst. The Night market is small and all crap cheap stuff? The market took less than 10 mins to walk from end to end.
Sand was very gritty and hurt to walk on. Didnt spend much time here as the weather was overcast. There were some beds and restaurants along the beach.

We stayed in the north part of Nha Trang where the beach is attractive with good views of the bay. It is like Moscow by the sea with Russians posing everywhere. Sun beds are 100,000vdn which is excessive unless you are going to spend the whole day on the beach but obviously tourist prices. The waves can be rough so care is needed.

Long sandy beach. You must bear in mind when visiting Nha Trang that it’s a city type environment with a dual carriageway parallel to the beach. The road is a nightmare to cross. The sea is quite rough, there are waves. Not many bars on the beach, only an odd one. Most bars and restaurants are over the road. Men are hassled, including teenage boys if they go anywhere without there wife or Mam.

It’s an Okay beach. I think whether or not you like this beach depends on where you come from and what standards you have. In Australia we are blessed with amazing beaches everywhere! Nha Trang doesn’t hold a candle to any Australian beach. But by the sounds of it, it is a good beach in Vietnam and Asia. Worth a look if you are in Nha Trang. The beach would have to be one of the best things to do there. Because the rest of the city sucks.
The beach is clean the water is clean. The downside are the rocks on the ground, which make it difficult to jump inside the water.

If you are looking only look for fresh air, walking along a beach and listening to the waves it is a good place for one day. Do not expect more. On the other side of the beach are a lot of hotels which destroy the scenery.No must to do if you know better places.

Numerous hotels along beach and great place to walk in morning/ evening. Nha Trang is FULL of Russian tourist- signs, menus, etc. predominately cater to Chinese and Russians.  It’s good if you want beach time, and shopping. Plenty of good/ cheap seafood. But 4 days was too long for me. Check out my tips for Vietnam in the forums.

Nice beach nice restaurant but the thing is. Its hard to communicate to locals they dont speak english maybe some of them understand a bit your lucky. Communication is struggle i encountered in nha trang but to sum it up the place is wonderful
strong wind and continuous waves seem to batter the beach throughout my visit here. there are a walking trek along the beach and seem to be quite a good place to hang around
This would be a 5-star beach if it was not for the endless cigarette butts the local simply throw on the sand as they do on the street.
Nice enough beach, reasonably clean and maintained, busy earl of a morning, locals and chinese. Daytime full of Russian

Beach is not beautifull as it was on pictures. Somewhere You can find trash. Beach is absolutely overcrowded. Sometime I felt that we are in Crimea or Anapa, to mutch russian tourists.It is not so suitable for little childrens, waves can be really strongs, at least at the end of September. Water is warm.
I have visited the beach on two consecutive days, am and pm. Rubbish was seen on the shore and floating in the sea. Sad to see that.

In 9 years of going to Nha Trang and the main beach, the difference in the amount of junk and rubbish floating in the water this time is enough to stop you from ever going again! Time the city banned all plastics and other waste items from the beach and main streets. Either you are swimming in rubbish or walking on it. Something needs to be done. Vietnamese have a bad enough throw over your shoulder culture with any containers and waste, let alone all the tourists. I won't swim there again.

The beach is beautiful but quite dirty... It's sad to see the locals don't care for their trash, especially when they have such a nice beach right before them. So much plastics and other waste are just floating in the water, felt a bit disgusting
For small price you get bed and umbrella. Good swim quiet clean water. But I'm sure there are better places in Vietnam to enjoy the sea

A lovely beach but there was a festival when we were there and to my disgust the beach and sea got filled with plastic bottles and waste each evening.  It was cleaned overnight for the following morning.  I think it is a lovely location if people respect it.

We may be spoilt having travelled around Mexico and the UAE where the beaches are stunning, but this beach was pretty average.  We were so disappointed.  A lot of rubbish on the beach and it felt like we were in Russia.  A lot of restaurants, beach clubs etc had menus in Vietnamese and Russian and the places cater to them and their food choices a lot.  We are travelling around Vietnam and are here for four days.  If I could have changed my plans then I would have.  We had a lovely day out snorkelling and spent a fun afternoon at the Star club - a Russian beach club.  Other than that we were ready to leave.

We arrived for the first day in Nha Trang city with a lot of expectations for our beach vacation but a bit shocked by plenty of Chinese tourist groups (TG) and Russian TG as well seem to invade this city! It was going to be worse than what we thought due to we even had no normal beach atmosphere at all. Sadly, jogging along with the rest of avoiding crowded people could be impossible and we might pick a wrong time to travel, might we???At least we got a tiny chance to walk by seaside at noon when less people go out due to super hot and sunny .

Nga Trang beach is quite a long beach and it changes it's style as you head south , from being completely open with no trees to an area where you have trees running along the hedge of the beach with places to buy drinks and food. So in a way you have two different styles to choose from, being open or trees and coconut trees, which is my favourite part of the beach.
Nothing special about this beach - pretty enough, and mostly crowded with Russian tourists. Few beach bars, but stalls to get refreshments.
Beautiful beach great city to wonder around, interesting food and shops to explore. Don’t be afraid of the back alleys they are full of places to eat and drink.

It should be a nicer beach people don't care they just leave all their rubbish behind such a shame because the it's ruins the place. People will stop coming here if it continues. I won't be back for one

The beach is beautiful with white sand. The  beach offers swimming enclosures plus para sailing and boats are regularly seen in the water. The Vietnamese use the beach from around 4:30am to 7:00am for swimming and tai chi. They return at around 5:00pm.  It is very busy early and on weekends. Beautiful coastline.

We spent 4 weeks in this beautiful Resort, beautiful place but the best thing was the staff, kind, thoughtful and helpful, we felt pampered and well-liked.  The SPA is very nice and the girls are all special, we had the ladies Hanh and Huê, even Bâo was very good took care of my husband .. we carry them all in our hearts, we hope to come back soon and see you all.
It is a beautiful beach and bay, however is full of plastic bottles, lids cups, bags, etc. Both in the sand and water.

The beach in Nha Trang is clean, sandy and nice, that is it. We tried to go on a sea walking tour, guide was late and on a mini van with 10 seats he crowded 12 people and as we were still going to pick up another couple, where he would put them I have no idea, and were already running late for 25minutes we decided to get off the van so people standing could sit in our spot and to just cancel the tour. I will point out the guide lied to us - when we left one hotel he said we were going to the harbour but it turned out to be just to another hotel with already fully cramped van, he just forced more and more people on. Also the AC wasnt working so there was impossible to breathe with so many people in such a small van. After we went off while waiting for another couple while 2 people were already standing in a van, and wanted to cancel the tour and asked our money back the tour guide became very rude and screamed at us to get back on, we refused and left with our voucher from the hotel and then had to get upset again in the hotel to finally get our money back. We also wanted to go to a massage in Nha Trang but the good places are more expensive than in EU and I am not willing to pay more than I pay at home were the standards of service and hygine are a few levels better than here. We also hate getting a body massage where everyone who are walking down the street can look at you so which describes most of the massage places in Nha Trang, also I read reviews that most of them scam you with demanding high tips at the end like 100-150%more than the price of the service, if you refuse they just dont let you leave and scream at you. We made a mistake of letting some guy talk us in getting a pedicure (I cant remember the name of the spa) since after we also read that most massage places try to offer special service and dont really know what a real massage is, we gave up on getting a massage in Nha Trang. When we went in there were a lot of girls eating, the place smelled, the girl tried to force us to take off our clothes and leave our belongings in small closets, which we refused since you dont need to unstrip for a pedicure and also the place look shady inside so we didnt want to leave our stuff there. Then the nightmare only began, the girl tried to convince us a couple of times to get a massage instead of pedicure, then she took us up the stairs in a dark, red and very hot room with no windows or ventilation and the AC wasnt working. The room was filled with massage tables and the vibe was this is not a place to get a massage but they probably run some other service here. The girl probably had no idea what to do with us since we insisted on pedicure and refused a massage, she (like many spas here) probably doesnt even know what a pedicure is. She left and closed us in that room. Me and my boyfriend just look at each other and we knew what the other was thinking, we just got up and left, two girls tried to stop us to leave but we just went. After we read other reviews about spas in Nha Trang we realized this is common practice around here, they offer service which have no idea how to perform and then demand big tips, we were lucky no guy tried to stop us, just girls were screaming at us angrily while leaving, while others were not so lucky by the reviews we have read. If we knew before the reputation of Nha Trang we wouldnt have come here since there are better beaches and cities to visit on Vietnam coastline. The beach was clean, but the water was not so much, but better than in Halong Bay were the water is dirty, smells bad and trash are swimming around you everywhere. The prices in the city are similar than in Saigon.

If you're looking for a quiet and white sand beach than this is not really the beach for you.It's a nice beach but because of the great number of people on the beach every day it is a bit dirty. Cleaning up after yourself has to become a thing worldwide but here for sure.
nah trang beach the par that i visited by the sunrise hotel was always very windy and the sea was quite rough. apart from that it was a beautiful beach.

The ground crew were sensational and the airport was nice.it's the hot season and the flight was fully booked.   We got delayed about 15 minutes and the flight attendants handed out blankets to nursing mums in the expectation that the air-conditioning would kick in.the cockpit crew were all male and sitting in their isolated air-conditioned office and never turned ours on.it blew Luke warm air and even the locals were reaching for the air nozzles. Come on boys we actually pay your wages so you need to look after us.  I would have said something but it was only 50 minutes.MY ADVICE TAKE A FAN

We walked from the cruise port to the beach.On the way we cut up some side streets and saw some locals houses which were very interesting and quaint.The beach was very clean with a pagoda at the start then private apartments belonging to a hotel Got into trouble for sitting on the hotel wall to fix my shoe.The surf is very high and it slopes so a bit too dangerous for us to swim.There are flags up showing the safest areas.Lunch in The Louisiana Brewhouse which we had seen recommended on trip advisor.It was really good and a reasonable price.Nice beach for a walk.

Nha Trang beach is covered with sun beds, but be aware not just to take the first ones you see, since there is a difference in price and facilities, pick one with access to toilets and preferably also showers or other possibilities to rinse of, since the high waves in the ocean puts twirls the sand into places you do not want sand. Otherwise it is a nice beach to rest on for a day or two.

Okay sandy beach, however, it quickly becomes deep. The waves can be very stong, so do not know if I would call it child-friendly. If your hotel doesn't have sunbeds on the beach, you can rent some all day for 300,000 dong (we got it for 200,000). There are toilets on the beach, costing between 5-10,000 dong to use (not the best). Kiosks and cafés close by. The biggest minus is all those vendors, who are constantly trying to sell something, and appently everyone thinks you speak Russian.

There is one word that perfect defines the beach: “unremarkable”. It is a normal beach in a normal city. No breathtaking scenery or nature around, no turquoise seas. Just a nice place to go to. I cannot imagine Nha Trang being on anyone’s bucket list but it is perfectly fine to spend a couple of days if it fits your itinerary. It is clearly a preferred hang out place for Russians and Chinese. It is more common to get a Russian menu than an English one in most restaurants. The beach could be cleaner too. It is not terrible but at the same time, it would be so easy to fix it. Of course the best way would be to educate people not to use nor bring any plastic material near the beach. But that is a tall order. It would suffice for now if the local government (or some sponsor) would put big bins every 20 meters or so on the sand. It is amazing how much that helps.
What could have been a beautiful spot to relax got spoiled by trash left behind by tourists! We hope the local government can control it better to maintain the quality of the beach

Lovely views from the beach. Busy area, with locals and tourist. Not a beach I would want to relax on as there are so many tourist and it is not peaceful here. I visited for a day from my hotel in Cam Ranh, would not stay in this area as very touristy for my liking

This beach is OK but not so great. The beach/sand itself is a bit dirty (rubbish) and there are not so much places you can rent good beds. The ocean itself is also OK but not so great due to the rough sand. We preferred to stay at our hotel pool.
The beach is quite nice, but there is a little bit carbige on the beach. There are big waves every day (three weeks in january).
was there near the end of jan  waves made swimming imposible but diving through them was fun, not for kids, strong undercurrent going back out.

Huge beach with a variety of services. The Sailing Club is a nice place to eat and watch people. There are many locations for beach chairs, etc.  Lots of litter coming in from the sea, the beach businesses could work harder to keep things neater.

We ended our trip this time in dalat then Nha Trang and I was hesitant due to the stories of the place being overrun with Russian tourists. Our 5 star hotel was north of the city which ended up being a blessing in disguise. There’s more authenticity in the backstreets up thereFirst night we caught grab bikes into the main area and I was a bit horrified by the over commercialization and multitudes of hard faced Russians The next day we met locals and had an absolute blast hanging out and eating at a local place on the river over the next 2 daysAs an Australian it’s hard to rate the beach, small waves, and not real clear. I didn’t notice too much rubbish but would probably avoid it in the future. It does look quite nice thoughI suggest jumping on a scooter and heading north or south out of the mayhem and into more deserted beaches and authentic local places, Wal the backstreets and try to meet the local crew

The beach is big, the waves are big and unfortunately there's alot trash sloshing around in the water and on the beach. Due to strong waves my 8 yo didn't get very far into the water. Plenty of seating and places to eat. Still worth a visit.
The water is pretty polluted. There is quite a bit of pollution on the beach itself as well. All the deck chair services along the beach are pretty run down.

It’s a nice enough beach with plenty of options for renting sun beds with free towels and Wi-fi for around 50K a day each. However the waves are big and the drop in the water quite steep, you are up to your chest in about 5m. Not a beach for gently cooling off in the sea unless you want to be knocked down by the waves.

Beach was pretty, not so crowded and quite clean have many empty breaches around which weren't free for you to sit as people owned it will charge for a fee. Just hope that there were more activities or souvenir shops around to attract tourist rather than taking money for seats.

Nice beach - some rude Chinese tourists, would prefer to find the beaches that don’t have the low class tourists. Also, these beaches should have the trash picked up off the beach and into the water. Be careful walking in the water, must look out for aluminum cans and glass. Otherwise this is a nice beach, has potential.
Nice sunny beach, quite strong waves and good for surfing. Need to have more souvenir shops and other beach activities to attract more tourists.
A Lot of trash in the sand and water(Its Asia!) can be some walking to get some easy take away food down to the beach.
Maybe due to the hard rainstorm the last days too, much rubbish at the beach.At hotel beach areas it was more clean, but the water cleaness only soso.Beach chairs: 50.000 vnd/per day.

It was unfortunate that our first two days were very wet. The heavy rain caused the water at the beach to turn brown for quite some distance from the sand until it eventually changed to blue. Probably about 500 meters. The sand is clean and there are day beds that you can hire for a reasonable price. Some tourists on the beach were wearing swimming clothes that were totally inappropriate for them. They obviously didn't care how they looked. There are some nice restaurants right on the beach,  particularly the sailing club. Bit pricey but nice.

Very beautiful beach with lovely landscape and scenery. The beach is very crowded. Lots of sales lady going around trying to sell you something. Deck chairs and umbrella can be hired for a minimal sum.
Nice beach, but no facilities, such as showers . A few public toilets at the main beach strip in front of the 5 star hotels.Construction everywhere. Mainly Chinese and Russian tourists

Spent 4 nights in central Nha Trang Beach. First impressions were what an amazing beach. Warm water, clear sand, cheap beer and lounge chairs... we though how perfect. Then a tide change hit and so did a plume of waste and rubbish. We persisted however decided it was time get the kids out when a dead puppy washed up. Such a shame. We still went daily, just timed it between tide changes (easy to view when rubbish is coming in). Would still recommend.
Lovely beach. But lots of rubbish at high tide. Though this is typical of SE Asia.   Local council could spend on clearing.  Watch for over priced services

The beach along Tran Phu street is very difficult to predict with strong waves and kind of cliff sand under the water (like in 10m from the sandbank, you'll get the water over your chest), the kids can only play on the sandbank, if they play at the edge, the wave was to strong to push them down and if they go further, the sea was so deep to reach the sand with their feet. I particularly did not like this beach, but the long beach in Nha Trang is very different, clean, not crowded, ramp, nice waves. If you go to Nha Trang with your family, better chose the long beach.
Chinese people are everywhere and they stay at every corner in Nha Trang, especially the beach. I planned to swim but it turned out a "great" place to take photos

I'm used to Australian beaches and Nha Trang Beach just doesn't compare. Water is very calm, nice to swim or just enjoy, it is not a surf beach. All advertising promotes lovely clear blue water and white sand when in reality I found myself dodging the odd bit of litter in the water (plastic generally) and the sand is gritty.  You can pay around 50,000 Dong to use a sunbed under an umbrella for the day. Dont use the ones near Central Park as they will charge you around 300,000 Dong. There is some litter about as I and others have said, but on the sand I noticed mostly that this was bagged up by the day bed operators.

It’s a decent enough beach. Nha Trang is a place with a lot of tourist activity so there’s always going to be a bit of rubbish knocking about. That said, it wasn’t an obscene amount. The water is fairly clean, though not so clear, but great for cooling off after a hot and humid day!

Nha Trang is set in a beautiful natural environment. Long sweeping beaches and mountains down to the sea. A bay of sparkling waters and off shore islands to explore. Unfortunately man is to blame for the pollution both in the water and on the beaches. Lovely yellow/white sand littered with plastic cups, plastic spoons, plastic bags and cigarette buts! The water was warm and easy to swim in with very little wave movement in July when we visited. However the beach is most crowded in the late afternoon, early evenings. A great place to visit for sunrise and an early morning swim before the crowd arrives and the sun gets too hot!
Nice beach , clean water, facilities available to eat drink and shaded areas. Also access to places with pools for a price.
The beach is long and always crowded with people. There are a lot of water sports going on and small food stall along the beach. Go for fresh coconut for only 20000VND.

Nha Trang you disappointed me! The visit left me sad for the local Vietnamese people as their city is being taken over by Russians. It lacks soul and has lost its identity. Many businesses owned and ooerated by Russians which totally spoilt the vibe. If I wanted a Russian holiday I’d go to Russia! The beach did not disappoint however, it was glorious, as did the visit to Mini Beach and the Marine Park protected area.

The beach is not the cleanest beach I have been to and there is also a lot of seaweed in the sea and on the sand. There are many nice restaurants around the beach however, they are very expensive. Me and my partner also booked a speed boat tour to go see all the white sand beaches near by which the company shows very pretty photos of... we turned up at the first beach and it was very dirty, no sand, and very small (nothing like the images they showed). On the bright side, there is a very nice open pool with good sun beds near by and can also access the beach.
The beach here is flat, no waves, mostly clean and across a busy road. Not great compared to Australian beaches but ok I guess. Reminds me of Balmoral Beach after a storm when the water is dirty

The beach in the city did not really satisfy me cos it was too crowded (perhaps cos it's summertime). I headed to another less visited beach to the south of the city beach. It's much better here. And the seafood was much cheaper, also.

There are hordes of china tourists everywhere, so many that the local businesses have adapted to learning to speak and bargain in Mandarin. There are shop signs and menus all in chinese.I was quite appalled.If you can turn a blind eye to the China tourists, Nha Trang beach is actually very pretty and clean.Lines of perfectly manicures trees line the beach, in al shapes that you can think of. It is really pretty! The sun rises magnificently over the waters in the wee hours before the china tourists awake, that is probably the nicest sight in Nha Trang.

The beach seems clean, but the sea has a lot of rubbish, feel sad that people travel here bring back their memory, but didnt bring back their rubbish.Beach chair is free if ur hotel has private section. If not, u have to pay a small amount that can enjoy it for whole day. But all beach chairs are charge after 4pm of 430pm (not sure)
