Beautiful beach and park next to it, problem is garbage in the morning and evening. Local people are careless and leave all the picnic leftovers on the beach even there's few garbagebins around...not many. So sad specially after some pig holidays or events.

Usual beach.. quite steep shoreline.. usual.rubbish left everwhere by the locals and visiting Vietnamese. Loads off Russians.. this is no problem..they are great people.. Just the waste left around spoils the beach.. ocean poloution is a massive problem for Vietnam.. plastic bahs and waste washing around all the time when you try to swim.. shame ..
this is a nice beach and close to the city but it is bit dirty with rubbish thrown all over. but you do find the cleaners clearing the rubbish. need to effort to make it a better beach.

The beach is Nha Trang is great to look at, lovely and warm water with fairly flat seas, unfortunately it has heaps of plastic bags, plastic bottles and assorted garbage floating in the water.This was a real turn off for us and means that we are not going to hurry back.
Nha Trang Beach is picture perfect.The water is clear and warm but full of rubbish.Standing in the water plastic bags, cups etc are floating around.Still worth the visit and the swim.

As the beach is surrounded by hotels, you can easily reach the beach at any moment. There are thousands of umbrellas set up by the hotels near by (you’ll have to pay to rent the seats though), you can spend all day on the beach if you wish! Just a minor reminder, the toilets along the beach required some fees. Other than that, it’s a great beach for sunbathing!

Gets very crowded with all sorts of tourists however if swimming is not your main activity, could be nice to lau on one of the many sunbeds available during the day. Beach is average with quite an amount of rubbish on the shore and in the water which is unfortunately a bit too common in Vietnam! With minimal upkeep, this beach would be beautiful but unfortunately, tourists and locals didn’t seem too bothered by it.

This is a very popular beach where the crowds gather in the summer from morning until night. The fresh food from the lady with the bbq that she carries is fantastuic and definitely worth a try. Cheap as well

Our family has been travelling the last 2 months through SE Asia, and were excited about Nha Trang. We had heard "best beaches in Vietnam". The city is large, noisy and impersonal. So much construction going on. The beach was OK. Although the water is clean and beach is large, the overall experience was of disappointment. In hindsight, we would have stayed longer in Hoi An and skipped Nha Trang altogether... Trip Adviser recommendations for restaurants didn't seem genuine, so beware.

Reminded me of a dirty version of the Gold Coast what with plastic bags flying everywhere. The beach is pretty decent, the water is hot but when we were there pretty dirty probably due to the wind and current. I suspect it would be great on a calm day. Enjoy......
The beach is ok, sun bed with an umbrella is 50K VND/person. During the day it's not crowded, but in the evening it's full of locals. Water is very warm.

Just like some hotels, our hotel had a section on the beach. That part of the beach is called Sunny Beach and is right in the centre of Nha Trang. A long stretched and wide beach with powdery sand. Our hotel provided  sunbeds, parasols and towels. So we didnt have to pay. But  sunbeds and parasols are also rented out by other beach exploiters. If you rent or buy something to drink or to eat from them, you get a wifi code. The signal is strong. Be aware that beaches in Vietnam are free of charge. You only pay for sunBeds or parasols.On this beach there are real and improvised beachbars. The restrooms are subterranean, clean and free. There are showers on the beach. Vendors are trying to sell you a lot of things. Some are pushy, some are not. There are  trash cans and the bech is clean. The little dirt you see is dirt that is thrown into the sea and has washed up on the beach.Its a pity that the water was so cold and the waves so high and wild. But that didnt stop many people from going into the water (brrrrrrrrr). Dont expect clear seawater like the Caribbean, The Seychelles or The Maldives. Its not clear but clean. Sometimes Its also windy on the beach, but when the sun shines it feels like a fresh breeze. But if there no sun, its chilly. There are lifeguard and there are rescue stations.luckily that there are no beach and water activities, such as jetski, bananaboat, para sailing ect ect. That keeps the beach relax and nice. Although the nearby beachclub "Sailing Club" offers  kite surfing and diving. The beach and the city of Nha Trang is flooded  by Russians and chinese from the Mainland. At First i was wary about the presence of Russians . Because i have seen their antisocial behaviour  on Cyprus, Turkey and in Phuket. But they behaved well. If you are on the beach you can see  Vinpearl in the distance.. The amusement park and resort. In the evening its nicely illuminated.  There is also a park with palm trees and benches next to the beach.  After 17.00pm the beach is frequented by locals. They stroll on the beach, in  the park and  on the long boulevard. The Tram Huong Tower, that iconic building is also on the beach. Next to that building is a square. In the evening there are a lot of kite runners  on that square. Very nice to see..

The area of Nha Trang coastline is beutiful with views of islands  out to sea and mountains in the distance , as faras the beach goes it is well organized  by the major hotels that all seem to have a designated  area of beach in front of them to set up beds and umbrellas which believe me you will want to be under one if you are thinking of spending some serious beach time as you can burn very quickly in Vietnam. If you can weave your way through the russian tourists it really is a lovely beach , just be careful swimming their as the swell breaks right onto the beach so definitely  no bodysurfing . As usual though their are plenty of lovely spots all along the coast you can get a car and driver for around 400,000 vnd a day who will be more than happy to show you around , my friend and i absolutely loved Nha Trang .
beach is cut up by the hotels and resorts slopes off into deep water quite early on very salty, not the cleanest sun searing hot would recommend factor 50 sand too hot to walk on
Long stretch of beach.Pretty from a distance.Play area for children which is good.Very busy with tourists.Lots of litter on beach.Very strong waves so must be careful if swimming.Would not go again.
A nice long beach across from street and hotels. One can walk beach path for a few kilometers. Lots of coffee shops across the street.
What a shame that this beach is so riddled with plastic pollution.  I hope as awareness gets better and we clean up our oceans beaches like this can return to their former glory

A lovely beach.. the sea and sand very clean. Great atmosphere around the bars and restaurants that scatter the front. Beach towels and sun beds readily available for hire at very reasonable cost. Nice day out..however full of Russians.. we thought we were actually in Russia .. hardly any locals apart from bar staff.

Arriving from the beautiful cultural town of Hoi An, Nha Trang was like vinegar to my eye balls.A combination of Benidorm, Dubai, Moscow, Beijing, Bangkok & Patong all in one. It’s a city with a beach. Half the city is currently under construction with monstrous high rise hotels being built. In 5 years time you will not know you’re in Vietnam.95% of tourists here are Russian or Chinese. No problems with the Russians as they were generally polite & well behaved. A high proportion of the Chinese however were ignorant, loud, lacking personal hygiene & lacking spacial awareness. The real upside to Nha Trang was the Bars & restaurants. Not one bad meal. Every night a great meal. That rarely happens on holiday. Would I come back? Definitely not. A real shame but this is place is the down side of mass tourism capitalism at the cost of tradition & culture.

The beach was clean enough. We paid 100,000Dong, roughly £3 for two sun loungers and an umbrella and they were so comfortable. There were people selling food, glasses and handbags etc. But they didn't hassle you or even ask really, just walked by unless you showed interest.

OK for an early walk. Current was reasonably strong in the sea. Windy after 11am and sometimes before. Unpleasant with wind blowing sand . Apparently it is like this every day of the year. Long beach at resort strip was worst. Impossible to enjoy sitting outside at the Anam Sports Bar? So we didn't have our free cocktail every afternoon after the first day! In the city it was very touristy and still with an unpleasant wind.

Overall, the beach is relatively clean in the morning as there was cleaning staff who try hard to sweep and keep it clean throughout the day. The issue is there are way too many inconsiderate tourist who intentionally throw their litters everywhere, while there are many trash cans readily available on the beach. I hope people would be more considerate and be mindful of keeping the beach nice and clean, so everyone can enjoy.

Naturally beautiful beach, plenty of sun beds with access to bars to purchase for about 35000-50000D. Or relax on a towel on the sand. Wind seems to get up in afternoons, so earlier morning the better. Also ridiculous high rises shadow the beach from 2/3pm. Has a busy, not peaceful vibe as many tourists, mostly Russian and Chinese. 3 stars given due to sad state of beach particularly at tide line. Lots of plastic bottles, packets and bags litter the beach, so you do end up swimming with it as well. It would be great if all those hotels (Sheraton, Novotel, Diamond exception was Premier who were making an effort) capitalising from selling the sun beds would put their staff to use to tidy up the tide line in their area instead of waiting around to pounce on you for cash as soon as your butt hits one of their sun beds. All in all though, we had a good time in Nha Trang! Worth the visit.

I’m Australian so we are spoilt for beautiful sandy beaches...that said, the water was too shoot for our toddlers and the sand here was so rough with tiny pieces of shell and littered with trash.  Is beautiful from a distance though. Lovely to lay on the sun beds under an umbrella and drink beers/cocktails though (you know, if we didn’t have toddlers to chase).

It was not what i expected of a Vietnamese beach. No pittoresque place. Crowdy and main language is Russian. Beach is clean, and you can hire 2 chairs and parasol for 200000 dong. Surrounding view is good.
The beach have lovely white fine sand but unfortunaately is full of plastic cup/bottles and litters lying around.  It is very commercial now compare to 8 years ago when we first came.

This beach could be the Gold Coast Australia, or Waikiki Hawaii. It's a very nice beach that has managed to loose all the things that is great about Vietnam. We left this town the day after we arrived.

Nha Trang was what you would expect from many beach tourist destinations. It lacked the culture and local feel that we found in other towns in Vietnam. I would recommend spending more time in Hue or Hoi An.
The beach is clean but so close to the mainroad of Nha Trang, that it’s too loud all day. No toilets, so you have to,go back to your hotel or to a restaurant.

Visited here due to it is just right opposite of our hotel. The beach is dirty and overcrowded, not to say racist but they were 99 percent of Russians on the beach instead of local people where the locals were there to earn money from the tourists. Bad experience where the vendors are really pushy as they know you're tourists and they seemed like trying to take advantage. Too touristy.
Nice beach . Like alot of asian countries you need to be careful of the touts. Be very aware of picknpockets etc at nite
The beach itself is nice but the location is awkward- just next to a very busy main road, so it's noisy. But I guess some people like that.

There are so many nice places, resorts and beaches in Vietnam but this one you could easily miss unless you like hoards of Chinese and Russian tourists. We did a boat tour out to the fish farms which was interesting but trying to avoid loud, rude Chinese tourists meant we didn't really enjoy it too much.

Lots to do, nice long beach. Didn't get a Vietnam vibe, was like any other resort beach.Lots of cars and motorcycles, hard to cross the road to get to the beach.Some very nice luxurious hotels, easy to find a cheap one if you want.Was okay for a few days.
It is not comparable to tropical beaches.The water is not transparant.... But the Sand is very clean, the area very spacious and everything is very organized.

It isn't the best beach you can find in Vietnam. With all the huge hotels just on the other side of the road you feel like you could be in Spain or in any other developed country. It is next to a busy road which has its pluses - many shops, bars and restaurants but if you are looking for a quiet, relaxing spot this isn't the place.

Sad to say there was a lot of rubbish washing up on the shore. Whilst there are ample public rubbish bins everywhere it was visibly noticeable that a lot of Russians, Chinese and local Vietnamese were happy to throw their rubbish on the ground. Disgusting habit. Generally speaking this area has a good vibe and alfresco dining places on the beach front (Sailing Club, Louisiane Brewhouse) are good quality and cheap.

Visited the beach with my family in December and the wind was too strong for us to even take a walk at the beach. The wave was too big, strong wind and cold weather. Not recommended during end of year.
I love to swim in the ocean, but it has been forbidden during my stay in Nha Trang. Great views, though. I wonder whether they allow swimming during other times of the year.

This was in December and it wasn't too hot or humid. The water felt great. The waves were treacherous though. I don't have a picture, but there are signs Tha say no swimming, which I saw later. Nha Trang is really nice. Full of Russians. Like packed with Russian tourists.
I like the free sports equipment in the parks. Long boardwalk is nice off season. Big waves but nothing unforgettable. It’s starting to be over-built.
Beach is pretty wide and cool. But the river nearby makes the water very dirty, it colors water in broken color and brings a lot of garbage
I think I went in a wrong season.... the beach is so wavy and not suitable to swim. The weather is also not good for swimming as well. Recommend not go there late Oct and Nov.

We came to Nha Trang beach in October and the beach looked rough and murky. The pictures that ive seen of Nha Trang beach is beautiful but nothing like real life, was disappointing. Im sure its beautiful in the right season but during October it didnt look very nice unfortunately.

We visited nha trang beach in a private beach as part of the anam resort we stayed at. This part of the beach was pristine and the water very inviting. We loved it here. But when we walked further up the beach - outside the private beach and saw a lot of rubbish along the beach and fishing nets. This was quite disappointing.

Not bad overall.  I guess the Surfers Paradise strip of vietnam. The water isn't as great as you may expect but warm and emerald green all the same. Not too much pressure selling from beach hawkers either
Conveniently located in the city centre, but the quality of the water and sand were not so amusing.  Good for a walk but won't recommend for swimming.

Although a nice beach to walk along amd enjoy the breeze, I wouldn't say it lives up to it's reputation as one of the top beaches in Vietnam. The couple of days I visited the sea is a little rough, with a strong under current probably not safe for small children to be swimming in. Sadly the is also a lot of litter.The beaches at Hoi An and Phu Quoc are nicer in my opinion.

The water is not the cleanest of this place, but the view is great. There are plenty of umbrellas on the beach that you can read, and there are quite a few Russians and Chinese people. Much better beaches in either direction, but the speech is convenient because it is by most of the hotels.

This beach is a very standard beach, not exotic but not filthy. The sand is quite coarse, therefore we rented sunbeds. If you walk along the beach to the quieter areas you can rent these much cheaper than in the busy spots.
The beach is not crammed with people, but a bit dirty. It is the same in the water as well. I have seen better beaches..:(

Lovely place to spend the day in the sun, we got 2 sunbeds for the day and some drinks for 40,000d which seemed reasonable.Very close to tall buildings on the strip which block out the sun and loud building works disturbed the peace of the day.
Very nice beach for all kinds of activity. But too hot to swim during the mid of the day. So good to have a long walk and chilling with beers!Kids love to play and stay there for hours.
This beach was hyped up on travel sites so I guess I expected betterIt was just a pretty average beach and a bit overwhelmed by deck chairs and umbrellasOn the plus side we didn't get hassled much!
No facilities at the beach to wash your feet or bath despite it is one of the most tourist packed beaches in
Sand is fine and golden and in most places is kept clean.  Plenty of sun beds available to rent.  Sea is full of litter so not recommended for swimming.

Whilst the sand is fairly white, the beach is incredibly touristy with Sun loungers and activities spread across. Full of Russian and Chinese tourists. It's nice for a refreshing dip though. Desperate for some shade we paid 100,000dong for two sunloungers, which was cheaper than what they were charging for Russian tourists! If you like sunbathing and don't mind crowds then it's a good enough beach.

Sea is pretty blue and there is space enough but to be honest there are more beautiful spots in vietnam Especially places with less waste.Not a review but simply fact there's a huge amount of Russian tourists at the beachNot a complete bad choice there but in my opinion Hoi An has more beautiful and cleaner spots for staying at the beach

This was a massive let down. The beach is literally over crowded with tourists who happen to be Russian. Really not worth going to - would skip if I did it again. Water is dirty and just not pleasant!!
This beach belongs to a bay, so it's not very airy and windy. You may have to use boating to the island for better experience. Service is quite poor but expensive.

I know Australians are lucky with our beaches, but the sand felt dirty and I questioned whether to put my head under water. The day beds we hired on the beach felt like they needed a good wash. It was fine but I preferred my hotel pool while in Nha Trang. Go to Long beach instead.
A long reasonably nice protected beach, calm water seemed to suit a lot of familys with kids. Downside, a lot of tourists seem to think that the beach is a great place to leave cigarette butts.

Whilst the beach is pleasant enough for a swim in a hot day it can get very crowded with large groups of Russians. Many beach chairs and umbrellas to rent for 40000 dong for the day.Beach areas in front of resorts are kept fairly clean. The water quality varies according to the tides there can be rubbish coming across from Vinpearl resort.Large afternoon's the Russians are replaced by large groups of noisy Chinese tourists and trash can be a problem.With so much construction nearby and a busy road there is a lot of noise so don't expect a quiet tropical locale unless you go very early morning.Much nicer beaches can be found elsewhere.
The beach is a nice place to visit , really hot though, need to hire umbrellas & chairs etc , along the beach is a lovely promenade perfect for picnic & a lovely stroll 👍

The water was far from crystal clear, it's busy and I swam once for the sake of it. There are umbrella chairs for rental everywhere, I wouldn't leave anything unattended although I suspect the city has done a massive clean up since last time I visited 10 years ago.

a lot of new construction works and buildup swallow sea views from the streets.It used to be a pleasure riding a bike a long Tran Phu road at any time of day, not anymore. Now the city is flooded with Chinese or Russians depending on time of years. Not the old Nha Trang town anymore.

It's not the best beach in the world. It's very big, but a lot of it is close to the road so even though you don't see vehicles, you will hear them. On top of that, there's construction a long the whole beach it seems with new buildings rising up. It's great to see a city thriving, it's not so great with all the noise it brings with it when your trying to relax on the beach.The sand here is nothing special, it's not white, more brownish color, full of trash. The ocean itself has the occasional plastic bag floating around, not so attractive.All in all, this beach wasn't anything special. I won't come back for the beach and I get the feeling many would agree on that with me. It's ok for a day or two of sunbathing, but not more than that.

Not my cup tea as I have come from beautiful Australian beaches and prefer the beach to be a little less touristy, but for those with limited beach experiences I can see the allure.Plenty of beach lounges (ranging in prices from 30-60k for day lounge/towels/wifi). Water is warm and a for those who don't like to fight the waves this is perfect.

The only reason it didn't earn a few more stars is that the water seemed a bit dirty and the guys that run the rental chairs are not the most friendly fellows in the world. Other than that, it's a great beach.

Not sure where the first foto is from but it isn't nha trang beach.  No greenery here. Only green stuff is floating in the sea.  Local authorities have to make an effort to clean more and obviously since the visibility is zero there is serious pollution here.  Quite amazing really.  If this is not sorted out soon the hotel you book better have a nice pool
Beach s dirty and overcrowded with Russian tourists.It is ok if you are aiming for classic beach holiday, less satisfying if you are up for a tranquille hidden spot for yourself.
Beach dirty. Water brown. Gondola fabulous! Vin pearl overrated.  Great dinner at sailing club. Lovely esplanade.

This beach is alright. When the weather is nice it is beautiful. The sand is nice and the sea water is clean. You can get sun beds for around 50 000 vietnamese dong which is reasonable. My biggest problem is that the beach is really overcrowded with tourists and there are lots of vendors walking around. This detracts from the vibe a lot.

Nice long beach but cleanliness was an issue. Lounge chairs and catering services are available along the beach. Going in the late afternoon would be a recommendation to avoid any burns especially during the month of July. There's a walkway along the beach which is nice for a sunset walk. Worth a visit.
The beach itself is nice but very crowded. Not too many people trying to sell you things and those that do are not too persistent.

After 2 years of travelling to several SE Asia beaches and a second month-long visit to this one, I offer a revised review and detailed assessment here for others.The long arc of beach is a very attractive asset for the city, fronting its entire length from the southern tourist area up to the river. Know though, that the northern areas closer to the river may be far less crowded, but the beach and prominade are noticeably neglected and less attractive. In the day time, the entire beach is relatively uncrowded, being populated mosty by the Russian tourists willing to brave the sun and heat. There seems to be plenty of lounge chairs and umbrellas available for rent, as well as abundant room to lay out a towel for free space - without feeling crowded. Between 4 and 5 pm though, the scene changes as swarms of Chinese tourist groups and local Vietnamese hit the water. This is a time of super crowds, noise, and frenzy - quite opposite of the tranquil day time scene.The beach sand is nice enough, but only average - being tan and moderately course, versus DaNang's to the north which is whiter and softer. And the water? With each rain, the river dumps its sediment load into the ocean, and from my 15th floor apartment, I watched this event happen several times. The entire bay and shoreline area would turn from pretty aqua-marine to churned up brown. Even without this though, I found the water to be only average at best - being clear enough to see where you're stepping, but not impressively clean or clear. During my swims, I did notice less floating trash and jellyfish compared to last year, but that's probably a seasonal factor too.The beach has a very steep shelf of clean sand, forcing most people to stay near the shoreline. During the day when the beach is lightly used, this is no problem. But in the late afternoon and evening when the hoards come out to swim and play, it makes for a packed area near the waterline. Know too that I saw the lifeguards actually working unlike most SE Asia beaches. During the day time, it seems that people can swim anywhere, regardless of the signs. But by late afternoon, the lifeguards kick into enforcement mode and force people to stay within the crowded, roped swimming zones. In May/June, I found the water to be acceptably warm enough even for my skinny body, with mornings being calm and afternoons offering wave action. Overall, it seems safe for all, despite the strong winds off shore that provide daily entertainment for the kite surfers.Besides sunbathing and swimming, there's an active presence of parasailing rides being offered late in the day, but only a few banana boats and jet skis. This, in my view, speaks well for the beach - unlike the madness to be found on Langkawi's beach. As for touts, yes they troll the beach, but not in huge or annoying numbers. Mostly, they set up spaces for kites, play toys, or food, offering sales only when you approach. There's regulatory signs well posted that limit many things, with some being reasonable and others being questionable - like walking on the grass. In any case, the beach is off limits to pets and organized sports play, and there seems to be only 2 or 3 volleyball net areas, with the best nightly viewing being 5 minutes south of the Sailing Club. I was pleased to see a water sports rental vendor providing snorkelling gear, paddle boards, and sea kayaks, though their quality is marginal and overall, it's not the best conditions for any of these sports here. Half of the beach environment entails the large, highly developed, prominade which consists of numerous walkways, sitting benches, exercise equipment stations, small food and drink vendors, and at least 3 major restaurants with attractive outdoor seating - including the famous Sailing Club of course. The work put into making and maintaining this prominade area is impressive, in large part to the extraordinary landscaping of attractive palms and artificially pruned trees. Really, it's more hedge cutting labor than necessary, but the result is neat and worthy of some photos as well as daily admiration. Within this long strip of man-made and natural wonder, you can also find a lot of abstract art features, an artificial stream area, and some large, open brick plaza areas that attract occasional events and crowds. These open areas also are well used by the various kite flyers, roller bladers, skate boarders, and new stand-up 2-wheelers.The prominade area, while very attractive, gets limited use during the hot mornings and afternoons. Despite the abundant number of trees, there's simply too much of the main walkway that's not shaded. For those hitting the beach or wanting to exercise on open walkways or equipment, this can be a convenient time to use the area, albeit at the price of some serious sun exposure and sweating. Otherwise, by 4-5 pm, the entire prominade, like the beach, comes alive. It's an interesting and pleasant time to go for a walk, do some exercise, or just sit on a bench and watch all of the activity (and chaos occurring at the waterline from crowds of swimmers). As for practicalities, know that there are numerous bathrooms along the prominade. The nice one on the south end charges a small fee and the dispersed outhouses, though free, are pretty disgusting. Your best bet is to look for the WC signs that point to the underground facilties. They're clean and free. There are also a few free individual semi-private dressing rooms. The shower situation is less clear, with some of the primitive stations seeming to be free and others being manned by people who probably collect a fee. In all facilities, you'd best have your shoes or flipflops on, given their gross conditions. Motorcycles and bicycles are managed pretty effectively by limiting them to specific, fee use, parking areas. There's also an information building located south of the monument tower - a great place to pick up a free city map if you don't find one at your hotel.  For swimwear packing, know that the entire beach scene is pretty casual and liberal for both men and women of all ages and sizes. And while there does seem to be a large number of naked children, there's definately no topless women walking around or nude sunbathing areas. It's a pretty tame and safe scene of mostly Russian, Chinese, and Vietnamese tourists and families. What's not provided is a safe way of crossing the street to and from the beach prominade. Most of the time it is merely an intimidating, annoying task of waiting for a semi-gap in the traffic. At rush hour though, it's an extremely frustrating and dangerous prerequisite for gaining access to the city's prime recreational area and asset. With no gap, one must resort to playing chicken with the vehicles and literally stepping out in front of them in order to force them to stop. During this visit, I had 3 very close, near misses, confirming that its not a mere cultural nuance, but an inexcusable hazard being neglected by the incompetent city managers.   In summary, the cleanliness of the beach and prominade is pretty good compared to many third world beach environments. There's people littering some, but it's the exception rather than the norm, and the litter is being cleaned up constantly by chair vendors and a large number of city workers (thank you). The beach and water are not world class gorgeous, but it's a nice beach and prominade that's been developed attractively and is worthy of a tourist visit to the city. It should and could be a 4 star beach attraction, but I've reduced it a notch specifically because of the dangerous street crossing access AND the excessive horn honking from the adjacent street that degrades the overall beach and prominade environment noticeably. Apparently, the same rude drivers that completely disregard pedestrian crossings likewise feel that constantly honking one's horn is an essential requirement for driving.
I was told that Nha Trang had great beaches.  Not so much.  Trash everywhere.  I'll stick with Back Beach Vung Tau.  Much cleaner and the weather is nicer.
There were many activities around the beach and the sunrise was wonderful. However the beach was dirty and you had to pay for the sunbeds.
The 6 km beach and the water is just beautiful, but what a shame that the beach is so dirty!!You have to pay for a sunbed and it starts from 50.000dong.

The beach itself is quite pleasant but nearer the city the beach is crowded with tourists and sunbeds which personally I'm not a fan of. If you are looking for quieter areas you have to walk very far down the beach or can go to other quieter beaches in the surrounding area. The view from the beach is really nice and there are lots of nice restaurants near by.

Beautiful long beach with golden sand. However, cleanliness was pretty bad. Food packaging, cigarette stubs, plastic bottles everywhere on the beach and in the water. Really off putting. Shame as it's a lovely beach, great weather too.You can get a sunbed for as little as 30,000 dong, negotiate. Quite a few sellers throughout the day will approach you too.
Would be nice if there was more harmony between human use and the needs of nature. Big flood lights and rubbish all over the sand and in the water, but at least they attempt to control the rubbish

Nha trang we found was quite a hectic city, but the beach was nice, with lots of options for getting a lounger quite cheap (negotiate!) view out into the see was nice, and water was clear and clean on our 2 first days but found quite a lot of rubbish floating around on our last, maybe to do with tides? Otherwise a very busy beach, taken over mostly by Russians, as is the whole city!!
A stunning beach and the cabanas were comfortable but we were at a resort and it was really hard to get service! Should have just gone to one the the much cheaper places down the beach.

The beach itself could be a wonderful beach l, definitely one of the best I visited in Vietnam if it wasn't for the rubbish everywhere. It just seemed that most people visiting didn't care and you are constantly seeing cans and food rubbish everywhere including in the sea. Really let down what could be a great beach.

It is ok for a couple of days but service can be improved, the guys renting those sunbeds need a bit of training and cleanliness deserves more attention since it is a busy destination. Sometimes water flooded until the sumbeds and wet around some holiday makers, need to be fixed, but all in all ok.Go in the morning or early afternoon as it gets super busy with locals after 5 as they do not like to stay under the sun. After 6/7 it is full as hundreds of them pic nic on the beach and swim (and unfortunately leave a lot of rubbish which gets cleaned by a track during the evening/night)

Nice beach , with interest , warm water for swimming .Too much rubbish on the beach though , locals need to respect their beach and environment . It's not ok to have plastic bags and bottles floating in the water .
Good for beach also worth visiting are po nagar temple and long son templeFor church freaks there is a nice cathedral with an asian vertoon of a virgin part statue
its an ok beach, long, could be cleaner and have more things to do and see, suggestion be like Hawaii and have banana boats or parasailing behind a boat.
Average beach, not clear water , lots of people. Worried about our belongings as always told people steal things around this area

The guidebooks seem to tote Nha Trang Beach as one of the best beaches Vietnam has to offer. Dont get me wrong, it's nice. There is a beach bar for drinks and for $70 000 you can buy two sun-chairs and an umbrella. You might be able to get them cheaper if you barter better than we did. The water is a nice temperature an it's a nice view of the city skyline from the shore. But all the development and pollution has taken its toll and there was a lot of litter on the beach and in the water. Not enough that it wasn't possible to swim but enough that it was a bit icky. It was also fairly crowded and busy. There is a water play-structure area that was cordoned off by rope. The rope extends through the water and it's really rough and course with sharp small barnacles sticking too it. It was so sharp that I got a few decent cuts on my ankle when I scraped up against it. I did hear talk of another beach a half hour outside of the city that might be better. Nha Trang is definitely a nice beach city with great seafood, decent diving, nice views and good shisha places. But if you are just in it for the beach, I would suggest to skip Nha Trang and head strait to Hoi An. Their beach is much nicer, less polluted and a lot less crowded.

We enjoyed walking along this long beach but I did not go  in the water. Currents far too strong for my swimming ability. I like the exercise stations they had set up on the beach. Lots of people out there in the early morning running, swimming, walking, doing  tai chi etc.

This beach has so much potential to be a perfect place. It is so frustrating and disappointing to see tourists throw garbage in the beach. I witnessed a family throwing their empty juice boxes and soda cans in the water and watch it float away. Just pathetic that people do this.
There are so many other very exciting beaches in Vietnam - this would not be at the top of my "must see list" - actually very disappointing.

Long stretch of sand. Water is warm. Unfortunately no waves. There is pollution floating in the water - plastic, etc. Not much shade unless you get a beach lounge. They are about 35000 dong for a day. Lots of colourful beach vendors selling cooked corn, sweet bread, beer, sunglasses, lobsters. Nice to relax and watch the day go by.

Nha trang beach is beautiful but the city of Nha Trang has unfortunately been taken over by unfriendly Russians. I had read about it but wasn't prepared for how bad it really is. If you're wanting to see the 'real' Vietnam, don't even bother visiting this place. We were sad we wasted 3 days here.
It's okay for a short visit. But Nha Trang as a whole lacked Vietnamese touch... I would personally avoid this part of vietnam.

I was looking forward to Nha Trang as I heard of its beautiful beaches and surrounds. However, if you are after a beach getaway in Vietnam I would instead recommend Phu Quoc Island. I felt Nha Trang was full of Russian tourists and lacked the individuality that other Vietnamese towns and cities hold. It was actually hard to find "local" cuisine or a restaurant that didn't have Western or Russian food. Most of the restaurants and signs have Russian names or translations. Obviously they get a lot of tourism from that side of the world but for someone wanted to experience Vietnam I was disappointed.

Not worth to visit nha trang beach dirty with no much activities and salesman every where try to rip you off and crowded Russian and Chinese tourists and its hard to find good food in here I stayed for a week first two day was amazingly boring as a solo backpacker then I decided to get an open water diving course (padi) and its saved my vacation otherwise it's not worth to visit nha trang there's so many places much better in Vietnam.

This beach is just a beach and nothing more, nothing less! There are a couple of expensive restaurants and pubs called Louisiane and Sailing club. They have pretty poor quality when it comes to drinks except the beer on Louisiane that is brewed locally. The food is overcharged but service fair.

The beach itself is not so bad but it is forbidden to play ball, frisbee, etc., there are too many vendors on the beach, there is a lot of plastic and other garbage in the sea, and the main street next to the beach is very noisy with cars and motorbikes honking constantly. It doesn't make for a very relaxing or pleasant beach holiday.

I didn't come to swimming or sun bathing here. Just to enjoy the view. Nice because there are so many chair near the beach for everyone to just sit and enjoy the good weather. We can see mountains across the ocean. Many locals come here on the evening. I like it. The beach is not for international tourists only. They're all friendly.
People are untertained by the waves. Underwater visibility is poor, marine life is nonexistent. Sunbed are for rent, restaurants and cafes nearby.

Our hotel actually had a private section of the beach for its guests.   We were given a pass and were entitled to 3 hours with a sun  lounge and umbrella and a towel each. The day we decided to go was raining slightly . The water was a beautiful  colour but with many rips close to shore. Also the water went from shallow to deep very quickly. Consequently with the rips we didnt swim. The beach was very crowded. Only spent an nour if that on the beach.

The reviews gave us the impression that this was a dirty crowded beach in the city. While, yes, there were a lot of Russians they all seemed quite friendly and there was lots of space to hang out. We didn't see any garbage on the beach as it is raked daily. We arrived at 5am and had no accommodations so we decided to grab a beer and go to the beach to watch the sunrise. To our surprise the beach had hundreds of Vietnamese (many up there in age) all exercising in various ways. It was actually quite pleasant to see.  Town is pricey for food and beer but a 40min walk along the waterfront (which is very nice) takes you over the bridge for cheaper eats and great seafood. Only complaint would be that the older Russian men and women wear bathing suits way way too small for their weight yetch!

Most days the water is perfect. Ocassianlly, if windy, garbage can pop up in the water. Nothing to serious. DONT GO NORTH. Stick to the beach near the city. Across the bridge was very few people. Only went once. Put my bag 2 feet the shore, 2 minutes of swimming later and bag was gone. Left my bag dozens of times near the city, lots of people, no theft.

A pleasant long sandy beach. More narrow and steeply sloping to the north end. There's a bit too much litter around for my liking. A fairly constant hive of activity both late into the night and very early in the morning. You have to cross a noisy, very busy main road in order to access the beach from the many hotels, tourist-trap expensive low-end restaurants, and shops. Take into account that this is a pretty noisy place due to the road, if you are planning a beach holiday and expect peace and tranquility!!! Some stretches have good public facilities and many things to see and do.

It's really a normal beach. The only character is big and long enough. The water here is not very very clear but it's acceptable. The sand is also not in very good quality. I recommend not to go if you have many spots to choose. Money is needed if you wanna rent an umbrella. maybe you can have a walk there after you had the dinner to enjoy the breeze.

The beach is situated in front of many rows of hotels, motels, guesthouses across the road. So if you're looking for a quiet peaceful beach front scene, you can forget it.I happened to visit the place in a cooling weather and water temperature is 24 degree celsius, a little chilly for someone who's never experience this kind of temperature but I must admit it's really comfortable.There're many local and tourists at the beach early in the morning but they tends to disappear after lunch, the waves can be a little rough for some so it's better to watch your safety while you're playing along the beach.Like I mentioned it's a so..so...only beach because I caught sight of a signboard that reads "No Swimming", so I suppose its swim at your own risk as there isn't any lifeguards or post around to watch your back.
My girlfriend and I had a romantic walk down the beach which was nice, we stayed directly across the beach and it gave amazing views, we didn't swim because the water looked rough.
Way better beaches in Vietnam with way less tourists and way quieter than Nha Trang...up to you to find out where they are though...

Nha Trang beach can be dirty and it is not like a crystal clear water like the Caribbean or something. In saying that we still enjoyed our time and you need to know that before going. The sand was nice and soft and we enjoyed spending the day on our lounger and soaking up the sun. The Sea still looks beautiful and we enjoyed out time there.

Its an ok beach next to the big road. Its filled with beach chairs, mostly from the big hotels nearby. The sea is usually super wild so no swimming allowed. The water further away is nice and blue. The water near the shore (the waves) is really sandy and browinish. The most comming thing to see is the russian tourist..
when we stayed in Nha Tang the beach is nice,but the water was not clear and waves are big. it was red flagged all the time.the locals said this was not normal

At first look, which was from my balcony in the Sheraton, the beach looked very attractive. It also turned out to be a very pleasant walk along the shore. However, a river feeds into one end of the bay, and the result is that the silt that it carries turns the water a muddy brown for the whole length of the beach. So if your idea of a great beach is clear turquoise water, you will be disappointed.
Went here with a few friends, the water looks a bit grim and not much going on, full of tourists on holiday.

Nothing spectacular except for its length. Sand is coarse. Winds are strong in the late afternoon. Beach is packed with locals in the afternoon. Good thing is not many touts so you can really enjoy your siesta.

The beach is ok---- it can be very dirty with rubbish in places, it's not a powdery sand but very gritty, the sea is quite fierce, with waves which are powerful so not a beach for small children to paddle. There are sun beds but a lot belong to the hotels along the promenade though we did use them and got away with it. There are a couple of bars on the beach it's a shame there aren't a few more . From around 11am the beach sellers arrive selling food, drink, souvenirs and massages . We've used this beach for 4 days and it's been ok .

I was there Last week of January. There were a lot of people because of Chinese new year. It was a very nice place and good for relaxing too. I travel alone but i had a lot of fun going around the place.

If you go in thinking city beach, rather than tropical resort beach, this should work. It has a nice boardwalk/promenade, and plenty of restaurants and services. The beach itself is not deep, and the waves are big, but there were plenty of folks having fun in the surf.  It is a good fit for the party atmosphere of the town.

The beach is very long. Lots of offered services. But... DIRTY! No security, nobody takes care of local regulations and laws about visiting of beaches. Didn't notice any policemen around. For me as for a person who has already visited lots of beaches around the world this place is not bad but not really good same time. Just an average quality beach. Nothing special. Vietnamese government could make a real perl from this beach because the nature is perfect around but nobody cares.

Having just come from the beautiful beaches of Cambodia I have to say that I am less than impressed by the beach here in Nha Trang.  Of course, the fact that it is raining and is forecast to remain wet for the duration of my eight day stay here is not helping, but even taking that into consideration, this beach just does not measure up to other Asian beaches.  The beach is nice enough, but there is a lot of litter, it is alongside a very busy road and there is a huge amount of noise from a massive amount of building projects that are underway.  Building projects that will take many months, if not years to complete, so it will be impossible to relax on the beach any time soon, even if the sun does come out.The sea was also very rough, with big rolling waves, and watching people enter, it is obvoisuly a deep incline to the water, as it appears to only take a few steps to go from ankle deepness to being fully immersed.  So, this cannot be a safe location for children, or those who are not strong swimmers.  On the plus side, I enjoyed walking along the beach even with the light rain.

Considering the beach is beside a very busy road, the traffic is not so noticeable. The waves were huge in December so was not ideal for swimming and water looked quite muddy. Beach is ok, reasonably clean with numerous loungers to rent, just be careful to check prices a few places and bargain.

Nice beach in the City.... but No very clean sand and many Russians. The water was nice though.. the beach is in the City of Nha Trang and Therefore quite a lot of noise from motos and cars using their horns... Bo and Susanne Copenhagen Denmark

Stayed here for 5 nights. It is a decent enough beach but does not measure up to beaches in other parts of Asia such as Thailand and Philippines. It is also extremely expensive and you will get much more for your money elsewhere. I was not impressed with the food compared to elsewhere in Vietnam. As with the hotels, you will pay more for lower quality. I will not return to Nha trang.

Nice beach mostly aside from the heavy crowds (many Russians and Europeans vacation here during the winter). We were disappointed in the amount of litter present and were never comfortable enough to leave our belongings unattended to each go swimming together.  The views are very nice and the local vendors that rent chairs/umbrellas are very friendly.
Looks nice. Pleasant to walk along and take in the sights!Tourists seem to love it. Water not very clear. Not as lovely as Sydney beaches!
Nice enough beach, but a bit dirty in some areas. Recommend finding a good spot in front of one of the better bars or restaurants where the beach is cleaner and there's lounges, umbrellas etc

It is a long beach, not the best for swimming. Some places for drinks like the Sailign Club and Lousienne. At night seafood sellers ply their trade and cook the clams, lobsters and sea snails while you picnic under the street lights. Come Christmas and new Year and there are parties with loud speakers and DJs. Park exercise machines and public toilets available.

The beach is very big with a lot of space. Unfortunately during the time we stayed the waves where huge and not so bathfriendly. Much People are walking around the beach trying to sell things and food and dont always take no for an answer. Can get a bit annoying after a while. You also have to pay for using the sunchares and some of the sellers tries to get more money then they are worth so dont be afraid to get the price down.
Its bkt a bad beach but its not worth the trip just foe the beach as the watwr.Is not a clear as aadvertised.

Average beech it is long but the sea isnt the cleanest and it is full of Russians well the whole of Nha Trang is. We wanted to try surfing but were told that the waves weren't suitable on that day which was disappointing.
Nah Trang is a place to go for shopping not for the beach which is dirty with muddy sea. In December the waves are dangerous therefore you can't swim. This is not a blue sea white sand beach.

The beach  looks nice and ornamental as the authorities made beautiful parks/parking and sitting areas near the beach. But the waters very dirty and has high rip current. So may not be a great place for swimming. The sandy  beach area is also very dirty and the type of sand is very coarse.The commercialization is at the peak. You have to pay 2-3 times the price for everything including  water bottles. They charge you around $3-5 for a deck chair and a not so clean towel and wifi(in few areas).Good for just a  day in the beach, but if you want to swim and enjoy the beautiful sea water in nha Trang, take a ferry to the islands nearby. The water there awesome.

The beach is big but not really as you expect asYou see the picture s maybe it was because of the season . There is a big Sandy beach it is public there are some beach clubs but the water is not really blue as you expect the beaches are clean overall the people here are verry clean.

Its not the best and not the worst. Quite clean, a few people trying to sell stuff to you, but they are not pushy and will simply walk away if you say no. Lot of sunbeds, restaurants are just by the beach.
Not the best of beaches very dirty the sea had bits of plastic in it as well I think you would be better to head to an island to swim

We are staying out of Nha Trang  (thank goodness).  After one day trip in to this beach side area we definitely didn't need to go back.  Thank goodness for our beautiful secluded resort.  We have had such a wonderful time immersing ourselves in local cambodia and Vietnam that it was a little disappointing to visit this area.  It is really just a tourist party beach destination with direct flights from Russia it seems popular with Russians.  All along the beach front there is area after area of deck chairs for hire and bar after bar.  After all the beautiful areas we have visited this feels like Surfers Paradise in Australia or Benidorm.  Places we would avoid. Very glad we are staying 20 mins away at Mia, a very secluded 5 star resort which, is proving great for a bit of RnR in the middle of a very busy holiday.  I think if I was backpacking (like I did in my 20s) i would have loved some sun and party time staying in Nha Trang central but right now it's not our thing.

1. Sewage drains right down to the ocean.  We thought about having a dip in the ocean but the water color was dark almost black at some points so we just walked along the beach, at one point we spotted black stinky water running down from the park to the beach.  Turned out the city drainage was overflowed and people dig a trend from the park to the beach to relieve the sewage water.  Do Not Swim here!2.  Food is not just expensive but it is horrible, and the service is terrible.  We walked out some restaurants because of their attitude.  All are tourist traps!

We came for a bit of rest in the sun as our hotel included the beach chairs and umbrellas. We went in for a swim and found the water to have some weird yellow bubbles on the surface that looked like some sort of pollution. The waves were pretty rough that day and carried us halfway down the beach. We got out pretty quickly and went back to the hotel pool. Maybe we're just spoilt by our own beaches at home but yeah I definitely showered after swimming in that surf.

The beach is a LONG strip, however it is not very clean. We were looking forward to a  couple of relaxed days on the beach, though after visiting Nha Trang, I would say the place could have been skipped altogether.

We spent a short time on the beach utilising the hotels area that we were staying at sun lounges and umbrellas.  This is certainly great to have available to you otherwise you can pay about 35,000 vnd to hire a sun lounge.  On this particular day the water was very rough and not that great for swimming.  As with most of these locations you can buy cold drinks and there are people wandering by selling their wares but not too intrusive.
Nice place to watch the sunset, however the effects of plastic pollution is every where!  Its time to clean up the ocean and band plastics!
I am so surprised that Nha Trang beach now is so dirty with rubbish but it still attracts Russian guests taking sun bath daily.
It is covered with Russian guests and only a very few locals. On busy beach bars people are peeing in the sand in stead of using the toilets. In positive words,  this was not too good

We were there in the rainy season after some pretty severe flooding in the central area of the country. This severely impacted the water quality at the beach; but I can imagine with clear water and clean beach it could be really lovely. A lot of Russians, a lot...really.

Honestly speaking, I was very much looking forward to our first beach in Vietnam as we made our way south  and we were disappointed...there is a lot of rubbish on the beach, the water is murky and filled with bits so you have to be careful when you wade in. Not the best, the pictures online are picturesque but in person a let down!

Perhaps not the best time to come There has been a storm battering the resort for at least three days which has landed a pile of debris on the beach which they are having trouble clearing up. The sea is full of rubbish, old paper, clothing and plastic which tangles around your legs if you go in the water.This isnt our favourite venue its a bit built up, lots of high rise buildings.It is apparently very popular with Russian tourists and most of the bars and restaurants target them. Most menu's are written in Russian and Vietnamese.The main road along the beach front is horrendous to cross as the traffic just doesn't stop, even though there are marked crossings.If your coming to Asia yu can find much nicer places to stay.

The majority of this beach is very nice, some parts out of the way not so much. It can get busy when the weather is favourable but there are plenty of places to go in the sun. Plenty of bars and restaurants toward the bottom end.
It's ok if you like to be surrounded by parrasols and sun loungers I like my beach to be a little quieter.Head south and you can find quieter beach

I am an Aussie, so I'm used to pretty great beaches.  The sand here is really coarse, the water was murky and it was pretty dirty. You can hire a seat under an umbrella for about $1 and you will also get table service, which was fabulous.

It's a nice beach, but very touristy. It's lovely at 5.30am when it's busy with locals exercising and swimming, breakfasting. It was quiet and peaceful. But the locals are gone by 7.00am. And then the hoards of Russian tourists rock up to the lounges. I found the amount to Russian signs, shops, restaurants disappointing. And I wouldn't come back next trip. Maybe a day or 2 stop over between travelling.
Liked the beach a lot. It was raining two days but you can walk and chill everywhere or sport on the public equipments.

We stayed in Nha Trang for 5 days to relax a little bit after traveling. The beach is not wonderful but decent. Most of the local people will arrive in the late afternoon and the beach will get crowded.

Our kids love the water and couldn't wait to get in the water. I was a bit weary as you can see rubbish in the water and it looked a bit foamy. They still had a blast although the water was a bit rough. The sand is a bit gravelly but we had no problems walking on it barefoot. I'd recommend a rashy for the kids to protect them a bit from the rocky sand. The water was surprisingly warm and the boys could have stayed in it all day but we decided to check out the rooftop pool at our hotel after a few hours.
there was a sea of humanity rather than a sea of tranquility. rude and rough people from foreign lands thronged the place.it was disappointing, maybe, i was in the wrong place at the wrong time.

A lovely beach, but unfortunately not entirely safe. It can be fairly rough, the depth drops off quite suddenly and the tidal pull is very strong. I went with my pregnant partner who is not a great swimmer and my 6 year old and my anxiety kicked in very early on. If they weren't getting dumped ruthlessly by the waves only a few metres from the shore line, then they were being rapidly sucked towards the next wave. I pulled the pin on swimming after about 2 minutes.If you're with a small family, stick to ankle depth, or better yet, your hotel pool. The surf is better left to the big hard Russian men that seem to dominate it anyhow!

I spent a few days in Nha Trang in September.The water was really warm (even when we had a 5am swim) and the floor in the sea is sand so it's nice to walk on.There are cushioned sunbeds available to rent for 50,000 dong (a bit under UK £2) for the day. you get a towel and also theres a few tables aswell.The beach is busy however and there was quite a few cigarette ends in the sand.There are people coming up to you trying to sell this including massages. On quite a few occasions we were massaged while repeatedly saying no thankyou!There are toilets but you have to pay about 10,000 to use them. not very much but they are very dirty and mostly squats.

The water isn't that clean and you will likely hassled by flys if you are near any of the bins or restaurants.We paid 50k for a beach bed which was nice. We were put off buying drinks however when we heard the couple next to us find out they beers they had were 200k each!!   (I appreciate they should have agreed before drinking them)Nice way to spend an afternoon once you have done all your cultural duties!

Been back and plan for long term stay in Nhatrang but disappointed this time as its developing and very commercial city now.Well, I know they need to promote local economy but so many Chinese & RussiansBeach was overcrowded on National holidays that I do not recommend  you go on special events

This beach is defintely not a beautiful beach, even if it is pretty cool to walk around by night. We decided to stay in the hotel in Nha Trang, rent a motorbike and go to Bai Dai beach, 21 km South, much better, White sand, cristal clear wáter ... the beach for ourself ... to bad thing is that the beach is a little dirty like many deserted beaches like that close to resort in constructions ... but nothing too bad obviously .. we had a good time there ... more authentic! Vietnamese  girls come and set up tents on the beach in the afternoon, they sell amazing sea snails, and fruits .. they are super firendly as well!
It is located in walking distance from a myriad of hotels and guest houses. The skyline blocks out the sea. Nothing really scenic have here. It is commercialized to the hilt.

Please Nha Trang sort out your rubbish issue! I spent hours collecting rubbish off the beach and from the sea whilst swimming. I saw others doing the same. Lovely beach at points however a serious rubbish problem! Such a shame as this is such a small proportion of coast line! The poor wildlife! Plenty of places to grab a drink along the beach and places to rent beds. Or relax on the sand with a towel for free :-)

The beach looks like a beach from a postcard.Pay attention to all the venders. In the beginning they told us we had to pay 100.000VDN/beach chair and that the prices are all the same. As soon as you start walking down the beach you will definitely find cheaper places. Bring your own drinks and food, as everything is totally overpriced.
The water was a bit dirty. But somehow people swim in it. Although I have to say, the local people understand a lot about safety, most of them swam with safety vest. Hats off for that!
The sand could be cleaner. Bring slippers:-)A lot of restaurants and bars on the beach. In most places you can be for free on your own towel but bed and umbrella can be rented for cheap.

The beach is not clean, there are some abandoned garbage and inside the water, so very dirty sea in some areas. I think also too crowded, to get a bit of relax my advice is to rent chairs and sun beds in a VIP area in some beach bars.

Having travelled to Nha Trang before I realised that it was more touristy than the rest of Vietnam and that the locals make their living from tourists.  Furthermore, I am more than happy to be approached by hawkers on the beach knowing this is the case.  That said, while I appreciate that, I am coming to the beach to relax and enjoy the sand between my toes and the sea breeze brushing lightly against my body.  All I can say is if that is what you are looking for, don't expect that here.  Just as you manage to sit down and manoeuvre your bed to the best spot you are pestered by... "You like massage?"  While the answer " no thank you" generally means no, you are then given the hard sell on how it will be a good massage and you will like it a lot.  After several more "No!" responses you are then encouraged to have a massage later or the following day.  At this point don't say "maybe" as the following day it begins again.Nha Trang beach is a lovely beach with lovely crystal clear water lapping on the gold soft sand.  The sun shimmers of the waves like a million tiny diamonds in the sea.  It is definitely not to be missed when you visit, but prepare yourself for the barrage of hawkers that you need to fight off in order to relax.

In truth I've been on worse beaches, but the seafront is now very commercialised and has that typical holiday destination feeling about it.  Whether you pay a little more for the 'VIP' lounger is irrelevant,  you will still probably hear the thumping sound of the music from the speakers positioned nearby. We came here for a few days purely for the beach and left disappointed.
Very attractive beach with lovely wide space between the water and the road.  Lots of things to look at and great for people watching but incredibly dirty. Has a real holiday vibe.

Although the beach is beautiful as such, I did not swim, because I was disgusted by the incredible amount of rubbish on tbe beach near the sea, as well as in the sea. Dirtiest beach I ever saw. There are some cleaner patches, so look for Those! Or rent your seats with Louisiane, which gives you access to their lovely pool....

Some days and parts of the day better and worse. Mornings seem quite calm with the sunrise and high tide with the beach quite clean. By lunch time the wind has picked up and with the lower ride has brung a fair bit of rubbish onto the beach. This is at the end of July. Not sure if this is how it is all year round. It's a nice spot catch some sun and go for a dip to cool off. Lots of food and shopping across the road in the centre of the beach. I would not go out of my way to come here,  especially if you are after a nice clean white sand beach.

A long wide expanse of gold sand which is fairly well manicured and looks spectacular against the sunrise and sunset, however it is not a relaxing or peaceful experience as the traffic noise is deafening. We decided to get up early to in the hope we could experience some tranquility on the beach, view a stunning sunrise and swim, but even at 5am, it was packed with swimmers, bathers, families playing sports and the deafening sound of the traffic was as prolific as it is throughout the day.

Most hotels have their own area. If you want a sun bed arrive early if you want to bake yourself. We didn't bother. Just hit the water for a paddle and cool down. Locals all head down after 5 which is cool as the water is so flat. Gets very busy then.

Everyone on here boasts about this beach, not sure why though. Rubbish in the water and people leaving their rubbish on the beach as well. Sand isn't as white as i was expecting. Australia's beaches are at least 6 times better than this.

The beach is quite nice but a lot of tourists from Russia are destroying a little bit the atmosphere . I hope Nha Trang wil not focus in the future on this kind of low cost tourism. The country side and especially the Evason Resort are marvellous

We felt Nha Trang beach was let down by all the debris and litter in the water.  The beach is a nice hot sandy beach in the city, plenty of loungers and umbrellas to rent - we paid 50,000 dong per lounger for the day.  In our experience there are better cleaner beaches in Vietnam.

Nha Trang is a crowded party town with a lot of russian tourists. A lot of Shops and Restaurants offer in russian language. Even the tax divers speak some russian. The beach is not very clean. We saw plastik bags, old fruits and other garbage swimming. But there are very beautiful situated luxury resorts. If you are looking for peaceful and quite vacations you should only go there if you can afford it. If you are looking for party night life, Nha Trang might be a good choice.
Beautiful  and tropical, but too much crowded, it is better to avoid the rush hours. It is better an evening swim, but you can enjoy your cocktail and relax.
It's just ok. I'm Thai. I saw a lot of beaches much more beautiful and cleaner in Thailand and Philipine. But why not if you visit southern vietnam? There are plenty of activities to do there anyway.

The beach is long and looks pretty well clean.There are designated swimming areas and some lifeguards on duty (although these maybe connected to hotels that have there own areas).Sun loungers are available for a price and are clustered around designated hotel areas.The main negative to the beach is the lack of natural shade or cover.Bring your own shelter or rent a lounger

The coast line is stunning and the water temperature is great. The sad part is the amount of rubbish in the water and the beach is a bit dirty. Compared to Boracay it is a let down. A real shame as it could be stunning.

I would give this 5 stars if it wasnt for the all garbage floating in the water. It was too dirty to swim in. I think the other reviewers didnt go in the water. It looks clean from the sand but once you go to the water you see. The sand is nice and was not over crowded

Its lovely looking at Nha Trang beach with its little huts and sun lounges lining the sand but when you walk along the shore it is full of litter like cigarette lighters, plastic spoons and I even saw a large plastic bag floating in the water. The water was warm to put your feet in but we chose not to swim in it due to the uncleanliness and swim at our hotel pool instead.

Nha Trang beach seemed to be very popular amongst the Russian and Chinese tourists as it was difficult to find a spot to relax in as they seemed to sprawl and use up as much space as possible. The sand is very nice and the water is warm, however there was a ton of garbage all over the place despite it being cleaned on a daily basis. Noticed a lot of the tourists throwing their garbage onto the ground which is very unfortunate.
The beach is ok... Very touristy and busy, go for a day visit but I wouldn't plan my trip round going to this beach everyday there are better places to visit in Nha trang

This beach is obviously popular with the locals who all are out in throngs at 5am. Obviously there is some ritual involved. Then as the morning evolves the tourists arrive which in June meant crowds of Russian & Chinese tourists.The water is lovely & warm but swimming amongst plastic bags was a major turn off for me.

Nha Trang beach was nice. When we arrived at the beach you notice a series of different exercise machines in the park under the coconut trees, vietnams version of Venice beach. There is areas of beach umbrellas and deck chairs. Some of these belong to the better hotels and if you have your room key to show them it is free, otherwise its 35000 dong per person. Each area has its own staff that will bring you drinks that are actually reasonably priced. In the morning I think the rubbish is picked up off the beach, but as the day goes by the rubbish that floats in starts to build up on the beach. The Vietnamese have no idea how to look after thier environment. Also be careful of the sun, even sitting under the umbrellas we still got burnt in only a couple of hours.

We arrived at the beach at 4.30am for our first visit, and couldn't believe how busy it was, with hundreds of people seemingly doing exercise! We sat by the main monument on the beach, trying to relax. A man decided to take a piss about one yard behind us which was a lovely touch. We walked off, and half the beach had a similar smell. We returned that afternoon, and struggled to get a sun lounger as they are either owned by hotels, or by people trying to rip off stupid white people. We had a little swim and decided to leave. Without a doubt, the less hyped beach in Danang was much, much nicer!

We had a better beach at our resort so didn't use this much just to walk along and stop for a drink or food. Lots of locals doing their thing so good for people watching. Nha trang is full of Russians and Chinese people which is not a bad thing but it can make nha trang a bit like benidorm is for British people! Like all places in Vietnam slightly spoiled by rubbish.

A positive is that no one hassles you on the beach trying to sell you things every 5 minutes. There are drink and food places close by which are reasonably priced. A beer was 20000 VND. The beach did have a lot of rubbish on it and the water wasn't that clean so I would avoid swimming especially if there has been a lot of rain a day before as they don't have a good waste treatment system. A lot of hotels have their own private beach so make sure you ask your hotel. I think to rent sun lounger is only 30000 VND.

We came to Nha trang because we had heard of the beautiful endless stretch of golden sand and turquoise waters. What we didn't know was that in those beautiful waters was endless streams of rubbish floating in from tour boats and bottles/food strewn on the sands. There are no bins to be found. No one clearing the beach except last thing in the evening when most of it has been washed away. It's a shame. The waters are warm. The beach could be perfect. Also too many bars and hotels have been allowed to divvy up the sands.
Unless you are Russian or Chinese do yourself a big favour and go to Hoi An instead. Better food, shopping, tailors etc.

It is a great and beautiful long beach. However, the water is filled with garbage, plastic, bottles and such. The sand is a bit grainy, but still nice, and there are good opportunities to get a sun bed.
Ok beach...very touristy! Lots of hawkers and be careful of ur valuables.Beautiful views of other islands. Quite lovely!

The beach is nice, but the ocean is poluted. There is plastic and other debree in the water. Too bad the Vietnamese people still seem to polute the water, making it unpleasant to take a dive in the ocean.
You can have long walk in Nha Trang beach. Water is bit wavey careful with your kids, water wasn't that clear, and there was a lot of rubbish in the coast line.

It's a nice beach, but unfortunately people throwing cigarettes butts, empty bottles and cans on a beach. And all this garbage ends up in sea, who wants to swim surrounded by garbage. Next to me was sitting family from Russia and they were throwing all their rubbish right on the sand, didn't reacted when I asked them if they can take their garbage with them and stopped polluting the place.
A great beach with great views, some care needed in the surf, dont underestimate it. If a very crowded beach full of sunburned tourists is what you seek you'll not be disappointed.

The beach is man made and being from Cornwall I have a high standard of beaches! However if you're up for just bumming around and wanting to work on your tan then the beach is a good spot to do this. Nice warm water as well.

Don't get me wrong, Nha Trang beach is quite pretty.  The main problems are two fold, i) for the region, the water is relatively cold (beaches in nearby Thailand are like sitting in a bath, amazing), and ii) the beach is in a city of half a million people.  The latter is the biggest problem because a huge swath of the beach is close enough to a major road that you can easily hear the traffic while you're relaxing on the beach.  That being said, the nearby islands are awesome, as are the beaches right outside of town. Nha Trang is worth a visit for those excursions, just don't plan for too much time on Nha Trang beach it's self because it's just OK.

The beach is packed with visitors from sunrise to sunset.compared with danung beach, beach size here is much smaller. However the beach has been kept at high standard and well supervised.I suggest family with little children can choose hotels in the north part of city beach. There is a bay and the water is much suitable for young kids to enjoy.

This is not a quiet little seaside resort, very busy with many local and Russian tourists.  The beach itself is beautiful, however on windy days the beach and water can be quite dirty, mornings are the best time.  However if you are wanting a quick, no fuss getaway, it is perfect, lots of great hotels, places to eat and night markets galore
The beach itself wax clean, but the water was still murky. We saw lots of people swimming, but didn't venture in ourselves. The water on the islands wax a lot cleaner.
It's okay beach. Very dirty water though. The other day we all were swimming and a big dead RAT floating pass us! Rubbish keeps on coming in. Not clean beach
As beaches go it's very pretty but very built up. The beach has plenty of bars and restaurants and sun loungers to rent but seems very commercial and isn't that charming.

The sand is clean and there's very little rubbish on the actual beach but there is quite a lot floating at the shore line. I only got in the water once because of this. The rest of our visit was pleasant and enjoyable.
This is a long white sandy beach with cafe, and long chairs. Busy and noisy. Music, drinks and west style. If you like this, it s ok. I don't !

So far I feel like the beaches don't live up to the hype. It is a nice long stretch of sand, but also of garbage too. The water is murky and a lot of both sea debris and some garbage float by as you swim.
Nice beach with clean water.  In the morning hours the beach is full of Russian tourists turning their white skin red.  Beautiful views of the nearby islands.  Miles of coastline to relax and swim.

It looks superb in photos and from a distance it is. There are two beaches . The main one by the city centre and one further back over the bridge. The one over the bridge where we stayed was full of debris. . Everything from bricks too litter.  You had to clear an area to but your towel on. The other beach is on a rather good slope and isn't inviting. The seas was lovely and warm. . But one night we found they had an open sewer in to the bay and it put us right off . Vietnam you need to be doing better

This is a nice beach however it really pays to walk South down the beach, as it is much quieter and you can easily find a section to yourself where you don't get bothered by locals selling sunglasses and coconuts etc. as they don't walk down that far. A couple of times we didn't walk down and hired a sunbed in the economy section; this was 30,000VND so this is really good value as you can stay as long as you like. We read in the Lonely Planet guide that people have reported locals taking photographs of western women on the beach. We didn't witness this at all further South down the beach, however we witnessed this happen by the sun loungers. One man was walking round holding his phone in his hand (not very discreetly at all) and stopping by women, obviously taking photos. I found this disgusting, it didn't happen to me as I was next to my boyfriend the whole time. So go careful, ladies!

We have visited here many times over the past 10 years, and the improvement effort is commendable. Beach and promenade is kept quite clean, more toilets installed and promenade extended. Hard to control the garbage in the water, though. Try Doc Let beach 90 minutes away on No. 3 bus. Cleaner and quieter.

We've been here several days now and have spent some time at the beach.  We are at the Sheraton so we have a nice place to lounge and there are umbrellas to keep warm.  The Water:  The water is decent and the days we were there the waves were fun for the kids and big enough to be fun for the adults.  The water seemed pretty clean compared to other beaches we've been too.  Way cleaner than Hua Hin Thailand.  The Sand:  The sand is nice.  There is some small garbage up near the boardwalk area but it seemed like people came along and cleaned pretty regularly to try to control that.  The sand isn't super white and soft or, at least it wasn't the day that we were there.  The kids were able to find some really nice shells and coral skeletons which got them pretty pumped.  Often on touristy beaches, it's hard to find decent shells but we were surprised at how many whole shells they were able to find. Other Things:  We liked that there were people walking the beach with little snacks and things that you could purchase if you like and it wasn't very expensive.  There were also toilets scattered along the boardwalk in the case of an emergency.  You can walk the beach all day long and as you walk there are things to do and places to eat.  If you wanted to kill an entire day just walking and stopping every once in a while for a drink or for food you could totally kill a whole day.  It was enjoyable.  We live in China and when we go to the beach people won't leave our children alone.  They are constantly trying to take photos and or just sneaking photos and making us all feel uncomfortable.
