Here they are very accustomed to "foreigners" so nobody bothered us at all.  It was so enjoyable to just watch the kids play and not have to ask people to stop touching our children and to stop asking them to take pictures with them.  If you are looking for an amazing beach that will change your life then I'd recommend that you keep looking.   If however you want a beach that is great for families, relaxing and has lots of activity and food options then this might be the place for you.

Maybe because it had been hyped up as one of the top 20 bays in the world but I was slightly disappointed in the beach.That said it wasn't crowded and there are ample shops and restaurants along the sea road.  Sand is gritty rather than soft and there is a lack of sun beds but it is a pleasant beach to sunbathe and take the family.The usual hawkers selling seafood etc but not too many.I would say it is a nice place for a few days beach break when in Vietnam but be aware it is predominantly Russians who holiday here.

Very busy and from afar it looks beautiful but close up its very littered and dirty, it is a pleasant place to stroll up and down and people watch, plenty of bars restaurants (very expensive) it would be OK for a holiday but for travelling on a budget there's much much better beaches and places (Mui ne, de Nang, Hoi an) it's also very pretty at dawn and dusk with people exercising in the morning and kite flying at night, and it's very bright later on at night with all the neon lights, it's also full of Russians which was different to say the least, but don't let that put you off, it's ok

this beach have the potential to be a nice beach but unfortunately at is  very dirty. the waves are ruff, it isn't a nice swim beach. the road is too close to the beach so you cant really relax with the noise.another bad thing is Asian children pee and poop everywhere on the beach and their parents don't care .im too afraid to relax on the beach because you might step on a landmine

Don't like that you have to hire sun beds but when you do just don't pay 100,000 for 2 people for them. You can get them for 60,000 -70,000 for 2. Just try a couple of different ones. Or we negotiated 90,000 including 1 coconut for us to share. The first couple of days we where there the water was nice the waves were small but by day 3 the waves were huge and I didn't dare go in. We just watched people constantly get dumped and trashed by waves. Nice to spend the day under the umbrella drinking coconuts but unfortunately as the huge waves develop very quickly and close to shore there isn't anywhere for kids to swim (or inexperienced adults) unless you get a nice calm day.

Not one of the best beaches in Vietnam.  Too many people trying to rip you off.  After being in Hoi An very disappointed with Nha Trang in general. Locals don't seem as friendly, will not be sorry to move on.

Nice beach but cant compare to Australian beaches. It was nice to not be harassed by locals to buy anything unlike trips to bali. It may be the season and time of year but the water certainly isn't crystal clear or blue like all the photos. Decent swell as well. There is a considerable amount of rubbish and litter strewn around the beach and the area which is disappointing but the rules are just not there to enforce it unfortunately. Plenty of umbrella & beach bed set ups and towel hire to use along the entire beach, not sure if these acquire a fee but i was never charged. For any snorkel or dive fans February is not the best time of year as the visibility is extremely low due to high winds, much lower temperature also. Locals have recommended Late March to April is the best time. Much more humid and warmer but better for the diving and snorkelling.

The beach is really huge, you can see the vinpearl island as well. Not too crowded that not many people having sunbathing. Because there are some washrooms and sun-umbrellas which you have to pay so not quite convenient unless you live along the beach (there are several hotels along the beach)!!!

This is a nice beach, but at times there can be a lot of rubbish on it.  A lot of the beach is taken up by hotels, but there is still plenty of space to relax and swim depending on how strong the waves are.  Unlike Australia, beaches aren't patrolled so you swim at your own risk.

This is a nice brach5, especially if you've spent time in the cities and need to feel a bit closer to nature. However, this is not a beach for swimming ad the waves are too large and dangerous. A good spot for sunbathing and the quickest dip!

Not the best beach I've experienced and if only people would take away their rubbish instead of leaving it and expecting everyone else to dispose of it! Nha Trang has lots of potential but somehow what the beach gains in trash the resort lacks in soul. Mainly geared to the Russian & Chinese tourist market,

We spent a day at Nha Trang beach just lounging on the day beds and swimming. The day beds are decent prices we payed 100,000 VND for a full day in the VIP section which was nice, you can have drinks and food brought down to the beds which is great, only problem is you pay for the bed but of you want to go swim you have to climb over all the people who lay on towels in front of the beds which can get annoying as we were also trying to relax in the sun and they were all making a lot of noise, its not the nicest beach and the sand is a little dirty but for Vietnam it was a decent beach
Beach was average, quite dirty in places. Sunbeds reasonably priced. Toilets good, very clean. Restaurants nearby offer a wide selection of Russian foods
So, not particulary clean beach, like every beach in vietnam there is lot of plastic trash in water. Waves are allso pretty high and when you come away from water you are covered with sand.

The beaches were lovely and clean. Coming from Australia we're a bit spoiled with blue water and white sand, but Nha Trang beach was a nice change from the hotel pool. Definitely recommend if you're wanting some sunshine and the sound of crashing waves.

Nice beach, but the waves break close to shore and are real "dumpers " with an undertow, so weak swimmers should take care. Bargain for the beach chairs and note that some include toilet use and others don't. No jellyfish or other nasties.

Main Nha Trang beach is really awful in January. The waves are big, the water is dirty, the beach is littered with rubbish.You cannot leave your belongings and go to swim because of many thieves in Nha Trang.We swam only once for two weeks and my girlfriend swallowed water because of great waves.
great beach,  lots of food vendors. government should clean plastic off the beach

The beach is rough but beautiful - picture like Hawaii / high risesBut long sandy beaches - rough waves !We loved  it here but there are lots and I mean lots of Russians - even menus are in Russian If that’s ok for you - you’ll love it

We spent two days here just after a typhoon.  The first day was overcast and the beach crews and the army were clearing away the flotsum etc. The second day was much brighter and great to relax along the beach front.

We walked along Nha Trang Beach and enjoyed its long, wide stretch right next to the city. The beachfront promenade is lively, with many cafés, bars and hotels, making it easy to stop and take a break. While it can feel busy at times, especially later in the day, the beach itself is pleasant and well maintained. A good spot for a walk, sea views and relaxed time by the water.
Free but was crowded a little dirty but ok for a city beach. Just sat for a little while deciding where to do next.Ok for a couple of hours

Nha Trang beach is a bit deeper than the beaches I have visited. The sand is short and there are many pebbles, making walking uncomfortable. However, looking out to the beach, you can still see the beautiful Vinpearl Island. It seems that many foreigners still like to sunbathe here in the morning and afternoon.

We loved Nha Trang! The white sand was perfect for our beach games, and the calm waters kept everyone safe. With a family-friendly atmosphere buzzing with energy, we ate street food, swam all day, and still had energy for night markets.
Красивый пляж как днем так и ночью, но мусора очень много выкидывает на берег и все люди у пляжа практически оставляют тут после себя хотя бы пластиковую бутылку

I recently visited Trang Beach and, unfortunately, my experience was marred by an unfortunate incident. While the beach itself is beautiful, I felt compelled to share my experience to warn others.  While I was enjoying my time at the beach, I left my beach bag, which contained my cellphone & money  & some personal items near my spot on the sand to take a swim. When I returned, my bag was gone. I was devastated to find that someone had stolen it.  It was incredibly frustrating and upsetting, as I was on vacation and had hoped to enjoy my time without worrying about my belongings. Although I understand that incidents like this can happen anywhere, I felt that the beach's overall lack of visible security or surveillance made it a prime spot for opportunistic theft.  I would strongly recommend taking extra precautions and being extremely vigilant with your belongings, especially when visiting this beach. Unfortunately, due to the lack of security and the distressing experience I had, I can't say I would return in the future unless improvements are made to address these safety concerns.

I traveled to in November (2024) and every time I travel to this city and go to the beaches of Beach it is always a pleasure and a charm and everything looks charming, it is a place that you fall in love with. The super extensive beaches, the buildings that border the area, the white sand, the sea without algae. I had days with little waves (some a little more, but some days the sea was very calm), ideal for getting into the sea, an incredible color of water, turquoise, and one who was inside could see to the bottom. And on the days when it was calm, there was no depth even 50 meters out to sea. What yes, as a barely negative point, the water temperature was barely cold (I think when I traveled previously in May it was 1 or 2 degrees higher) but always visiting this beach, (at least for me) is an incredible experience, and the city above all things!!

We loved Nha Trang! The white sand was perfect for our beach games, and the calm waters kept everyone safe. With a family-friendly atmosphere buzzing with energy, we ate street food, swam all day, and still had energy for night markets.
Красивый пляж как днем так и ночью, но мусора очень много выкидывает на берег и все люди у пляжа практически оставляют тут после себя хотя бы пластиковую бутылку

While I was enjoying my time at the beach, I left my beach bag, which contained my cellphone & money  & some personal items near my spot on the sand to take a swim. When I returned, my bag was gone. The super extensive beaches, the buildings that border the area, the white sand, the sea without algae. I had days with little waves (some a little more, but some days the sea was very calm), ideal for getting into the sea, an incredible color of water, turquoise, and one who was inside could see to the bottom. And on the days when it was calm, there was no depth even 50 meters out to sea. What yes, as a barely negative point, the water temperature was barely cold (I think when I traveled previously in May it was 1 or 2 degrees higher) but always visiting this beach, (at least for me) is an incredible experience, and the city above all things!!
The sea water appeared cleaner and greener here, The beach is beautiful and the sand white. Stores and restaurants are open and beach is very clean and Grab motor taxi was convenient to catch !

Soft sand graces both the shore and the seabed, a haven for comfort. The water, delightfully warm and free of seaweed, invites endless hours of play. Beach beds at an affordable 40k a day complete the picture of beach bliss.

Les commentaires très négatifs me font sourire. En effet nous ne sommes pas ici sur une île deserte, la plage ne se situe pas dans un lagon (vagues), la propreté n'est pas terrible comme dans beaucoup d'endroit au Vietnam, des touristes russes, chinois, anglais, des tours de 40 étages, de nombreux  hôtels et magasins de luxe et tout ceci je le savais avant de venir !J'ai apprécié mon séjour car la plage, sa  longue et belle promenade, la météo sont agréablesEn cherchant un peu on trouve d'excellents petits restos pas chers, la ville est animée, quelques sites intéressants à visiter, des prix très bas pour tout, studio  50 m2 luxueux avec vue mer à 30 euros la nuit ...

Der Nha Trang Beach ist ein sehr schöner langer Sandstrand, der von den Hotels durch eine stark befahrene Straße getrennt ist. Hier gibt es einige Fußgängerüberwege. Das Südchinesische Meer ist recht wellig, zumindest, als wir im Dezember dort waren. Als mittelguter Schwimmer würde ich nicht unbedingt ins Wasser gehen. Gegenüber vom Strand sieht man einige Inseln, die zu vietnamesischen „Disneylands“ umgebaut wurden.

When I arrived the sea was really dirty. They must have cleaned after my first couple of days here and it became a totally different place, hopefully, it stays this way. It's a pity about the kind of tourists it attracts (I literally saw a Chinese man pissing in the middle of the beach and another one throw his wrapper in the water).Nice and pleasant beach when clean; I just wish the tourists who come here respected the sea (and the people around them) more.

I was there for five days in Sept and am going back next month.  I get the feeling that the people who write some of these negative reviews own hotels in Vung Tau.  The beach is clean.  The water nice.  Parks by the beach have women doing dancing exercises in the park.  The mornings and evenings are full of families living life.  Enjoyed eating fresh fish and drinking beer in restaurants by the sand and watching the young ladies all dressed up taking pictures of each other.  They seem to take pride in their beach and activities and it is a focal point for them.

Many say it is a dirty beach and not so nice. That is not the case. Is it the best beach, no it is not but it is pretty good. Believe it is about 6 km long and has a walking path at the top for most of the way. Plenty of restaurants and bars along the way with many having sun loungers for rent. Plenty of toilets and excercise areas with equipment. Great to watch the locals playing around in the early mornings.

The main beach in this city. It’s about 7 km’s long and the pathways, trees and shrubs are very well maintained. Some spots along the beach have garbage on it, but not very much. In the centre of the beach where I have been staying, the beaches are kept very clean with umbrellas and chairs for rent. The water changes depending on season, so sometimes it isn’t as clear. Not much for waves on this beach. The ones that are there are small and break only about 20’ from shore.
Shame about the ships cleaning their tanks on the beach. Had to buy alcohol to clean the tar off my feet.

The beach spreads the length of the town-town business district and provides plenty of opportunity to work-out (all sorts of exercise equipment available free of charge), enjoy the crystal clear water, or just chill out.  Just across the road from a large number of international and local hotels and lots of eateries.Best place to stay in Nha Trang.

Everyone visit Nha Trang it is a must to visit the beaches. it is liken an Asian "Gold Coast". The beach is very long and clean, a lot of happening activities there. There are also a lot of restaurants and shopping complexs there plus others sightseeing places nearby.

Water is blueish colour but there are some rubbish washed ashore. Full of Russians sunbathing during the day but no Chinese tourist due to Corona Virus outbreak. High rental at VD100k for beach chairs.

Nice looking beach , and clean. Only downside is the overweight Russians in their budgie smugglers and large ladies wearing what can only be described as circus tents.If you can look past that then you will find some decent bars/ restaurants.
Nice clean beach nice views and no hassle from vendors. Also quiet but this could be because of corona virus and no Chinese here

This is my sort of beach, long and narrow. One could spend a day walking from one side to the other. A few bars along the way but not so many shops once you are past the middle of town. Certainly busy but if you are willing to find yourself a spot there are plenty!
Nice beach with lots of white sand and place to sit and relax. Spent 5 days and only had one day of mass garbage floating in the ocean, not sure who dumped it overboard. Good beach overall.

This is a really nice beach! Soft white sand and clear sea with big waves (although the sea is a bit cold!) luckily there was a collection of sun loungers we could use as the other ones were reserved for the hotels! Would come here again!
So nice and clean have many people on the beach so if you want to find some where to relax or doing activity i am recommend
Very clean beach with plenty of sunbeds to rent. Numerous hawkers selling food, goods etc. The sea was quite rough when I was there with a strong undercurrent. Lifeguards on duty.

Our windows overlooked the sea and the beauty of the beach was always before our eyes. The beach itself is sandy, well-groomed enough, but big waves prevented swimming and having fun at the beginning of December. But we are still very pleased

We wasn’t expecting much after being in Mui Ne but was told it’s another city and over run by Russians (they weren’t wrong). The beach was wet crowded and Russians everywhere. But it was so much better than expected!! We needed some time to chill by the beach and that’s what we got. The waves are very rough and big but still fun to play in. The beach has sun beds most of the way along, you can rent for the day. Being backpackers we walked up and down to get the best price. We got two beds right by the water for 150,000 VMD and two free beers! Perfect!! This way you have your own little space and it’s comfy. If you can get over the big waves and the Russians everywhere then You will definitely enjoy it. Also weather is perfect in February.
Walking here on a normal sunny day, not so humid like in the south, really cruisy. Recommend for anyone who'd like a stroll.

This beach is safe with quite shallow water but can have strong waves depending on wind direction. Lots of loungers and street vendors selling cold drinks and hot food. Some area’s are shaded by two o’clock depending on high rise hotels blocking the sun.

6 kms of beautiful sandy beach with gorgeous blue waters. Unfortunately the sea is pretty rough with a strong undertow where the waves break and not recommended for small children. Also the sand is pretty gritty but provides good exfoliation for your feet, LOL! Not much trees for shade either (except at the south end).

Nice beach. Beautiful sunny weather. The water was a bit cold but that didn't stop us. The waves are really fun and on the 2nd day got so big our youngest was asked to refrain from going into the water. The kids were happy to play in the waves all day!

We had a fantastic time chilling out on Nha Trang beach. There are always plenty of shaded loungers to hire. Experience somewhat detracted by the free-for-all smoking, but it was definitely relaxing. Fresh seafood cooked for you on the beach and massages if you want. What more could you ask for?
Lovely beach in front of our hotel Sheraton with good sun bedsStreet traders no real bother and sea warmer than hotel pool

There is a wide, long, and clean street that spans many kilometers of beach. It is generally a public beach with some kiosks, public sports training equipment, and locals selling stuff.Some Hotels manage a private spot for them with deck chairs and some services.However, the beach generally lacks enough deck chairs- for example we stayed at Sheraton, who share the beach spot with Intercontinental. You need to cross the street and go to Intercontinental beach spot, and fight for a subbed, as it is almost always fully booked. We couldn’t get any!Also, can get windy in January, and water on the cold side, so if you want beach, I would visit March onwards.
It's full of bars and small restaurants, some could be a bit overpriced. It's getting deep quickly. Sunbeds and umbrellas are cheap.

Very nicely maintained beach and it is the highlight of Nha trang...i can say it's far better than Pattaya Beach In Thailand... Comparing both as the entire set up is similar. Very nice smooth sand and water clarity is good to take bath... No pristine but good. Beach is being cleaned often making it clean. Entire stretch of nearly 5kms are set up with restaurants and bars where one bar had a good swimming pool which helps to take a quick dip in the hot sun. Lots of places to visit around

It's a long sandy clean beach, sun lounges have various prices but the one by the restaurant was decent, toilets and food + drink all close-by, with sturdy wooden comfortable sun lounges/umbrella including clean towels! The beach itself is very quiet, family orientated, no noisy music, with some occasional local hawkers selling sunglasses etc. The waves usually get strong by midday. Nice view from the beach to the mountains and distant islands.
Sat in the beach for hours. Kids loved the waves...no rocks under the foot and lots of waves to leap over. Changing facilities would have been welcomed.

A pretty nice beach, I really enjoyed the view of Vinpearl in the distance too. Would recommend looking around for sunloungers before settling, as the price varies (from the places I checked) from 50k-250k - some including a drink too.
Was surprised to see how well maintained the place was and that too with all facilities.Good for a walk or Jog.

The beach was clean and is a long stretched one. It is kid friendly too. Could not see any water sports though but that could be cause of the weather conditions. Have many restaurants and bars lined up next to it to chill and relax. Also the beach chairs were sufficient and costed reasonable, though one should never lose the chance to bargain.

This is a beautiful beach. At the moment of our visit, almost an empty beach. So very relaxing. No loud sounds, no speedboats, watersport activities. Just the place to chill. And a great beach for a nice barefoot walk on the hard sand in front of the ocean. Enjoy the view. Tip:Bring your own strand gear, food and drinks. There are no shops nearby. And don’t forget to take  your garbage with you, or dump it in the trash bins near the man street.
Nice beach with enough sea wave. Maybe good for windsurfers. Famous for its length of the beach, can spend a few hours with sea breeze.

Unfortunately during our 3 day stay in Nha Trang we did not swim as the sea was rough with big waves crashing in. The beach is several kms long and has quite a few restaurants along the beach, with a wide promenade. I was here also 26 years ago when there was basically no restaurants and don't know if i prefer it today. Great swimming back then when the seas are calm, so guess it is still the same on a good day.

Lovely beach, even in December when the waves are crashing into the sand and the boat that’s half way onto the beach. Volleyball games till the early hours and lots of restaurants and a gym along the beach. Nice place to chill out :)
- clean sand,- relaxing,- safe and happy place,- near a street,- a lot of people,- a places to rinse feet,

They say trees are manicured 😁. Excellent quality of life, security and affordability. Transit is bad chaotic. Vietnamese people don’t follow road rules they drive like kamikaze they are loud people Hey moi every second word but I strongly recommend visit and stay. From  flag rower, lotus tower, Pagoda, catholic cathedral, diving and Vine-pearl island I must see
Mit der modernste Teil von Vietnam und gilt als das Miami des Landes. Hier kann man durchaus einen schönen und günstigen Strandurlaub verbringen.

The best thing about Nha Trang is the beach. Blue waters and super clean. Not much difference between high and low tide barring the fact that it gets super breezy with high tide. Weather is awesome and nights do get a bit of a nip.The sight of over weight Russians wallowing around like Walruses on the beach is a sight best avoided. After my first day i stopped going to the beach which was just across my hotel.The worst thing are the excess number of Russians there. Easily the most unfriendly group of tourists in the world. They seem to have escaped from the war by landing up here on long term visas and by the thousands. They stick to themselves, lack public etiquette and they need to go back to primary school to learn a bit of travelling English and manners. I thought Indians were bad but these guys are almost as bad.

Just Returned From: 5 days in Nha Trang, Vietnam.Verdict: A dynamic and sunny beach destination perfect for those who want a mix of relaxation, water sports, lively nights, and a taste of local culture. It’s not a secluded paradise, but a full-sensory coastal experience.Having just unpacked my suitcase, still sandy and sun-kissed, I can confidently say Nha Trang left a strong, positive impression. If you imagine a beach city with a pulse, Nha Trang is it.The Star of the Show: The BeachThe main beach, a long, sweeping crescent of sand along Tran Phu Street, is the city's living room. The sand is soft and light, and the water is a stunning shade of turquoise, especially in the morning. It's remarkably clean for a city beach. Don’t expect empty shores—this is a social hub. You'll share it with joggers, local families, volleyball players, and fellow tourists. The energy is infectious! For a slightly quieter vibe, head further north towards the luxury resorts.Beyond Sunbathing: Things to DoNha Trang is anything but boring.Island Hopping: A must-do! We booked a budget-friendly boat tour that took us to a few islands for snorkeling (decent coral and colorful fish) and a fun floating bar experience. For more luxury, the Vinpearl Island via the iconic over-sea cable car offers a full-day resort experience with a water park and amusement rides.Cultural Touchstones: The Po Nagar Cham Towers, ancient Hindu temples dating back to the 7th-12th centuries, offer a serene and historical contrast to the beach scene. The Long Son Pagoda with its giant white Buddha statue provides breathtaking panoramic views of the city.Foodie Paradise: From fresh seafood grilled right on the beach (try nem nuong – grilled pork rolls) to bustling local markets like Dam Market, your taste buds will thank you. The oceanfront cafes are perfect for a cold Saigon beer or a Vietnamese iced coffee while watching the sunset.The Vibe & ConsiderationsNha Trang is developed and tourist-friendly. The central seafront is lined with hotels, bars, and restaurants. This means fantastic amenities and a buzzing nightlife, but also some traffic and persistent vendors on the beach (a polite "no, thank you" works). Russian and Chinese influences are noticeable in signs and menus, adding to its international flavor.Final Impressions & Tips:Go if you love: A mix of beach time and activities, vibrant energy, great food, and water sports.Look elsewhere if you seek: A quiet, remote, untouched beach getaway.Tip: Rent a motorbike to explore hidden coves north of the city like Bai Dai Beach. Also, a mud bath at one of the local spas is a uniquely Nha Trang, wonderfully messy experience!In short, Nha Trang surprised me with its versatility. I returned home not just with a tan, but with memories of ancient temples, the taste of incredible seafood, the sound of crashing waves from my hotel balcony, and the feeling of pure fun. It’s a coastal adventure served with a side of sunshine
Превосходная набережная, пожалуй одна из лучших во Вьетнаме. Ухоженные дорожки, подстриженные газоны. Убирают утром и вечером. Прекрасная зона для прогулок.

Nha Trang Beach is absolutely stunning – golden sand, crystal-clear water, and a relaxing vibe all around. I loved walking along the shore at sunset and swimming in the calm, warm sea during the day. The beach is clean, lively but not too crowded, and surrounded by cafes and seafood restaurants. A perfect place for both relaxation and adventure. Definitely one of the most beautiful beaches I’ve ever visited – a must-see in Vietnam!

Провели три недели в отеле TUI BLUE, отличный отель 💯На муниципальном пляже есть территория принадлежащая отелю, где стоят лежаки хочу отметить работу сотрудника службы безопасности на пляже TUI BLUE, Хо Ван Хуан, который отлично выполняет поставленные задачи обеспечивая отдыхающих удобными лежаками и чистыми полотенцами! Контролирует правильное использование лежаков согласно расписания, то есть если Вам, по согласованию, назначено время, например на 11:00, будьте уверенны к этому времени место для отдыха Вам будет гарантировано. Желаю ему здоровья, успехов в работе и всего самого наилучшего!

Жили в центре Нячанга. Пляж замечательный. Чисто, волн нет, вода чистая и очень теплая. Лежак можно купить в любом месте. Вдоль побережья множество уютных мест кафе и бич клубов. Народу много утром до 9.00 и вечером после 17.00, т.к. приходят отдыхать местные. Но всё очень культурно, дружелюбно. Рекомендуем

Мне понравилось море, песок, беспощадное солнце с которым я подружился! И сладкая и очень вкусная кукуруза! Море в основном было чистое, а как-то ночью был штиль и я с местными дядьками с фонариками охотились на крабиков вдоль побережья, прикольная рыбалка. Бывало и приносило мусор , всякого рода и щипались как крапива медузы!

Прекрасный пляж. И важно заметить что пляж муниципальный, и оценивать его я буду именно как муниципальный. Для него пляж очень чист, несмотря на огромное количество людей на пляжах. Песок белый, мягкий. И чем ближе к воде тем тоньше. Мы были в конце мая, когда обещали дожди, которых не было. Вода очень теплая, соленая. Вся необходимая инфраструктура расположена на полосе пляжа. Понравился то ли небольшой парк, то ли сквер. Отдает Марина Дубаем

Nice long stretch beach that is free to swim in. There are free feet washing stations and restrooms to.  There both paid and free parking along the beach. beautiful walk way and soft sand. I really love swimming and taking walks here.There're always some type of dance group you can join for free. The workout energy here is awesome. It's always clean because there're always people cleaning it up. It's definitely my favorite beach/walk yet. There're both free and paid parking.  There are also life guards here! Lights come on at night as well. Omg, it's the best.
Невероятно обидно и досадно, что в этом поистине райском и таком притягательном своей красотой месте установлены правила, которые не позволяют насладиться освежающим купанием в его водах.
Прекрасный, просторный и ухоженный пляж. Есть возможность спрятаться в тени пальм от знойного солнца. Широкий, достаточно чистый, заход резкий

Nha Trang Beach is a true paradise for beach lovers. With its crystal-clear waters, soft golden sand, and vibrant atmosphere, it offers the perfect blend of relaxation and adventure. You can enjoy swimming, snorkeling, or simply sunbathing under the warm tropical sun. The beachfront is lined with lively cafes, seafood restaurants, and luxury resorts, making it a great destination for both budget and high-end travelers. Whether you're looking for a peaceful retreat or an exciting beach getaway, Nha Trang Beach is definitely worth a visit!
고운 모래사장과 맑은 물은 생각보다 더 매력적이었다. 파라솔 아래 누워 책을 읽거나 바다에서 함께 수영하며 시간을 보냈다. 저녁노을은 정말 환상적이었다! 다만, 해변 주변 상점들의 지나친 영업 제안은 살짝 피곤하게 느껴졌다. 그래도 따뜻한 햇살과 바다는 모든 걸 보상해줬다.

White sands, blue seas - what’s not to love?! Great infrastructure- flat, level paths which run along the beachfront. Good for walking/running/cycling Lovely beach bars & restaurants. Lots of sun loungers which are in good condition. Well groomed beach with no litter.On an evening it’s lively and good fun with music, dancing and a great vibe.
We just floated, laughed, and forgot everything else. Randomly, a crab tried to steal our snacks. 10/10 would get crab-robbed again!

We walked along the beach at 6am in the morning whilst waiting for our hotel room to be ready. At this time it was a hive of activity, with the sea full of people swimming. Others were doing their morning exercises.It was fascinating to sit and watch all the activities. The sea waves were quite big, whether this was because of the different tides? Lots of sun lounges and umbrellas.

The pristine white sand and crystal-clear waters were perfect for our romantic getaway. While it can get crowded during peak hours, early mornings were blissfully peaceful. The beachfront restaurants offered excellent seafood, and the sunset views were absolutely magical. Worth noting that beach vendors can be persistent.

The long stretch of pristine sandy beach was perfect for our romantic walks and sunbathing sessions. The bustling beachfront promenade added a lively vibe to our trip, with plenty of spots to grab a quick bite or refreshing drink. We loved people-watching and soaking in the energetic atmosphere. Whether we were splashing in the crystal-clear waters or lounging under colorful umbrellas, this beach had it all.

two weeks ago, i revisited Nha Trang, i came to Nha Trang beach twice. mainly for pics taking purpose. i dint swim in the ocean. but did lying on the sunbed, and had a short but memorable relaxing hours here. the beach just beside many high-rise buildings, make it quite different from other beaches in Vietnam. the feeling here is good ,very relaxing, if i revisit Nha Trang next time, i will spend more time here.

Wow, this is the best beach in Vietnam with long walking white sand beaches next to the city (7km long). It's very clean. There some rubbish you can see but not very much given the fact that the beach is right next to a big city, it's very clean. City workers, local people are some tourists clean the beach daily. It feels like a huge swimming pool with clear water. A lot of fishes and beach wildlife (shrimp, crabs, starfish...). People here swim and exercise until midnight because it's very safe and the city has beach's lights on. Food is great and fresh. Just don't use the beach chair because someone will come and ask you for money (if you don't mind paying for it). I have been to many beaches in Vietnam and so far Nha Trang is the best one with warm weather, no storms, chilling night and beautiful ocean.

I was staying in  a hotel by the beach so it's nature that I was drawn to it. Living by the sea back home and having been in the sea for 58 continuous months this was heaven. A couple of swims after breakfast and another 2 in the afternoon, month 59 was a breeze. The warm, clear flat water won me over!!
눈부시게 빛나는 에메랄드빛 바다, 잔잔하게 밀려오는 파도, 그리고 황홀한 일몰까지. 나트랑 해변은 그야말로 완벽한 열대 낙원이었습니다.  사랑하는 사람과 함께 아름다운 자연 속에서 휴식을 취하고 싶다면 나트랑 해변을 강력 추천합니다.

我和爱人最爱芽庄海滩的夜生活！白天我们在沙滩上享受阳光，晚上这里就变成了热闹非凡的不夜城。各种酒吧、餐厅、夜市沿着海滩一字排开，灯火通明，音乐声、欢笑声此起彼伏，让人感受到这座城市的活力。我们喜欢坐在海边的酒吧，吹着海风，品尝着当地美食，欣赏着来来往往的人群。形形色色的游客和当地人，构成了芽庄海滩独特的风景线，让人忍不住驻足观察，感受不同的文化和生活方式。总之，芽庄海滩的夜生活绝对不容错过，推荐给大家！

The scenic beauty is truly breathtaking,with soft white sand and crystal clear water.  We spent our days lounging, swimming, and soaking up the sun in this unforgettable destination.  Highly recommend for couples seeking a relaxing and romantic getaway!

This destination was perfect for a couple. The delightful weather, providing both coolness and sunshine, made our diving experiences and reef exploration even more enchanting. The pristine white sand was the perfect backdrop for leisurely walks by the water. The fresh and delicious food enhanced the enjoyment of our trip. It was truly a trip to cherish.

Nha Trang beach has a very beautiful view and is clean. This place attracts many foreign tourists to visit, sightsee and swim.Along the beach is Tran Phu street filled with traffic, 4-5 star hotels, and shops...Along Nha Trang beach, there are small parks with small benches for people to sit, relax and walk.

Es gibt eine riesige Auswahl an Hotels und Resorts für jedes Budget und direkt am Strand spenden malerische Palmen angenehmen Schatten. Wir haben die leckeren Snacks an den Straßenständen probiert und der Vergnügungspark hat uns super unterhalten. Perfekt für Paare und Familien, die einen abwechslungsreichen Strandurlaub suchen - wir kommen bestimmt wieder!

Sauber und nicht überfüllt! Man wurde auf seiner Liege in Ruhe gelassen und das Wasser war herrlich mit weichem Sand flach abfallendem Meer ! Ebenfalls kein Lärm durch Boote :) Perfekt für einen Badetag am Meer 🏝️👍
The sun rises over the beach along Nguyễn Thị Ngọc Oanh Street. The fishing boats returned around 4:30 - 5:00 am.Deux amoureux sur la plage.

Пляж в Нячанге очень хороший. Его ежедневно чистят. Но туристы и местные иногда продолжают оставлять после себя мусор. На пляже крупный песок, вода в основном теплая даже зимой, в летний период волн меньше. Отличное место чтобы насладиться видом, подышать морским воздухом, отдохнуть... Вот с отдохнуть, я думаю, будут проблемы. Так как пока мы были на пляже к нам постоянно приставали навязчивые местные. Я понимаю что они пытаются заработать денег. Но турист не может купить все у всех предлагающих. А после вежливого отказа, навязчивый вьетнамец или вьетнамка могут ещё и послать тебя далеко... на английском и вьетнамском. Предлагают морепродукты, фрукты, очки, кошельки, картины и много другого...через каждые 10 минут. Релакс....Но это только один минус, в остальном место прекрасное❤️
Early in the morning, on the beach, people concentrate with meditation exercises, take yoga poses or use the sports facilities along the Bãi dương beach.
The beach is definitely one of the best and extremely eye catching. Lots of cafes, bars along about a 10km sea front. Water is warm and very clear, perfect for a little soaking
Ra Nha Trang thì phải đi dạo vòng biển rồi, gió mát, cũng không quá đông đúc, lâu lâu được tắm biển thích quá trời

Nhắc đến Nha Trang chắc hẳn nhiều người nghĩ ngay đến tắm biển. Nơi đây có những bãi biển đẹp và luôn đông khách du lịch trong nước và quốc tế. Những bãi biển đẹp của Nha Trang là điều không cần bàn cãi: bãi biển thoai thoải, cát mịn và làn nước trong xanh như pha lê
Nếu bạn đã một lần đặt chân xuống mảnh đất xinh đẹp Nha Trang thì chắc chắn bạn sẽ mãi vương vấn hương vị của gió, nắng và cát mà bãi biển Nha Trang nơi đây mang lại
Bãi biển đẹp và trãi dài, bãi cát sạch và trắng. Thu hút được nhiều du khách nước ngoài đến tham quan tắm biển.

東南アジアで経験したなかでは最高のビーチです。パウダーサンド？と表現すれば良いのか、きめが細かく、砂利、石ころ、貝殻の破片、海藻、ゴミが全くありません。ダナンのミーケビーチ同様朝焼けも素敵で、しかも水もキレイです。(ミーケビーチは濁っていました。ニャチャンはダナンと違い、珊瑚礁のある海で気候が違うからとの現地ガイドの説明でした)これもまたミーケビーチ同様、現地の人はサンライズから沢山の人が海に入りにきたり、皆でヨガやダンス、体操をして、その後仕事に行くそうです。陽も昇り、暑くなってからは海外ビジターが多くなります。
A very good and beautiful beach, the water is warm, the weather is also good. Sometimes it gets a bit dirty but the water itself is very clean.
Clean nice beach. Full of local crowd with lots of activities on the beach. Suitable place for family with kids outing.

We were on our honeymoon when we visited the beach. It is called as the most relaxing beach in Vietnam and there is no doubt on that. Both of us enjoyed the entire tour and we got the ultimate relaxation here at Nha Trang beach.

Amazing, stunning beach with many choices beachfront hotelsVery soft and white sand beach Many choices of local good foods around the connerHotels and foods in Nha trang are much cheaper than other Vietnam tourist destinationsGreat choice to stay in Nha trang

Nha Trang is a beach town. And personally, for our family the best find in recent times. We love  beach towns that are not very TOURISTY yet have an Element of Fun and different food options and minimum hawkers. And i don’t know whether it was the post covid effect or what , the beach was clear of dirt , not too many and pushy hawkers , like in many other asian beaches in Thailand  Goa and even Bali are full of. So the beach is lined by a beautiful and wide walking and track , then a beach road and finally line of sea facing Multi storied Hotels and Condotel . We were there in September and the water was warm , swimming was fun as the buoyancy was good and yes it suddenly gets deeper, just a few meters away from the edge . So watch out . Be careful. Though the waters were not very choppy as it is a bay . And the most attractive thing is it is very long . So if you’re s runner or walker , or even a casual holidayer , you will love your uninterrupted time. We totally loved the time spent on the beach. No loud shacks other that a sole beach club that starts its high decibel machines later in the evening until late at night, to keep the hotel guests staying right opposite awake 🤨. All in all 👍
Excellent beach. Beautiful but tide in February was high. There are many good restaurants are located in near. Watched sun rise through clouds.
if you want to see this beautiful view, you can just go you tube - then find 'JelajahAsiaJourney' channel about Nha Trang beach...I made video on the beach you guys can see it and review...
The beach is absolutely magnificent. Truly beautiful, great location, Beach is around walking distance to the city center. Wonderful services.

We really enjoyed Nha Trang beach. Had a partial view of it from the 29th floor of our apartment. Nice beach bar right on the beach. Prices were not too bad. Lots of umbrellas and chairs. Beach was not the real fine sand, but still was lovely. Hope to get back there one day.

Our stay at Nha Trang got better by the day. The beach is long, perhaps 3 miles, very clean and just great in all aspects.The only slight negative comment would be that some of the skyscraper hotels mean that large parts of the beach are in the shade from around 2.30pm.
Sparkling blue water, immaculately clean. You can hire lounger, towels, umbrella and have drinks and food brought straight to you. Street hawkers add to the atmosphere.

Beautiful beach with reasonably priced loungers (70,000) restaurants are a little over priced but everywhere near the beach front is, expect to pay a little more or eat before arriving. Water is crystal clear and amazingly refreshing

Big, great for swimming and lounging. Very organized chair systems, great walking paths and parks, as well as vendors and food and drink accessibility which if you’ve been to Danang which has far less organization Nha Trang Beach is a breath of fresh air.
Great Beach! Ocean is nice to swim in. Lots of shops around when needed. Really liked hanging out here.

The first thing I noted on this beach that came to mind straight away was how clean and litter free it is As I Scuba diver I hate to see plastics and rubbish in the water and make a point of picking it up. Noticed this being done by a local and a beach clean on a night by a machine Walking along this wonderful beach where you can buy lobster cooked in front of you, T-shirt’s, cigarettes, dresses and most other  tourist things, without any hassle We used this beach for four days and felt very safe and trouble freeSwimming is good once you have negotiated the surfNot sure I would allow a child to stray as it slopes off and their is a heavy pull from the surf All the same we had an excellent time and we thoroughly recommend it
Stretching almost seven miles of pristine sand that warms the souls of your heart and makes you never want to leave. Warm balmy water.
Great Beach clean Nice beachwalk sunchairs and Cloud to town.. dont miss it! Great place för every one
Go for a walk early in the morning and join everyone in some exercise. It’s a great experience and a perfect way to start your morning.
The beach area is excellent for swimming and a variety of water activities--or for just relaxing and haveing a drink.
The beach is definitely one of the best and extremely eye catching. Lots of cafes, bars along about a 10km sea front.

We really enjoyed visiting here. We bought sun loungers for the day opposite the Novotel hotel. They were very comfortable and came with umbrellas for 100 vnd per sun lounger. I gather that they can be found for somewhat cheaper along the beach.The sea was a very nice temperature and the huge waves were a delight to splash around in. There was also a toilet block just behind the sun beds where we were not charged.

You cannot visit Nhatrang coastal town without dipping your feet in this beach. A fine sandy without any pebble beach stretching for 5 km and more, bordered by beautiful hotels and good restaurants and shop, this is one of the best in the world and can be compared to Miami or Cancun beaches.

This beach was wonderful, it does remind me of Miami a little bit as well with the boardwalk and beautiful beach. Everything is very clean and the water is so nice. You can rent chairs and umbrellas for fairly cheap and enjoy a nice day on the beach.I went across to the other islands to enjoy the beach there but from a budget perspective you should just do this, the beach is beautiful enough to enjoy.

Loved it! Spent time walking up and down this 5km stretch of beach. Lots to see and do near by. The waves can be a bit rough so it is not really a swimming beach, but we had a blast being pushed around by those waves...it stays shallow for quite a while.

The beach is a great walk.  It goes for miles.  There are plenty of vendors along the way in case you might need a cold beverage. Lots of different restaurants directly on the beach and right across the road.

Beautiful beach with nice large waves just right for body surfing. There is not much natural shade, but plenty of sand and people. The water was a nice temperature for swimming and, as I said, the waves breaking on the beach were big and lots of fun to play in. I recommend going in the morning. Not too many people on the beach at 8 AM. By 10 AM it was really filling up but only a few people were in the water. The wind picks up in the afternoon.
Beautiful beach with park all the way along to get shade. Shade from the palm trees on the beach too and see if your hotel has a private section. Just gorgeous

Nha Trang Beach is very long some areas are divided by rock outcrops, so that if you are a beach lover and don’t like one area you can walk or drive to another area. I liked the rock outcropping that you can walk to from the main Blvd. there are Beach Clubs and all kinds of drinks and food on offer too. It was amazing to see lobster everywhere, in the windows, on the streets, even on the Beach! Nha Trang, has a lot of Russian tourist. Everywhere things are written in Russian. On the signs, on advertising too. They even speak to me in Russian. When I can’t speak Russian they drop me like a hot potato and chase some other Russian. If you want to try some Russian food Vietnam style head to Nha Trang....

This is an excursion that definitely should not be missed.
Located about 9 Kms south of Da'Nang,  Ngũ Hành Sơn or Marble Mountains are a group of 5 limestone and marble hills named after the five elements in Eastern philosophy: Kim (Metal), Mộc (Wood), Thủy (Water), Hỏa (Fire), and Thổ (Earth).
The mountains are dotted with caves and tunnels used historically as Buddhist sanctuaries and even hospitals during the Vietnam War, Buddhist pagodas and temples, and panoramic viewpoints. The largest and most visited mountain is Thủy Sơn (Water Mountain), with its breathtaking caves among which Am Phu Cave is an absolute must.
Entry is 40,000 Dong plus an additional 30,000 Dong if you choose to use the elevator (highly recommended) to reach Am Phu Cave instead of using the stairs.

I visited Ngũ Hành Sơn (Marble Mountains) on a clear morning and it stood out as one of the most memorable natural and cultural sites around Da Nang. The cluster of five limestone and marble hills rises dramatically from the flat surrounding area, and just approaching them you get a sense that this is a place with history and presence. The name itself refers to the five elements in traditional Vietnamese belief, and each mountain has its own character and features.

The experience begins with the walk up steps and pathways that wind through greenery, small shrines, and weathered stone. It is worth starting early to avoid the heat and enjoy the views without too many crowds. As you ascend, you come to several cave entrances and temples carved into the rock, each with details that show how locals have used this landscape for spiritual and cultural practices over centuries. The Huyen Khong and Tang Chon caves are especially impressive, with natural light filtering in and creating a calm, almost reverent atmosphere inside.

From several vantage points near the summits you get panoramic views of Da Nang, the coastline, and surrounding countryside. The contrast between rugged stone formations and the soft blue of the sea in the distance makes for great photos but also for a satisfying sense of space and perspective. There are spots that feel quiet and contemplative, and others where families and visitors gather, but the flow feels natural rather than crowded.

Service at ticket booths and guides around the site is friendly, and there are well‑marked paths and stairs so you can explore at your own pace without worrying about getting lost. There are also small vendors selling drinks and snacks at base points, which is handy after a few flights of stairs.

Ngũ Hành Sơn manages to balance natural beauty, cultural depth, and accessibility in a way that makes it worth a full morning of your day. Whether you are into nature walks, temple architecture, or just great views, it rewards the effort with scenery and atmosphere you won’t forget.

Absolutely incredible, do yourself a favor (if you are in the health to do so) and take the stairs. The sense of accomplishment from the hike up adds to the splendor of this magnificent marvel. I was absolutely awestruck and so glad I didn't skip this. There are big beautiful  and giant Buddhas in these caves!!! We didn't know how long we would spend but I would say be prepared to spend some of your day here, we enjoyed exploring and spent somewhere between 2-3 hours. Marble Mountains is one of the well-known tourist attractions in Da Nang. The area is well organized with proper ticket counters, signboards, and maps to help visitors navigate the site.

There are two main attractions inside, and separate tickets are required for each. The caves and rock formations are natural and quite interesting to explore, with temples and viewpoints inside the mountains.

However, the place feels a bit commercialized compared to what you might expect from a purely natural site. Since the area is relatively small, many attractions are packed closely together.

Overall, it’s still worth visiting if you’re in Da Nang, especially for the caves and viewpoints.

Ngũ Hành Sơn là một trong những điểm tham quan nổi tiếng tại Đà Nẵng, thu hút du khách bởi vẻ đẹp hùng vĩ và giá trị tâm linh đặc sắc. The Marble Mountains are one of the most famous attractions in Da Nang, captivating visitors with their majestic beauty and rich spiritual significance. This complex consists of five limestone hills representing the five elements: Metal, Wood, Water, Fire, and Earth, creating a unique landscape near the coastal city. Visitors can explore mysterious caves, visit ancient pagodas, and enjoy panoramic views of the sea from above. The atmosphere is both sacred and poetic, making it suitable for sightseeing, worship, and photography. The Marble Mountains are an ideal stop for anyone discovering the charm and culture of Da Nang.

2 cô bán vé thang máy điểm du lịch Ngũ hành sơn lúc 16h45' ngày 27/2/2026 nói dối khách rằng: chỉ có vé đi lên thôi không có vé từ trên xuống, khi lên rồi thì tự đi bộ xuống. Tôi là khách từ An Giang vượt đường xa đến, do chân yếu nhưng quyết tâm tham quan một phần Ngũ hành sơn nên tôi hỏi: Mua vé đi lên, vậy ở trên có vé xuống không, tôi hỏi hai ba lần đều nhận được câu trả lời như trên, thật sự lúc ấy tôi nghĩ là vậy, tôi tiếc nuối đến chân thang máy để nhìn đỡ tiếc, vô tình thấy khách từ thang máy đi ra nên tôi hỏi anh nhân viên bảo vệ (cao to nước da ngâm) sao có khách từ trên xuống? và anh ta trả lời có vé từ trên xuống nhưng trước 17h25'. Nếu tôi mua được vé tham quan khi đến giờ ấy cũng thõa mãn. Thử hỏi nhân viên như thế thì có xứng đáng phục vụ khách ở điểm sáng du lịch hay không? Xin lãnh đạo Ngành du lịch Đà Nẵng xác minh và xem xét vấn đề trên để Ngành Du lịch ngày càng tốt hơn. Chân thành cảm ơn. Merry Hotel 23h57' ngày

I stopped at Marble Mountains for 2 hours enroute from Da Nang to Hoi An (it added 250k to the price of my ride for the car to wait).  I arrived at 9am, and was swiftly intercepted by the lovely Su who said I should go the 2nd entrance and do the anticlockwise route as it was quieter.

I spent about 90 minutes visiting the caves, temples and the highlight, the viewpoint from the peak. The views were spectacular.  The steps are steep and made of marble, so caution is advised.  The hospital cave is also a beautiful place.  I had a coconut in cafe area between the hospital cave and the viewpoint, it was delicious.

As I made my way round to the main temple (10.30am), it was extremely busy, so I didn't spend as much time there.

On the way out, as expected, I was again intercepted by the lovely Su, who after much haggling exchanged a piece of the marble mountains sculpted into figurine for a not insignificant amount of dong!

I had a coffee and left for Hoi An.

It's worth it for the view alone.  Get there early to avoid the crowds and good luck avoiding Su!

Góry Marmurowe (Ngũ Hành Sơn) w pobliżu Da Nang to jedno z tych miejsc w Wietnamie, które absolutnie warto zobaczyć. Już samo wejście między wapienne wzgórza robi ogromne wrażenie – połączenie natury, duchowości i historii tworzy wyjątkowy klimat.

Największe wrażenie zrobiły na mnie świątynie ukryte w jaskiniach. Naturalne światło wpadające przez otwory w skałach, kadzidła unoszące się w powietrzu i monumentalne posągi Buddy tworzą atmosferę spokoju i refleksji. To miejsce ma w sobie coś mistycznego, ale jednocześnie nie jest przytłaczające – można tam spokojnie spacerować i chłonąć klimat.

Widoki z punktów widokowych są spektakularne – panorama na Da Nang, morze i okoliczne wzgórza robi ogromne wrażenie, szczególnie przy dobrej pogodzie. Schody bywają strome, ale wysiłek zdecydowanie się opłaca.

A spiritual and cultural highlight in Da Nang – Highly recommended!
After some rather "touristy" experiences in the city, the Marble Mountains were a breath of fresh air. This place offers everything I was looking for: deep culture, impressive history, and breathtaking views.
The blend of natural limestone caves and beautifully integrated Buddhist pagodas creates a unique, spiritual atmosphere. Climbing the stairs is worth it for the panoramic views over the coast, but the highlight for me was the intricate stone carvings and the quiet energy inside the caves. It feels authentic and respectful of its heritage.
If you appreciate history and want to experience the "real" Vietnam away from the flashy tourist traps, this is a must-see. It’s affordable, well-maintained, and truly memorable. 5 stars without hesitation!

The Marble Mountains
are a stunning blend of natural beauty and spiritual heritage. The panoramic views from the top are absolutely breathtaking, offering sweeping sights of the sea and surrounding landscape. The mountains are home to beautiful temples and pagodas built within caves and along rocky paths, creating a peaceful and sacred atmosphere. Exploring them on foot involves a long and sometimes steep trek, but the effort is well worth it for the incredible views and unique cultural experience.

The Marble Mountains are a renowned cluster of five limestone and marble hills. The mountains are situated about seven kilometers from downtown Da Nang. Visitors can explore the caves and temples, climb the stone steps, or take an elevator to the top for panoramic views of Da Nang city and the coastline.

Some nice views of the Marble Mountains (Ngũ Hành Sơn) and the surrounding village in Da Nang, Vietnam. The Marble Mountains are a cluster of five limestone and marble hills, each named after one of the five elements: Metal, Wood, Water, Fire, and Earth.
The image shows a view from one of the mountains, specifically Thủy Sơn (Water Mountain), which is the only one accessible to the public and features numerous caves, tunnels, and Buddhist pagodas. Visitors can climb the steps or use a lift to reach the pagodas and enjoy panoramic views of the coast and the nearby city of Da Nang. The area is a popular tourist destination, blending natural wonder with spiritual and historical significance.

I just visited Marble Mountains in Da Nang, and it was a wonderful experience! The area is incredibly beautiful with its dramatic cliffs and impressive pagodas tucked away in the lush landscape.

One thing I really appreciated was the trail itself. Even though there are quite a few stairs and climbs, there are plenty of spots to take a break along the way. You don't feel rushed, as there are natural stops where you can sit and enjoy the view of the city and the coast. We chose to take the stairs all the way up instead of waiting for the elevator, and it turned out to be much faster than standing in line!

Practical info:
Facilities: There are good restroom facilities along the route, which makes the trip much more comfortable and manageable.

Refreshments: You don't need to carry too much water yourself, as there are several places along the way where you can buy cold drinks. It was a very welcome relief in the heat!

The caves are an absolute highlight – the light coming through the ceiling creates a truly magical atmosphere. Highly recommended if you are in Da Nang and want a trip that is both beautiful and well-equipped with facilities!

五行山由五座石灰岩峰组成，水山最值得游览。门票便宜（约2-3万越南盾），可乘电梯上山节省体力，但雨季可能停用。山顶灵应寺历史悠久，玄空洞光影神秘，明王洞描绘阴间景象颇具特色。山顶可俯瞰岘港全景，风景绝佳。游览约需3小时，但台阶陡峭湿滑，不适合老人、小孩及行动不便者。注意避开雨季，防滑倒；警惕当地"热心人"引导后讨要小费。建议穿舒适鞋子，Gate2上Gate1下，顺路游览美溪海滩。虽部分区域攀爬费力，但洞穴奇观与海景值得体验。

Started our Danang trip from this beautiful spot. The marble mountains are a group of five limestone karst peaks.

The pagoda temples and cave temples are atop the marble mountains which can be accessed by road / steps or capsule lift erected on the mountain. We being senior citizens took the easy path - the elevator which was a short ride.

The sea beach and cityscape from the mountain top are wonderful views.

There are a couple of pagoda temple on the top and also a few caves with various idols inside. Surprisingly, one of the caves house the stone carving of Hindu god Shiva and Parvati.

Overall a great site to visit in Danang. Must visit.

마블마운틴은 한마디로 “동굴 속에 불상과 사원이 들어있는 산”이라는 게 가장 큰 매력입니다. 동굴과 석굴을 따라 걷다 보면 곳곳에 불상과 제단이 이어지고, 전체가 산속 미로 같은 동선이라 탐험하는 재미가 있어요. 저는 여러 동굴을 둘러봤는데, 특히 마지막 포인트(안쪽 깊은 동굴)는 규모가 큰 동굴 내부에 불상이 자리해 분위기가 압도적이었습니다.
다만 이곳은 생각보다 오르내림이 많아서 체력 소모가 큰 편입니다. 처음부터 걸어 올라가면 꽤 힘들 것 같아요. 저희는 엘리베이터를 이용해서 훨씬 편하게 관람했는데, 가족 단위라면 엘리베이터 선택이 정말 현명합니다.
입장료: 1인 약 40,000동(별도)

일부 동굴/포인트는 추가 입장료(저희가 간 곳은 20,000동)가 따로 필요했습니다. (현장 안내판 기준)
중간에 전망 포인트로 잘못 들어가서 막다른 계단을 내려갔다가 다시 올라온 구간이 있었는데, 이건 정말 힘들었어요… 😅 동선 표지판이 헷갈릴 수 있으니, 초반에 안내 표지/포인트 번호를 꼭 확인하고 움직이는 걸 추천합니다.
그리고 린응사원처럼 여기에도 강아지들이 여기저기 바닥에 편하게 누워 쉬는 모습이 자주 보여서, 아이들은 은근히 그걸 더 좋아하더라고요.

Marble Mountains is a tourist place near Danang which has five beautiful decorated caves, fully illuminated and decorated. King Minh Mang in 19th century named these caves on the five eternal elements of nature i.e. Water, Wood, Fire, Metal and Earth. Indian mythology has the same eternal elements except wood and metal which are replaced by sky and air. A beautiful place to visit. To know more please watch my videos by searching my youtube handle DRSKVERMA-ze9qi. Vietnam has a separate playlist along with China, Hongkong, Kualalumpur, Singapore, South India, Delhi, Bihar, Gujrat.

Las Marble Mountains (Ngũ Hành Sơn en vietnamita) son un conjunto de cinco colinas de mármol y piedra caliza.
Lo especial es perderse por los senderos, cuevas y subir muchas escaleras si quieres ver
sitios increíbles. Combina naturaleza, historia y espiritualidad.
En su interior hay cuevas naturales, pasadizos y santuarios budistas que se han convertido en lugares de culto y peregrinaje durante siglos.  ￼

Thuy Son — la montaña principal: Esta es la más visitada y la que concentra los mejores miradores, templos y cuevas accesibles para turistas.  ￼

Vistas panorámicas: Desde varios puntos en lo alto puedes ver la ciudad de Da Nang, la costa y el mar cercano — ideal para fotografías, especialmente al amanecer o atardecer.  ￼

Cuevas y templos:
• Huyen Khong (cava iluminada por la luz natural).
• Am Phu (cueva con esculturas y simbolismos budistas).
Aquí puedes sentir la atmósfera espiritual y la historia tallada en piedra.  ￼

Pagodas y santuarios:
Templos como Tam Thai, Linh Nham o Xa Loi Tower ofrecen espacios de calma y arquitectura tradicional.  ￼

Miradores y naturaleza:
Las escalinatas y senderos llevan a puntos panorámicos donde apreciar el paisaje y el contraste entre el mármol blanco y el mar azul.  ￼

Non Nuoc Stone Carving Village (al pie):
Al bajar, puedes visitar talleres donde los artesanos transforman mármol en esculturas, recuerdos o piezas decorativas.  ￼

Tips prácticos:

Tiempo recomendado: Reserva entre 2 y 3 horas para explorar con calma.  ￼
Calzado cómodo: Hay muchas escaleras irregulares y caminos de piedra.  ￼
Evita horas de mucho calor: La mañana temprano o la tarde suelen ser más agradables.  ￼
Vestimenta respetuosa: Especialmente en templos y zonas religiosas.  ￼
Ascensor opcional: Si no quieres hacer todas las escaleras, hay un ascensor opcional (con costo adicional) que facilita el acceso a zonas altas.  ￼
Costos orientativos
• Entrada a Thuy Son (la montaña principal): unos ~40 000 VND.  ￼
• Ascensor opcional: unos ~15 000 VND por trayecto.  ￼
• Entrada a la cueva Am Phu: aproximadamente ~20 000 VND.  ￼

📍 En resumen

Las Marble Mountains son una visita muy recomendable si te interesan la cultura, historia y naturaleza de Vietnam. No es solo una formación rocosa: es un lugar donde puedes:

✔️ Explorar cuevas antiguas
✔️ Admirar templos budistas
✔️ Disfrutar de vistas espectaculares
✔️ Conocer artesanías locales

The Marble Mountains（Ngũ Hành Sơn） 是越南岘港附近非常著名的自然與文化景點，也是游客必访的地方。其名字 Ngũ Hành Sơn（五行山） 意味着五座石灰岩山峰，分别代表 金、木、水、火、土 五行。

⸻

🏔️ 基本介绍
• 位置：越南岘港市南部，距离市中心约 9 公里，靠近美溪海滩。
• 组成：由五座石灰岩山峰组成：Kim Sơn（金山）、Mộc Sơn（木山）、Thủy Sơn（水山）、Hỏa Sơn（火山）、Thổ Sơn（土山）。
• 特色：山中有天然洞穴、佛教寺庙、雕塑、观景台，是自然景观与宗教文化结合的地方。
Énormément de monde

Visiting Marble Mountains was one of the most memorable experiences in Da Nang—a beautiful mix of nature, spirituality, history, and panoramic views.

We explored Thuy Son (Water Mountain), the most accessible of the five. You can climb the steps or use the elevator partway up, which helps conserve energy for the caves and temples. The caves are the highlight—natural formations with shafts of sunlight, quiet shrines, and a calm, almost meditative atmosphere. The pagodas scattered around add to the spiritual feel, and despite the popularity, many corners remain peaceful.

The viewpoints at the top offer sweeping views of Da Nang city and the coastline—great for photos and for simply soaking it all in. The complex is well maintained with clear paths and signage, though there’s a fair amount of walking and stair climbing, so comfortable footwear is essential.

One practical tip: after rain, some steps and stone paths can be slippery. Take your time, wear shoes with good grip, and be cautious—especially inside caves and on shaded stairs.

Overall, Marble Mountains is a must-visit in Da Nang. It offers far more than just views—an immersive experience that blends natural beauty with culture and spirituality. Absolutely worth the time and effort.

Las Montañas de Mármol (Ngu Hanh Son) son un conjunto de cinco colinas de piedra caliza y mármol en Da Nang. Cada una tiene el nombre de uno de los cinco elementos (metal,tierra, fuego madera y agua), hay cuevas, santuarios budistas y unas increíbles vistas panorámicas.
Estas montañas han sido un lugar de peregrinación budista durante siglos,y son una mezcla de espiritualidad y naturaleza. 🙏🏼❤️
La principal, Thuy Son ( del agua), tiene la Cueva Huyen Khong, un ascensor de pago o escalera...

I didn’t expect much at first, but this mountain was beyond my expectations. It’s not just about the pagodas and temples, mostly Taoist-style. but also the amazing city and sea views from the highest peak. The entrance fee was cheaper than many other places too. Definitely a hidden gem in Da Nang.

Exploration
Thuy Son (Water Mountain) is the main, developed mountain with pathways, pagodas, and caves . But the real magic for adventurous souls lies beyond the main path. The "wild" slopes of Hoa Son (Fire Mountain) offer a thrilling, off-the-beaten-track experience, where you can find hidden altars in deep caves and discover secret passages to the summit with nothing but the sound of bats for company . The sense of discovery there was unparalleled.
Hiking on Top and Into the Depths

· On the Summit: The climb up the ancient stone steps  is rewarded with absolutely stunning 360-degree panoramic views. From the highest peak on Thuy Son, you can see the skyscrapers of Da Nang to the north, the coastline of the South China Sea stretching to the horizon, and the ancient town of Hoi An fading into the south . It feels like standing on top of the world.
· In the Caves Below: Exploring the cave networks is mind-blowing. The largest, Huyen Khong Cave, is a cathedral-like chamber where shafts of sunlight pierce through openings in the ceiling, dramatically illuminating ancient Buddha statues and creating an ethereal, spiritual atmosphere . Another cave, Am Phu Cave (the "Cave of Heaven and Hell"), features fascinating light installations that depict Buddhist cosmology .
Pagodas and Tranquility
The pagodas are seamlessly woven into the landscape. Linh Ung Pagoda greets you with an ornate archway, while the nearby Xa Loi Tower houses 200 Buddha statues . Tam Thai Pagoda, dating back over 400 years, is especially serene in the early morning light . These sites offer perfect moments of peace.
Practical Tips for Your Perfect Visit

· Timing is Key: Go as early as 7:00 AM when the air is cool, the light is soft for photos, and you can have the pagodas and paths mostly to yourself . Avoid the midday heat (11 AM - 2 PM) .
· What to Wear/Bring: You'll be climbing many steps and exploring uneven terrain. Wear sturdy, closed-toe shoes with good grip . Dress modestly for the sacred sites (cover shoulders and knees) . A headlamp or strong flashlight is essential for exploring the deeper, unlit caves . Don't forget water, sunscreen, and a hat .
· Logistics: The entrance fee for the main Thuy Son mountain is 40,000 VND. An elevator (15,000 VND one way) is available if you prefer not to climb all the stairs .

Final Verdict: The Marble Mountains are a non-negotiable must-see. Whether you seek spiritual reflection, historical depth, adventurous caving, or simply some of the best views in Vietnam, this place delivers it all in one unforgettable package.

Definitely worth visiting if you’re in Da Nang. It’s a unique mix of nature, caves, temples, and viewpoints all in one place. The mountains rise straight out of flat land, so the contrast is impressive, and once you’re inside the caves and pagodas, it feels calm and spiritual.

There’s quite a bit of walking and stairs, but the views from the top are rewarding — you can see the coastline, the city, and the surrounding area. The caves are interesting and atmospheric, with natural light coming through openings in the rock.

Overall, a solid stop near Da Nang that combines history, nature, and views in one visit.

The Marble Mountains (Chinese: 五行山) are a scenic area consisting of six limestone mountains rising above a coastal sand dune, covering an area of approximately 2 km², located about 8 km southeast of Da Nang city center, Vietnam, right on the Da Nang - Hoi An road; now belonging to Ngu Hanh Son ward, Da Nang city. The Marble Mountains include: Moc Son, Thuy Son (the largest, highest, and most beautiful), Tho Son, Kim Son, and Hoa Son (with two peaks: Duong Hoa Son and Am Hoa Son).

From the summit of Thuy Son, a part of the Marble Mountains landscape can be seen.

On March 22, 1990, this scenic area was recognized as a National Historical and Cultural Monument by the Ministry of Culture (now the Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism).

According to the Decision approved by the Prime Minister on July 11, 2023, the Ngu Hanh Son scenic area in Hoa Hai ward, Ngu Hanh Son district, has a total planned area of 1,049,701 m2. The eastern side of the scenic area borders Truong Sa road and coastal resorts; the western side borders Co Co River; the southern side borders Co Co River and existing residential areas; and the northern side borders Pham Huu Nhat road and the resettlement area.

Regarding functional zoning, the Prime Minister adjusted and expanded the scope of Protection Zone I of the scenic area to 189,821 m2, including the area of the landscape of the six mountains (Kim Son, Moc Son, Thuy Son, Tho Son, Duong Hoa Son, Am Hoa Son), Ghenh mountain (8,373 m2), and the rocky mountain southeast of Am Hoa Son (328 m2).

The scenic area features valuable religious and architectural structures and relics, residential buildings combined with businesses specializing in stone carvings, functional zones, themed green spaces, water features, sculpture gardens, and temples commemorating martyrs and heroic Vietnamese mothers.

The Marble Mountains are a really fascinating place to visit in Da Nang and definitely one of the city’s most unique attractions. I visited as part of a tour, which made everything easy and gave helpful context about the history, temples, and caves in the area.

The mountains are filled with beautiful pagodas, stone steps, viewpoints, and natural caves, and the views from the top are absolutely worth the climb. There’s a strong spiritual and historical atmosphere throughout the site, and it feels very different from anything else in the city.

That said, some of the caves and pathways can get quite tight and congested, especially during busy times. If you’re claustrophobic, it’s something to keep in mind — a few areas are narrow and can feel crowded. Taking your time and following the open paths helps a lot.

Overall, it’s still a memorable and rewarding experience, and I’d highly recommend it to anyone visiting Da Nang — just be prepared for stairs, crowds, and a bit of adventure along the way.

Перед посещением стоит учесть несколько важных моментов, о которых обычно не пишут в путеводителях.

Во-первых, парковка для байков. Официальной парковки рядом с входом для мотоциклов нет — есть только автомобильная. Охрана намеренно не пускает туристов на байках, чтобы вы оставляли их у ближайших лоточников. Там с вас либо возьмут 1–2 доллара за «парковку», либо будут настойчиво уговаривать что-нибудь купить. Самое удивительное — при мне местный вьетнамец спокойно заехал на мопеде без каких-либо проблем. Когда я указал охране на этот момент и спросил, почему ему можно, а мне нет, в ответ получил лишь ухмылку. Такой вот «сервис» в одном из самых туристических мест региона.

Как только вы съезжаете на дорогу к входу, к вам буквально будут выбегать люди, размахивать руками и уговаривать припарковаться именно у них. Не останавливайтесь, не вступайте в диалог — спокойно проезжайте дальше и ищите любое свободное место, главное — не прямо напротив торговых лавок.

Во-вторых, кассы и билеты. Если вы даёте крупную купюру и ждёте сдачу, будьте максимально внимательны. При мне девушек передо мной обманули на 300 000 донгов, а затем попытались сделать то же самое со мной. Кроме того, кассир «по умолчанию» добавил к билету платную карту и какие-то фото за 15 000 донгов. Сумма небольшая, но дело принципа — я вернулся, потребовал вернуть деньги и отдал эту карту обратно. К слову, карта абсолютно не нужна: она бесплатно висит на стенде перед входом, а также есть в отзывах с фотографиями.

Вывод простой — будьте готовы к тому, что попытки обмана там встречаются на каждом шагу.

Также обязательно берите воду с собой. На территории продают бутылку за 20 000 донгов, тогда как в обычном магазине она стоит 5 000. Не стоит переплачивать и поддерживать этот «туристический бизнес».

Что касается подъёма — ступенек действительно много, ходить придётся вверх и вниз довольно долго. Рекомендую брать билет на лифт: он реально экономит силы, а стоит всего 15 000 донгов. Поверьте, по лестницам вы ещё успеете находиться.

Visiting the Marble Mountains is definitely worth it. The place is full of history, spirituality, and stunning scenery. The views from the top are beautiful, especially overlooking the coast and the city, and the temples and pagodas hidden among the mountains create a very peaceful and unique atmosphere.

If you plan to use the elevators, be prepared for some waiting lines, especially during busy hours, so it’s a good idea to allow extra time. Wearing comfortable clothes and proper shoes is highly recommended, as some caves are humid and the stairs can be slippery.

One thing to keep in mind is that there are many shops around selling jade products, statues, and souvenirs. While they look nice, most of them are not authentic, so be cautious and don’t buy them assuming they are real jade.

On the positive side, the walk is very diverse, combining nature, culture, and architecture. The caves are impressive, with natural light coming through the openings, creating a magical atmosphere. Overall, it’s a very enriching experience and quite different from other tourist attractions in the area.

2 cô bán vé thang máy điểm du lịch Ngũ hành sơn lúc 16h45' ngày 27/2/2026 nói dối khách rằng: chỉ có vé đi lên thôi không có vé từ trên xuống, khi lên rồi thì tự đi bộ xuống. Tôi là khách từ An Giang vượt đường xa đến, do chân yếu nhưng quyết tâm tham quan một phần Ngũ hành sơn nên tôi hỏi: Mua vé đi lên, vậy ở trên có vé xuống không, tôi hỏi hai ba lần đều nhận được câu trả lời như trên, thật sự lúc ấy tôi nghĩ là vậy, tôi tiếc nuối đến chân thang máy để nhìn đỡ tiếc, vô tình thấy khách từ thang máy đi ra nên tôi hỏi anh nhân viên bảo vệ (cao to nước da ngâm) sao có khách từ trên xuống? và anh ta trả lời có vé từ trên xuống nhưng trước 17h25'. Nếu tôi mua được vé tham quan khi đến giờ ấy cũng thõa mãn. Thử hỏi nhân viên như thế thì có xứng đáng phục vụ khách ở điểm sáng du lịch hay không? Xin lãnh đạo Ngành du lịch Đà Nẵng xác minh và xem xét vấn đề trên để Ngành Du lịch ngày càng tốt hơn. Chân thành cảm ơn. Merry Hotel 23h57' ngày

Trải nghiệm tham quan thì mình không muốn đề cập tới vì đó là cảm nhận trải nghiệm của từng người. Mình chỉ muốn lưu ý các bạn mua vé phải cẩn thận. Mình không muốn nghĩ xấu, nhưng hành động của các chị bán vé làm mình cảm giác như mình bị lừa. Giá vé tham quan là 40k, giá thang máy là 15k/chiều. Trước khi tới quầy mình cũng nhẩm trước (nhóm mình 3 người). Có 2 trường hợp: TH1 là vé + 1 chiều thang máy : 55k x 3 =165k. TH2 là vé + 2 chiều thang máy : 70k x 3 = 210k. ( Mình chia 2 trường hợp bởi vì trên bảng bán vé chỉ ghi thang máy 1 chiều lên, mình tính lại hỏi chiều xuống có bán ko ) . Khi mình lại mua thì nói là mua vé tham quan và thang máy đi lên cho 3 người. Chị bán vé cũng đưa vé và báo giá 195k. Mình cũng hơi ngờ ngợ nhưng cũng đi ra và nghĩ là chắc có khi là bán vé thang máy hai chiều lun , mua 2 chiều sẽ đc discount. Không hiểu sao tự dưng thấy gì đó cấn cấn, cái cầm 3 vé quay lại hỏi ( mình chỉ bước đi tầm 5 bươc là quay lại liền). Hỏi chị : ủa chị em mua vé này là thang máy 1 chiều hả chị. Chị gật đầu đúng rồi em, xong mình hỏi tiếp ủa nhưng mà nếu vậy em tính phải là 165k chứ đâu phải 195k đâu chị. Chị mới nói tiếp ủa vậy là em không lấy bản đồ hả? Mình mới nói ủa em đâu có mua bản đồ gì đâu, mà chị cũng đâu có đưa em bản gì? Trong lúc mình nói mình có để ý chị kế bên chị bán vé mới cầm 3 cái xấp bản đồ định đưa mình, nhưng nghe mình nói xong rút lại liền. Sau đó chị mà nãy giờ trả lời mình trả lại 30k cho mình. Từ những hành động đó mình thấy từ đầu mấy chị tự đưa giá mà ko hề nói mình có bản đồ, và ko hề đưa bản đồ cho mình. Đến lúc mình quay lại thì thaoo tác rất là nhanh như biết trước tình huống vậy.

This was the only negative experience we had during our entire trip to Vietnam. We visited this site after doing our research and knew exactly what we wanted 6 entrance tickets with elevator access. We clearly repeated this more than three times at the ticket booth, but the staff gave us entrance-only tickets and told us the elevator payment was “on the other side.”

When we got to the elevator ticket booth, the staff told us we had the wrong tickets and needed to buy another 6 tickets that included elevator access. I asked what we should do with the tickets we just bought, and she casually said, “Go refund.” When I returned to the original booth, the woman there abruptly said “No refund,” dismissed me, and literally walked away before closing the booth.

It was clearly a scam, especially since the person behind us had the exact same experience—but with even more people in their group. It felt like they were deliberately taking advantage of tourists who don’t speak Vietnamese.

Very disappointing and dishonest. We will not be returning or recommending this place to others. Tourists deserve to be treated with respect, not scammed.

При том, что они периодически делают какие-то поручни у лестниц. Достопримечательность не приспособлена для посещения некоторых людей. Даже может быть опасной.

A total tourist rip-off. We bought tickets at 4:45 p.m. and went into the cave, but we didn't know that it closed at 5 p.m. No information but taking the money. At the deepest point of the cave, the lights suddenly turned off. Luckily, we had our cell phones with us, so we didn't have to imagine what it would be like for people without cell phones to survive in the dark cave. There was no one at the exit and no one checked whether all the visitors had left the cave - a safety disaster.

The tourist spot itself is stunning and offers beautiful scenery, well worth a visit. However, the elevator service is a complete disaster. There were no instructions provided, and when we returned to the elevator at around 5:30 PM, it was already closed. Upon calling the hotline, we were told that security had already gone home. It was shocking that no staff ensured all tourists had safely left the area, essentially leaving us stranded in what felt like a “jungle” before they shut everything down and left for the day.

While I’d give four stars for the natural beauty of the location, the service deserves ZERO stars for such poor management and lack of responsibility.
