This place itself is beautiful, beautiful culture of Buddhism and a historical site. But I had some strange experiences here. The bottom area is a bad tourist trap. You will be harassed to buy things regardless of if you say no and keep walking you will be followed around.
**I paid the 40k entrance fee and purchased a map for an additional 15k, which came with some beautiful postcards. Once I was on the mountain I was admiring the first temple. A monk came up to me and literally snatched the map/postcards out of my hand and ran away. I saw him do this to one other tourist as well. This was the official map/postcards from the ticket office, but apparently this monk decided the postcards were offensive or something? I don’t know… but he took it upon himself to literally steal them from me and run away. So irritating. “Robbed by a monk” is not something I would have ever expected lol.
I’m very curious about this experience. If you know anything about what his motives might have been, feel free to respond.
Cheers

Rubbish strewn approach. The antithesis of soulful. No traffic lights in area hence you take your family’s life in your hands when traversing the roads.

Here is our experience as two 26 year old frenchies. The place is very beautiful but unfortunately the Vietnamese have no respect for nature, they have moved far away from their traditions. From beginning to end it is polluted by waste absolutely everywhere in the trees, in the caves, on the paths. I literally saw a family of Vietnamese (pictures attached) who have a stand where they sell drinks to tourists, throwing bags and bags of waste into cave holes that are several million years old while there are also signs telling tourists not to throw it on the ground, what a shame. Also sad, the Vietnamese apparently do not like the noise of nature, birds, there are loudspeakers every 50 meters with music (like Pirates of the Caribbean, it's not a joke), or authenticity as they put fake Flowers around each pagodas so the Instagram pics can look better. In addition to this sad story, a lot of tourists who do nothing but take pictures in front of a pagoda and leave, sad tourism linked to our social networks. Honestly we would have not come if we knew.

Definitely not worth it. Too crowded. No safety rails on any of the stairs other than the main one. Most people are smoking publicly even though it's a hazard on top of the mountain with so much greenery around. Very steep stairs.

There are 2 entrances , one to visit the cave and one to visit the top of the mountain , need to pay VND 20k per person for the cave then move on to the other side to move to the top , where they make money for entrance fee (VND 30k per person) plus VND 15k per person  for elevator just to go up. After you visit all the places climbing up and get tired and come back to the lift and they tell you that you need to pay another 15k for the lift down. Never seen this kind of steal anywhere.
They have a few temples built recently , and constructions are still going on, may be in another few years down the line there could be some history made behind this to sell this out. If you have elders with you then dont plan this out as there is a lot of climbing to be done.

Beautiful place, however, be careful to not get scammed by the rude ignorant lady near the elevator counter.

She charges an extra hidden fee for a "map" without telling you. So please becareful and make sure you count your change.

She knew she was wrong when I went back to her and told her my change was wrong. So shes doing it on purpose to pocket the change.

SO BE CAREFUL OF THIS RUDE IGNORANT LADY AND HER SCAM

この施設、大手の観光情報サイトにも釣り銭詐欺の注意喚起がありますが、本日チケットブースで
釣り銭詐欺に遭いました！
クレームしたら返金されましたが
びっくり‼️
ベトナムダナンに詐欺師がいたとはびっくりしました。
政府も警察も取締りに動いて欲しいですね！

Horrible place useless place. Very expensive and worthless. The entry doesn’t include elevator ticket. And you need to pay separately to come down as well. Absolute nonsense waste of time place. No need to go here as a tourist you will regret. No point terrible nonsense.

We went with the assumption it was going to be a beautiful walk through some beautiful caves but ended up being completely man made and had a weird “heaven and hell” theme. The “hell” theme was really weird and creepy and completely put me off, while the “heaven” theme was full of the typical religious women statues and drawings. Just wanted to see some cool caves but ran into another man made scam 😕

NEVER EVER EVER AGAIN!!!
The place doesn’t worth it but worst thing it’s a SCAM! They make you pay for elevator and ONLY WHEN YOUR UPSTAIRS in a SUPER RUDE way they kick you out and ask to pay again if you wanna go downstairs!!! Super rude man with unacceptable behaviour toward guests, they are all people arguing with him and he scream and make faces and just keep you blocked for half an hour!!!
A SHAME!!!!!!
Really avoid this place!

Völlig uberfüllter Tourispot. Es gibt 2 kleine Fahrstühle an denen Massen von Menschen stehen. Wir haben uns dann dagegen entschieden hoch zu fahren. Auch der Ort ist überfüllt und man wird von allen Seiten bequatscht.

I wouldn’t even give it that!! So disappointing. Bought tickets to go up & down lift & when our group went to go on the lift we had to pay again…. It’s not the point of x dong but how we were spoken too. The mountain is beautiful but how the employees were I would stay by the pool and enjoy a beer.

Demasiado artificial y para ser algo  de reciente construcción el acceso a personas mayores, niños y obesos es muy muy complicado. Una señora anciana se cayó por la escalera delante mía. Perece un parque temático de religiones. No trasmite nada este sitio de cuevas. Las mismas esculturas dentro del recinto que fuera en las tiendas de alrededor.

This place is very overrated. At best it is just a place for killing time. Entrance fee is 40k dong and one way elevator ride is 15k dong. Once you are up, there are still some stairs that you need to walk to reach the caves. The caves floor are slippery.  Not sure what is the hype about the caves.  The best thing to see in my opinion is the view of the city once you are out of the elevator and that’s it.

Tourist trap. Huge crowds. Aggressive touts screaming. It is in poor condition. Unsafe as it’s a wet cave and there’s no traction on the floor. Slanted small steps. Absolutely hideous and cheaply made sculptures everywhere. Look like they’re made by an 8 year old. The neon lights ruin the atmosphere in the cave. Why do the locals turn everything into a wanna-be, low-budget Disney World?

SCAM ALERT!!! The aggressive vendors outside will force you to park at their shops and make you buy souvenirs. No government official will be present to give you directions to park. Although i can see "dear guests parking inside" board. the vendors see bikers are cash cows. Park a little further away from this spot at a cafe by having a coffee from them

볼거 별로 없고 힘들고 평일인데 사람도 너무 많고ㅠ
이런 곳에 입장료와 엘리베이터 비를 내고 갔다온 내가 바보 ㅠ

Horrible experience !
Don't go here.
It may spoil your holiday.
We tried to visit on motorcycles, but we were pestered constantly by shopkeepers wanting us to park at their shop - supposedly for free.
They were worse than timeshare sales people !
One followed us everywhere we went on a scooter.
We eventually left without seeing anything.
Una mugre por donde mires, 0 mantenimiento y cuidado. Una pena

Perhaps the only negative tourism experience in 3 weeks travel across Vietnam. Scammed by old women on scooter who flagged us down to park our bike for ‘free’ at her shop. When leaving, purchased a tiny statue from her, charged x5 of what it should have been. Not much to see at the attraction, over crowded and poorly managed

Wir hatten hier keine gute Zeit!

Die Marble Mountains sind sehr überlaufen und man hat nie kurz Ruhe. Die Aussichtspunkte sind teilweise zugewachsen. Die Aussicht selbst ist oft auf eine relativ verbaute Vorstadt. Es liegt einiges an Müll herum. Die Aufstiege sind teilweise glatt und rutschig.

Bei einem Besuch den teuren Aufzug vermeiden (Fahrtzeit 20 Sekunden für 40.000 Ding pro Person + Eintritt). Der schönere und ruhiger Aufstieg ist per Treppen ein gutes Stück links vom Aufzug bei Gate 1. Alternativ ist etwas näher rechts vom Aufzug auch Gate 2, wo man hochlaufen kann.

Zuletzt noch eine brauchbare, schönere, ruhigere und günstigere Alternative: Monkey Mountain und Umgebung im Norden der Stadt.

If you’re a foreigner you will get many rude locals trying to get you to park your bike and pay them on sidewalk. I declined and parked on street away from them but saw many falling for the trap. The energy there felt very dark and it just felt like a tourist attraction that didn’t have much to see. I was in and out in 20 minutes.
It costs 20,000 dong

La gente que vende son MUY pesados. Eso se junta a los que quieren estafarte por aparcar la moto. Es una imagen malísima del país. Mucha suciedad dentro del monumento. A resaltar la cueva principal, el resto es muy prescindible. Recomendable subir en ascensor, ya que dentro vas a seguir subiendo muchas escaleras

입장권구입시 인원에 맞게 계산되었는지 반드시 확인하세요. 어수선한 틈에 1인분을 더 추가하여 계산했습니다. 관광지 매표소라서 방심 했네요.
내려오는 엘리베이터 가격은 영수증이 없습니다.
보행이 불편한 경우에는 관광이 대단히 한정적이니 참고하세요.

진짜 직원 존나 싸가지 없어요 아무리 그래도 관광지인데
길거리 밥집보다 손님응대가 형편 없어요 다신 올일도 없지만 베트남와서 이런곳 처음이네요. 엘리베이터 티켓팅하실땨 주의하세요

이 뜨거운 날씨에 불상보러 사람 붐비는곳...
정신 올바르면 안갈듯 합니다.
생각없이 갈 필요도 없는곳..여기 갈시간에 그냥 시원한 카페에서 쉬세요

Shocking.. everything from everything. From the parking, to the entrance fee, to the behavior of the locals at the site, and also the fact that there is not much to see. If you still feel like it, go there and enter a small cave with 1000 other people
Cool scenery, but the vendors are terrible. An old lady used her motorbike to try and herd me into her parking area and almost hit me. I almost feel sorry for them after seeing how they behave.
It is a very crowded place with many statues of people torturing. We did not go to the top so I am not sure about the view.  But I am reviewing based on what we saw inside the cave.
Marble mountains are beautiful, however the management is wicked..no directions and you have to pay for the elevator two times..to go up and then to go down

NOT ENJOYABLE. As soon as you arrive there are multiple people waiting for you urging you to park so they can make a profit. The places they have you park would be free anywhere else in Da Nang but since they provide a ramp they get to charge? And there is a huge parking lot at the mountain but apparently only vans/buses are allowed.

Also, the mountain was quite boring. Way too many people and not able to enjoy. I would completely skip this and go somewhere else. Views were not good, caves were meh, and people were everywhere. Better off going to lady Buddha.
매표소 직원이 돈을 횡령하는것 같습니다. 엘레베이터 2way로 달라했고 돈도 그만큼 냈는데 나중에 내려오는 엘레베이터 타려고 하니 1way티켓이라고 돈을 더 내라고 합니다. 다들 조심하세요

حتى نجمه كثير عليها ، لانها ماتناسب المسلمين عاديه جدا ورسوم في كل شي تدفع للدخول وتدفع للكهف وتدفع للاصنصير طالع وتدفع له نازل ودرجات متعبه حتى للرياضي وفي النهاية مافي شي تقول عنه يستاهل بصراحه مضيعه وقت على الفاضي بالمختصر تنفع فقط للبوذيين عشان كلها معابدهم ورموزهم واللي يحب يستطلع يروح

Terrible place to visit. No brochures or map given. Alot of steep steps up and down. You need excellent physical strength to visit here. No site people to assist you for direction or if you are injured. One way ticket up and buy another ticket down.
旅行團必排行程，有來自各國的觀光客。但整個很無聊，真的建議可以不用來。

The cashier tried to steal 100k from me. Based on the reviews and the way how easily she gave me the money back it is probably something common.
Only one cave was worth the visit.

Очень красивое место, но отвратительная работа персонала

Мы уже подходили к лифту, чтобы спуститься вниз (лифт закрывался в 17:30, мы подошли в 17:25) и прямо перед нами закрыли ворота и не впускали. Мы купили спуск вниз, но персонал нас не впускал

На горячей линии нам ничего не ответили, только посмеялись

У меня была сломана нога, просто ужасно испортило все впечатление

Надеюсь этот работник будет всю жизнь подниматься на 100-й этаж по лестнице и будет всегда застревать в лифтах

Staff there are rude (for Vietnam standards), sights are interesting, but they’ve all got statues carved in them. Would be much better if it was left the way they were naturally. There’s zero safety measures there, probably alright for a dry visit, but so many people were slipping during the wet weather. Take care if you choose to visit, wear appropriate footwear as there’s heaps of steps, the elevator only takes you to the start.
Good place for avid hikers, NOT for family with kids. NO HANDICAP personnel allowed.

Same issue with the elevator scam. Told the ticket seller for 2 way elevator trip and in the end was given 1 way and 1 entrance ticket despite repeating for many times. You will only find out upon exit that the scam and the tickets are all written in Vietnamese language. I wonder the local authority is looking into this?
В праздники особенно с детьми делать здесь нечего, заранее изучайте маршрут- лабиринты!!! Блогеры как обычно все приукрашивают, ничего особенного..
밑에서 엘베 왕복 티켓 끊었는데 위에서 다시 사라고 합니다. 위에도 티켓 사는거 있으니 왕복 끊지 말고 편도로 사세요. 눈탱이 오지게 침. 볼 것도 없으면서
바닥이 엄청 미끄럽고 햇볕이너무뜨겁고 더워서 구경이고뭐고 제대로 안봄..ㅋㅋ 일사병걸릴것같아서 30분도안있음. 엘베는 올라갈때만타고 내려갈때는 그냥 걸어내려옴 ㅋㅋ부모님과같이갔는데 덥고 힘들어서 짜증내고 비추ㅋㅋㅋㅋ야생선인장과 산세베리아 자라고있었음ㅋ 얼마나더우면 선인장이... 숨이턱턱막힘ㅋㅋ
Rien d’authentique, rempli de touristes, très bruyant à cause de tous les guides, des vendeurs partout même dans la montagne…

Parkt nicht bei den ganzen Zigeuner Frauen die einen dann nach der Tour nicht rauslassen wollen, ohne das man etwas gekauft hat. Wir wollten paar Souvenire kaufen, der Preis für einen kleinen Kopf aus Stein 500k, die Kinder wollten es unbedingt haben. Habe dann am Straßenrand für 80k gekauft, bei einer netten Oma, dort hätte man auch umsonst normal parken können. Tickets billig, der Berg nichts besonderes, geht zu der Lady Buddha, ist viel schöner und angenehmer. Es war sehr schwierig sich von dort wo wir geparkt haben, "ein Souvenir Laden" zu verabschieden, gehört stattlich reguliert, reine Abzocke der Touristen!

왜가는지 이해를 못했다...
사람만 많고 안전요원 없고
천국은 위험하기만 하고 쓰레기 더미에 볼품 없었음

Unless visiting the many (beautiful) statues is a must for you, then you can give this a miss. Whilst inside is beautiful, it is one cave with a few statues. You can find nicer temples around Vietnam that are much more accessible.

1 star is for the rude, arrogant lady at the ticket office. She won't listen to any of your queries, just keeps yelling NO! The place is beautiful but the staff are rude. Inside the caves it gets really dark post 4pm. I would suggest taking the elevator instead of the stairs. The stairs are very uneven and steep! If it rains it's even more dangerous. Also try to complete the visit before 4pm, after that there are less people. It's on a steep hill, grab drivers take really long to accept the ride after 5pm. There are fake Grab drivers who follow you saying grab app, grab bike, just say a firm NO to them.
You can’t even ride towards the entrance without being bombarded with 20 different people trying to get you to park at their place to charge you some outrageous fee
不友善的地方很陰森  路難走潮濕時路會打滑 階梯跨距過大 廁所需脫鞋會踩到水 不建議特地前來

입장료가  경복궁보다  비싸요
볼게 없어요 왜 돈을 받는지  차라리 영흥사가 훨씬 깨끗하고 편안해요  영어  안내도 없고 관광 안내 팜플렛도 돈주고 팔아요 어이가 없었습니다

Just when you arrive every shop owner is yelling at you to charge you for parking. Then you go to the entrance, you buy a ticket for 20000 just to learn later that actually that ticket is only for the first cave and that you have to pay an entrance again for 40000 + 15000 for the elevator (one way only). After all that you will have the pleasure to watch the caves accompanied by thousands of tourists and the original soundtrack of Pirates of the Caribbean.

Очень странная страна. Тут огромный паркинг комплекса, но только машины и автобусы могут тут парковаться. А байки должны искать себе где то место не звестно где - и тут целая мафия из местных жителей которая предлагает эти услуги рядом с их палаткой.
Никогда не видела ничего настолько плохого и дискоиминирующего по всей Азии.

티켓팅하는데 엘리베이터 신청했는데 10만동내고 3만동 받았어요.  올라가는것만해서 1.5만동인데..
사기치지마십쇼

Als ik 0 sterren kon geven had ik dat zeker gedaan. Als eerst waren we naar de grot gegaan wat opzich prima was. Wel jammer dat de tekst niet in het engels was want wij konden niks lezen.

Wij dachtten dat met de ticket van de grot je ook naar de lift toegang kon krijgen dat was niet zo geen probleem mijn vriend haalde een nieuwe ticket voor de lift. Toen we onze nieuwe ticket lieten zien zei de man dat we alsnog geen toegang konden krijgen. Toen wij vroegen waarom kregen we een raar antwoord dat deze voor de lift was en niet voor het uitzicht. Toen we opnieuw wouden vragen waar deze ticket dan wel voor was kregen we alleen een nee.

Mijn vriend ging een nieuwe ticket halen en toen hij vroeg of hij tickets kon omruilen voor de goede zei de ticket verkoper dat hij geen tickets meer kon krijgen omdat hij vroeg om de tickets te ruilen.

Verder is het een grote touristen val ik kom hier nooit meer terug🫠.

Nice to see, but seems to be a scam hub so be aware of scams and pickpockets. Driver may be part of the scam, telling you there is a pit stop to use restroom and water break at a near by marble store. And reason was that there is no restroom on Marble Mountain, which is not true there are restrooms on the mountain. Once you walk in the store staff pressure you to buy, even telling you that you must buy because you used their bathroom. Overprice fake trinkets. Also be aware of pickpocket on the mountain steps. Hope this helps others from not being scammed or theft.
볼것도 없고 직원들도 불친절하고 세치기는 일상인.....다시는 가고 싶지 않은

왜...가는지 이유를 찾지 못한 곳.
1. 일단 이곳의 모든 직원들이 다 불친절하다. 제일 최악은 티켓 판매원들.
2. 관광지인데 안내가 불친절하다. 물론 검색을 제대로 안한 탓도 있겠지만, 엘베 타는곳과 그렇지 않은 곳이 이어져 있지 않음을 몰라서 이중지출을 해버림.. 어지간하면 엘베만 타고 올라가서 보는 장면들을 추천합니다.
3. 안전에 취약함. 특히 그 엘베 아닌쪽의 동굴 내부의 계단은 진짜 너무... ^^ 전 거길 다 올라가면 엘베탄 사람들과 조우하는줄 알고 험한 길을 열심히 올라갔지만... 같이 올라간 우리엄마 + 주변 외국인들 다같이 허탈 ㅋㅋㅋ
4. 멋진 경관을 망치는 인공의 구조물들. 대체 왜;;?
5. 중간에 크메르제국을 연상케하는 곳들도 있고해서 뭐.. 그치만 걍 앙코르왓을 한번 가보심을 더 추천합니다.
Not a tourist place that must be visited. Although there is an elevator to go up but there are still many stairs to climb. Not worth visiting.
No worth to pay for only 3 level lift. The view was nice.

Not worth visiting per my experience. Seems more like a tourist trap. At the entrance, shopsellers will walk with you for a while and ask you to buy something from their shop. The view from the top is ok but not something I'd climb for again. Only the main cave was fine. The elevator is chargeable 15K in addition to entry fee of 40K. In fact when I went to use the toilet which was labelled as 'free of charge', at the top of the mountain, the lady over there did not allow me to use it. The place was stinking. She demanded I should purchase something from her to use the toilet! I was not in a rush to use the toilet but Imagine if someone was really in need to use the toilet up on the mountain, they are forced to buy something before relieving themselves. Very bad experience as a tourist! Vietnam is a beautiful country and I love Da Nang, but experiences like this do not leave a positive impression.

Overall as well, the place is okay but was not worth the visit for me.
very aggressive cashier. I couldn't pay via OR code of Vietnamese bank, they only accept cash. disappointment

Accès au temple par ascenseur, aucune authenticité, et rempli de touristes chinois qui hurlent... On entend les travaux dans la ville même en haut !
Aucun intérêt en bref.
정신없고 관광객은 가도 살 게 없음
티비에서 보던곳보다는 매우 실망. 가는길 미끄럽고 가파르고 시설관리 전혀..입장권비쌈
Turistada como la copa de un pino. Cuevas hay 1, el resto son surcos en la montaña.

Well to be Brutally honest, this place is over hyped and nothing to be fancied about. (Detailed Vietnam video on YouTube #explorewithshourya )You can see such sculptures across multiple places on earth. You are not missing out on something. I mean it’s a good place to visit if you have extra time to kill. Don’t stretch to fit this into your itinerary. It lies on the way between Da Nang & Hoi An. Plan accordingly but don’t go separately only for this. If going, buy tickets with elevator included. Also not to forget - it’s buzzing with crowd and mostly once they reach the top, I found them confused :)

입장권 카드결재 거부하고 현금만 받습니다. 비오는날 절대 가지 마세요. 계단이 대부분 대리석인데 아주 미끄럽습니다. 가파르고 위험한 계단구간에도 옆에 안전한 난간이 없는 경우도 있습니다. 무릎 관절 약하신 분들은 가지 마세요. 유료 엘리베이터 타고 올라가서도 계속 계단입니다. 걸어서 내려오는 코스도 계속 계단입니다. 계단이 많은 것만 문제가 아니라 한 계단마다 높이가 높습니다.
Bei Ankunft Überfall durch penetrant aggressive Souvenir Verkäuferinnen, sind nach 10 Minuten wieder gegangen.
صعود ونزول كثير بالدرج الصخري ومتعب جدا مع العائلة والاطفال واذا كان مطر الله المستعان

I liked the place, but I would like to warn visitors about the old lady scammer who manages the toilet. The toilet is free of charge but she said that I need to buy something from her. I decided to do a good deed and help the lady. Of all the useless plastic things, I chose a mini snickers bar for 50,000 dong (2 usd). I gave her 500,000. At first she was in no hurry to give me change, thinking that I do not know how to count zeros, then realizing that I still expect change, she gave me banknotes 20,000 + 20,000 + 10,000 in the hope of confusing me with zeros. Of course, I told her and received my change. I regret that I bought anything from her at all.

Not worth it . Confusing ticketing ,very poor instruction and no assistance from staff. even if you baught lift tickets , lifts take you only half way. this info is written no where, its rip off.there are steep uneven steps to climb. I am reasonably fit and climbed steps and reached the peak but saw most people gave up., if you have young children,or not very fit skip this place, on a hot day its like a punishment,you rather stay in hotel pool or beach and relax.
**** main spot / Cave shown in pic is at very top and only few people make it
分2處分開購票，參觀前並沒有做調查，只聞五行山是旅遊點，地獄洞非常恐怖，另一面可以搭電梯上落（需要另外付費），沿途多梯級，6月初遊覽已屬盛夏，職員對待遊客並不友善，不建議到訪。

Horrible. Les employés sont extrêmement désagréables, les « villageois » vous suivent et essaient de vous vendre leurs cochonneries (absolument pas artisanales) de manière agressive (ils vous insultent en Vietnamien lorsque vous dites non ou les ignorez). Le site est rempli de touristes bruyants. Les « grottes » et « temples » sont inintéressants. Bref, un véritable piège à touriste sans intérêt sur lequel tous les guides et tours essaient de vous amener pour vous faire cracher un maximum de dôngs.

The ONLY reason I give this place 1/5 stars is because the woman at the ticket booth took our money.
The price is 40.000 for entry and 15.000 for the elevator. We were in a group of three so the price should have been 165.000. She took 180.000 from us and when confronted she yelled at us.

The views were great and everyone else on the mountain was very kind but we will never be returning. Buy your tickets in advance!

Fuimos al baño, había carteles donde claramente ponía que el baño era GRATUITO, “free charge” literalmente, cuando acabamos de mear la chica que custodiaba el baño nos intentó vender toallitas y pinzas para el pelo, le dimos las gracias y le dijimos que no, porque no necesitábamos nada de lo que nos quería vender, se puso agresiva y empezó a gritar y nos amenazó con la fregona, muy mal educada, nos hizo el viaje por la montaña y los templos muy incómodo, no se trata así de mal a la gente, no debería estar ahí esa mujer, un 0

여기 직원들 진짜 진짜 4가지 없네요 보기도 전부터 기분상해서 입장했어요. 두명이라고 표달라했더니 말다씹고 지들할거 다하고 들은척도안하다가, 몇명이냐 묻고 엘베 가격이랑 해서 맞냐물어보니 신경질팍팍내네요 앞에보니 거스름돈도 대충그냥 던져주고 그냥 딱 금액맞춰서 주는게맞을듯요 4만동 입장료+편도 엘베15000동 입니다 베트남잘돌아다니고 사람들 다착했는데 이사람들때문에 기분엄청 상하고갑니다

お土産の押し売りが酷いのはまぁ慣れたけど、チケット売り場の人までぼったくってくるのは
衝撃。。。

眺めてた感じ、日本人には吹っ掛けてるみたいで反論しているのも日本人だけだった。

まぁわざわざ行く必要は正直無いのかもしれないと個人的には感じました。ダナン市街地からも結構離れてるし、先ほど記載の通り山以外は何もないので。
行くのであればチケット等は多少手数料かかってもホテルとかで取ってもらったほうが嫌な思いはせずに楽しめるかと。。
그다지 . 10분만에 나왔습니다. 어르신과 아이들 조금 위험합니다.
볼것도 없는데 입장료가 싸지도 않고 그냥 인위적임

I'm sure it's worth a visit by organised tour, but I'd advise against turning up on a motorbike. You get hounded from the second you turn down the road with the "park at my shop for free" "where are you from" etc...
I'm sure there are options and maybe you'll find them, but I'd much prefer a designated parking area where you can pay a set charge.

As nice as the Marble Mountains may be, it’s beauty is completely over shadowed by the swarm of hawkers that surround you.
They continually block your path, tip you & wave their hands in your face.
I’ve honestly never seen it as bad anywhere else in Asia
(That includes the hawkers throughout India)

Before arriving anywhere near the mountain their tactics become apparent as they drive along side your bike to ask the usual “where are you from / where are you going” questions.
But once’s at the mountain it’s an absolute nightmare…

Groups of locals surround any tourist in sight, telling them to park “for free” at their shop. Of course this is a lie which they will demand payment for later.

Unfortunately the only official parking spot is for tour buses/vans, so guards on duty will also point you back to the locals for a place.

I can not describe in one review how annoying, in your face & draining they are, especially in the mid day heat.

At one stage we decided to leave the area to get a cool drink in a cafe down the road & to honestly just regroup as our minds were melting from the tormenting locals.

3 ladies on motor bikes followed us & while driving along side kept insisting we park at their shop when we return. Throughout our drinks they parked across the street waiting for us to move so they could hassle us again.

The whole situation has gotten passed the ridiculous stage & really needs to be policed as many tourists were leaving before even seeing the Mountains.

Until that happens, all I can advise is for you to avoid this area like a plague

Well chalk this place up
to another location ruined by over tourism. I miss the pandemic. They  bring people in by the bus load now from South Korea, but still only one elevator. Ridiculous, last year there were tourists sure , but it was balanced. Now it’s a typical Vietnamese scam , to just rob your money for an experience that is more stressful than relaxing.  Don’t come here , it’s ruined.

alles in allem bezahlbar. Oben geht’s dann aber los: Menschen Menschen Menschen. Ghé ngày 13/02/2022, địa điểm tham quan, có thang máy lên đỉnh núi. Giá vé thang máy: 40k/người/lượt. Đồ đá phong thủy, đá lưu niệm gia bán rất cao, nên trả giá xuống dưới 50% giá bán. Đến Ngũ Hành Sơn, du khách thường đến ngọn núi lớn Thủy Sơn, chùa Tam Thai, các hang động Huyền Không, động Linh Nham, động Vân Thông, động Lăng Hư, động Vân Nguyệt… Dưới chân Ngũ Hành Sơn có làng đá mỹ nghệ Non Nước với những tác phẩm nghệ thuật điêu khắc bằng đá được bàn tay các nghệ nhân địa phương cần cù tạc nên.

Opzich is het idee leuk, maar we kregen maar een uur van onze gids, wat nauwelijks genoeg is om 1 grot te bezoeken. Daarnaast moest je voor de lift omhoog én naar beneden betalen, terwijl ze beneden zeiden dat de lift naar beneden gratis was. Verder stond ik er ook bijna alleen maar stil door een grote groep voor mij die het rustig aan deed, laten we maar zeggen. Het uitzicht was wel mooi.

Перед посещением стоит учесть несколько важных моментов, о которых обычно не пишут в путеводителях.

Во-первых, парковка для байков. Официальной парковки рядом с входом для мотоциклов нет — есть только автомобильная. Охрана намеренно не пускает туристов на байках, чтобы вы оставляли их у ближайших лоточников. Там с вас либо возьмут 1–2 доллара за «парковку», либо будут настойчиво уговаривать что-нибудь купить. Самое удивительное — при мне местный вьетнамец спокойно заехал на мопеде без каких-либо проблем. Когда я указал охране на этот момент и спросил, почему ему можно, а мне нет, в ответ получил лишь ухмылку. Такой вот «сервис» в одном из самых туристических мест региона.

Как только вы съезжаете на дорогу к входу, к вам буквально будут выбегать люди, размахивать руками и уговаривать припарковаться именно у них. Не останавливайтесь, не вступайте в диалог — спокойно проезжайте дальше и ищите любое свободное место, главное — не прямо напротив торговых лавок.

Во-вторых, кассы и билеты. Если вы даёте крупную купюру и ждёте сдачу, будьте максимально внимательны. При мне девушек передо мной обманули на 300 000 донгов, а затем попытались сделать то же самое со мной. Кроме того, кассир «по умолчанию» добавил к билету платную карту и какие-то фото за 15 000 донгов. Сумма небольшая, но дело принципа — я вернулся, потребовал вернуть деньги и отдал эту карту обратно. К слову, карта абсолютно не нужна: она бесплатно висит на стенде перед входом, а также есть в отзывах с фотографиями.

Вывод простой — будьте готовы к тому, что попытки обмана там встречаются на каждом шагу.

Также обязательно берите воду с собой. На территории продают бутылку за 20 000 донгов, тогда как в обычном магазине она стоит 5 000. Не стоит переплачивать и поддерживать этот «туристический бизнес».

Что касается подъёма — ступенек действительно много, ходить придётся вверх и вниз довольно долго. Рекомендую брать билет на лифт: он реально экономит силы, а стоит всего 15 000 донгов. Поверьте, по лестницам вы ещё успеете находиться.

В целом впечатления от горы довольно спокойные. Это обычный природный объект в черте города. Лично я не испытал каких-то сильных эмоций — большую часть времени смотрел под ноги, чтобы не поскользнуться и не упасть, а не наслаждался видами

By being foreign, you may think that friendliness and hospitality is a common human trait but beware, these are facades.

Before turning on the street we pulled up alongside a cute older Vietnamese lady who greeted us with a smile, asking where we were from and if we were visiting Marble Mountain today—“her home”.

Of course we both turned down the street—nearing the entrance she chaperoned us towards her shop opposite the entrance (I’ll attach a photo). She willed us to leave our scooter with her, imploring us to go and enjoy the mountain and that our scooter will be safe for when we return. More on this later.

We pay for our tickets and ascend the stairs (our newfound host had told us that being young and fit we wouldn’t need the elevator). We noticed foreigners ignoring signs saying to remove footwear before entering the first temple, moments later indulged in seeing a old Vietnamese lady scold and slander them for being inconsiderate idiots.

The first cave we entered we had a very animated man bark very obvious directions as to the flow of the cave (where to go—when there’s clearly one direction). He almost tried to usher people along, although at the time there really wasn’t that many people so I didn’t understand his hustle. Despite this, near the exit, he had the audacity to take off his hat in a sign for charity? I told him I gave all my money to the person who issued our tickets—as that was the price to be here. He seemed beside himself that I said no (in the local tongue mind you), almost showing signs of distress as he moaned and borderline wailed while sulking ahead of us towards to entrance to the cave.

Nevertheless we begin our ascent. One of the caves we entered required some hands-on climbing to get into, on the way out my partner reached for a bolt that had been screwed into the wall—unfortunately for her it had a really sharp edge, cutting her finger.

We approached the area where some have mentioned the locals sell drinks all the while throwing their plastic into the caves behind them. We asked for a serviette so as to wipe the blood away only to be told— “you pay!”. Oh the humanity!

We continue on, blood dripping from finger, to a rather large cave where we bare witness to holier-than-thou tourists pretending to ascend to greatness in a sliver of light that has pierced the cave roof. A range of theatrics from corny to cringe.

On our venture towards the exit, we follow a sign to a pagoda, instead finding ourselves in somewhat of a surreal Japanese garden—with wind chimes, serenity and a view we didn’t expect. Continuing towards this supposed pagoda we realise we hadn’t seen anyone be that tourist or local in a while, eventually popping out a side road on the other side of the mountain. Turns out you didn’t have to pay to enter at all?

Now to return to our scooter, and despite my gut saying there’s a scam on the horizon, my heart really wanted it to be a wholesome exchange of local and tourist. Should have gone with the gut.

We were given wet wipes on arrival (needed) then insisted upon that we take a walk around the old ladies shop (filled with mass produced marble and stone miniatures). We didn’t want anything, but felt like we owed it to her to at least browse. You begin to feel a pressure to buy, with the attentiveness of the shopkeepers. When we hinted at maybe buying the smallest cat figurine, we were quoted 700,000 dong.

Laughable.

We left. Attached is a Google maps photo of the shop from the street—beware of the bucket hat. As for the mountain, as some have mentioned the rubbish is a turn off, but there’s a great cave to see and if you’re up for some cardio, a mountain to climb with views from the top.

五行山由五座高數十公尺的石灰岩石峰所組成，分別代表中國五行裡的金、木、水、火、土，所以被命名為五行山。因當地曾出產玉石，所以又叫大理石山，英文名稱為Marble Mountain。

五行山位在距離峴港市區以南約7公里，前往會安時會途經五行山，因此行程安排上，可將兩個景點安排在同一天，先到五行山爬山健行，傍晚再到會安，剛好趕上欣賞會安的夜色美景。

五行山最大山頭是水山，也是觀光客最主要的參觀重點。上頭有兩座觀景台，東邊是望海台、西邊是望江台，顧名思義，一邊可眺望海岸、另一邊可眺望漢江景色。

攀登五行山難度不高，如果不想爬樓梯，可購買電梯票，直接搭乘電梯即可直達半山腰。如果覺得自身體力還可以，也可以選擇爬階梯上山，約莫10分鐘即可抵達半山腰。攀爬五行山階梯時，要特別小心留意的是，部份階梯和石塊都是大理石，表面光滑，很容易滑倒。由於山上並未設置無障礙空間，因此對於行動不便的人而言，並不太方便。

五行山除了有多間廟宇和佛塔，包括三台寺、慈心寺和靈應寺外，在洞穴石窟裡，也有供奉多尊菩薩、羅漢塑像，雕刻都相當精美。因洞穴內溫度較低且陰涼，爬山爬累了可以在洞穴內稍作休息，端詳佛像莊嚴的面容與姿態，心情也會跟著平靜下來。

如果想要到各洞穴石窟探險，可以三台寺為中心點，洞穴分布在三台寺四周，每個洞穴裡頭可以說都是別有洞天，很值得前往冒險一探究竟。

要注意的是，部分洞穴的洞口很小，僅能容一人通過，需手腳並用，且洞穴較昏暗，攀爬的時候需要多加留意。爬出洞口時，視野頓時遼闊，可遠眺海邊及市區景色，對於喜歡來點冒險的人來說，是個值得前往的有趣景點。

◆五行山：傳說有神仙住過，因此整座山都是香的，非常神奇。五行山原為海中島，多少年的變遷成了陸地上的山。一開始名為五蘊山、五指山，西面有翰江，東面臨南中國海，在海邊平坦的沙灘上，水、木、火，金、土六座山峰（其中火山為雙峰）拔地而起，氣勢雄偉。由於風光秀麗。古人有詩寫道：「何處景色勝五行，不遜仙境是蓬萊。山光彩石峰浴翠，古寺香霧繞雲岩。」五山之中以水山最美最大，水山位於東北方，海拔一百零八米，山上有華嚴雲洞、華嚴石洞，靈岩洞、陵虛洞、雲通洞、藏珠洞、雲月洞及天龍洞等。洞窟安置有佛像，常有信徒前來膜拜。

Die Attraktion, war so überlaufen, dass wir kaum die Möglichkeit hatten, sie wirklich zu genießen. Es gab kaum Platz zum Atmen, geschweige denn zum Drehen oder um Fotos zu machen. Die Atmosphäre in den Höhlen war dadurch komplett verloren gegangen. Es war extrem enttäuschend und fühlte sich wie Zeitverschwendung an.

Der Massenandrang von Menschen, die wie eine Flut auf uns zu kamen, machte den Besuch kurz und frustrierend. Es war unmöglich, die Schönheit der Attraktion zu erfassen, geschweige denn sie zu genießen. Wir wurden einfach von der Menge an Menschen mitgerissen, ohne die Möglichkeit, uns zu entspannen und die Erfahrung in vollen Zügen zu genießen.

Es ist wirklich schade, dass solch beliebte Attraktionen oft von Touristen überlaufen sind und dadurch ihren Reiz und ihre Schönheit verlieren. Es ist wichtig, bei der Planung von Ausflügen solche überlaufenen Orte zu vermeiden, um echte und ungestörte Erlebnisse zu ermöglichen.

Insgesamt war dieser Besuch eine Enttäuschung und keine Erfahrung, die wir weiterempfehlen würden.

This place as so much amazing potential, small little shrines, with lovely views out over the sea and around the mountains.

Having said this, I’ve given the whole experience 2 stars as you arrive on a bike, you get about 10 people step out onto the road and try and make you park in their shop (don’t, as they will try and make you buy stuff after) just say no to those guys.

There is, in theory, official parking, but the staff want cars to park there as they have to pay more, so don’t let bikes park, in the end, we found a side road and parked down there.

Now this is only the parking part, now onto the mountains them selves.

There are hardly any signs telling you where to go or buy tickets, we had to to go 3 desks before they wanted to sell us a ticket, as we did not want to take the lift for 15k Dong.

Entrance is 40k each.

The stairs are not hard to get up, but bring some water, you’ll need it at the top.

The place was packed, full of people, kids trying to climb up things and preventing the flow of people, which meant a lot of waiting. I advice going in the afternoon.

Overall nice views, terrible parking and way too many people getting in the way of a lovely place.
Not much special unless you like to exercise a bit by hiking up instead of taking a lift.

Interesting place, but be prepared for crowds and hidden costs
Overall, it’s an interesting site to visit and worth seeing – but there are some things you should know in advance.

The area is extremely busy, and the atmosphere can get chaotic, especially when climbing the stairs. Many visitors are impatient and don’t respect any kind of order, which can make the experience stressful.

Be aware that the entrance fee is not all-inclusive. You’ll need separate tickets for different parts of the site, so don’t be fooled by the initial low price. There’s also an extra charge for the elevator – it’s optional, as you can also walk up, but still something to consider.

We found the staff on-site to be unhelpful and rather rude when we asked for information or assistance, which was disappointing.

On a more positive note, the souvenir shops nearby are great – prices were surprisingly fair compared to other tourist spots, and the selection was nice.

All in all, it's a nice place to see, but arm yourself with water and patience!

I stayed no more than 45 min and visited only 2 (out of about 10) attractions at Marble Mountain. Other than the heat and scorching sun, I didn't like the concept of having to queue for and pay a separate entrance fee at each of the attractions at Marble Mountain. Based on my experience, this place was overly crowded with rude tourists. Don't get me wrong the architecture and religious statues in there were very beautiful. However, we found that many of the visitors were not mindful of the experience of others. People were impatient, creating a bad environment. One older lady actually pushed my wife as we were descending the steep skinny staircase while we were waiting for the family in front of us move. This may not sound like much, but this narrow staircase is basically a vertical ladder ascending about 70m. After our experience, I would not recommend to a friend unless they were willing to endure the chaotic cluster of tourists.

ข้อเสีย
1. ภายในถ้ำจะไม่มีป้ายชัดเจนแสดงทิศทาง เพราะ ขนม. ต้องการขายแผนที่ราคา 15k
2. ภายในถ้ำ สภาพพื้น ลื่น และ ทางบันไดชัน ไม่เหมาะกับเด็กและผู้สูงอายุ อันตรายมาก
3. อากาศภายใน ไม่มีการระบายที่ดี นักท่องเที่ยวจะเผชิญกับความอึดอัด และ เสี่ยงต่อโรคระบาดที่ติดต่อทางลมหายใจ
4. เมื่อขึ้นไปถึงบนสุด ไม่มีอะไรน่าสนใจ เพราะเป็นเพียงภูเขาเตี้ยๆ
5. ร้านขายของ ร้านขายของชำ หน้าถ้ำ ขายสินค้าในราคาที่แพงกว่าท้องตลาด ประมาณ 3 เท่า ยกตัวอย่างเช่น กาแฟกระป๋อง ที่นี่ราคา 30-40k vnd.

Tính đến tháng 4 năm 2018, vé vào cửa có giá 40.000 VND và vé thang máy là 15.000 VND. Vé vào cửa là bắt buộc. Hang động phía sau rất tráng lệ, nhưng vào các ngày lễ, nơi đây có thể trở nên đông đúc với nhiều người chen chúc lên xuống cầu thang hẹp.

Touristenfalle !

Not meant for people who have climbing difficulties. The elevator service is only upto the 1st level. Thereafter you have to climb up and down every steep stone-cut stairs to multiple levels. Only advised for young fit persons.
If you have even mild knee problems, DO NOT GO!
Pls note this place is not advisable for those who can’t climb the steep flight of uneven steps to the temple entry.

Sicherlich ein schöner Ort. Wir kamen mit dem Moped und wollten unser Moped lediglich auf dem vermeintlich kostenfreien Parkplatz abstellen. Wir haben uns unsicher gefühlt.

입장료.엘베2장씩 110k인줄알고 갔는데 표랑 엽서를 은근슬쩍 넣어주고선 계산기에 125k 막 찍음.ㅋㅋ잔돈도 은근슬쩍 한장 빼고 주고.장난꾸러기.한두번 한솜씨 아닌것 같으니 조심하세요
한국 사람들이 왜 높은 평점을 주는지 모르겠다. 덥다고들해서 아침 개장시간 7시에 맞춰갔어도 산이랑 동굴이 한국이랑 다르게 바람이 불거나 시원하지 않다. 엘레베이터 덕에 편하지만 정자나 산꼭대기 지점도 덥고 볼게 없다. 동네 뒷산느낌. 엘베타고 9번 10번 동굴 갔다가 3 2 1순으로만 보고 오는 걸 추천. 7 8 코스는 수고대비 실망이커서 제일 비추.

Don’t go on a rainy day as this will completely ruin your experience like it did ours. We didn’t spend every long here as the rain did not stop. We saw a couple of caves and ended up going back down. Also the ticket for the lift at the bottom doesn’t cover you for coming back down so you have to buy another ticket for the lift at the top. Also there are a lot of people around trying to get you to park in their shops so you go in after and spend money.

Uma das experiências mais vazias e caras que tive neste país. Todos os acessos são pagos e eles não te avisam antes. As pessoas são muito mal educadas e os visuais não são tão impressionantes quanto nas fotos. Não recomendo.
Total langweilig und sehr touristisch. Einige sehr schwierige Stellen, also sollte man feste Schuhe haben

This place is a tourist trap! Do not ride your bike there if you’re obviously a foreigner. There are people living all around the mountain who will harass you and follow you up the road. I understand this is possibly their livelihoods but it’s a lot. Get a grab/bus there or park a long way away.
Overall the caves was interesting but the downside is the ticketing booth for the elevator. Those ladies in the booth treat foreigners like idoit and making rude comments in Vietnamese.
overrated, agressive sellers of goods. only good thing  is cheap ticket.

It has beautiful views up top but many of the ocean view has been blocked out by tall buildings. The caves are not a good place for senior patrons as the steps are uneven.

Full of crowd and rowdy tourists. If you're wanting to go just to say you've been, then that would be the place for you.

Am Wochenende wenn möglich meiden, hier sind viele Schulklassen und Reisegruppen unterwegs, auch schon früh morgens.
An der Kasse wurde absichtlich mehr verlangt als ausgeschrieben ist, unbedingt nachrechnen.
Sehr viele Stufen, mit teils rutschigen und losen Steinen, für ältere Menschen und kleine Kinder nicht geeignet. Es muss u.a. geklettert werden um am Ende zu sehen, dass man nichts sieht! viele Aussichtspunkte sind zugewuchert. Das was man sehen konnte war schön, aber trotzdem die Anstregung nicht wert.
Tôi không biết tại sao chúng tôi lại đến đó. Họ nói đó là thiên đường và địa ngục. Các mô hình rất thô sơ, và các hang động lại thông gió kém vào mùa hè, khiến chúng rất ngột ngạt.

Cực kỳ thiếu tổ chức, từ việc chỉ dẫn lối vào, loại vé có thang máy và không có thang máy, đến việc thiếu bản đồ hoặc thông tin về số bậc thang cần leo. Thêm vào đó, hang động/điểm tham quan không ấn tượng như mong đợi.

Nếu bạn bắt Grab và nhìn thấy cầu thang ngay trước mặt, bạn có thể đi bộ lên.

Giá vé vào cửa là 20.000 đồng.
Bạn có thể đi bộ lên với giá 20.000 đồng và tham quan nhiều hang động khác nhau, nhưng trời nóng, khó chịu và bạn phải đi dép lê.

Nếu bạn rẽ phải, bạn có thể đi thang máy. Giá vé vào cửa là 40.000 đồng.
Bạn có thể tham quan một hang động bằng cách đi lên với giá 15.000 đồng và tổng cộng là 55.000 đồng. Mất khoảng 30 phút để đi xuống các bậc thang đá.
Nếu bạn đi vào sáng sớm, bạn có thể thong thả ngắm cảnh.
Nếu bạn đi vào buổi sáng, có rất nhiều nhóm du lịch trọn gói, vì vậy bạn sẽ phải vội vã.

Không có nhiều thứ để xem, nhiều nơi để leo trèo, hãy cẩn thận nếu đi xe máy, các cửa hàng địa phương sẽ cung cấp chỗ đậu xe miễn phí nhưng sẽ tính phí khi trả lại. Điểm tốt duy nhất của nơi này là quang cảnh và không có nhiều thứ để ngắm …

Điểm tham quan rất kỳ lạ. Tôi mừng là vé vào cửa chỉ 40.000 đồng vì có vẻ như hầu hết các tòa nhà đều mới được xây dựng bằng bê tông hoặc được trùng tu rất kém. Hang động thì tạm được, không có gì đặc biệt. Đá cẩm thạch thì cũng tạm được. Tuy nhiên, nếu bạn muốn mua đá thật thì ĐỪNG MUA bất kỳ món quà lưu niệm nào ở đây. Tôi thích địa chất và có thể nói với bạn rằng không chỉ đá cẩm thạch mà tất cả các loại đá khác cũng vậy. Sự kết hợp của thủy tinh, thạch anh màu kém chất lượng và nhựa thông. Tôi cũng bị cả người bán nước trong công viên lẫn chủ cửa hàng làm phiền rất nhiều. Chắc cũng tạm được…

Generally, all vietnam caves and temples are quite poorly maintained. But the only blessing for this is you get to experience taking a lift up to the mountain. But not worth the sights to climb the stairs.

동굴만 우선 비추천.

Extremely overrated. The place is dirty. However, if you have free time in Da Nang, go check it out for fun. I wouldn't set aside a whole day for it. 굳이 여름에 들어가는건 정말 추천하지 않음.

Apart from a bit of exercise climbing the stairs, dont waste your time nor money. Unless you're into super religious stuff it's not worth the stop. 40k to climb up, apx 2min climb or 55k using elevator but ques were long. Caves ok but nothing to write home about. Too many tourists... crowded.

Would have given a 5-star rate if not for the very disappointing never-again experience in being ripped off by a crooked ticket lady at the official ticket booth near the main entrance/elevator. Beware everyone (foreign visitors especially)!!! In such hecticness, she was really handy in distracting your attention by giving some instruction while returning your change (NOT in the right amount of course). She successfully took VND 195,000 from me for tickets that were only worth VND 80,000. When I realised what she'd done, it was all too late. You give such a bad name to such a beautiful place, lady!! Karma please!!

Ngũ Hành Sơn là 5 ngọn núi nằm sát nhau, cái này đi 1 lần cho biết, mình cũng ko ấn tượng lắm. có lối leo bộ và thang máy. thường leo lên thăm quan bằng đường bộ xong xuống bằng thang máy cho đỡ mỏi chân

Động tự nhiên khá thú vị. Còn rất hoang sơ. Bạn có thể đi thang máy lên chùa Linh ứng là khoảng giữa núi. Giá vé 15000k 1 người/1 chiều. Sau đó mình sẽ đi bộ đến các hang động. Có nhiều hang động để bạn tham quan, nhưng nói chung không có gì đặc biệt, nếu bạn thích tự nhiên thì có thể thích khám phá, nhưng so với những hang động nổi tiếng thì bạn sẽ hơi thất vọng, vì không có gì ngoài 1 hang động bình thường.
Mình đi mùa hè nên khá là nóng, vào hang động hơi mát một chút nhưng cũng không mát lạnh như mọi người quảng cáo.
Có một đường lên đỉnh thương thai, khá cao và bậc thang cũng khá nhỏ, nếu người lớn tuổi thì rất khó đi, nhưng nếu bạn còn trẻ thì rất xứng đáng để trải nghiệm, vì bạn có thể nhìn thấy toàn cảnh thành phố và biển khá đẹp. Ngũ Hành Sơn là tên của một danh thắng gồm 5 ngọn núi đá vôi nhô lên trên một bãi cát ven biển, trên một diện tích khoảng 2 km². Ngũ Hành Sơn gồm các ngọn núi: Mộc Sơn, Thủy Sơn (lớn, cao và đẹp nhất), Thổ Sơn, Kim Sơn và Hỏa Sơn (có hai ngọn là Dương Hỏa Sơn và Âm Hỏa Sơn), nằm cách trung tâm thành phố Đà Nẵng khoảng 8 km về phía Đông Nam, ngay trên tuyến đường Đà Nẵng - Hội An; nay thuộc phường Hòa Hải, quận Ngũ Hành Sơn, thành phố Đà Nẵng.

Dưới chân ngọn Thủy Sơn là các cơ sở điêu khắc đá mỹ nghệ phát triển từ các làng nghề có tuổi đời trên 400 năm, tổ nghề là Huỳnh Bá Quát, tiên tổ của Đô ngự sử Huỳnh Bá Chánh. Từ các loại đá cẩm thạch có ở Ngũ Hành Sơn, người thợ đã chế tác các tác phẩm tinh xảo.

Tại ngũ hành sơn thì được chia làm 2 động, 1 là động địa ngục và 2 là động thiên đường.
- Động địa ngục thì có giá 20k/người. Động điện ngục thì đi bộ vào sâu bên trong rất đẹp và tham quan các tội dưới địa ngục. Có cầu thang để lên tới đỉnh động rất mát nhưng đường rất nhỏ và khó đi.
- Động Thiên đường thì có giá 40k/người (chưa bao gồm thang máy lên). Động thiên đường thì mắc hơn địa ngục, mọi người lại hay giỡn rằng thiên đàng lại có giá hơn địa ngục ^^. Nhưng ở thiên đàng lại có 2 cách đi: 1 là đi thang máy (với 15k/lượt) và 2 là đi thang bộ khoảng 10-15p. Ở trên thiên đường rất cao và rất mát nhiều cây cối nên gió liên tục. Giữ xe ở ngoài thì 10k/xe máy.

Nhân viên ko niềm nở, ko hướng dẫn khách. Bãi giữ xe máy tự phát rất bát nháo. Giữ xe máy 10k/chiếc. Miễn phí vé cổng.

Điểm tham quan cho những ai chưa biết chứ không có gì là đặc sắc. Phía dưới đường có nhiều cửa hàng bán vật phẩm lưu niệm đẹp bằng đá nhưng giá thì không biết đường nào mà lần.

Rất thích đi ngũ hành sơn nhưng điều k ưng là khi bán vé lên thang máy, người bán vé ngang nhiên bán kèm cả đống ảnh với bản đồ gì gì ấy mà k hỏi trước -_- mình phát hiện ra và trả lại thì nvien khá thái độ

Đang được miễn phí vé vào cổng (giá bình thường 40k/người). Có thang máy đi lên đỡ mất sức (15k/lượt), nhưng đi thang máy xong vẫn phải đi bộ rất nhiều giữa các điểm tham quan. Ở chỗ bán vé có bán luôn bản đồ trong núi (giá 15k kèm ảnh các địa điểm), xem rất dễ hiểu, đường đi cũng có các bảng chỉ dẫn. Nếu cần tìm hiểu sâu hơn có thể thuê người hướng dẫn tại chỗ. Một số nơi thờ cúng nhưng hương hoả rất nguội lạnh. Cảnh quan mình thấy khá bình thường. Nếu bạn dư thời gian và có sức đi bộ thì đi cho biết cũng được.

Hai năm trước đến nơi này mọi thứ thật đẹp, giá cả rẻ thoải mái, nhưng giờ giá lên gấp mấy lần (40k 1 vé/ ng) và dĩ nhiên tôi không mua vì không đáng.
Mặt bàng gửi xe để đó nhưng không được gửi phải ra các nhà dân phía trước gửi, quá kỳ cục giá gửi xe rất mắc 10k/xe trong khi đó khu resort sơn trà 10k vừa gửi xe tắm nước ngọt vui chơi thoải mái.

Đã đi chơi thì không ngại khổ.Chuẩn bị hành trang cho một ngày đi bộ thám hiểm thì các bạn nên chuẩn bị hành trang: nước uống, giày thể thao, tránh bộ cánh rườm rà và sức khoẻ thật tốt.
Ai có sức khỏe tốt và muốn rèn luyện thì mình khuyên nên đi bộ,để khám phá hết tất cả những gì đẹp nhất mà thiên nhiên tạo hoá ban tặng cho ngũ hành sơn.

Marble Mountains offer a calm and meaningful experience, especially if you enjoy places that blend nature, history, and spirituality. The mountains are dotted with caves, temples, and viewpoints, making the visit feel both scenic and reflective.

The caves are the real highlight—naturally formed, atmospheric, and beautifully lit by sunlight filtering through openings above. Temples inside the caves add a spiritual touch and are well maintained. The views from the top are rewarding and give a nice panorama of Da Nang and the coastline.

The climb can be a bit tiring, particularly in the heat, though there is an elevator available for part of the way. Some paths are steep and uneven, so comfortable footwear is important. Like most popular spots, it can get crowded at times.

歴史を知ってればもっと楽しめそうな感じでしたが、歴史が苦手なのでとても楽しかったって感じにはならなかった。エレベーターで頂上？まで上がれるが、それでもかなり階段がある。その階段の１段１段が高いので結構しんどかった。真夏に行ったらかなり疲れると思うので飲み物はあった方がいいかも。

ダナン市街地からもそれほど遠くないので初めてのダナン旅だったら行ってもいいかな？って思います。

날씨가 좋을 때 방문 하기를 추천 합니다.
동굴에 부처상, 사원, 너무나 흥미롭고 신비로운 곳 입니다.
입장료, 엘리베이터 비용은 올라 갈때와 내려올 때가  따로 계산 됩니다. 아예, 왕복 티켓이 편할 수도 있겠네요.
저희는 비오는 날에 방문 하는 바람에 많이 돌아 보지는 못 했습니다.

Very nice place for photo taking. At the peak of the mountain, you can oversee the horizon of Da Nang. I particularly like the pagoda and statue of Buddha. Cave is a nice place to enter too but it gets spooky when the chanting starts. Overall, it’s a nice place to go but I find the visit too short as I complete the mountain and cave within 2 hours.

Coming from a country with similar tropical attractions, this place was not too much of a surprise for me - so i guess i had high standards.

The place was nice to explore and had beautiful temples, but were all over the place and overall hard to navigate and walk around, even for younger and fitter individuals like myself.

Overall, the views were great, the caves were cool, but i wouldnt recommend older individuals going because there is alot of walking and climbing to do.

Also, the tickets up the mountain was only for a one way trip and youd have to buy another ticket to go down the elevator..... lowkey felt squeezed as a tourist.

メリアダナンに宿泊しましたので、歩きで五行山行ってきました。気合を入れてエレベーター無しで登ってやろうと思って登りましたが相当きつかったです。欧米人の方のほとんどはクロックスやサンダルで来てましたが、スニーカーじゃないと絶対にやばいです。石は滑りますし、階段の段差は相当きついです。あと荷物はリュックとか両手が空いていた方が登るのに絶対に良いです。エレベーター無しのチケット売り場で買うと、他のみなさんが手にしてるじゃばらの案内図がもらえなかったのは安いからだろうか。順路っていうのは正直無いと思います。手元に地図が無いので、看板の案内図を写真を撮ってそれを見て色々進みましたが、ここは何処なのかよくわからず難しかったです。2時間半くらいうろうろしましたが、全部回れなかったと思います。そしてとにかく暑い。汗だくでした。年配者、体力の無い人や小さなお子さん連れは絶対に行ってはいけないと思います。偶然出会った子猫と犬に癒されました。
This place is fine, you have to pay to get on the elevator and to enter the cave.

The Marble Mountains:

Service:
- The lady who I brought the tickets from was extremely rude!
- Rating: 1/5

As one of the main tourist destinations in Da Nang, to avoid the queues I recommend you get there early.

It’s a lovely place to visit, but unfortunately there is a lot of litter 😢

Buy some water before you enter as a small bottle of water cost 25K instead of 6K in a supermarket.

Overall Rating: 3/5

Die Treppen kann man, wenn man noch gut zu Fuß ist, laufen. Oben warten dann ein paar schöne Pagoden. Die Höhlen sind auch Sehenswert. Nur in Acht nehmen sollte man sich vor den Verkäufern direkt am Gate 2. Sie verlangen für ihre Souvenirs völlig überteuerte Preise. Zum Teil das 4-5 fache.

The site has some interesting caves and pagodas, but the paths are crowded and not always well-marked. Climbing requires a moderate level of fitness, and the view from the top is decent but not exceptional. Facilities around the area are basic, and the overall experience feels a bit rushed if trying to see everything in one visit.

Völlig überlaufen. Lange Wartezeiten und ehrlich gesagt ist der Unterschied von Insta zur Realität riesig.
Kann man machen . Muss man absolut nicht.

We reached the Marble Mountain around 4pm, just right before the sunset.

The ticketing staff near the cave side was not friendly, she didn’t seem to keen in selling the tickets to us but kept pointing to a ticketing booth near the elevator…. & after some tough conversation (plus checking with some tourists) we were finally able to join the elevator queue!

Sunset view up there was beautiful but certain area to some caves are not safe for elderly or people who has knee pain problem.

It is a good place for those who wants something adventurous 😊😊

It's nice, and the cave mostly. The other parts are pretty basic. The cave is fun if your able to walk properly. Inside there's an old stone stair to the top which is hard to manage if you're not able to walk properly.

Be aware: the ticket booth near the entry to the cave is only for the cave. The other part has its own ticket booth near the elevator.
I dony't know, the staffs were not helpful at all, they just gave me the map instead of explained me how to get to the cave. The place is so confusing for solo traveler, less direction indicators!

You need to set your expectations right before going to The Marble Mountains or else you can be quite disappointed.
You can either go up via elevator with a really long waiting line or you can take the stairs which are very steep and can be quite dangerous if you don’t watch your steps.
If you are not a Buddhist then all you can really do is take a good look and take some photos for memories.
The craft and architecture and having all those statues on the mountain top is quite impressive and really makes you wonder, but that’s about it.
Overall, it’s worth visiting The Marble Mountains once, and you get a decent exercise too and you can buy some gifts and souvenirs at the bottom too.

別名、五行山と言う洞窟寺院で洞窟の奥に行くと地獄が表現されており、ここに入るには入口正面の売場でチケット購入。内部は涼しいけど湿度高いんでタオル必須！入らず頂上に行く場合は、入口右に行くとエレベーターのチケット売り場が別にあるのでそちらで購入。
早晨來個身體健行活動，觀賞景點走一下路.從正門口進去2萬越南幣/電梯那一面4萬電梯+1.5萬入場費.

峴港包車到會安一日遊的行程中，途經的一處景點「五行山」，由五座高數十公尺的石灰岩群峰組成，分別代表五行中的金、木、水、火、土，因當地曾出產玉石，所以又叫大理石山 Marble Mountain。

五行山入口處有一景觀電梯，可直通半山腰，對懶人如我們而言，絕對是要買票搭一下，省掉爬階梯的時間和體力。門票約新台幣20元。

五行山主要參觀的是水山，是五峰中最大的一座，上頂有兩座觀景台，東邊望海台、西邊望江台，顧名思義，一邊可眺望海岸、另一邊則是眺望漢江景色，風光秀麗，可惜當天有點陰天。

五行山有許多的廟宇和佛塔，像是三台寺、慈心寺和靈應寺，也有許多石灰岩構成的溶洞，石窟中供供奉多尊菩薩、羅漢塑像，雕刻都相當精美，可說是別有洞天，很值得前往一探究竟。

Cool mountain to visit with some impressive caves. That's about it.
Many stairs to climb, up and down. Price for the elevator is one way!!!
Some kitsch pagodas and statues... everything made for tourism. There are many more interesting visits to do while in Danang. Your choice...

If you really have to kill some time, visit this place together with Lady Buddha in the morning. Nothing much to do here so you can see the caves, climb a flight of stairs to the top for a view of Da Nang, and still have plenty of time to visit Lady Buddha before you go for a late lunch.
PS: Don't bother queuing for the elevator. The flight of stairs is not that long and it's free. And you need separate tickets for the cave, the mountain, and also the elevator rides (they charge by per ride).

Rather than entering the cave, I recommend taking the elevator up and back down. The elevator ticket is a one-way trip, so to ride the elevator back down, you'll need to purchase two elevator tickets in addition to your entrance ticket. Considering whether it's worth visiting from downtown Da Nang, I don't think it's worth it.
It's a good place if you have time left. You will have to walk a lot here on the mountain. So it's better to avoid coming in afternoon. Overall quite an average place

다낭에서 방문해볼 만한 자연과 문화가 어우러진 곳.

- 이곳은 산책로를 따라 다양한 불교 사원, 동굴, 전망대를 만날 수 있으며, 아름다운 자연 경관과 함께 다낭 시내와 해안을 내려다볼 수 있는 탁 트인 전망이 일품인 곳.
- 다양한 크기의 동굴과 불교 유적이 어우러져 신비롭고 독특한 분위기를 느낄 수 있으며, 정상에서 다낭 시내와 해변 풍경은 바라 볼 수 있음. 길이 잘 정비되어 있어 비교적 수월하게 탐방이 가능함.
- 계단이 많아서, 엘리베이터를 이용하는게 편하며, 관광객이 많아 조용한 분위기를 기대하기는 어려움. 특히, 계단이 많아 비가오거나 할때는 필히 운동화를 착용하는 것이 좋음. 점심 무렵에는 너무 더워서 아침 방문 하시길 추천함.
- 결론적으로, 뭔가 대단한 경치가 있는 것은 아니지만, 다낭 자체가 관광할 곳이 많이 없기 때문에 시간이 되시면 한번쯤 방문해도 괜찮음. 단, 계단 등이 많아  노령의 부모님이나 아이와 함께 더운 날에는 가급적 방문하지 않는게 좋을 것 같음.

A network of limestone caves with temples, shrines and intricate carvings that's one of the to-do things in Danang.

There's an elevator at Gate 2, but you can also take a short hike up from Gate 1. Make sure you get dropped off at the correct gate if you're not part of a tour.

Очень интересное место, внутри прохладнее, чем снаружи, ноинакануне шел дождь, внутри пещеры капала вода, было очень грязно, местами скользко. "Рай" того не стоит, наверху небольшая смотровая площадка, подняться то не проблема, а вот спускаться опасно, потому что ступеньки крутые, мокрые, скользко.

The mountain consists of the 5 elements of the universe in Asia culture, consisting of the Kim (Iron), Mouc (Wood), Thuy (Water), Hoa (Fire) and Tho (Earth). Visitors here will discover the mysterious beauty of caves combing the sacred atmosphere of Buddhism and visit the Tam Thai Pagoda and the Linh Ung Pagoda.

You can choose to climb up via the steep rock stairs or take the elevator up (one way is 15,000 dong) which we chose the latter. The view here is nice and you can feel the peace and tranquil. If you are into Buddhism, you will surely indulge in this place where you can get up and closed with the many statues of Buddha. And there's a cave behind the temple and is nice and serene.

As the passage and stairways are made of uneven rock and bricks, do be very careful in walking around.

Do take note that the elevator service closed at 5.00pm.

There's also a small scale touristry street market near the main where you can grab souvenirs and gifts here.

Worth the visit but manage expectations. No information on anything, workers are not friendly, people everywhere (a bit chaotic), and some areas are dangerous to climb through even though that is the route, this is especially true for the cave and if it is raining. Granted you don’t have to trek through but what’s the fun in staying behind 🙂

Ngũ Hành Sơn is a beautiful and spiritual destination in Đà Nẵng, famous for its stunning caves, pagodas, and panoramic views. The scenery is breathtaking, making it a great spot for photography and exploration.

However, there are not many restaurants around, so it's best to eat before visiting or bring snacks. On the bright side, there are plenty of souvenir shops selling marble carvings, jewelry, and local crafts—great for gifts but be prepared to bargain!

Overall, a must-visit for nature and culture lovers, but plan ahead for meals.

Vé vào hang động là 20.000 đồng, vé thang máy là 15.000 đồng, và vé vào Ohaengsan là 40.000 đồng. Tôi không tìm thấy đồng hồ của mình trên bản đồ.

Có quá nhiều loại vé và lệ phí, và cá nhân tôi thấy phong cảnh núi non không có gì đặc biệt hấp dẫn. Lưu ý rằng người dân địa phương trên núi có thể sẽ giới thiệu một số bức tượng Phật và sau đó hỏi xin tiền boa. …

Độ khó của tour này thấp hơn bạn nghĩ. Với những ai lo lắng về cầu thang... nếu bạn còn trẻ, thì khá dễ dàng (khoảng 2-3 phút lên, 1 phút xuống). Nếu bạn ở độ tuổi 50 hoặc lớn hơn lo lắng về xương khớp? Chỉ cần trả 15.000 won cho thang máy, …

Đây là ý kiến cá nhân của tôi, vì vậy hãy tự mình trải nghiệm nhé.

Trước hết, ngọn đồi quá dốc và có quá nhiều bậc thang đến nỗi tôi đã bỏ cuộc …

Đây là một điểm du lịch bẫy khách! Có những cửa hàng bán đồ lưu niệm giá cắt cổ. Tôi không khuyên bạn nên mua bất cứ thứ gì; tất cả bọn họ đều đang cố gắng lừa đảo bạn. Ví dụ, họ có thể ra giá 500.000 cho một món đồ, nhưng nếu bạn di chuyển xung quanh, giá sẽ giảm xuống còn 15.000 – một sự lừa đảo trắng trợn. Nơi này là một hang động, và mặc dù nó có thể có ý nghĩa nào đó đối với người dân địa phương, nhưng điểm thu hút duy nhất của nó là lớp hoàn thiện bằng xi măng và vật liệu hiện đại.

Un poco sin más, no lo considero imprescindible pero tampoco está mal. Las cuevas son curiosas si no has visto más en Vietnam aún. No hay mucho más en Da Nang de este estilo entonces si tienes tiempo, ven a verlo. Nosotros pasamos unos 40 minutos en total pero tampoco nos paramos mucho en todo ni íbamos con guía. Subimos en ascensor y bajamos andando
Sympa pour une petite promenade. Un peu trop de monde avec leur ascenseur qui est inutile

Il posto é carino.
Si tratta di un complesso di pagode e templi, alcuni dentro grotte suggestive.
Ingresso a 40k dong ( poco meno di due euro a persona) , e 15k dong a tratta  ( se prendete l 'ascensore) . Se avete problemi con scalini , lasciate stare non é un posto per voi, altrimenti godetevi il percorso. Di per se, valgono la pena due grotte la 9 e la 10, il resto se avete fretta potete saltarlo.
Ci sono venditori di alimenti  ovunque sul percorso.
Da visitare certamente.

There's much walking and stairs to do, especially for the highest peak, so be prepared! The temples, pagodas and caves are very beautiful, I'm just disappointed that one cave (Van Thong) was difficult to go up top: I had a heavy jacket that couldn't hang anywhere, so with just one hand free it was even more difficult to climb, especially with the rainy weather of that day and slippery steps. I suggest either going when it's not rainy or bringing a light raincoat to always have on you.

We bought the elevator ticket at the beginning of the tour in order to go back down and avoiding the slippery stairs we did going up top, but sadly it was of no use since there are two entrances (gate 1 and gate 2) and the tour naturally ended on the opposite gate.

Another disappointing thing was a cave that is accessible from the ground: when buying the tickets, they didn't tell us there was this other cave that could be seen...and it wasn't included! So you had to make another ticket, even though it was in the same exact mountain. Moreover, we discovered this cave only when we bought the map (another thing we had to pay for!) or I imagine people would know only when going up top the mountain and see the big map there for orientation.

These problems aside, it's a very beautiful place I would recommend visiting if the weather is at least decent and if you're a sporty type, otherwise you will get tired easily. I give it 3 stars because of my personal experience. Service could be better and indications of prices&places to see can be improved.

Входной билет 40.000донг+ 15.000 на лифте. Были в среду 18 декабря. Очень много туристов. На лестницах иногда приходится ждать, чтобы пропустить людей. Обязательно обучайте удобную обувь. В пещерах может быть сколько даже в солнечную погоду.
สำหรับเราน่าจะเป็นแค่จุดชมวิวเมือง,ถ้ำและพระพุทธรูปไม่ตื่นตาสำหรับฉันคนไทยเพราะบ้านเมืองฉัน สวยกว่านี้แต่สำหรับต่างชาติอื่นจะชอบ,มีลิฟให้ขึ้นไปบนสุดหรือจะเลือกเดินขึ้นด้วยเท้าควรใส่รองเท้าผ้าใบ

五行山(Marble Mountain)。2024年3月遊。晴。早。按景區豎立的導遊地圖遊覽。東口(Gate 2)上山，先抵靈應寺(Linh Ung Pagoda)，有藏珠洞(Tang Chon Cave)，探雲通洞(Van Thong Cave)，繼往華嚴洞(Hoa Nghiem cave)及玄空洞(Huyen Khong Cave)，再登附近山丘頂最高點(The highest point)，下來往三台寺(Tam Thai Pagoda)及一眾廟宇，然後南口(Gate 1)下山。全程約1.5小時。
遊畢，覺得宗教景觀大於風景價值。景區主要景點是廟宇和溶洞。其實溶洞也可看成廟宇一類，洞窟内有各式神像、佛像或觀音像等，供善信遊人參拜。除此，無堪景致。至於最高點山頂、望海臺、望江臺等，景觀不外如是。
主要景點玄空洞(Huyen Khong Cave)，是山上景區最大洞窟。内有矮石廟宇供奉神祇。洞窟中央坐佛像，居高至上。洞窟頂破開。當陽光束從頂破口射進洞窟，會添加洞窟宗教的莊嚴和神秘。到訪當日晨光仍煦，又或許陽光射入時機未至，未見此景。洞窟倒有幾分陰森。
廟宇及洞窟神像可能是保存下來的歷史古跡，亦可能是重建或重新塑造。
在登山升降機不遠停車場那邊有陰府洞(Am phu cave) 。另外收費。並沒内進遊覽。
五行山(Marble Mountains)門票：VND40000(2024年)
登山電梯(約上8層樓高度)：單程VND15000(2024年)
陰府洞(Am phu cave) 門票：VND20000
五行山指金木水火土五山，只知水山和金山在景區内，具體不詳。其餘木火土三山的所在，毫無所知。遊五行山的，不少是本土旅客和本土旅行團，相信五行山自有其歷史淵源、故事傳說及勝景，唯異客獨遊，未能領會。

Not really an amusement park. Really just a giant shopping mall with souvenirs items on sale, often 300-600% more than what you find in the local market at Da Nang. It wasnt a terrible experience but I certainly will not be back.

Very interesting historic site. The crowds made it very difficult to navigate as there were many steps to climb and we moved slowly For this reason,it wasn’t the best experience. Go early in the day for a more fulfilling visit.

Considering that this is one of the few karst limestone mountains in the area, it's certainly worth visiting, especially for such a reasonable price. Whether you take the elevator, which is only 15,000 VND or climb, you will be straining yourself as you go up the many steps which could be uneven and slippery. We went at 3:00 p.m. in the afternoon and found it to be ideal for sun exposure. There is one large cave which is interesting, but another one that is so narrow and dark that you truly risk falling by going through it. It is only one way, you need a flashlight, and when you exit the other side you really don't know exactly where you are.

As to where to park or to pay, there is little signage in English, and not much in the park itself except for a few major directional signs. But the views from the top are quite nice.

Nice place to visit. There is a lift u can buy the ticket separately for around 15 000 vmd each. But once u arrive u have all the steps to go to the pagodas.  The place is beautiful and interesting with different budha images and statues. It's worth  the price but everything  is not really ancient as I thought. The mountain is a small mountain and there is all the infra structure u will need as snack bar, toilets etc

Good Place who were continuously in cities and concrete jungles. No need to wait in long lines for the lift and paying for it. Can take steps. Lift only for Elder People and Who are not physically fit. The view from up top is good. Better to visit early in the morning or in the evening as it is humid and also for the pictures.

Ticket counter lady gave the attitude like I’m buying the ticket for free. Be notified that there is one elevator to go up. They charge you to just go up the elevator and again to come down you have to buy another ticket. And they don’t even inform you before go up. I still have no idea why they charge separately to come down. There is a good view yes, but not worth the ticket. Ridiculous ticketing system

坐電梯上山一下子就到了（電梯裡沒風沒冷氣
上山還要走15～20分鐘才會到
對我來說就是個很熱🥵的景點
外頭有許多商家賣各式各樣的包包
價錢就是殺價殺下去就對了

Two "attractions in one place"... Cave- 40k to get into it, which is sort of nice n cooling... Once inside, u could chose to climb up to a level to get a view of Da Nang city, or down to see "the 18th level of Hell" ... Or u could just walk around it inside... (4 ⭐ for the cave)... Pagoda - 15k one way for the "lifts" to go up (u could chose to walk up n down if u like) 40k to get to walk around the pagoda, reckon if I chose to walk up, u may not even need to pay the 40k, as the 40k is together with the 15k lifts to go up?? Cos if u chose to go up by lifts (15k), the entrance to the pagoda is right outside the lifts, n comes down from the others side of the "hill" by stairs...While the caves are indeed very big and astonishing, I would have to comment that this area is extremely inaccessible to children, the elderly and those on wheelchairs. The steps were often very high and uneven which can be dangerous for some. It is a beautiful place but if you are not fit and not ready to climb a lot of stairs in sweltering weather, I suggest not to come. However, if you are a fan of mother nature it is a good visit as it is cheap and also beautiful.

Please ensure you wear proper covered shoes when visiting this place. The steps leading into the cave can be very dangerous, especially after rain. I strongly suggest that the management take this matter seriously and implement enhanced safety measures.

A few different caves to see and a few beautiful lookout points. I think we spent about 2 hours exploring. It costs extra to go into one of the caves, to go up the elevator, and down the elevator.

這個景點，參觀時間一個小時就很足夠
不是很重要的大景點
如果當天沒有其他行程可以去，可以排入
들어가면 시원할 줄 알았는데, 초입에는 시원했는데 들어가니 그렇지 않았어요. 입장료2만동 이었던 것 같아요. 동굴은 그렇고 엘리베이터는 따로 입장료를 내야합니다. 인당 55천동 내야하는데, 이걸 타더라도 정상까지 가는게 아니라 또 걸어 올라가야합니다 . 내려올때도 엘리베이터 티켓을 다시 사야합니다.참고하세요.

Short scooter ride from Danang or Hoi An.

As soon as you enter the main street leading to the entrance you are harassed by Hawkers trying to get you to park at their stalls. Its made a bit harder by not knowing where the entrances are to go up to the Mountain.

So we paid for parking 5K VND (60c) lol and were harassed to buy crap from vendors at the bottom. An older Vietnamese lady was helpful with pointing us to the entrance. (Remember this for later)

Ticket price to go up and explore mountain reasonable - 40K VND This was easy enough, bring cash.

Site lacks any sort of explanatory signs to give visitors a better understanding of the area - the main signage are boards which show where you are and where you can walk.

Again once up top on the mountain you'll be harassed to buy useless trinkets from vendors and cold drinks (even had my own water in my hands and wanted to sell me more? ) lol.

Exploring the various caves is very cool and is interesting but again zero information or sign boards to explain anything there to give you an understanding of the history of the area.

Nice views from the top! If you've got kids don't let them roam free some deathly drop offs at some of the lookouts.

On the way out via Gate 2 you are forced to walk through all street vendors selling crap again and guess what !

Same lady that showed us the entrance was there expecting us to buy stuff from here shop.

My wife thinks I'm being overly negative but I disagree, have to post this stuff to teach people who may not be as well versed travelling in south east Asian countries.

For all the above reasons only a 2☆ for this one! Go but just be prepared to be harassed constantly here too! Enjoy! 😀

It's ok. Nothing to rave about. Insane numbers of tourists (I know, im also contributing to that number). The view across the city from the highest point was mediocre at best. You mostly see across low rise residential areas or large resorts blocking most of the views of the sea. The caves and Temples hidden with them were the highlight.

Det er ok at besøge, så man kan sige man har været der, men for os virkede det mest som en stor turistfælde. Vi har besøgt mange bjerge og huler og derfor imponerede dette os ikke særligt. Hvis man ikke er vant til det, så vil man helt sikkert synes det er et imponerende sted.
Skiltning er ikke særlig god og der var mange der ikke kunne finde ud af at få kort og skilte til at passe sammen.
Man skal være obs på at hulen og marble mountains kræver to forskellige biletter og hvis du vil op med elevatoren skal der købes endnu en billet.
Vi gav 40000 vand for entre og 15000 for elevatoren op. (Det kræver endnu en billet at komme ned, men vi gik bare ned)
Der er ingen forklaringer på hvorfor de forskellige huler er udsmykket som de er eller nogen som helst anden form for information undervejs, så jeg tror man får en bedre oplevelse hvis man har en guide med.
Da vi stod ved sky view blev vi mødt af musik fra Pirates of the Caribbean hvilket var en ret syret oplevelse.

五行山，跟團到訪的景點，坐電梯上到岩山上參訪寺廟與洞窟佛像，但階梯非常陡峭，推車輪椅不適合

エレベーターを含んだ入場料の売り場が来た方向によってわかりづらい。エレベーターを待つ列が長いのに係りの人は適当だから1人だったから、2回も抜けて聞きに行った 。観光客が多すぎて疲れる。頑張れるならエレベーター側と反対から徒歩で上がった方が時間はかからないけど上りは急すぎるかな。
ハン市場の近くの教会前から16番のバスに乗れば15vdで行けます。

五行山是峴港市五行山郡的一座山峰，是峴港三座著名的〔靈應寺〕之一，特色是可以搭電梯往返山頂或步道入口，規模比〔山茶半島〕的靈應寺小。
停車場對面及下山步道口攤販很多，這裡adidas帽+女編織遮陽帽共3頂帽才新台幣100元，涼鞋2雙150元，女圓盤帽2頂100元（如照片），比飯店門口機車攤販便宜很多，除機場及超市外，新台幣很好用不用換越南盾。

一個要收門票的廟，不得不說這間廟很普通，沒有什麼非常厲害的建築，也沒有非常厲害的自然風景。

不過在廟附近有很多攤販，販賣的東西比名勝景點便宜一些，反而是小攤販比較有看點。

建議要前往的旅客可以三思是否值得上山，不然看完其實覺得蠻空虛的，畢竟其他地方有更漂亮的廟宇建築。

A temple that requires an admission fee, it has to be said that this temple is quite ordinary, without any particularly impressive architecture or stunning natural scenery.

However, there are many vendors selling goods near the temple, and their products are cheaper than those sold at scenic spots. The small vendors are actually more interesting.

It is suggested that travelers who plan to visit should consider whether it is worth going up the mountain. After all, they may feel quite disappointed after seeing it, as there are more beautiful temple buildings in other places.

We didn't go there cause I was mistaken. Instead of we went to the Marbles Mountain cave. Lovely to see the cave but what a terrible fairground annex Christmas lighting for such a beautiful sightseeing. It's a shame because the cheap lighting does spoil the atmosphere."
