We happen to visit the Golden Bridge - Bana Hills in Dec 2022 in a weather which was partially rainy and partially winters. As pretty as the Bana shills would be in summers, but a few pointers to everyone who are thinking of going in rainy/winter months to golden bridge specially is as follows: 1. The whole cable ride and the bridge itself will be surrounded by clouds making the visibility 10 percent only. Very pissing to spend that kind of money to be surrounded by clouds only. 2. Most of the activities were closed due to the visibility issue.3. Weather is super super cold. Be sure to carry warm wear or jackets. No information about weather conditions is mentioned anywhere which is highly annoying. This was the experience with most of the people at the airport. Most of them couldn’t see the beautiful bridge in its full glory all throughout the previous week too. Travel date - 12th Dec 2022. Think twice before heavily splurging on Bana Hills in this specific season. Image for reference

Dass die Brücke eine Touristenattraktion (gebaut 2017), als Teil eines "Vergnügungsparks" ist war uns bereits vor der Anreise bekannt. Das für die Besichtigung der Brücke der komplette Eintrittspreis für den Park bezahlt werden muss auch. Um das Fazit meiner Bewertung schon einmal vorweg zunehmen - ein Besuch lohnt sich nicht!Wir reisten aus Da Nang bei 30° und Sonnenschein an. Das Wetter in den Banahills ist relativ unbeständig und schwer vorher zu sagen, es regnet häufig. Trotz guter Vorhersagen war dies auch bei unserem Besuch der Fall. So erreichten wir die Brücke bei windigem Regen und 20°. Hierfür kann der Fahrt nichts, sollte aber bewusst sein.Die Fahrt dauerte ca. 45 Minuten pro Strecke. Der Ticketverkauf des Parks findet in einem unfunktionierten Restaurant statt und erzeugt trotz mäßigem Besucherandrang erhebliche Wartezeit. Wer mit Kreditkarte bezahlt muss mit 2% Aufschlag rechnen. Mit einer der Seilbahnen geht es nun in den eigentlichen Park. Leider hatten wir weder Aussicht, noch konnten wir den Besuch aufgrund des erwähnten Wetters genießen. Auch wenn dies nicht der Fall gewesen wäre, bin ich der Meinung, lohnt sich weder der zeitliche noch der finanzielle Aufwand. Die Fahrt kostete pro Strecke ca. 16 €, der Eintrittspreis p. P. liegt bei 35 €. Dies für eine 150 Meter lange Brücke?! Der Rest des sogenannten Vergnügungsparks ist ein schlechter Scherz und erzeugt eher ein Gefühl von fremdschämen.Sollte man sich dennoch für einen Besuch entscheiden, lohnt es, für die Abfahrt die Waterfall-Seilbahn (hinten im Park) zu nehmen. Dort hat man noch einen interessanten Ausblick auf den schönen Wasserfall im Dschungel.
The 850.000 vnd entrance fee is not worth it!  Way too expensive! It’s very weather dependent and quite often foggy. Total tourist trap!

Avoid at all costs. There was nothing enjoyable about this expensive day out. The bridge, while impressive, was completely ruined by the mobs of people trying to get the perfect photo. You could barely walk! Not to mention, it was only built 4 years ago. Furthermore, the ‘French’ village was the most contrived tourist trap I’ve ever come across. I struggled to decipher anything French about it, between the medieval architecture, German beer hall and Swiss yodelling  - it was like an atrocious Eurodisney (as imagined by someone who has never been to Europe).

The entire area, Ba Na Hills, was just a complete tourist trap. True, the giant hands were quite a sight. But, the french village was so fake. It was almost atrocious! I can only say, please avoid this attraction completely, if you're not into tourist traps.

I just got back from traveling near here and I didn't go and I'm glad I didn't. It wasn't until I got to Vietnam that I discovered it's an amusement park and not even a real Bridge! More than that it's completely crowded and ridiculously expensive. Don't do the basketballs either. it's completely staged to an even though it was on a real River It felt like an amusement park as well. They play pop music and have a fisherman who is paid to throw a net in the air on to the water so that you can take a picture of the net in midair. Of course tips are encouraged. If you're a cultural traveler I would skip going to Hoi An... period.

The bridge is small, and the whole place is crowded beyond belief. The ticket is expensive, (driving from Da Nang is not a free ride either) and in bad weather you can see just about nothing at all. The bridge is from 2018, in case you were wondering if it was an ancient place. Would not go again, and neither should you. I guess the view could be pretty great in sunny weather though. But at a noticeable price.

The Golden Bridge was nothing more than phenomenally disappointing. The structure itself looked cheap and tacky in person — the hands feel like plastic, and even the moss was artificially painted on. The bridge is small, and packed full of hordes of extraordinary rude tourists; it’s nearly impossible to get a worthwhile photo. The French Village was a joke, again wildly artificial, with terrible food and restaurants with menus offering very little variety. Save your time, energy, and money and spending your day exploring Hoi An or Da Nang. This place just isn’t worth it.

Teníamos muchas ganas de ver el golden bridge porque habíamos visto fotos espectaculares en las redes sociales y pensábamos que era un elemento arquitectónico aislado en la montaña, algo místico y espiritual con la propia función un puente.  Pues resulta, que ingenuos de nosotros el puente no es nada más y nada menos que un reclamo para un parque temático cutre y salsichero. El puente es mucho más pequeño de lo que parece en las fotos y está llenísimo de turistas chinos, en diciembre,  no quiero imaginar que será eso en verano.pFuimos con un pack de excursión porque vimos que por nuestra propia cuenta salía mucho mas caro y con el pack entraba bus, entrada y otras actividades en la montaña y bufet. Nos dijeron que para ver el puente debíamos comprar la entrada a la montaña. Recalco entrada a la MONTAÑA, no dijeron entrada al parque temático. Y que en esa montaña había un sitio con restaurantes y algunos jardines. Entendimos que eran actividades separadas complementarias, es pero claro, ya que íbamos hasta allí pues veíamos todo. Que equivocados... El puente es parte de el parque temático.Para colmo nos pillo un dia de frío y lluvia, con una niebla que flipas, no veíamos a 5 metros de nuestras caras. No pudimos ni disfrutar del teleférico porque todo alrededor era niebla. La montaña estaba llena de tiendas souvenir  y comida, todo 4 veces mas caro que en la ciudad. Un ‘pueblo francés’ de cartón piedra que no pudimos ver bien por la niebla, pero que tampoco lamentamos ya que parecía super cutre. El buffet horroroso, muy mala comida y encima la ÚNICA opción vegetariana era arroz blanco. El parque de atracciones eran 3 plantas bastante pequeñas con atracciones muy cutres, decoración hortera y atracciones rotas. Había una montaña rusa que ni vimos ni pudimos usar, por la llovizna. Todo con colas de más o menos una hora de espera. El bus de vuelta al hotel salía a las 16, a la 13.00 ya estabamos deseando salir de allí. Buscamos sitio para sentarnos y no pudimos encontrar ni una silla. Hicimos cola 20 minutos para unas butacas de masaje, y ahí nos quedamos hasta que fue hora de irse. Fuera no se veía nada y llovía, y lo poco que se podía ver, tanto fuera como dentro era feo, cutre y caro.Lo mejor de esa excursión fue cuando vino la guía (que ella si fue súper agradable y simpática, la pobre no tiene la culpa de guiar gente a un sitio tan mediocre) a buscarnos para volver al hotel.

We took an organised trip on 27/10/19. This is an overcrowded theme park on a mountain of rain forest, it rains 8 out of 10 days according to our guide. The French stored wine there originally due to the cool climate.There were no views from the golden bridge due to the weather,however, there were so many people on it we would’nt have seen anything anyway. It is shoulder to shoulder, like a tube station at rush hour. The park itself was a let down, there were games machines and a couple of indoor cinema experiences. The food however was nice. If you do decide to visit don’t at weekends as locals have half price admission and it’s very busy. Oh some have no concept of queuing. Our trip left the park 3 hours early thank god. Best of luck.

We travelled to the Golden Hands Bridge in Ba Na Hills, a 1h15 minute drive from Hoi An. We were so excited as the photos on instagram looked amazing, but we were SO disappointed when we got there. Expectations VS reality! We left the hotel at 6am - to ensure we got one of the first lifts up to the bridge. However, we were among a million others doing the same thing!Don't get me wrong, the views from the cable cart of Ba Na Hills were amazing and was the only positive thing about the trip. However, one couldn’t help being distracted by the 5 models in wedding dresses along with their full on crew sat next to us. It only got worse when we go to the top. There were dozens of brides. We thought we'd just walked into bridal magazine shoot!!We were being told to move by the photographers, shoved about on the bridge by brides and found the whole bridge experience on the bridge quite upsetting! However I budged a bride out the way for a photo. We walked to the 'french garden' saw that it was fake and then we took the cable cart down. We were there for an hour MAX and the whole thing cost £100. TERRIBLE experience we had.
No merece la pena en absoluto. A los asiáticos les encanta pero no creo que a ningún occidental le aporte algo y menos aún europeos.

First of all, the property itself gets 5+ STARS! The Golden Bridge is gorgeous & it looks/feels like you’re in heaven! Everything up there was well thought out & had great rides, photo ops, etc. Fantasy Park is like Vietnam’s version of Disneyland. The property isn’t even fully done & more phases of construction is happening, so you can only image how it will look like. The cable car ride up to the mountain top was probably my favorite part of the day! Wow, what a beautiful view!Here’s where it goes downhill. People are rude as hell! There isn’t good crowd control on the bridge, so there’s HUNDREDS of people there at one time. Let’s not mention the umbrellas people like to use while you’re trying to walk through &  take a decent picture. Good luck in trying NOT to go off on people who poke you with them! No one says excuse me & it was a problem for me because I have 2 toddlers that were being trampled as we tried to walk through!THIS WAS THE REASON I PLANNED OUR TRIP TO VIETNAM! I was SO disappointed in my whole experience that it ruined my mood the rest of the day! I couldn’t get over the rude a$$ people everywhere we went & wanted to go back to our fabulous resort to get my zen back!

Definitely NOT as nice as the photos make it look.  Besides being way way over crowded, the only option is to buy one ticket for everything.  At 1.5 million VD for 2 people, I would rate this as a major rip-off of the tourists.  My recommendation - DON'T GO THERE!

Disappointing! Having seen all the amazing photos of the Golden Bridge all over social media, I was looking forward to seeing the bridge. Not worth the time and money! I was there very early in the morning to avoid crowds so it wasn't too bad but the bridge is small and unimpressive. The theme park is tacky. The whole complex is a weird collection of buildings. I was expecting to see some historical buildings as the French built a resort in Bana Hills at the beginning of 20 century but the only found was royal villa which was closed. I would not recommend this place to anyone.

Extremely disappointing. My friends and I have seen pictures of the bridge across social medias and looks appealing. Do not be deceived! Smaller than the pictures portray and ridiculously over crowded, 4 couples actually getting Wedding pictures up there too with many photographers. The 'theme park' leading off the bridge is tacky and expensive (even after paying just under a million dong). Very little rides and over priced food. Please save your money and go elsewhere ! X
This is part of a theme park. Bridge does not look like photos. Its new and made to look old. Busloads of tourists all wanting the selfie. Rest of theme park was tacky. Bridge highlight.

It‘s extremely expensive for absolutely nothing!! we where really disappointed! you pay 30$ for the park and the bridge was overcrowded so you cannot take a good picture and there is not really anything else interesting. Please save the money and time and enjoy da nang.
View was nice from the cable cars... but it stops there. The overall experience is a bizarre amusement park with terrible attractions and a mess of unorganised lines with people pushing and shoving.

I had high hopes for this place but I was disappointed, overpriced for what you actually get. I would really consider going before booking as if you want a terrible theme park then go ahead but if you want to see a nice bridge with views (can’t actually move or do anything) ensure it’s clear as our party could not see anything today. I will not be recommending to anyone.

This was by far the worst attraction I experienced in Vietnam. Such a waste of time and money. The cable car ride was the only thing that made it worth it. I really wanted to see the bridge but then I realized it’s the beginning of the end of some sort of amusement park trap. And I’m all about nature and I feel whoever thought of that idea just goes around ruining the beauty of things kind of like the rest of the people do in the world. Very unfortunate it was done in such a pristine location. I’m happy to have spent the money to maybe help deter one or two people from going there.  It could have been super majestic but instead I felt like I was in a weird Disney World atmosphere which I can’t stand to begin with.  I would imagine the feeling to be similar of having your arms where your legs go and legs where are arms go which is just strange, confusing, and isn’t going to get you anywhere you like.  I like my limbs where they are...... Thanks Sun Coast!

We just visited and because of the fog, there's basically zero visibility. The pamphlet photos show stunning views when the weather is nice... But don't expect to get a good photo because there are so many people!

NO!  come on!!! what s that price! and it s just a few meters long come on! need to wait for people to give you a lil space to take pictures, the way up is nice on the cable car, the so called bridge is short and crowded -- cool to impress your friends who will never go if you manage to take a nice picture on that stuff. good luck

As others have said check the weather first or else this will be a major disappointment. A few points here 1 - the park should not sell full priced tickets during a winter/off season as everything outside was closed when we went and the view on and of the bridge was virtually nil due to the fog2 - the food here is really poor quality and whilst it is a choose your own buffet style much of the food I expected to be hot was in fact cold and the quality of it very poor3 - the amusement park interior is mainly for kids and video gamers. As a mid 30’s male with my 30 year old female partner there was very little for us to do - the ghost train is 30-45 seconds of pure rubbish, the 4D penguin film is utterly pathetic and the rest of the attractions were either closed, had to pay extra for or were arcade type games 4 - for anyone using the references ‘Disney themed’ in their review please ignore them. As a person who has been to Disney on several occasions - to compare this to Disney world is laughable. Disney puts effort and thought into its creations, this is a poorly thought out attraction which is not even on the same level as Disney - as a Brit I would even compare it to Alton Towers. This place has no plan B for bad weather and it’s interactive place is hollow and crass. The Korean tourists who I’m guessing this is built for seemed to lap it up but it’s not worth wasting your money for the bridge visit of the weather is bad.5 - whether naivety or deliberate this final point stuck out like a sore thumb. All of the performers were segregated into different colour uniforms - I.e. white people wore one uniform, black another, Hispanic another etc and they were performing some moronic Christmas panto themed show - all of this felt very uncomfortable for us both - in addition to their being no locals in this whatsoever.A very hollow and crass attempt at Westernising a theme park. Not a good experience at all

My friend and I are traveling through Vietnam and had this bridge on our list of things we wanted to see. We drove our scooters 1.5 hours and once we got there, discovered this was in an amusement park. You CANNOT just go see the bridge unless you pay full entry price into the park, which is $30 USD each. So it would have been $60 for us both to see it. We decided this was extremely overpriced and turned back. So sad that they make this the only option.

I saw the Golden Bridge popping up all over Facebook in the lead up to my trip to Vietnam and just had to see it! Very, very disappointed. Turns out this “iconic” bridge is part of a theme park called Sunworld located on the Ba Na Hills. Very good marketing tools from Sunworld. Not worth the visit to see the bridge in a tacky park which cost is 700,000 Vietnamese dong to enter (~$30US). Fault does go to myself for not researching this properly. Can’t always believe the media it seems. Save your precious time and money and explore other parts of Vietnam. Sad to see the beauty of the hills being taken over by a theme park.

Have absolutely loved my trip around Vietnam, though my trip to the bridge which is in a theme park would of been better spent elsewhere. I should have researched better. Back to Hoi An which is amazing.

We saw all of the amazing photos of this bridge and added it to our bucket list! Unbeknown to us was the amount this day was going to cost us when all we wanted to see was the bridge. Our hotel arranged for a driver to take us to and from Ba Na Hills costing 600,000. What we did not realise was that the bridge was part of this weird part child’s entertainment park , spiritual gardens as well as some restaurants which is UNDER construction! Also some hotels that probably cost a fortune. Our driver took us to get tickets before we arrived, as soon as we saw the cost we were in shock- 700,000 each!! The driver didn’t speak any English and told us we had to pay so eventually we did. After about 45 minutes we arrived at a cable car station to which they did not accept our tickets!! After a lot of persuasion that we had already paid they let us in and we were off up the cable car! As soon as you get off the cable car the bridge is pretty much there, very spectacular but VERY crowded, everyone’s bathing into each other just to get a photo. Anyway past this we walked around to the Lady Buddha and through the gardens etc not realising that we were supposed to get another cable car to the village. As soon as we got there we wanted to leave, it was so tacky and cheap looking however we had spent 2,000,000 just getting in so we sucked it up and went inside one of the buildings (turns out the only one with games/rides) we waited for 20-30 minutes each time for two rides that lasted what felt like under a minute. We went to their wax works which was horrific. The only thing good about in there was a dinosaur world! Anyway we went to try and find food, bought two hot dogs as a snack for 90,000 each. We looked through all of the menus everywhere and nothing seemed to be below 200,000 for a meal that you would spend 120,000 on (if that)! We eventually ended up with a croissant each for 110,000 and thought to get a hot dog. We went to several hot dog stands and turns out they shut at 3! We went back to the restaurants and they didn’t serve anymore food until 17:00! We also went to a vending machine to get some water as everywhere refused to sell us some, we used all 5,000 notes and of course the machine refused to accept the final 5,000 (yes we tried 3 different 5,000s & none worked!) and funnily enough there was no refunds or change given!Anyway to top off an overly expensive day spent in crowds of people and queues not being respected as well as starving, we ventured down the hill only for our taxi driver to be 50 minutes late!So yeah if you’re just going for the bridge, it’s not worth the hassle nor the price!Total price of the day was over 2,450,000 or around £82

The Golden Bridge is part of a resort. Takes 45 min from Da Nang and it costs 700.000DNG to enter. It takes 5min to see the Bridge, which has an incredible view but nothing else. The resort is quite banal and tries to reproduce different parts of Europe. I personally didn't come here to experience the Oktoberfest or a French garden. There are much more interesting site here around, not worth visiting the Bana hills.

We gingen vanuit da nang met een gehuurde scooter naar de Golden bridge. Deze brug is onderdeel van een pretpark midden in de bergen. Zie het als een soort Disney qua uiterlijk ( maar dan net niet en zonder figuren) vooral een wandelroute met leuke spots voor foto's . Entree is 950.000 vnd . Binnen wat attracties die je apart per stuk moet betalen. Het uitzicht is mooi dat dan weer wel. Ben je fan van pretparken ga vooral maar voor een cultureel tripje sla dan over....

We understood at 1.5km elevation, if you want people in Vietnam outside all day, doing it on a cool mountain top is probably a good idea, but we couldn't understand what this 'park' is meant to be, and why it is what it is. It's a really weird place.We think it's a continuing work in progress, that when complete it will have hotel/holiday accommodation and be a fully fledged Theme Park with the usual roller coasters and thrill rides to entertain, but at the moment, it has none of these.There is the French village which seems to be a mash up of architectural styles (not all originating from France) literally ruined by a big glass multi-story building thrown in the floor plan. There are these brilliant buildings (all replicas of European styles) - but they have zero purpose, are empty with walkways and nothing more. The provide no utility other than to be observed.We got there early so avoided the heavy crowding which would have only grown through the day. Even then, it was quite busy trying to capture a photo on the "bridge to no-where" which also provides no function other than to be a frame for a photograph.There is also a Funicular (mountain train) which is there basically because they wanted a Funicular (as it seems to provide no genuine transportation justification) experience at the park.The buffet lunch was an extreme free for all - food was OK - but (appreciating we ate early because we attended early) it was already a crush when we ate, and can only imagine it would have been more chaos than a school lunch time when it got busier.Should you visit? I don't know - can't answer for you, it certainly was an experience, even if it was one we couldn't quite understand - but assuming it is still very much a 'work in progress' - perhaps in years to come it will become a must visit attraction.

Premetto che il sito è imponente, tutto curato alla perfezione peccato che siamo arrivati con una nebbia fittissima che non ci ha permesso di vedere quasi nulla. Molti dei ristoranti e/o bancarelle erano chiuse…. In giornate come queste dovrebbero chiudere o perlomeno avvisare. comunque reputo che sia solo una trappola per turisti non la consiglio

Attrazione turistica assolutamente priva di senso. Finte ricostruzioni di città europee, punti ristoro per spennare i turisti, lunghe cable car e in sostanza niente di reale e di bello. Solo finzione. E non sarebbe neanche tempo del tutto perso, visto il paesaggio circostante, se non fosse che in pochi periodi dell’anno c’è buona visibilità quindi anche la maggiore attrazione il Golden Bridge risulta immerso nella nebbia. Quasi una truffa. Il tipo di turismo che fa male al Vietnam

We are all slightly speechless at this experience and we really should have done more research. The highlight of the trip was the buffet. The roller coaster was good. The cable car was clean and well maintained and the views good. The gardens were ok. We had a tour guide but could have, and should have done it without, however the maps aren't great and don't really explain what to expect around the park. The actual bridge is much shorter than Instagram would lead you to believe and massively crowded even on a quite day. They say there was in attendance 15k ppl on our day and on a busy day they expect 50k. Id hate to go when it was packed.If you like over the top amusement parks this might be for you. Would we go again? Definitely not.

Visiting this bridge was a disappointing experience. First of all, in order to get to the bridge, we had to pay the full price of entering the Sunworld amusement park, which wasn’t cheap. The bridge itself was filled with people, and it was almost impossible to squeeze through. The view isn’t as spectacular as advertised, especially if the weather isn’t right. The bridge goes around the cable car station, making it seem like it isn’t actually found in nature. In summary, there are a lot of better places to visit in the Da Nang area that truly highlight Vietnam’s natural beauty.

Ci si arriva con una delle funivie più lunghe del mondo, e il viaggio offre spunti panoramici davvero spettacolari. Il ponte, di recente costruzione (2018) è diventato uno dei simboli del paese per questo per noi tappa irrinunciabile. Nella realtà dei fatti è stato parecchio deludente: innanzitutto è inserito in un parco divertimenti il cui ingresso è carissimo (intorno ai trenta euro, che rispetto alle altre attrazioni vietnamite è una cifra astronomica). Il numero di turisti che lo visita (anche in una giornata piovosa) è talmente alto che è quasi impossibile godere della passeggiata sul ponte. Infine, data l’altitudine può capitare che il tempo sia poco clemente e il panorama sia coperto da nebbia.

We didn’t research this as well as we should have. :(We foolishly thought this was an iconic/historic bridge. Actually it’s in a Disney type amusement park (though it falls well short of Disney’s standards) and is a bit cheesy, packed with tourists/influencers/brides, all having g their photos taken and causing big queues on the bridge. The cable car to the bridge is pretty cool, and the longest in the world! After the bridge we took another cable car across to the amusement area. Mixture of a roller coaster (closed for maintenance), a ride that sends you up and down a pile (like a donut on a stick) and various VR rides. Lots of food concessions. And some dancers. There’s a hotel in the park, but as everything closes at 5.30, including g the cable cars, it would be more like a prison camp? !Go if you fancy it, but there are better things to spend your money on.

Es un parque temático, un mini Europa. Eso sí, casi exclusivo para los asiáticos; casi imposible andar entre ellos y sus camaras y palos selfies etc. El parque no vale para nada, una pérdida de tiempo y de dinero por supuesto. Lo único curioso es el teleférico en el que te tiras 20 mins para llegar al maldito parque. Desde el puente tienes unas vistas espectaculares, pero eso si te dejan... con tanta gente es imposible.

If you enjoy your entertainment 'sugar-laced' then a trip to Ba Na hills is an ideal option. However, if you are purely visiting to marvel at a piece of engineering and take in the majestic views, you will need to set the filter to '10' to prevent a bout of nausea. At 900,000vnd it doesn't come cheap either and to my knowledge there's no way of getting there under your own steam. Finally be prepared for queues and crowds. Each to their own.
Visited earlier in 2019. Very average experience. Looks MUCH better on social media. Not very impressive and so crowded. Wouldn’t return

The bridge and the cable car actually arent all that bad, but once that is done youre stuck with crappy asian disneyland. Then its either enormous crowds where everything is an hour long queue, or if its quiet, just about everything is closed. If youre with a tour operator, that means youll have to kill time for 3-4 hours before heading back down the cable car.  If, like me, it was cloudy, theres not even a view to appreciate.If there was a way to just do the cable car and bridge, that could be worth the effort, but unfortunately there is no such option without paying full price.

Please be aware that The Golden Bridge is just a tourist attraction at Vietnams version of Disney Land.Probably my own fault for not doing proper research, but for some reason I thought this was an old structure made many many years ago. It’s not, it’s just a new structure (fairly impressive) up up in the mountains at a theme park that is still being built.You get a cable car up 850,000VND (for the cable car and all attractions) & if the weather is good, you’ll get some great views. If theme parks are your thing, then great, but if you’re expecting culture, then think again.Only really reviewing this to hopefully stop others making the same mistake we did.Loved Vietnam though!!!

We arrived at the bottom of Ba Na Hill and everything looks fake and plastic like in Disneyland. We took the cable car which has an amazing view on the mountains and the forests. At the top we went on the Golden Bridge, it looks nice…BUT!The bridge is crowded with tourists and our guide told us we were lucky that day because there was only about 5000 people. On holiday season, you’ll find up to 25’000 people. Don’t expect to take nice pics, too many people…Behind the bridge is hidden a giant amusement park but I should say tourist trap (we didn’t know before going). Everything looks artificial, fake and made of plastic. The park feels like Disneyland, showing european stereotyped village. We ate at the buffet and most dishes are cold because they are not kept warm on plates. Moreover the food doesn’t taste fresh besides fruits and a few meats they cook in front of you.A lot of the park is still under construction and will be probably much bigger. We’re not hardcore ecologist or anything but seeing people destroy such a natural place to build a giant park seems like a bad idea…We’ve been lucky our hotel made tours there but it’s expensive compared to other places (marble mountains, lady bouddha) you’ll find in Da Nang and in our opinion not worth it. We feel it’s all a trap to make you spend money hidden behind a beautiful bridge.

We planned a trip on 13 March 2022 to find out after getting here the bridge is not going to open until 18 March because of COVID lock downs. Thats assuming it will open at that time. Kind of disappointed!

It's very crowded, like crazy crowded and they don't have any solution to separate, it's kinda dangerous when many people on the bridge. And honestly you can't take a good photo in a bunch of rude tourists.Even you try to come soon, there's always have guests who stay overnight, also wedding shooting shows. And noone can help you if they're rude and tell you to move for their shots.And don't come if it's windy or cloudy, you won't see a damn thing.

Dalla pubblicità sembrerebbe un ponte nel vuoto e uno si aspetta una emozione da brivido, invece è un grandissimo parco di divertimento. Ideale per il turismo cinese e coreano, ma non certo per un turista europeo o americano abituato a ben altri parchi divertimento. L'idea è curiosa ,ma  veramente è una immensa costruzione  per turisti.

Very very VERY turistic and VERY plastic fantasticPlease note following before consider to visit:1. This is very very VERY touristic and crazy crowded.2. Everything is plastic fantastic and not authentic at all. 3. To like this you need to like big crowds and the feeling of doing sterotypical tourist stuff.

Car was 20mins late for no reason and the driver couldn’t speak any English. We got a bottle of water. You pay 750,000 per adult for a ticket to the Hills (~$50aud) We hassled for a free trip to Da Nang airport the following day as compensation.

The bridged was a little bit dissapointing. It is very small, but yes, has a nice view. It is located inside Sunworld, so it is very expencive to get in. Sunworld is big though, so maybe fun with kids. But not worth it, if you only want to see the bridge

Went to Sun world yesterday, could not see a thing. Was a little annoyed at myself for not checking the weather as you cannot see anything whilst it’s foggy. Was a complete waste of money and time as you literally cannot see anything. Definitely do not go unless it is sunny. You do not get any refund or discount when the weather is bad either.

While the design for this 1 year old circular sky bridge is nice and the views on 50% of the bridge are nice when not facing towards the theme park, the place is a total tourist trap.  Bana Hills is a fake normandy village/mini disneyland theme park geared towards large tour groups and to get up to the bridge you first have to pay for the gondola ride up to the theme park and then try and not get a black eye from all the selfie sticks being waved around.  If you are lucky enough to get on the first gondola up to the park, maybe you can get an unobstructed view, but more likely you will just be jostled and slapped about by tour groups rather than enjoying the view.  I would not wish this place on my worst enemy but in fairness the design of this bridge is nice

What most people don't realise is the Golden Bridge is part of an overcrowded 'theme park'. We found it hard to appreciate the bridge given the mass amounts of tourists and the rest of the area left us confused and underwhelmed after paying 750,000VND per person. Highlight of our visit was the scooter rider there.Best to avoid if a manufactured tourist attraction isn't your thing.

The bridge is part of the Ba Na Hills theme park, built for tourists about 15 months ago. It's a stopover on the cable car up the hill to the rest of the theme park. We somehow didn't realise that from what we saw online. Vaguely interesting but has no history and is jam packed with tourists.

Went for a day trip up to the Sun Work in the hillsCost was 850,000 dongs , including the return bus tripWell worth the money just to wander about . Took the second cable car to avoid the queues of people going straight to the bridge, which was much better, plus when we got to the next cable car station, we had a vernicular train ride higher up which was goodYes the town is a little tacky, but certainly well built, with lots of gardens, flowers and lots of places t sit and admire the viewsSpent most of the day walking around the village, then caught the cable car back down to the bridge , which was nice and quiet so no crowds to bother withIt’s ok, but very overated, smaller than it looks on photos, but still worth seeingThe cable car is excellent too, clean and efficient, with great views all the way to Da Nang

Wasn't what we expected. Theme park style destination with ok show,fake French town and poor quality gardens. The bridge was ok but hoards of tourists. We could barely move forwards or back!! The cable car ride up and back was the only nice part of this place. We were very disappointed.

As part of our transfer from Hoi An to Hue along the Hai Van Pass we stopped to see the Golden Bridge.  We stopped in the carpark of this attraction. Sunworld Ba Na Hills is the name of the attraction and then took a short bus ride to the attraction.  Its like a plastic fantastic Disney type place.  The tickets were around US$130 for the family.There is a bit of a walk along a covered walkway which has pretty carp ponds and greenery along the way to the cable car entrance, there was hardly any waiting at this point.  We had a cable car to ourselves and were whisked away on a very scenic trip up the beautiful mountain, the views were spectacular both out to the sea and of the mountain area.Once up on top of the mountain the Golden Bridge is a short walk from the cable car, there was no crowd control, it was very very packed on the bridge, of course everyone was trying to get a good selfie, it was a cool bridge and the views were great but the crush of getting over the bridge was not fun, be careful with small children they will be able to see nothing.  There whole area is packed with people.Other than the bridge and views there wasnt really anything up there, a few gardens where a throng of tourists were laying on the garden trying to get there best selfie.  There are a few places to eat and some very grim toilet facilities with very long queues.  It was a really crowded hot place to be, there was no shade unless you went inside to eat.  There were long queues for everything from an ice cream, to gaining access to the views, to going to the toilet.  The toilets had water all over the floors and were unpleasant unlike the ones at the bottom of the cable car.  As a family it was not worth the cost for us and I would not recommend it.  This was my only planning error on our fab trip to Vietnam, for us it was a waste of time and money.

We arrived at the village, and there is no way finding signage for the bridge. One of the most iconic elements and it’s not indicated on the map on the site or the brochure. It was extremely overcrowded and ruined the experience for us.

Firstly it's pretty small. It is definitely not what it looks like in photos. Costs about 30 USD to get into the entertainment park. Gets pretty crowded on most days. If this was in universal studios you might have skipped it as a walkway.

What they don’t tell you about visiting the golden bridge is, you CANT just visit the golden bridge. You have to pay the full price for the whole park including the theme park which we had no interest in. Each ticket is 750,000d. The views are incredible and it is worth it when you are there whoever the price for what most people want to see is far to steep! Queuing can be up to 30 mins to get on the cable cart at the best of times. Also be prepared for people pushing in front of you. Queue system out here is non-existent! Staff have minimal English so if you have any queries makes sure you sort it before you go! Do not be fooled by some advert on here which says 100,000d for cable cart as you do have to pay the full 750,000d

We arrived early morning and after leaving the cable car we found we were in a theme park with all that has to offer. The bridge was crowded with people. If you like theme parks then this place is for you. We left early.

Unfortunately the day we went it was rainy and misty so we had no views at all. However, the bridge is just a small part of the overall theme park, which if you like a Disney themed mock French village perched on a hiill side that is fine but if you don't like us it is just crass in the extreme! the buffet lunch is mainly luke warm and poor quality, not surprising as it is mass catering. Overall we regretted going.

The golden bridge - we had big expectations on this day trip. We rented a car from Hoi An (about 1h 15min) to the place and were shocked to see it’s essentially a adventure resort. You take a cable car to the mountain top (700k per person) and get surprised by a French medieval castle “complex”. Parts are still under construction. The bridge itself is handmade and opened earlier in 2018. Its shorter then we expected and unfortunately it was foggy (due to altitude). Be aware of it being very touristic - we got caught by surprise and left rather disappointed.

Two stars because the bridge was cool to see and the views from the cable car ride up to it were spectacular (pictured). However, be aware that the online marketing of the bridge is really misleading. Some light research will show that it is a feature of a larger theme park, which I felt marred the experience of visiting the bridge. The wider theme park was insufferable due to the amount of tourists pushing each other and snipping over getting in the way of each other’s photo opportunities. Shuffling past the masses of people on the bridge left us irritated and unable to appreciate it. Save yourself 700,000d and have a beer on the beach instead.

The title speaks for itself. You can barely even walk on the bridge let alone talk a half decent photo. The fantasy park is just as bad, lines everywhere flocked with Chinese tourists pushing in. The hands are cool for about 2 minutes before you just get hit in the face with constant selfie sticks and tourist giving you dirty looks like you had just stolen their lunch because you’re in their photo... there’s literally no room to move. If you want to see wannabe Instagram models, this is your place. Will not be returning: 2/5

They made a French village with the bridge supported by hands a 90 minute drive from Danang on the top of a mountain. The view is indeed very good...however for Europeans familiar with the originals it is a disappointing place. The rather poor entertainments by some East-Eu youngsters make it even a bit sad. Those are more about the selfie opportunities for locals then anything else. Even the performers seem a bit embarrassed. Indeed a waste of time and money.

We expected this bridge to be set in the hillside on main highway ...an iconic gateway sculpture ! Seen and enjoyed as you took the scenic road of the Pass ( particularly as it was govt funded!) It’s Not !It’s absolutely miles off the highway , embedded in a most bizarre plastic and concrete Theme Park costs $30 each entrance fee to ride a cable car and enter the Theme park . The bridge links nothing , it’s just there at the top of the cable car - next to a row of vibrating massage chairs and a DJ playing very loud French "pop" music ...if there even is such a thing !!! which annoyingly is blasting out all over the place .Don’t waste time or money unless you have an excess of both ...disappointing to say the least !

We were on a conducted tour, run by an Australian tour company, Holiday Max, and this was part of the itinerary.  In other words, we did not choose the trip to the Golden Bridge.  However, if you are doing Vietnam on your own, I would recommend it, IF you have the time to spare.  For us it was a whole-day affair, as our group was staying in a hotel in Hoi An.  If you are staying in a hotel in Da Nang, you could perhaps do it in half a day, either morning or afternoon.  Your hotel could easily arrange it for you.  Our day was spoilt by bad weather....it rained incessantly, so if you have some control on when to go....try and choose a bright sunny day.  Plenty of photo opportunities, especially from the bridge.  It is indeed an unusual sight, with the huge masonry hands holding up the curving bridge!   But, please remember this is purely a "tourist" attraction....there is no history behind it....just an unusual bridge, and some nice mountain views....nothing very remarkable.  There is a restaurant on top where you can have a meal...we did not pay, it was included in our tour package....but I suspect it would be quite expensive.....it looked quite fancy.  There is a nice garden, but again, nothing super unusual.  In short: if you are staying in Da Nang, and have half a day to kill, you might consider this. If you are staying in Hoi An, it would take a whole day.  Skip this, if you are pressed for time.  Also remember, this is NOT a place of any historical or cultural significance.

Il ponte è carino da vedere. Sospeso tra le montagne.Per arrivarci bisogna entrare nel parco divertimenti e pagare il biglietto. Prendete quello con il pranzo incluso così con pochi euro in più potete avere un buffet vario e(eravamo al Four Season).Per arrivare al ponte c'è da prendere la funivia. La nostra è durata 32 minuti all' andata e poi, arrivati su, per arrivare al ponte abbiamo dovuto prendere un'altra funivia e da lì, riprenderne un' altra (che però era più veloce...e ci ha messo la metà del tempo) per scendere.Il parco divertimenti è solo un accumulo di negozi e ristoranti. 5 minuti sono più che sufficienti.Se ne vale la pena?Si, se non dovet sacrificare altre escursioni, e se non avete paura della funivia e delle altezze.Diversamente...non andate.

It's a good location for photos, but you have to arrive very early or be very lucky. There are always lots of people, so getting a good shot is really difficult. If you visit the Ba Na Hills amusement park, however, walking over the bridge is a must!

It is overrated. But french village is beautiful and worth it. Food in French village is expensive. There is a stunning view from Golden Bridge, but I don't know how many days in a year clear view is available because of humid weather.

Spettacolare? Si se la giornata è bella. Ne vale la pena? Forse no, visto il prezzo, le code e, in generale, l’atmosfera da trappola per turisti che respiri tutto il tempo. Carina la salita e discesa in funivia. Il ponte è sempre stra affollato quindi armatevi di pazienza e non salite se il tempo è pessimo.

There are a few things you really should know before going here.This, in essence, is a $20 photo opertunity.The bridge is a 20-minute cable car ride, and on the day we attended, it was very wet with low clouds, so the cable car ride was somewhat diminished for photos, etc.You don't need a guide, so as to be stuck to someone else schedule.You really don't, just get a grab, buy your ticket on arrival, and be assured there will be many taxis looking for your business when you come back down. Get there early or late, all the tours and coaches arrive at circa 09.30 and both the cable car and bridge are mayhem, yet when we were desending at 14.00 many of the ascending cable cars were empty.The bridge is a novelty photo opportunity and possibly worth a visit just for that.A further 5 minutes in another cable car (already paid for with your initial ticket) will take you to the top of the hills, which is in essence a Disneyland type creation and unless you have kids in tow simply don't bother. It's just plastic creations looking like medieval villages and gothic type churches, etc, and a myriad of food options.That said, we ate in the little Tokyo barbecue and sushi buffet, which was OK, but only because we were there anyway.So, no guide, get to the bridge for the 07.30 opening or shortly thereafter, or else wait until about about mid day.One hour is more than sufficient to get your photos, skip the hills,  taxi back to your hotel and your wrapped in less time than you will queue with a tour and thereafter be stuck there all day, wishing you'd listened to my advise.

Leuk om even te zien,maar houd er rekening mee dat het echt enorm toeristisch is,wij vonden het echt veel te massaal !!Maar als je van het pretpark gevoel moet je zeker gaan,het is zeg maar een beetje het efteling idee.Wij hadden niet in de gaten dat je in een pretpark beland,wij dachten met de kabelbaan omhoog en dat is het,maar dat idee was dus fout.O,ja als je een tour hebt geboekt willen ze je daar ook laten eten,doe dat NIET !! Je komt in een massale vreetschuur terecht waar nog minstens 1000 man zitten te eten van het buffet,echt niet leuk,veel te lawaaierig en te vol.

Ba Na Hills: station un kitsch mais belle vue sur la région de Da Nang. Trop d'affluence. Certains bâtiments ont été construits avec du marbre venant d'Italie.  Dommage que que l'accessibilité ne se fasse que par téléphérique. Pas d'alternatives en cas de coupures d'électricité assez fréquentes au Vietnam. Certaines activités non accessibles aux personnes à mobilité réduite...

Absolutely no view at all. Was completely foggy and couldn't see anything. Kind of a bummer to drive all this way and take the long tram up to see nothing. Fun to climb around the top and grab a glass of wine.

Wer bereit ist mehr als 100 $ von Da Nang aus mit einem privaten Fahrer, für ein Instagram Hotspot zu bezahlen ist hier richtig. Mittlerweile zahlt man 40 $ pro Person für die unabdingbare Gondelfahrt hoch auf die Ba Na Hills zugegebenermaßen eine schöne Gondelfahrt über den Dschungel. Für ein passendes Foto ist sicherlich die Mittagszeit zwischen 13 und 14:00 Uhr die Beste , da um diese Zeit es meist aufklart. Keine Garantie! Wir haben tolle Fotos machen können.Über den Rest hüllen wir den Mantel des …………….

Absolutely no view at all. Was completely foggy and couldn't see anything. Kind of a bummer to drive all this way and take the long tram up to see nothing. Fun to climb around the top and grab a glass of wine.

Wij hadden niet in de gaten dat het een pretpark is Mistig dus weinig te zien Grote tegenvaller voor veel geld De rit met de kabelbaan is wel ontzettend mooi
The bridve its self and cabe car ride ae fantasticWas toldwhen we paid some attractions were closedWhen we got to top would say 80% of the shut but this had no reflection in ticket price

Ok, so I rated this average but it would be between average and good. Its very touristy and I ended up driving a motorbike to Bana Hills from Danang. It was a fairly easy ride and arrived close to 1pm. I had read that if you avoided the mornings, you would avoid some of the crazy crowds. The drive was fine (just got lost once) and pretty decent road. It took me 1 minute to get my $40 cable car ticket and then you get whisked thru the lines to get on the cable car. I got it all to myself and really the best part of the whole thing is the cable car ride to the top. Music in the car, serene, beautiful views, about a 20 minute ride. Even though it was misty/rainy/foggy, when I got out to the bridge there were still A LOT of people for my taste. It's a nice view if you can get photos of the people not in them. I get it, the bridge is all over social media but would I do it again? Probably not as it felt like Disneyland. The young folks who work there are all super nice, and the French village "unique", but it felt like I was in an amusement park. Next time, I probably would opt to visit another site; however I did enjoy that I didn;t have to wake up at the crack of dawn, took my own sweet time on my motorbike and it was fairly easy and less crowds (however I would have hated to see it in full crowds. IMO go see it for sunset if you have the time and much less crowds.Even though I'm glad I took the ride out there, and it was only $40 for the cable car (versus $150-$200 for tours) I probably would not ever go back.
Der Ausblick von der Brücke ist sehr beeindruckend!Die Brücke selbst ist schön angelegt.Da es ein sehr beliebtes Fotomotiv ist, ist es sehr vollAm Besten gegen Abend besuchen

We took the Hoi An/ Da Nang - Ba Na Hills - Golden Bridge Deluxe Small group tour. Our tour guide, Su,  was lovely, and very interesting information was shared during the drive. Our first stop was to a marble works studio where you can buy statues/carvings. There was a bit of pressure as the staff was very much at hand to assist you with a purchase, but a polite no mostly sufficed. Arriving at our destination, we took a cable car up to the bridge and walked it. There were many, many visitors but there are enough opportunities to grab a picture, some at the other end looking back. The  banquet/buffet which was very busy and targeted to feeding the masses over quality, was not somewhere we would have chosen to eat but there are other alternatives available.  The unruly behaviour of the visitors was what really put a damper on the afternoon, with queue jumping and disregard for others to the point that the staff were having to police the lines. An afternoon storm that delayed the trip back down did nothing to make that better but the cable car engineering was fascinating as were some of the stories about what was being depicted at the park. Overall seeing the bridge was definitely worth the trip, but I would suggest a private tour so that you can get there very early and leave before the rush of crowds.
Scenery was quite good, but there are so many tourists. Everyone tries to take a photo in this narrow bridge among the hundreds of people.

I thought this was expensive for what it was. Cable car ride was about 20 minutes, might have been more impressive on a clear day.  The bridge itself was ok but full of tourists  taking photos at every turn. I know to expect it but this was nothing exceptional for me. The rest of the park seemed to be aiming for a sort of Disney Park eventually. Probably ok if you have children or want posing photos with a sunflower garden, wooden bridge, cartoon cat.

The bridge itself isn’t actually that impressive, but I guess it does make for a good photo. The views when we went were completely covered by clouds so we couldn’t even appreciate that. We were getting bashed into by the sheer amount of tourists wanting to take 1000 photos of the bridge in different poses. I think we paid around $40 for a ticket entry, but this gets you into Sun World, which is a really terrible amusement park and tourist trap next to the bridge. Severely disappointing experience and I would recommend you save your money.

Golden Bridge in Da Nang really quite an extraordinary sight.  Although ideally you would see it on a sunny day to get it's full magnificence, even on a rainy misty day it was a memorable experience. This 'hand bridge' has become another in the long list of Da Nang things to do, and rightly so.  Getting a cable car is usually a fun activity to do in itself but here it's just the start. The huge stone hands are really something.  A great photo opportunity and very popular with people taking pics so you may have to wait before getting a clear view. There's also a German style beer town, a beautiful garden with colourful flowers and so much more.  A really fun filled day.

Ba Na Hills: If you’re going just for the hand bridge, don’t bother. It’s about a 45 min car ride from Da Nang waterfront. The bridge is basically the welcome entrance to a larger amusement park (“Sun World”). But once you get to the park ticket gate, you have to purchase tickets (35+ usd per person) for the whole amusement park. The actual bridge is a 15-20min gondola ride away from the park main entrance. At the end of the gondola is the hand bridge. We were the second people in line (arrived at about 7am in the morning). The gondola starts at 8am for tourists, but once we took the gondola up there were already people on the bridge. (Possibly people who stayed at the amusement park overnight). Very hard to get non-crowded photos. Nice ride on gondola, but I’d give it a pass and stay longer at Hoi An or try going to Hue.

The bridge is beautiful, but if the weather isn’t nice I don’t think it’s worth the 750000 dong to get up there. It’s a long drive from the city and the rest of the Ba Na Hills park isn’t very exciting compared to American theme parks. I’m sure it would be nicer in the sun, but you don’t know what the weather is like at the very top until you get there, since it’s different than on the ground.
As is fairly common, it gets cold, foggy, on the bridge. There is no view at all sometimes. It is also very cold and wet. Would not go unless the weather conditions were decent.
The timing has to be right or else you won’t  be able to see much . Low clouds meant poor visibility which spoilt our trip but can’t be helped.

Disappointing. I just wanted to go up and take a picture, turns out you need to buy an entrance fee 0f 30 usd or more to go but once you get there you realize you bought a ticket to a theme park. Very cool and very lame at same time.You will be done walking through the park and bridge in an hour and then are stuck in a park with rides and people in costumes.

Unfortunately I wasn't able to gret tree amazing experience Id hoped for but this was only due to the weather. It was overcast,  rainy & cold & at some stages we couldn't see 5 meters in front of us & it made the walk ways & steps very slippery.It was still a nice sight but not as great as we had hoped for bridge is only very short so I can only imagine how crowded it gets when the weather is great.  I had hoped I could just see the bridge & be on our way but its not like that at all you are inside the amusement park that you have paid entry for so kids wanted to check it all out.

The bridge itself and views would be spectacular on a good day, inevitably as it is so high the weather and visibility is often poor.  The bridge is actually a lot shorter than it seems in photos and doesn’t take long to cross.  Be aware that the bridge is actually part of a modern (well thought out) theme park - very Disney inspired.  If you are travelling with a young family you may enjoy this.  When we googled the bridge we didn’t pick this information up, if you are looking just to see the bridge and spectacular views then you maybe disappointed by the crowds bring shepherded Disney style.
The bridge is only 100 meters long and good luck to you if you want that selfie as there are hundreds of folk who want the same. Bear in mind this bridge was build specifically for the photo shot
Siamo stati in Vietnam nel mese di dicembre e abbiamo visitato Banna hill.Carino ma nulla di eccezionale ; forse più adatto a una clientela asiatica !È abbastanza un pomeriggio per vedere tutto!

Make sure the weather is clear . Unfortunately we couldn’t see much as it was foggy and raining and for 750.000 VD I would strongly advise you anyone plan their visit accordingly to the weather- there is free bike parking( we drove by bike ) - take a map on the entry as the whole park is big and without knowing where you going you may miss out on stuff- the french village isn’t what we went to Vietnam to see , do A NO for us- the bridge itself is much smaller than I imagined but it is pretty ( if the weather is clear)-take pack lunck if on the a budget, food is utterly expensive - take rain cover as it is humid up there -the cable car stops in 2 points

We drove there by scooter from Da Nang (could recommend to do so, nice trip and super cheap).Took the cable car up, which was already a nice experience with beautiful views, and arrived right at the Golden Bridge.What you don't see in all the Instagram pictures is the surroundings with a big circus tent and all kind of themepark buildings, so this was a bit surprising :-).Nevertheless, we enjoyed being there and scrapping it from our bucket list. The view from the bridge is very nice and the themepark is actually also pretty enjoyable.

When we sightsaw the Golden Bridge and village, we couldn’t see the view clearly due to foggy & raining cats and dogs. However, they were so beautiful especially the bridge view. We didn’t regret to come. Knew that most of the people came to the bridge, there was so crowded and the people couldn’t take a nice photograph that many heads were there. When we walked through the bridge, not many people. We could take photographs with different poses that no people obstructed. Due to the bad weather, so upset to have no roller coaster opened. Actually, we looked forward to having a try of it before coming. We should wait till next time. So impressive to get on a ride of the cable car. In the bad weather, the wind blew a bit strong and our cable car moved right and left. A bit exciting!

If you like a real tourist built destination then the golden bridge and ‘French village’ is it. The gondola ride is scenic, rides above the rainforest and river and is worth the trip. No need to take a tour as we did, leave early to beat the crowds. Overall not my cup of tea.

This is not exactly a bridge from one side to another, but more of a short walkway that extends outside the edge of the building. In pictures it looks great, but in person I found it looks pretty fake, just like the rest of the Ba Na Hills theme park, and it is overcrowded and overrated. Three stars only for the spectacular view over the surrounding countryside. You are literally above the clouds. If you're going to visit, go early in the day. By late afternoon the clouds start to descend below the walkway and you won't be able to see anything. Certainly an interesting experience, but not really worth going out of your way to visit.

Netter Versuch für einen Themenpark aber leider schlecht umgesetzt vor allem Service. Mehrer Fahrgeschäfte gleichzeitig geschlossen, Funpark ist nur indoor viel verschenkter Platz zu wenig interessantes hauptsächlich Spielautomaten. Haben ein Tag bei aufkommenden Regen erwischt, alles wird vorzeitig geschlossen selbst Restaurant‘s , die Mitarbeiter schließen allen und verlassen in großen Schwärmen das Gelände und als Gast ist man dann allein.  Lohnt nicht wirklich. Das Geld kann man sich sparen mit dem Drumherum. Das einzig interessante war die Auffahrt mit der Seilbahn. Die Aussicht ist sehr schön. Sonst der obere Teil billiger abklatsch von Disney World.

The Golden Bridge is an experience. Lots of publicity and you say to yourself - “I’d like to go there!”What you don’t know is that it is really part of a theme park. And it’s a great place to go if you want to share it with 100,000 of your newest ‘best friends’. A great 15 minute cable gondola ride to the top, but is totally over commercialised. I’m not sorry I went, but it was not what I expected. “At least I can say, I’ve seen it!”

Great views from the top of the mountains.Good spot for sunset if the clouds allow.The bridge itself looks better in pictures.Fibreglass hands very obviously fake once you get there, whole village a theme park.Best part is the cable car rides.

This bridge looks great in the photo but the novelty dies there - it's just a bridge with giant hands on them.The ones in Da Nang and Hoi An are heaps better, with more history behind them and they're free to use and get to.At the end of the day, it's a small part of the fun adventure up in Sunworld Ba Na Hills.

If you’re in Da Nang it’s definitely one it the top place to visit. It can be very crowded so need to be patience for that perfect picture on the bridge. I would not come to Da Nang just for this. We came in Sept during the storm so there were hardly any visibility when you’re on top. It’s definitely much cooler at the top which was a nice break from the heat. We were a bit mislead by YT thinking this place was an authentic remodel of a hidden city. Our tour guide told us it’s an amusement park basically with some small remnant of a couple original structure. The long cable car ride to the top had nice views.

The bridge is a small part of Ba na hills theme park, which we enjoyed.The bridge is a fun sight but much smaller in real life and crammed full of people taking selfies and getting annoyed that your on the bridge infringing on their photos! Not really a must see place for me.
The view from the bridge is amazing, you can see the entire city of Da Nang. But it is way too overcrowded by people taking pictures.
6월에 다녀왔는데 굳이 다녀올 필요가 있나 싶습니다.사람은 무지무지하게 많으며 새치기는 기본이며 더워요.보니깐 땅에 토? 한 것도 있는 것 같은데 안 치우고 그냥 가시네...골든 브릿지는 그냥 사진 한번 찍고 바로 나와버렸습니다

The first thing you need to know is that the Golden Bridge is in a theme park called Sun World at Ba Na Hills so it is not possible to go the bridge without paying for the theme park! For a day trip here we paid 1,100,000 VND which is about the average rate however, you can get a shuttle bus or taxi and pay for your tickets on the door the average price is around £32-40 for tours or paying a taxi/ entrance fee. To get to the park it’s about a 45 minute drive from Danang. To enter the park you have to go up on cable cars which is a really good experience they are so high up and around 6km long in total so you get great views. The theme park it’s self is a little weird but an interesting experience and overall enjoyable! The rides are average and not as good as the UK but it’s still a fun day! On other reviews we read you had to pay for individual rides we didn’t experience this and went on a fair few! The best part about the rides were the locals reactions!  The bridge itself isn’t like the images on google, they are taken with drone shots when no ones on the bridge, while the bridge is good it is soooooooo busy so you will struggle to get photos, also expect to get hit in the head with umbrellas frequently throughout the day it becomes very tedious. The food and drinks at the park aren’t too expensive but of course expect to pay a little more than usual! Overall it’s worth a visit but it’s a bit of an odd place!

The cable car trip to the golden bridge is excellent, but once there you’re fighting the crowds the whole time, yes the views are good and it’s an amazing piece of workmanship so if you brave the crowds it’s worth it
dissapointed we could not drive to the location and catch a cable cart there are a zillion tourist and its very hot and humid in the winter
Though the bridge is an attraction, the place is just crowded with "Selfie" obsessed tourist.  We could hardly find place to enjoy the bridge and view and people were only busy clicking selfies here

The bridge itself is nice, its in a nice mountain top... however, it's overcrowded (and people don't mind stepping over you to get where they want, beware). Too bad they force you to visit the rest of this awful place... It's also much colder than Da Nang (at least 10-15 degree less), be prepared.
Gret views but too many people. Not suitable is you do not like crowds. You need to pick a day that is not a public holiday in the neighbouring countires.

The golden bridge is a newer addition to this theme park and was the main reason for my visit. Booked a return trip by taxi from Danang and admission plus buffet with Klook. This part was effortless and we avoided the line up to buy tickets. Park is extremely ovecrowded with busloads of Korean tourists. Going later might help with the long lines but be aware its very foggy regardless of the weather. Long lines for cable cars. Gardens were quite beautiful but I didnt quite get the roaming, non asian, roman garbed gladiators. The golden gate is a wonder but forget sbout getting a decent photo. Cable car system can be a bit confusing. The faux french village was an interesting visit but I like kitchy 🤭. Food is severely overpriced. We bought the vietnamese buffet at the beer garden. Chaotic and crap food which I suffered for 2 days. It was like a kill in the Serengeti trying to get to the buffet.We found the amusement centre a bit late and could have sat out the 2 hours of non stop rain there. Old games but fun nonetheless. 5D and 4D were a bit of a joke. We caught a bit of a show and I was horrified at the insensitivity of using non asian performers for a native indian dance number, with stomping and war cries.  A traditional vietnamese performance would have been more appropriate. Sunworld needs to review their program.Would I recommend? A visit to see the bridge is worth it...the rest, could be a miss- unfortunately it comes as a package...and pricey at best.
The bridge itself is beautiful but it's so crowded with people that you can't really enjoy the view.

The Golden Bridge is a perfect example of selective advertising and we, me and my girlfriend, felt that it our experience could have been better if we did our research better (our fault) and had the right expectation. So in Hue, Hoi An, social media and basically everywhere, the only thing that is shown is the Golden Bridge and we, and others we encountered, assumed that we could just drive up to the bridge (maps.me shows a road going straight to the bridge), pay a few 100k and see the bridge. However, when we got to the base of the mountain, we found out that you can only go the bridge if you enter Sunworld, a France-themed fun park and you have to pay $30. Both of these things would have us normally say 'Hell no!', but we were there, we set our minds to it, so we decided to make the best of it. As we got there 11am and it was a public holiday, the park was packed and it took us about 1.5hrs to get from the entrance to the top of the mountain. The park itself is an interesting showcase of Vietnamese amusement and what they consider 'fun & amusement' (it struck us that all the entertainers were Western...). As we were there anyway, we decided to go into the Alpine ride, which was not worth the long wait. The bridge itself was ok, but packed, and the views were pretty cool. But the best part was the gondola ride, which takes you over a dense and diverse jungle and on our way up, follows a river with several waterfalls. Summarizing, the bridge was ok, but I don't feel it is worth the money and effort. It felt like a 'been there, done that' experience. We feel that we could have had a better experience if we didn't go on a public holiday (which wasn't possible as my girlfriend only gets off on public holidays) and had our mind set on going to a fun park instead of just the bridge.
It was crowded with many visitors, and yet cloudy weather at 10am. Better be there around 8am morning

My partner and I decided to pay a little extra and get a private tour guide to help us with the BaNa Hills experience. The bridge itself is of course smaller than the pictures depict, and it is quite busy (although it was fairly quiet for us as we decided to go earlier than most tourists). I would recommend you make this a part of the whole experience of BaNa Hills, as the views are breathtaking. I would also recommend arriving prior to 8am before the influx of tourists. Overall, the bridge itself, while stunning was not the best part of the experience

This is part of the theme park Sun World. You need to pay a very expensive entrance fee to visit the bridge even when you are not interested in other attractions. The fee is 700,000 VND!! The park is still under heavy construction, and there are limited facilities. The bridge looks impression on Instagram and social media. However, you will soon realise the site alone is not worth the entrance fee.However, they have this great cable car system that most tourists will enjoy.To me, the price is too steep as it is not a fully operation theme park.

The title says it all really. It's a 150m long bridge that is a semicircle that doesn't really go anywhere. The pics were fun to take - there were a lot of tourists but if you're patient there are gaps, We went after 4pm and they have a reduced ticket which include a buffet (I think it was about $24 each). The cable car is amazing - 5,800 metres long and 1,400 metre high.) The cable car was a highlight of my holiday especially because my girlfriend and I went later in the day and got the entire cable car to ourselves. The views were amazingThe theme park is poor. The buffet was poor.

The Ba Na hills are beautiful, and the cable car ride up to the top boasts stunning views of the forest and large stones below. I was not very interested in the theme park or the french village, as it was all man-made and not truly historical. In addition, the park and bridge were very crowded with tourists, sometimes even difficult to walk through. The bridge was also quite crowded and not as breathtaking as it looks in the pictures. Despite all this, I think it is still worth seeing if you are in DaNang/Hoi An.

The Golden Bridge as an art installation is fairly  epic. From afar, hands magically jut out of the side of a mountain  cradelling a walkway or bridge. It's only when you get close that you see the bridge is and hands are created  from fibreglass and flimsy metallic  paint. I went early around  9 am on a Saturday and the bridge  was already jam packed  with  tourists jostling to get selfies and photos.  The bridge is part of a larger Dysney =esk them park Ba N.a. Hills. You pay around  $30 USD to be corralled into a gondala line with hundreds of other people, mostly in tourist  groups. The Gondala  is really  the star attraction as it's 5km long and is the longest in the world  with  a Guinness World Record certificate on display. The rest of the Ba N.a. Hills  complex is also a  fibreglass worderland with recreated French village and Chinese  temples then with music and staged dance shows at it's centrepiece. Again there are many  people jostling for viewing positions, lines for food and bathrooms. I would  have been happy to see the Golden Bridge on it's own without the theme park entertainment. However, many people obviously enjoy this type of entertainment judging by the crowds. The bridge is a very smart and sleek design and very  clever as an art extension, Ba N.a. Hills on the other hand mat not be for everyone.
It was very busy and cloudy at the same time. Not as picturesque as i expected since it was a bit rainy but definitely loved the experience and the chance of making it there. It was a dream come true

We came because we wanted to see the golden bridge but it's in a sun world amusement park where the entrance fee is extortionate. You move around the park on cable cars which is pretty cool but really I have no idea what you are meant to see here it's all fake buildings and temples, food is pricey and it is obviously full of people. We came on a clear day so the views were spectacular and a litre of the "German yellow beer" is relatively cheap. It's ok but don't go out of your way to come here.

What a shame I couldn't get to this bridge instead of whole amusement park. I wanted to take a picture at the bridge and I didn't know it's so deadly touristic. But anyway, it will take you at least half a day to get there and spend there some time as you need to get a tour to get there and you go through the whole complex, not to the bridge only.

From what I am told, the views from this bridge are amazing, but only when there is no cloud. When I visited, it was a beautiful sùnny day in Da Nang but the resort was still in the clouds. This bridge certainly is unique but it essentialy goes nowhere and at $32 USD to get in, I can't see the value if this is all you have come to see. The world's longest cable car to get there is also fun, but again, if it is cloudy, you can't see a thing. Visit the Ba Na Hills on a sunny day.
Beautiful but was just crazy with the tourist crowds. The bridge was short as well! Best time to visit is be there early like 8am

The day we went it was sunny at the bottom but by the time we got to the top it was extremely foggy. Our guide said that was often a problem. So the views were not great. The tram ride is fun and you take different tram's depending on what area you want to see. Some times the trams will stop working no reason was giving.The bridge is crowed we could see nothing but fog and the hands holding up the bridge. The gardens were pretty but that been said it is not comparable to Buchart Gardens or even most of the US them parks. I was someone disappointed but many in our group thought it was worth seeing. The drive up was the best part for me. Could have been different perhaps if the weather was better.

It’s important to know that the golden bridge is located in the ba na hills and is accessible through sun world. It’s pretty expensive for what you get. The bridge is gorgeous, but is often obscured by misty weather. If it is raining don’t bother going. You won’t be able to see the beautiful views, which is the point of going into the hills. Cut your losses and go to marble mountain or Hoi An instead.

We went here for the golden bridge. But due to the fog and rainy weather we spend more time at the arcade hall than the bridge itself. The “French” theme is a bit off, but that makes it funny I guess. They even tried to build the Notre Dame church. I think it’s money well spend even though we didn’t get the chance to see the golden bridge in its full glory (because of the fog/rain). It’s a laugh for a foreigner; all those funny people doing funny things at this strange themed “funpark”.Tip: Bring your own snacks / drinks because it’s way overpriced!Tip 2: Obviously, try to go when it’s not rainy weather 😅

We had always planned to visit the Golden Bridge whilst in the area of Da Nang, but were very surprised to hear that it is set in a theme park. Yes the cable car ride and the views of & from the bridge are certainly worth the d700,000 or $30 but make sure you check the weather forecast for the top of the Ba Na Hills before you pay the entrance fee or you could be highly disappointed like we were on our visit. Visibility was very poor and a number of things to see were closed due to the rain,also the place was saturated with self obsessed selfie freaks and a lot of your time is taken up fighting through the undergrowth of addicted mobile phone users. Along with that, don’t expect much from a tacky theme park, if you been to either Disney resorts thrres just no comparison.Given a pleasant days weather and your in for a breath taking experience.

Get informed before you decide to go there : this is a fantasy parc. The village was founded by the french colonists but rebuild quite recently into what it is now. A masterpiece of engineering:  long cable cars at high altitudine.  The village resembles a cute French village(all buildings are brand new). Ample places to eat good and a huge amuzement park.  Check the weather before you go there. We found the densest fog that all of us ever experienced (less than 5 m)

We debated going to this bridge for a few days because of the high cost (700000 Dong) and eventually decided we couldn’t leave Da Nang without seeing it. I would love to say it was worth the splurge of cash (which is a big splurge if you’re backpacking like we are) but it isn’t. You are essentially paying to get into an amusement arcade with the added bonus of a bridge. After reading reviews- we opted for an early morning drive which takes a little over an hour from Da Nang city (We rented a scooter and made our own way there via google maps which is a pretty easy drive). We were under the impression we would be one of the first in the bridge when the cable cars opened at 8- we were wrong. The cable cars actually start running at 7.15 so the bridge was pretty busy when we arrived- there go the amazing photo opportunities. The bridge itself is impressive- although the view wasn’t amazing- it was super cloudy for most of the day we were there. There were also several couples having wedding photos on the bridge which meant waiting and getting caught in a backlog of tourists who all wanted the perfect shot. The arcade itself is quite fun- there are three levels all with games you can play for free- to be honest- this was probably the highlight of our trip! The French village was lost on us- if you’re from Europe I guess you won’t be impressed by it- typical European architecture spattered with overpriced restaurants and food stalls. Word of warning- the food here is expensive- we would take our own food and drinks if we were to go again. The weather was also bad which dampened our spirits- it rained nearly the entire time we were there even though it was sunny in Da Nang City. The cable car ride up and down was good- the views from the car were Spectacular. Unfortunately, all in all, it wasn’t worth the money, particularly if you’re wondering if it’s worth the big splurge.

Travelling on my own around vietnam.  Day trip to golden bridge on weekday with tour group. Early start at 8 from hoi an dropped back at hotel 5.30 in time for dinner. As long as you know it’s going to be a day that’s a bit of fun then you will be okay.  Another side of Vietnam.  Take sensible shoes for walking around, water, hat and sunblock and a book for down time. Saw a lot of tired limping tourists by the end of the day.   Some with shoes off.  Tour guide was just georgeous Hoi from turtle tours. Funny bright and very patient.  Gave us a lovely insight into her life and living in Vietnam.  Priceless!
