This is like a Vietnamese version of Disney.  I’ve visited the one in America and Japan and by  contrast this one is mainly about the food as mentioned from another TripAdvisor posting. Rides and attraction a bit like a small time fun fair geared to small kids.  As a big kid I did the 3D movie. 10 minute wait -  very hot downstairs where entertainment is.  Movie okay.  They don’t give you fresh clean glasses just use last persons taken from a basket.  Gave mine a quick wipe noticed guy next to me followed suit. Inclusive with tour I had the Buffett lunch on the second level which was a standout.  Highlight of day actually - fresh and huge variety.  My group arrived for lunch second level at 11.30 so had first dibs before hoards of tourists arrived. Things got a little mucky after that. What is startling is that there is at least 3 to 4 levels of identical buffet restaurants running simultaneously to manage demand.  Gives you an idea of numbers passing through. Very efficient and clean. Before lunch we stopped at Lower level where the bridge is.  Ride on cable car from car park took 20 minutes. Best part of day. Okay, this is where it gets crazy.  Tourists everywhere taking selfies.  As was I. I knew this beforehand having read the reviews so no surprise.  Bridge is not long which is good in a way as you would have to move through a sea of more people to get to the other side.  Gridlock in the middle no one moving.  So you have to show some gumption and just walk right in front of those taking pictures. And keep walking.  You have to cross back to get back to second cable car to the theme park level. Worth it?  Yes, in a way.  I found a tranquil garden just to the side on the first level with seats.  Was very cool and away from the hoards.  I sat and looked out at the view.  Music blaring from speakers but all part of the fun. Amazed no one in the garden. All taking selfies on the bridge. A few people wandered in the garden while I was there and had a packed lunch.  I didn’t bother wandering around the upper level theme park after lunch as too hot and not much to see. After I saw the 3D movie I grabbed an ice cream found a shady chair and watched the world promenade by for a couple of hours until I had to meet up with the group.

The bridge itself is very nice and worth a visit. It is a little smaller than some photos might indicate but the views from it, and the cable car ride up, are lovely. The bridge is part of Sunworld Amusement park - which costs VND700,000 per person. So the question is do you want to pay that price for the bridge and cable cars alone, or do you want to spend the rest of the day in the Amusement park.If you decide to go to the park, you should know:a) it is under construction - expect to see lots of cranesb) there are some lovely gardens and a massive Bhudda statue near the bridge, about 3/4 of the way up the mountain (same stop as the bridge)c) if you go all the way up to the top of the mountain you end up in an east Asian variant of Wonderland - a 'replica' French City in the same way that Disneyland is a replica 'town'. It feels like it is aimed at Korean and Chinese tourists. However while Disney parks have lots of rides, this park has lots of hotels / restaurants / shops but only a few rides. We queued for 1.5 hours to ride the toboggan but there was little else of interest for adults other than perhaps the restaurants.Most of the rest of the entertainments are aimed at younger kids (e.g. climbing wall, dodgem cars, lots of arcade games, a dance act on a small stage) and a wax works museum which costs VND100,000 per person extra.d) queues for cable cars were very short for us, and ticket purchasing at the centre was easy. e) toilets, eating areas etc are all clean and well managed.The park took about 1 hour to reach from central Da Nang and we paid the taxi VND640,000 for return including waiting time.Summary:Bridge and cable cars are great, gardens and massive Bhudda are nice, the rest of the park is geared towards younger kids who like electronic and indoor entertainment. Unless you are very early or very late, expect queues for the rides.

this bridge was the main reason that we went to the ba na Hills amusement park, which this bridge is a part of and you must purchase an entrance ticket for. we were even there during the low season it was still pretty busy, so you won't get those early Instagram famous type photos there. The View was beautiful though and we spent probably a good 10 minutes just enjoying that.

I was happy to visit golden bridge as this was my wish to see when visiting Vietnam. But be prepared that there is many people on the bridge and is not like you see on those instagram or facebook pictures. The view is nice but if you really dont wish to visit this bridge then is no need to visit this place as there are many other nice views in Da Nang.The entrance is quite pricey (700.000VND or 30$). Have a jacket or something to cover as it can be a bit cold if it gets foggy.On the hills is totaly European vibe from buildings to singers, perfomers, etc.It can be fun for children as there is also a fantasy park with games and adventure rides on Ba Na Hills.Go early in the morning.

I heard and read a lot of positive about the Golden bridge or “Hands of God”. I was very disappointed when visiting the bridge. The bridge was short and built/screwed down on the mountain. The way bridge/design was not very impressed. More for picture taken rather anything else. The Grand Canyon Skywalk is much more impressive (I thought Golden Bridge was similar as The Grand Canyon bridge). Not worth the money for sure (VND 800k without the buffet/food).

Saw a lot of hype on internet about the bridge. But experience was not up to the hype. A very short bridge (may be less than 100m) with wood on the floor, so you cannot see down. Unlike other bridges, this one is not that spectacular. The only saving grace are the hands. But the hands have nothing to do with bridge structure. Visit only if you have to.

Golden Bridge is located in Sun World, a jumble of a place with confusing, unwelcoming staff. We entered, approaching ticket counter to be told by the attendant to go to the next window, as she smirked at her co-attendant she had sent us too. Seemed an inside joke. This attendant was friendly enough but gave us terrible directions to get to the bridge. No map offered.  Inside the park, we struggled to find our way with no employees offering help. The tram to bridge was very nice with A/C;  the bridge fine, an amazing view but packed with tourists. As an aside, they will store your luggage at no cost, helpful if you, for example, are coming from airport and transferring to Hoi An afterward. Also after 4pm, entry is reduced from 700K VND to 550VND. Entry does include a buffet, of which we did not partake.
가는날이 장날이라 흐린날씨에 비가와 잘 보지 못했는데 잠깐 개인 날씨에 전체를 볼수 있었다.과연 바나힐 테마파크 랜드마크라 할수있다.

We stayed at the Mercure in the French Village so got to visit at different times.Early morning misty magic one time and blue skies and sunshine later.(Avoid at all costs if the vanity of the selfie-obsessed instagram glitterati taking gazillions of pics of themselves is NOT your thing.)We enjoyed the quick wander on the bridge and also the garden and surrounds and cable car rides.Much more to do than just see the bridge.Great views and photo opportunities otherwise.
Absolutely stunning views from the bridge looking over the Ba Na hills and below Sun World. It's a fantastic photo opportunity as long as you are patient and alert to any gaps.
Em novembro chove muito , a  visibilidade e muito pouca.Faz muito frio.Nosso guia falou que a melh6 época  e janeiro e fevereiro

I arrived expecting a truly massive structure, so I was slightly surprised that the bridge wasn't as big as I thought it would be. However, any minor disappointment about the size was quickly overshadowed by the undeniably beautiful view it offered.

It is shown on all the pictures you see of Vietnam so who does not want to go and see the Golden Bridge. It is a big must when you are in the area. Bridge is very crowded as expected, but at least you can say you were the if you see the photo in a few years. Bridge is only but a small part of the bigger Bana Hills experience.
Uma hora de trajeto  com estrada ótima. Complexo com toda estrutura turística  muita organização. Capacidade pra receber centenas de turista com todo conforto
골든 브리지는 따뜻한 날에도 멋진 경치와 시원한 산바람을 제공합니다. 이곳의 인기를 고려할 때, 여행 시간이 길어질 수 있으므로 경험을 완전히 즐기기 위해 하룻밤 숙박을 강력히 추천합니다.

Picked up from our hotel on time and a steady ride in a taxi to the cable car station.Weather was nice at the top and got a fair few photos on the bridge, there were a few other things to see but our guide only gave us 20 mins to have a look around so it was rather a rush to be honest and he'd got the cable car tickets.When we got dropped off at our hotel the guide gave the impression he wanted a tip but at £130 for the trip we'd spent enough!

The Golden Bridge in Vietnam is truly an impressive monument — the design is stunning and walking across it feels like something out of a fantasy movie. The cable car ride up was also a lot of fun and really added to the overall experience, giving the whole visit a unique atmosphere.Unfortunately, the weather wasn't on our side — it was very foggy, so the beautiful views were mostly hidden. Also, it was extremely crowded, which made it nearly impossible to get a good photo without dozens of people in the background. Still, it’s definitely worth seeing if you’re in the area!

Ich habe von Hoi An eine Easy rider tour (easyridersvietnam.org) für 59€ gebucht. Hoi An - Hue mit Stopps: Golden Brigde, Coastline of Danang, Lang Co Beach, Lunch, fishing village of Cau Hai. Mein Fahrer war sehr zuverlässig und ein sehr guter Fahrer.  Der Eintritt für die golden bridge kostet 978.000 Dong. Mit einer Seilbahn ging es nach oben. Oben angekommen landet man in eine Art kitschigen Freizeitpark, was ich vorher schon wusste. Regenbedingt bin ich direkt zur Brücke weitergelaufen. Ich habe nur an einem Schaufenster Halt gemacht, wo man sehen konnte wie Nudeln frisch zubereitet werden. Die Brücke war extrem überfüllt, aber das ein oder andere Foto konnte ich machen. Es gibt verschiedene Weg entlang der Brücke -  natürlich kann man auch auf der Brücke entlang gehen. Nach der Besichtigung bin ich direkt wieder runter gefahren. Sowohl im Tal - bei Abfahrt - als uch oben im Freizeitpark sind die Preise für Getränke und Essen relativ hoch. Trotz der vielen Touristen fand ich die Brücke beeindruckend.
Purtroppo  il muro di fitta nebbia ha impedito ogni visione panoramica, peccato perchè desideravo molto questa visione panoramica.
ダナン旅行の一番の目的の場所 前日から朝方迄 小雨と霧で平日 それでも客は多い 撮影スポットは多いので 並んだり待つ事も無かった 橋からはダナンの街と海が見える 行きはケーブルカーで行き帰りは歩いて降りた 途中にユニークな大仏像と竹で作った家など観る場所は多かった 何も無かった山奥に橋を作って世界中から人が観に訪れる 凄い企画力です。

The Golden is an engineering marvel and gets place to visit. Great to tick off the bucket list and have memorable pictures. The ride to the Golden on the cable car is highly recommended and worth it. The Golden is quite crowded and you have to be patient to be able to get the right picture with so many people walking around and trying to do the same, it’s worth the trip and this is just one of the many things activities and things to do and see and Ba Na Hills complex. Worth it.

This was definitely amazing the pictures we had seen had not done it justice. The gondola ride up the mountain beautiful. Although it was very crowded it did not spoilt the visit. We did get a private car high which was great. It meant we could arrive and leave when we wanted too!

Golden Bridge was a part of a tour that my girlfriend and I did last year.  The tour itself was fantastic and our tour guide was very knowledgeable and friendly.Bana Hills was amazing and vibrant and you definitely for a sense for the locality and peacefulness.  The entry was enormous but the walk through to the cable cars was an amazing experience with the gardens and French influence.The cable car to the top where the Golden Bridge is situated offered such an amazing view of the world below you with dense forestry, a full panoramic view and a never ending view of the sky above.  The Golden Bridge itself I would agree is world heritage but has unfortunately been taken over by tourism and is vastly overcrowded to the point where it's almost shoulder to shoulder.  It's only a short walk but one can definitely immerse themselves in the architecture and history of it.  It's such an amazing experience.

The views were indeed special but we went at the wrong time of year! Very rainy, foggy and cold. Hot or even warm water was hard to find, even in the nicer hotels. We were relieved to return to warm, sunny Thailand.

It is very lovely bridge in the mountings and it is definitely worth the visit. The only downside is that you have to buy the Disneyland type package, which include visiting fake France and fake Buddha temple. These attack huge crowd from South asian counting. We simply skipped them ad focuses on the this exceptional bridge - be ready for it to be crowded, but if you can ignore it, you would have short but excellent tour.
位於巴拿山上的佛手金橋，是到巴拿山必到的景點。橋長百多米，當中有兩隻佛手托着，因而著名。我們在十二月到訪，很大霧，景色不能清楚看到，注意要準備雨傘及雨衣。

This was the very first stop when we took the cable up to Ba Na Hills. As it was foggy with drizzling, it was chilly! Nevertheless, we had a good walk at this Golden Bridge, it was magnificent! But it was foggy and was drizzling, so the view wasn't clear and we didn't manage to take good and clear shots (but the sun was out later that afternoon but offered a better view), but we enjoyed the nice and cold experience. We felt we were at the top of the world and with "bird eyes' view" of the surrounding, it was awesome!The pathway maybe slippery when we were there so be careful and put on good shoes and also there were many people walking around, be take good care of your belongings! At the end of the pathway, you could take photos with national flags there as a momento.It's good to personally experience it than watching it on TV or online.
It's a very beautiful building. I felt like I was flying while on it. The nice weather also increased its impressiveness. However, it may feel different on a cloudy day. Nice, I liked it.

The Golden Bridge is tucked away in Sun World, Ba Na Hills in Da Nang, central Vietnam. The view of the landscape from the bridge is stunning! You are literally in the sky when you are on the bridge.It opens to the public from 8am to 6:30pm, while guests in Sun World get the chance to go early, from 6am or later at 7pm. It could get quite packed during the day. You get there by cable car from Sun World and the ride to the bridge is pleasant. It costs 450,000 VND for guests of Sun World and locals, while it costs 750,000 VND for others. My friends and I stayed in Sun World, so we went during the day and saw how crowded it was, then the next morning when it was lot more quiet. That was nice. Besides the bridge being a wonder, there  isn't more to it, so don't go there expecting it to have some cultural significance. It is definitely worth a visit.
This is part of a theme park at the top of a set of mountains. It isn't an ancient monument, but it is impressive. Crossing the bridge means you are high in the clouds and seeing gorgeous landscapes.

Very nice location for photos but it can get quite crowded in the afternoon. It is also better to visit in the morning as the clouds start to develop in the afternoon and the views become obstructed.The cable car ride is also very pleasant and enjoyable.

Lots of people and we were early at a very un busy time.  Got across the bridge took the pics, and more at the end of it.  It is pretty clever what has been built.  Not cultural in any way just a bit of fun

The Golden Bridge is amazing - truly an engineering marvel.  So far as the rest of the Ba Na Hills complex is concerned - see the bridge then go home.  While the bridge is very crowded, it is still worthwhile.
Walking on the Golden Bridge in the Ba Na Hills was surreal! The giant hands and mountain views were absolutely breathtaking.

Yes, you basically pay 50$ to see this heavily advertised bridge, which in short time became one of Vietnam symbols, despite country's rich history, great cities and fab landscape. Other attractions included in that ticket are mediocre at best - pretty kitschy French village, ordinary cable car and underwhelming rides.And, that Vietnamese symbol is a theme park bridge. My daughter asked me if it has some historic importance, and was surprised when I told her it was built 10y ago.However, as most of you will come up to see it yourself (like myself) I point down some tips to make the most of your day and not leave disappointed.Despite my intro up, I must admit the bridge is impressive, set in a unique place - often above clouds.It is overpriced, but you'll pay that price despite of that and they know it.TIPS:- WEATHER is the most important factor. Watch the weather forecast for the following days and choose the day showing clear weather,- BE there at 7.30 AM to catch the first cable car up. Website is showing they're open from 7 AM, but this is only for Sun World hotel residents. Outside visitors are let at 7.45 AM to go up,- Grab ride from Da Nang costs around 15$ one way. At 6.30-7 AM it needs around 45 min, later significantly longer due to traffic,- Arriving so early is double effective: you'll have the bridge for yourself (and very few others) and, you'll have more options (time) to have better weather. When we came up at 8 AM it was totally clear with amazing visibility and almost no one around (see the pics). At 10 AM it was totally cloudy with hordes of people.- BUY the tickets when you arrive at the theme park entrance (NO need to do it beforehand),- Buy the tickets WITH BUFFET lunch included. For around extra 12$ you get a real feast at 4 Seasons restaurant. Huge choice of quality food: Viet, Korean, Jap, Thai, western cuisine,...juices and water included, great deal. Have your lunch earlier, before or around noon - at peak times 1-2 PM also huge crowds and fight for food,- Do the bridge first, anyway it's right there when you exit the cable car. After, you have all the time to explore the theme park according to your likings,- To avoid even those few who came with you walk first to the other (further) hand, as everybody will naturally stop by the first hand

Die Gondelfahrt über die herrliche Landschaft von Da Nang hoch zu Berg war atemberaubend. Ba Na Hill wirkt sehr surreal und wie in einer anderen Welt.Die Golden Bridge war trotz des Nebels ein Highlight und wir hatten großes Glück vom Timing, bevor der große Ansturm am frühen Nachmittag losging. Gefühlt waren von plötzlich Tausende Menschen oben auf dem Berg unterwegs. Man kann zum Glück zu Fuß in den Parkanlagen ausweichen und spazieren.Die kitschigen Themenparks haben wir nicht besucht, da es nicht unsere Intension war. Für Kids bestimmt ganz nett.Für uns war es eine neue besondere Erfahrung und das Besondere an Ba Na Hill und Golden Bridge ist definitiv die schöne Berglandschaft und atemberaubende Natur von Vietnam!

Die Golden Bridge ist in den letzten Jahren ziemlich bekannt worden und war einer unserer Ziele auf unserer Vietnamrundreise. Die Brücke befindet sich auf den Ba na Hills und ist nur mit einer Gondel zu erreichen. Das Problem ist in der Höhe wo die Brücke ist, herrscht teilweise sehr viel Nebel und wir hatten am Anfang Angst die Brücke nicht wirklich zu sehen. Schlussendlich gab es am Nachmittag ein kurzes Fenster wo das Wetter aufmachte und wir somit die Brücke bei einigermassen gutem Wetter erleben durften. Aufgrund der Tatsache, dass die Brücke nicht einfach zu erreichen ist und es eine ziemliche Völkerwanderung bei der Brücke ist haben das Erlebnis zwar etwas gemindert, trotzdem war es ein Erlebnis

BaNa Hills is such a mystical place and the bridge was my favourite. It’s definitely worth booking a night at the Mercure at the top so you can skip the line and go straight up in the hotel cable car. You also get early morning access to the bridge before the crowds arrive by staying at the hotel.

Посетили место в августе 2023. Купили билет в парк через местного оператора за 800000 донгов. Было включено: трансфер из Дананга и обратно, вход на территорию парка с 15.00 до 21.00, Луна замок, Золотой мост, езда по рельсовой линии и ужин. Главным местом было, конечно же, посещение Золотого моста. Когда видишь это вживую, то понимаешь, всю масштабность постройки. Мост и парк построены в горах. С моста вы можете увидеть окрестности местности. Мы встретили закат и вернулись осматривать дальше парк развлечений.Было много народу, это ожидаемо. В вечернее время фуникулеры от моста к парку и обратно ездят каждый полчаса, возьмите на заметку.Рекомендую одеваться чуть теплее, вечером бывает прохладно.
Interesting infrastructure but super crowded on the bridge. Quite picturesque and cooling at the top. Cable car ride up to this place was impressive - fast and long ride
Golden Bridge is as expected. Glad we saw it as quite unique. Quite crowded and lots of selfies happening, it’s a bit cooler in Ba Na Hills in July, so good way to spend a morning

The Golden Hands Bridge is probably one of the most iconic landmarks in Vietnam!  The bridge is slightly curved and supported gracefully by a pair of separate enormous hands.  Walking the bridge also provides some of the most scenic and panoramic views of Da Nang city and the valley below.  It is an extremely popular tourist attraction and it's usually better to go there early in the morning or after 5 PM to avoid the crowds.  There is no admittance fee to walk this bridge.
Nice but busy place.The air was fresh and the golden bridge was highlight.You can do by yourself without tour.

J'ai trouvé la visite fort intéressante, je sais que ce n'est pas du goût de tout le monde, j'ai lu quelque part que c'était un piège à touristes mais des pièges à touristes comme çà, j'aimerais en voir tous les jours en France.La visite de la cave à vin était très intéressante. J'ai apprécié la reproduction du village français.J'ai assisté à un spectacle de danse en plein air.Le pont tenu par deux mains n'est pas une mauvaise idée mais un peu difficile à prendre en photo en entier.J'ai passé une très bonne après-midi, malheureusement il y avait de la brume, donc je me suis passée du soleil sur les collines de Ba Na.Visite privée avec mon guide personnel.

Yes it is crowded and full of locals more than tourists.  They have a million selfie sticks and push and shove.  But you kind of expect that and just get the photo you want and get out.  A group of Koreans were on a tour and taking up a large area trying to perfect a shot with them all in - so annoying, they ended up getting shoved out of the way as they were holding up large crowds.Couple good options for pics and one if you walk over the bridge, is at the end and literally waited 5 mins for our turn to get a photo, very easy and people nice, helping each other get a group photo and moving on quickly for others.
Nice but too busy.You can do by yourseft without to book any company.And the air here too cold so please wear jacket
Every toutist came for this. up on the mountain with lots of people.. take photo with the God  Hand requires some patience and skill

This was a mixed experience. The cable car ride up into the mountains to see the bridge was spectacular and the hand structures extraordinary. However, the bridge itself is shorter than photos would lead you to believe. Plus, it then leads into a massive faux French theme park that offers strangely little in the way of interesting activities and decent food.

We came in march and the clouds had come in…heavily…it was quieter than usual but much foggier. We got the early entry so got an ok view of the bridge but on the way down you could barely even see it through the clouds.The guide said it had been this way for 25 of the last 30 days.A great attraction but hope for a good day.
Casi lo mejor de Vietnam. Nos gustó mucho tanto el puente como todo el complejo. Muy chulo el teleferico que te sube, el buda gigante, las atracciones,  restaurantes. Todo muy limpio y bonito.

One of the things on our to-do list for our trip to Vietnam was to visit the bridge, and it was certainly worth the trip.Until we actually booked the day, we didn't realise there was a 20 minute cable car ride up a mountain ro reach the bridge so if you don't like cable cars don't go. It is also relatively expensive at around €40 per head.We also didn't realise that the bridge is actually at the entrance to a Disney type theme park. So, if you like theme parks, it is actually good value for money.To be honest, theme parks aren't really our thing, so after visiting the bridge, we only stayed for about two hours - which included a poor quality lunch at their equivalent of a KFC.Overall, we felt the cable car and bridge worth the money, and another item ticked off of our list.

We were there on 12 Dec. The whole week was raining and we are just so blessed that 12/12 , no rain. The weather was fantastic but the fog was very thick. We could appreciate the nice scenery as much. A bit disappointed that the roller coaster rides were closed . Nevertheless, it was a good experience up at Ba Na Hills

Golden Bridge is one of those surreal images that pops up so many time on the internet then it is a shame not to visit it at least once if you are in Da Nang.It wasn’t an overcrowded day, coming after the flooding of Da Nang two days earlier. The cable car ride was fun with great views of the landscape. The bridge itself is pretty amazing and again affords fabulous views. I would imagine it would not be as pleasant on a super crowded day. I really enjoyed our visit and took plenty of photos. I have attached a handful.
Great photo spot in nice weather and lesser crowd. Best visit during meal times when crowds are lesser, else is just a mad crush in the bridge.
It was a little bit cold up the mountain because it was misty but I was happy to see this bridge and also the small little french town a little bit up as the bridge with another cable car.

I hired a private driver thru a local travel agency in Hoi An (for 950,000 vmd) to drive me there and wait for me and my husband while we took the gondola up to the bridge. We arrived around 11am and it wasn't too busy (coronavirus mat have affected this also) but it was filling up quite a bit by 1pm. I hadn't realized there was so much other stuff to see there - thought the bridge was separate from the theme park but it's actually smack in the middle on the gondola up so I really wish I would have given myself more time there. Had told driver we'd be at meeting point in two hours which isn't a lot of time since the ride up is 17 minutes.It was a foggy day but I still found the bridge really cool. Had a slight freakout on the gondola ride up once we entered fog. Found it slightly hard to breathe but got thru it lol.

- Was it worth the hype? YES! Ba Na Hills is a beautifulllllll little village on a mountain above the clouds, and it is soooooo picturesque and breathtaking! The village is home to the famous Golden Bridge which is only a couple months old. There are cute restaurants, an arcade/game building for children, temples, flower garden, and even hotels on-site (which would be very romantic for one-night if you ask me!). - Downfall is that they receive 10,000 to 30,000+ tourists A DAY. If you want a half-decent photo on the bridge, you must literally be the first one to arrive in the morning, otherwise forget it there's wayyyy too many people. Also, they have 2 rollercoasters, which were underwhelming, and felt a little unsafe.- Strongly recommend to book a round-trip shuttle bus from your hotel beforehand because getting a taxi/grab at the end of the day was impossible, and in-turn landed me in what could have been a potentially dangerous situation. - Pay attention to the weather because if its foggy or raining, you won't see anything! I went in January, and the weather was just right! - I don't think tour guides or packages are really necessary. It's nice to have the freedom of roaming around the village at your own speed. I spent about 4-5 hours up there.- I believe the village is linked to the Sun World amusement park in Da Nang's main city area, but don't get confused because it's about a 2-hour drive away, and another 20-30min gondola ride up the mountain to the village.ENJOY! :)

Came here very excited to see this great piece of architecture. The bridge itself is magnificent, unfortunately when we went there it was very cloudy but we still managed to get some great pics. Arrive early as it gets really busy

Allora, veramente bello il ponte, veramente una cineasta il resto. Ci si arriva comodamente con un auto privata da Da Nang ed in un pomeriggio si vede tranquillamente tutto. Spettacolari le funivie che portano in cima alla collina, una di queste è la più lunga al mondo. Arrivati in cima ci si accorge che a parte il ponte il resto è un parco dei divertimenti tamarro come pochi. Ci sono una serie di edifici in vario stile, che non offrono nulla se non riproduzioni di vari edifici in stile Europeo. Poi ci sono delle giostre al coperto, stile lunpark americano. Insomma il Golden Bridge è bello e spettacolare ma il resto anche no.

There are so many people here, it can be quite hard to get a good spot to take photos. However, the view is really lovely and I would encourage people to brave the crowd and take a look since it is part of your ticket price anyways.

The Golden Bridge is located inside Sun World at Ba Na Hills, which we visited from our hotel in Hoi On, about an hour drive away. It was an interesting visit, to say the least. Sun World is actually a giant amusement park, Vietnam’s answer to Disneyland. We went on the last day of the Tet holiday so the place was packed. You arrive at a large complex at the foot of the mountain. From there you take one of three cable car routes about 5km to the top of the mountain where there are several groupings of sites, at one of which is the golden bridge. On the day we went the mountain happened to be in the clouds, so visibility was extremely poor. The remainder of the huge complex, however, has everything from a faux French Village to multiple buddhist temples and pagodas made to look old. How about a wine tasting cave with a giant pig out front? The mix of experiences was jarring and fascinating. Because of the need to get around to the various sites, you need to leave at least 4 to 5 hours, minimum, at the location. And be prepared for crowds!
Çok güzel bir yer. Bir gününüzü ayırabilirsiniz. Önceden Hava durumunu incelemenizde yarar var. Dönerci bile var! Günlük  motosiklet kiraladım onunla gidip geldim.
ฉันได้เดินทางจากสนามบิน และรถรับส่งส่วนตัวเพื่อไปยังเมืองดานัง และ Golden Bridge and Ba Na Hills ซึ่งฉันชอบมากเป็นสถานที่ที่สวยงาม และบรรยากาศดี แต่น่าเสียดาย เวลาที่ฉันไปมีผู้คนจำนวนมาก

Interesting but very crowded. Maybe if you choose to visit it early in the morning will be better. Make sure to choose a sunny day for your visit, because you'll loose half of the beauty of the place in the foggy weather
Good with catastrophic view. Crowded with tourists. Misty in the morning. Suggest to visit in the later part of day.

Had a really enjoyable time visiting here. Didn’t realise how cold and wet it would be. Ended up buying a jacket but definitely worth it for warmth. The Golden Bridge is great to look at but the scenery is just as nice.

The bridge was spectacular! Not too far from Da Nang and made a good afternoon trip. The gondola to the top of this hills has beautiful views. More than just the bridge at the top, which we did not realize before going!

We visited golden bridge both with ba na hills with a tour from Da Nang. Although it was foggy it was an amazing experience. We were lucky on our way down to our bus the foggy had been dissapeared and we took several better photos.The only disadvantage is the large crowds and some annoying and rude tourists that dont respect anything.A must do in Vietnam
Ba Na hills is a must do activity when in Da Nang and you cannot miss the Golden Hand Bridge when there.Amazing.

Well before you go to Golden Bridge (which inside the Ba Na Hills) you should check the weather forecast.Or maybe just visit it where it's summer, and November is definitely not one of them 😁.Well I do get the misty and majestic kind of view, and the temperature is really nice for me, but the sad part is you can only see the hand and the bridge (well yeah, no nature part for this month).Maybe the rate will increase if I get chance to get back here during summer time !!

I really enjoyed the bridge and village the views were amazing and overall it was a great experience visually and physically, downside is the amount of tourists there taking photos but cant complain your one of them.

The bridge and French village are very nice, the cable car journey is very pretty but they allow way too many people entry per day. Apparently we visited on a quiet day and there was 7,000 people, in peak time apparently there can be almost 30,000!!Other than the number of people it would have been amazing!!Come as early as you can because there are less visitors at sunrise!

ダナンに2泊しましたが市内観光は1日で十分。てことでゴールデンブリッジ見たさに滞在先のホテルからバナヒルへ向かいました。（メーター仕様で往復4,000円程でしょうか。所要時間は片道1時間ほどでした）道中に入場券を購入するべく地元のチケット屋に寄ります。（タクシー運転手が寄りました。少し安いのかも）もちろん、現地でも買えますのでご安心ください。到着してみると、そこはテーマパークの様な入り口でアジア系が多数です。特に韓国人は多いですね。日本人は非常に少ないですね。そしてケーブルカーという案内表示を頼りに歩き続けます。途中、エスカレータもあるので足の不自由な方や高齢者の方にも安心です。ロープウェイは3箇所あるようですが、我々は案内表示に従い歩み勧めた先に乗り場が。多少並んでましたが相乗りで回転も速いのですぐに乗れます。（1台8人乗りでしょうか）ゴールデンブリッジも含め、この施設のポイントはロープウェイではないでしょうか。めちゃくちゃ標高も高く、いくつもの山を超えていくので日本のロープウェイの比較にはなりません。めちゃくちゃ怖いです！そして間の悪い事に途中でストップした時はゴンドラ自体が船の如く揺れて生きた心地はしませんでした。止まったのは1分程ですが地獄の様でした。正確には測ってませんが10分以上は乗ってるんだと思います。到着したところは千葉や大阪にある、あれらのテーマパークを模した雰囲気の施設があり。お子様も楽しめるのではないでしょうか。しかし、非常に高い標高ですので上着や羽織るものは必須です。私が行った時は雨季でしたので山頂は雨混じりで非常に寒く、お土産屋さんで変なパーカーを買うか本気で悩みました。（買わなくて正解です）その施設からさらにロープウェイを乗り継いだ先にゴールデンブリッジがあります。橋は晴れていたら綺麗なんだと思います。我々は一寸先も見えない濃霧でしたので何の感慨もありません。そして早々にロープウェイで引き返そうと考えますが、ここが最もカオスな状況です。我先にロープウェイでテーマパークに引き返したい人たちが集まるので大混雑です。そして何処に並ぶとかルールもないので殺気立ちます。ベビーカー や車椅子は厳しいでしょう。そして中国人のツアー団体などは横入りお構いなしです。落ち着いて行動するのがベストです。天候が全てですが、こちらは特に雨の日に行っても何も楽しくないです。しかし、あのロープウェイに乗るだけでも価値はあります。晴れていたらその景色は絶景だと思います。
Let's come early and enjoy all day for all activities, make sure you bring your coat, umbrella. Bring some water and snack, full power of cell phone

Crowded packs with tourists.Disappointed went to Bana Hill Golden Bridge last week the weather was not good it was Raining, foggy thru out the whole day not able to take any nice pictures in this,Golden Bridge and many other places. The bridge is nice however wrong month to visit in October.

I think, currently the most favourite destination of Vietnam. The architecture is perfect, you just think that what kind of a man design a bridge like this at 1500 metre'' but if you find something perfect as this bridge, you have some disadvantages. First one is too crowded, second one is weather, you are at 1500 meters so climate changes rapidly. But if you check weather conditions and stay qt bana hill , you may visit at calmly. But you should go for different experience. Cable cara, atmosphere, views are worth to visit it.

So I wanted to visit the stunning Golden Hands bridge and decided to find my way there from Hoi An on my rental motorbike.First mistake. Google maps took me to the foot of the hills but the route was needed by the enormous Sun World resort. I asked plenty of guards etc if I could ride up the mountain to be told no. The only way is in fact to park your bike in the resort's secure bike parking and purchase a ticket to ascend the mountain by cable car, which I did.The process was reasonably easy but this resort is huge so if you are not part of a tour group, grab a resort map to find your way to the correct cable car station.The weather was reasonable as I parked with cloud apparent further up the mountain. The cable car goes up the mountain over some stunning jungle scenery but, as we reached half way the fog and cloud seen from the bottom. On reaching the top it was a complete white out.I was gutted at first but I still got to see the bridge albeit in a slightly ethereal setting.So the top here is if you want the supposedly incredible views from the top over the entire region, check the weather carefully first.Unfortunately the resort decided to shut the cable car I used just as I wanted to head back so I joined the huge queues to take a vernacular train down to the next cable car station from where I descended. That said, the resort and grounds are stunning and given the number of visitors, reasonably well organised.Worth a visit.

We stayed overnight at the French Village so we could go to the Golden Bridge without the hundreds of day trippers. We got there early and walked the bridge with very few others....partly because it was so foggy you could barely see both of the supporting 'hands' at the same time!! So we had no beautiful views, which was a shame as on a clear day this would be awesome! Instead we had a more surreal and eerie experience with the supporting 'hands' looming out of the fog and the cable cars literally 'popping' out of the fog at the last minute. The gardens around the bridge are lovely and well worth a walk through too......even in the mist! We will try for a clear day next time!
由於出發日是陰天，到巴納山所觀賞的風景都是霧，且微雨！但沒有影響我們心情。不過部分設施如機動遊戲等都暫停服務。而金橋由於天氣關係都不能仔細欣賞。大霧關係在山上好像探險似的。必有一番風味！天氣好的話必定會再上山拍照留念金橋的雙手！

Bought the tour from a shop in Hoi An, half the price offered by our Tour Guide. All amazing except for the weather - cloudy and very wet.  Still had a good look around and a highlight of the trip, but did ponder what may have been.  Excellent buffet lunch as part of our bus package.  Such a lot to see...  Not too many people - because of the weather!!

The views from the bridge are spectacular and as we headed there in late afternoon, crowds were smaller than expected. Yes, it is a selfie paradise but we were able to get a number of photos with few or no other visitors. Views of the bridge from the French area shouldn't be missed.  It is an amazing piece of architecture that shouldnt be missed. Not cheap because you cant just go up to visit the bridge, ticket includes Bana Hills complex which is another whole story!!

Private transfer from Hoi An took 1 hour and early arrival was advised to avoid huge crowds. Lovely long cable car ride to top affords great views of Da Nang and the coast. The Golden Bridge held up by two hands is spectacular. A must do visit if in the area.

Just an interesting artistic piece  at the top. We could spot it coming up on the cable car. We had to take another cable car on west side  of banahills. It takes 5 minutes and because we went in the morning, no crowd. There are a few hands embracing the bridge....just very interesting.Walk about and take your photos with artistic twist. Like hands onnyour head or you are grabbing the fingers, you are taken by giant, lying on the palm or the giant stepped on you....you will be impressed by those photos!

We were collected from our Danang Hotel at 8Am and arrived at the base of Bana Hills about 9am, thankfully we had a brilliant guide from Travel Mate who went to purchase our tickets for the ride to the top of the mountain...,,incredible ride up and the scenery is fantastic.     At the top and heading for Golden Bridge it was walk to wall people and became even more so as the morning progressed....the bridge and scenery is spectacular as are the gardens and layout of the French village.Just be prepared for huge numbers of tourists, none the less we had a great day.

Let's be honest. One of the most main landmarks in Danang is definitely the Golden bridge, and it is definitely worth the hype. The views are great (spectacular to be honest), the bridge itself is extremely beautiful and an architectural marvel, and it is one of the most iconic places on earth. The only problem is, that you are always in between a lot of tourists clicking selfies and you really find it hard to find space to enjoy yourself. I would still recommend visiting Golden Bridge without a doubt.
Places for teenagers check in. Spend 5 minutes here then nothing left. The atmosphere is good. You can see the whole da nang city from here.

It's kinda weird, but I suppose all the online photos gave people the wrong impression that the bridge is really long and the hands are gigantic. Ignore the drone photos....! The real thing is much smaller than I thought, plus you have to work through the tons of tourists trying to get a selfie in there. I couldn't spend any more than 10 minutes on the bridge. A real shame, because it is quite a unique structure, too bad about the tourists.Definitely come during sunset when the crowd has gone, and you'll get more amazing photos with the hills in the background. I saw a few drones too, perhaps you can get better photos with those.

If the highlight of your trip is to see that epic Golden Hands Bridge, you’ll want to read this first! Just wanted to share a tip on the best time of day to visit the bridge, because all the online reviews say 730-9am before the crowd arrives, but getting up there at 7am is insane considering Ba Na Hills is actually an hour drive from Danang and you would still have to take a cable car up. I actually I observed that around 9am is when the crowd is absolutely at its thickest because that’s the time all the tour groups and day trippers arrive, and the Golden Bridge is their first stop, because it’s the mid-point going up Ba Na Hills. One suggestion would be to skip the bridge when you first arrive and head all the way up to the French Village first to enjoy all the rides (Alpine Coasters, Bumper cars, Fantasy Land) and enjoy the top of the hill while everyone is crowded on the mid-point. Then after 3pm when the top of the hill is crowded with the day trippers having moved up for lunch and entertainment, go back down, and you’ll find the bridge so much less crowded without all the tour groups! We observed when we were leaving just after 3pm that the cable cars coming up were all empty, and there was about 80% less crowd, as compared to when we visited at 9am.

We were on tour to Ba Na Hill for whole afternoon. The temperature is much better than in the city, as it is about 1400 meters height. In this area the ground offers a lot of of interesting activities, shows and the staffs walking all around to meet visitors. The golden bridge is great, you can have very good view over the city.But, do bring an umbrella with you, no matter its rain or not, because it rains suddenly.

We took a private tour to the Golden Bridge in the late afternoon. I highly recommend this if like us you wanted to see just the bridge and views as it was much less crowded than going during the day. We were not interested in the fantasy park. It is a great location to see out over Danang and Hoi An and the bridge is stunning.
This is the most attractive place we, a group of four all eager to visit. It’s simple yet interesting. An awesome icon in Ba Na Hill and Da Nang.

We rode out to the Ba Na hills from Hoi An, it took about an hour and the route is straight forward and easy to follow.Once you get to Sun World you have to get a cable car to take you up the mountain. The cost is 750,000, about £28 each, so it is expensive. There is one cable car that takes you straight to the bridge but there are another 2 that come up further over to the right. We went in the central cable car and then got the funicular to take us up to the bridge.The bridge was very busy but you do get great views out over Da Nang in the valley below. In this area are some gardens which are quieter and nice to wander around, such as the Marvellous garden, the Memory garden, the Love garden. They are very pretty with statues, plants etc. There is also a huge white Buddha, from here you can walk down steps and paths back to the funicular and cable car stations.You can get another cable car up to the French Village. This is completely bonkers, a complete ‘French’ town built on top of a mountain. There are numerous restaurants and a German beer garden and German bands playing Beatle songs, lots of German beer too! It is really quite bizarre but impressive at the same time. There are a number of hotels here too.There is a fun park for kids so lots of activities for them to do. We were going to try out the rollercoaster but couldn’t actually find the start! However when we were going back in the gondola it looked like it was just for children and we were probably too big!We found the whole complex very busy, a bit like Disney World on steroids. I loved the bridge and the big Buddha and enjoyed wandering in the pretty gardens. The French village was just mad, and expensive with beer 200,000 (about £8 a pint!) An interesting place to visit but once was enough!

We really wanted to see this attraction and it was well worth the visit. There is a lot to see so be prepared for a lot of walking. The bridge was very crowded, as were most of the attractions. This was fine but you would need to be prepared for this when going there. Overall I would say it is worth the money and our guide was fantastic. You can do as much or as little as you want. We had an awesome meal at the buffet restaurant at the top. It's definitely a must-see if you are in the area. See the bridge towards the end of the day and there will be far less people on it.

This is my second trip to Golden Hands Bridge. We went up on the Cable car. SOOOOO High... Fabulous views right out to the ocean. Lots to see and do in BaNa Hills. Lots of entertainment. Tons of food outlets and shops for souvenirs. Will definitely go again to see all the new buildings still being completed.
It was an amazing time. They have a lot to do there and the view is beautiful. Make sure to go early or else it’s way too crowded.

We travelled here on a private tour from Da Nang. It's an easy trip in about 1 hour and when you arrive, cars/coaches etc are segregated to separate areas for parking and then shuttles take you to the entrance area. There are multiple cable cars to take you to the top of the mountain, each having it's own queuing system, it was busy and reminded me of being at a Disney Park or similar.When you get to the top after about 15' on the cable car, there's plenty to see including the Golden Bridge, plus gardens, a giant marble Buddha, Buddhist Temple and a recreated French town including a chateau. I think the idea here is to try and create a small theme park type complex and there's a Mercure Hotel at the top as well. Personally speaking, the bridge together with the views and the giant Buddha make this reason enough to visit, and in June the drop in temperature from the coast is also very appealing.

I was almost put off of going because on the reviews saying how busy it was. So don't let the negativity change your mind!!I didn't book a tour, instead I took a motorcycle and drove up from Da Nang, which only took 50 minutes. The ride was easy following Google maps and was less than ½ the time that was shown 1 hour 51 minutes. On arrival I packed in the under cover shed and walked across to by a ticket, which includes the cable car got 700,000. I actually had the whole gondola to myself... Usually seats 12. The arrival at the Golden Bridge was spectacular as was the walk. I shall leave the pictures to do the talking!

The bridge is part of Ba Na hills and is connected with different cable cars. At first we thought it was rediciliously expensive but that was until we understood that we had access to see the bridge and use the theme park and cable cars throughout the day. The cable car going to the bridge was most breathtaking and I loved the 20 minute ride. It was a very hot day so I also loved the smooth breze we got on top. The views are beautiful, green and breathtaking. The bridge is amazing. One of the best sights I’ve seen. However, the theme park is nothing special. A few rides, lots of expensive restaurant and some mediocre entertainment. There are plans to build this area out. An area with hugh potential!

The breathtaking Golden Bridge in the Ba Na hills is not only very aesthetically pleasing, but also offers an incredible view of Da Nang. This Bridge is located within a theme park called "Sun World". The park is made of two tiers, the first is made up of instagram worthy art installations and flowers gardens, this tiers includes the Golden Bridge. The upper tier is quite disneyland-esque though without as many rides and with far less to do. It's built as a pseudo-medieval French castle or town? the Park is currently undergoing some serious construction as two other castles/towns are being built with different themes. It would be incredible to go back to after a few years as I understand that the theme park is being built around the basis of a kingdom built of multiple lands(The land of ice, magic, flowers, joy etc.). Once these other themed land have become a reality I believe the over all park would become a more enjoyable attraction.To recap the park itself is pretty mediocre, whereas the first tier including the golden bridge is ABSOLUTELY STUNNING, and well worth the trip.*****DO NOT GO IF YOU CANNOT HANDLE CABLE CARS OR CROWDS*****

We took a taxi from our hotel in Da Nang at 1 pm. Fixed price and they helped buy the tickets. Arriving around 2 pm we saw what we had hoped for: more people leaving than coming. Was picked up again at 7 pm. Less crowded and good photo opportunities on the bridge. Short lines for amusement park activities Downside: they start closing restaurants and activities mainly from 5 pm. Should we go again we would definitely go mid day and not morning as everybody else do
Golden Bridge is good but it can get a little crowded up there. It is reached by cable car & some fantastic sights can be seen from there also

We left Hoi An with Venus Travel around 0815 and arrived at Ba Na Hills about an hour later. It was super crowded. We were packed like sardines no matter which part of Ba Na Hills we went. We rode one cable car and an incline to get to the Golden Bridge. But it was like seeing Palace of Versailles all over again. No elbow room for pictures.

Ticket cost 750,000 vnd ( 30 USD), From Bus stop you have to take a 5 minute shuttle bus to the Cable car Lift, To get to Cau Vang You should take Hoi An station, and about 15 minutes, the beautiful and  scenic ride, you will get to the Golden Bridge.It was super crowded!
endroit totalement dépaysant,dans la fraicheur,jardins à la française,boudha blanc de 27m absolument magnifique;pont suspendu et tenu par ces deux mains de géant,bluffant!
Good to see, but spoiled by the poor manners of many tourists, some of whom have no concept of patience, and can be dangerous with their selfie-sticks!

This Golden Bridge's popularity simply exploded after a drone-taken picture of it was featured on TIMES travel and social media did the rest of the propagation.While it's true that the bridge itself is pretty cool, hanging out of the mountain top like that with gorgeous views, it can be quite a turn-off when you see the crowds jesting around to get a good photo. What's worse, is if you get pushed around constantly by others. It can be pretty stressful for parents who brought young children. However, if you are from around Asia, and witnessing numerous Chinese/Korean/Vietnamese tourists crowds is kind of a common sight to you and you're calm and well travelled, perhaps you'll be less intimidated, since you should probably be pretty used to it by now.Having said the above, you just need to know --- the bridge is pretty cool, the crowd is kind of a turn-off, but as long as you came with the right mindset, Bana Hills has many other things that will still lift your moods. For one - an all-inclusive price of less than 50usd per person (can't remember the exact VND tiered prices) that lets you enjoy all the attractions, shows, gorgeous gardens, funicular ride,  theme park, playgrounds, self-controlled mountain roller coaster, an entire medieval-looking village to explore and also the breathtakingly beautiful cable car ride.... I do still think it will make a good holiday destination BUT try your best to avoid the peak seasons (e.g. especially public holidays of Vietnam and China, international school holidays, etc).

This unique design, 150-meter curved walkway-bridge, connects the cable-car station to the Thien Thai Garden of Sun World Ba Na Hills. This architectural wonder is a great place for taking scenic photos of the mountain, and the distant lands and sea. It is the two large concrete hands which seemingly hold the pedestrian bridge over the trees makes it extraordinarily exciting.

We had been looking forward to our visit to the Golden Bridge as part of our 20 day Vietnam/Cambodia tour and the day trip was almost perfect but...the queue to get into the cable car for the ride up to the bridge was long and tedious..the ride up in cable car was however fantastic...the bridge itself was spectacular but the crowds were over the top which spoilt the visit somewhat...and signs should go up advising people to leave selfie sticks and umbrellas at home before someone loses and eye on the bridge..ride down the mountain was excellent...done once but not again we think...

We were traveling to a few cities in Vietnam (booked months earlier), and my husband happened to see a mention of the Golden Bridge just 2 days before we were due to drive from Hoi An to Hue.  We were allowed to add this detour to our pre-booked journey, but knew that we would only have time to see the bridge, itself, and not any of the other areas of the park.We were not pleased that we were required to pay the fee for the entire park, knowing that we would visit only one part, but the biggest shock was that we had to pay the sizable price IN CASH ONLY - no credit cards!  How can an attraction that has clearly been constructed to host huge numbers of international tourists not take credit cards?!However, thankfully we had enough cash with us, we DID enjoy our time there.We were worried that our experience would be dampened by the fact that it was a cloudy day, but surprisingly, I think it was actually more interesting that way!We were the only ones in our tram car, so it was very mysterious/spooky/fun on the journey up (and back) when it appeared that we were alone in the fog, with the other trams appearing/disappearing into the mist on the other track.And even though we didn't have a view of the mountains / valleys once we were on the bridge, itself, the fog added to the "otherworldly" experience of being held up by giant hands, and added to the "magic" of the surroundings.Yes, it's crowded, and you will never be able to get a photo of the bridge without many other people on it.  But on a positive note:  it is necessary for people to be in photos to fully appreciate the size of hands holding the bridge.Another benefit of visiting on a cloudy day is that the temperature was wonderfully cool at the top of the mountain.  (Those who are easily chilled should be sure to bring a jacket.)I would say that this is well worth seeing, despite the price, and even if the weather is cloudy.
Unfortunately the weather was not great so we did not get the stunning views and it was pretty wet but we had a fun day and can it was worth the trip.
This bridge was very nice & unique architecture & a very good place for check-in in Ba Na hills but it's too Chinese tourists!

Absolutely gorgeous bridge atop the cable car ride...amazing background with clouds and mountains... very picturesque...also included in the canoe ride ticket is the French Village- amazing full shoe French town replica -and amusement park
The idea was good. The bridge is a very interesting attraction. However, felt like the whole world was there.

We travelled to Ba Na Hills as two families inclusive of three children in a taxi from Da Nang which took roughly 30 minutes to get to. In order to go up to go to the Golden Bridge on needs to purchase a ticket to enter the Sun World which will allow access to the cable car networks that will take you up to the Golden Bridge. One can only access the bridge in one way and one must try to get as many pictures as possible as it is popular and can be very busy. You also need to be prepared to walk so wear comfortable shoes. The are very many angles to see the Golden Bridge and it is an amazing sight that is an experience.

The bridge is set in a beautiful spot the cable car that takes you all the way up allows you to start having a clue on the beautiful views that one can enjoy from the bridge. The bridge itself is very nice but too crowded when we got there. worths the visit.

Take cable car to top.  Again, must walk quite to see the bridge and other places of interests there.  A lot of statues.  Cable car ride excellent view.  Love it here.  Best in Danang.  Don’t miss it.
Golden Bridge is one of man bridges in the area! This one is one of the Best looking one!! Enjoy it!!

I went to Sun world specifically to go to this bridge. This bridge itself is awesome with really nice views. The two big setbacks are 1) its very crowded (but what major attraction isnt) and 2) the cost of $32. Now Ill admit that I didnt explore the rest of the theme park, which many people feel definitely adds a lot of value. For me, the experience was worth it to see the bridge but understand that it may be cost prohibitive for others.

A small group tour via our hotel in Hoi An. Prompt pick up and ticket purchases etc however our tour guide was unhelpful and just escorted us there with not much information imparted except when we probed for it.The Golden Bridge itself was packed with tourists and I was surprised at the short length of it (somehow assumed it would be longer?) The French quarter was quaint and well designed. The beautiful gardens and variety of colours was truly breath taking and more time was required to immerse oneself in its beauty.  The included meal at Beer Garden was amazing (huge variety of everything for that volume of people was astounding!)

These are basically the times you should come for great photos. They open at 0700 and you have to buy the expensive admission ticket VND700,000 which covers the cable car ride (longest in world?) and admission to Sunworld which is "Meh".Anyway, we left Danang 0730 and by 0830 the bus loads of tourists have arrived as well (yes, we are one of them ... albeit by private car). As a photographer there are some interesting angles and views but in the searing heat and million other tourists often wielding umbrellas, it becomes difficult. I would suggest a 20mm lens and don't forget your flash. Bright sunlight (assuming you arrive mid morning to mid afternoon) will create harsh shadows. Don't bother with a tripod, others will trip on it and you won't get a proper setup.It's one of these sites that will become a tick box for most people. You will get some nice photos and garner many "likes".They should consider a separate fee for this and set apart from the other 'attractions' up on Ba Na Hills. It's not cheap and some may find that it's not worth the effort.

1. Do not underestimate the crowd although on weekdays.2. Wear a hat, bring water bottle.3. Wear nicely because the scenery so great and your picture will be nice.4. Be brave and try all the games.5. Please bring your brave heart, you will ride 15~20 min cable car and the cable car is nicely smooth.6. Please come early.7. The storage room is free.

It's a most wanted bridge to see in this Ba Na Hills by now. You may find it challenging to shoot any photo without another person jamming in to be your guest. The trick is to shoot quick flashes, especially those selfies.
It’s a great attraction to see it’s in Bana hills It’s a bit smaller than I thought it would be but the views from there are great and it’s a must see when visiting da nang

We stopped at Ba Na Hills on the way from Hue to Hoi An.  The car park is huge, to accommodate the many tour buses. Our driver walked us to the entrance and we paid 700,000 dong each – the most expensive entry fee we paid to visit a site during our trip to Vietnam.  On the left of where you pay the entry fee there are some camera shots of the bridge. This gives you a good idea how busy it is and also how clear the view is from the bridge.  My advice is not to go on a wet, rainy day as you will be shrouded in cloud and not see anything, something you need to consider in view of the cost.The area at ground level is quite extensive and we dutifully followed the arrows to the cable car entrance. After about 10 minutes walk through gardens, the signs brought us back to where we started – very annoying.  We then decided to follow a guide and tour group, going past a No Entry sign but eventually leading us up to the cable car entrance.  There are lots of cars and they move quite quickly so we didn’t have to wait long. Although the walls of the car are glass, the floor was solid, which I was glad of with my fear of heights.At the top, we walked through some shops and then onto the bridge where we were hit with a log jam of tourists taking photos and selfies.  Once we managed to push through them, the way did clear a bit, although it was still hard to take photos between other people.  Amid the chaos of tourists, there were loudspeakers along the bridge blasting out music (Celine Dion singing in French!) which for me spoilt the effect. The bridge was definitely worth seeing, however, although it’s not very long.At the other end of the bridge there is another cable car station so we walked back along the bridge to return from the same station we arrived, so as not to get lost.Our driver was waiting for us and we asked him if he had been onto the bridge.  He said that Vietnamese people cannot afford it.  How sad!
I do not know how long it took to build this amazing bridge but it is well worth a visit. Two huge hands holding a bridge for people to walk over, amazing.

Located at Bana Hiils park (brilliant site) you will find this wonderful man made attractions, be prepared as nothing here is what it seems, more of a man made movie prop.  A great day out for families and you must leave yourself plenty of time to see the entire heme park.  Loved it.
Outstanding view from here lucky that we stay overnight here otherwise you can’t get single shot like this because during daytime many outside guest visit a bit noisy and crowded
Something you just have to doBusy - but still able to take good photosThe view is amazing and there just isn't anything quite like it anywhere else
One of the famous place to visit in Ba Na hills and it was just too crowded with many tourists, took some nice photo with the golden bridge.

The bridge itself is beautiful and worth a visit, although arriving too late can result in a very crowded experience. It's within a theme park and I found admission to be very expensive compared to other sights in Vietnam. The cable car up to the hills is very beautiful and offers great scenery. The staff within the park are very friendly and the facilities looked new and clean. I didn't stay very long as I wanted to see other sights around the area. I would recommend staying at the park for a bit if you have time as you're paying full admission when paying to see the Golden Bridge anyway.
The bridge is curved, imagine the architecture and workers hard work to construct it. I took many photos there thought it was quite crowded.
loved this bridge. very busy trying to take pictures. as everyone wants to have their photos taken here. we even went back early in the morning. to take some more pics. wonderful place.
Take the. Chairlift to the top of the hill and walk on and round the Golden Bridge. What an engineering feat, and what views.  Excellent, get there early as can be crowded. Worth the effort.

Our hotel booked a return taxi for us on the basis that the total trip took 4 hours. Given the taxi trip is 45mins that gave us two and a half hours at the site. This cost 420k. The taxi stopped on the way and we purchased our tickets, 700k each, at a small shop. There were many shops selling tickets, I presume this is to avoid lining up at the site. We arrived there at 8.30 and we were ushered to the lift from Hoi An station to  Marseille station where the Golden Bridge is. All 3 chairlifts can be used, but may not all be operating early. We looked at the Golden Bridge, took another chairlift to Louvre Station walked around the French Village, and took the chairlift from L’indochine back to the base. The chairlift ride is 20 mins, the walk through the facility, buildings, at the base another 10-15 mins, this area was now (10.30) extremely slow and crowded with arriving tour groups. The time restraint meant we were unable to line up for rides at the top, but gave us time to look around. We were back at our hotel in under the 4 hours. We didn’t need a sweater, it’ was lovely and mild. It was actually better than expected after reading the previous mixed reviews. You need to know that it IS fake, just like Disneyland, and definitely worth a visit.

Ba Na Hills lets you walk out on the bridge as soon as the cable cart drops you of, do it, take the walk - you will have to be there early or late if you want that glamorous Instagram photo without many other people in the background - it is chaos :)The bridge is only a small part of the bigger Ba Na Hills by Sunworld, a beautiful place with a unique history.Make sure you read about the park also, you pay for the whole experience - not just the small bridge :)

But dont you even think of getting that perfect picture without having a mob behind you, around you or infront of you. Even if you go early, there already mob of tour group waiting to get it. So, go there with an open mind and know that there will be lots of people around you. Despise all that, the view is fantastic. I did it and would not go back. It's really a one time thing.
It was too foggy in the winter so you can barely see any view and the hands are not very clear in photos. I’ve heard that it is nicer during the summer as you can see the view clearly.
Very unique and a beautiful spot for photo-taking. Walk the bridge to reach the Le Jardin flower Garden and enjoy the breathtaking scenery from atop the mountain.

Located as part of the overall ticket to Sun World, Ba Na hills. The bridge is architecturally stunning. The views are unreal. Only downside is that it gets incredibly overcrowded, which makes good photo opportunities quite difficult to come by. I suggest going very early in the morning on first opening or later in the afternoon past 4pm. The bridge may also get quite misty, hence may need to revisit later in the day. Otherwise incredibly experience!
The place was crowded with tourist and it was cloudy weather so we could not enjoy the scenic views of the bridge.The attraction is part of Sun World amusement park which is located in Ba Na Hills .
It was misty and raining when we were there. However, I was impressed by this amazing structure. It was crowded about 9-ish when we were there, hard to take a good pic with the mist and tourists.

Pretty busy when we went. Also cloudy and rainy. Nonetheless the bridge is pretty awesome. Difficult to get good pictures as there are so many people around. Very atmospheric in the rain and cloud. Little bit smaller than it looks, but still good. Cost a fair bit to see it though as it is part of a theme park that have plans to get bigger, and therefore busier.
Really love the design and concept of this unique bridge. It is a really a Vietnamese Masterpiece. It is very crowded due to its popularity, so go early in the morning before the tourist come it.
So many tourist and the weather is really not good in January. very foggy and rainy and the view is not as beautiful as the photo shows. Too many tourist as well.
It was foggy and raining. But there was still great views. Still very cool to see. And would be even better in good weather.

Amazing views when there's no fog! Also, its rather amusing to see all the crazy tourists and people jostling each other. Just beware of your belongings. You can get some great shots when it is clear otherwise, you will feel like you wasted your money if it's foggy. Roll of the dice unfortunately.
Was starting in HoiAn and took a car service to this bridge. It’s breath taking. So nice. You have to buy a ticket to a theme park, it’s around $30

We went yesterday, it was an overcast day and not raining when we arrived.  We travelled up a cable car up the mountain and most of the view was obscured by the clouds.  When we got to the top the temperature dropped considerably and it was quite cold and pouring rain.  Very crowded.  We put on the double layers of the thin plastic rain coats that were given and ventured out onto the bridge- we had gale force winds and it was pouring buckets.  It was so awful -the weather- that we were laughing hysterically.  We got our pictures, admired what we could see of the bridge and left.  I'm glad I got to see the bridge but seriously would consider doing this only on a sunny day.   The rest of the park that I could see -gaming area/ restaurants-  were overcrowd and because of the pouring rain we weren't able to walk through the French Village.  Loved the bridge hope you see it on a good day or go in the worst weather if you want to experience a weather event!

Yea, I don't think I would have gone to Ba Na Hills if it weren't for this new bridge opening up the past year.  I definitely would have been upset paying 700,000 VND without it.  Despite being a bit overcrowded with groups of tourists all trying to take the best picture, it is an amazing structure.   I guess it's amazing because you're kind of aware that your seeing (and on) something one of a kind and unique in the world.  When we on the bridge first on the way up, couldn't really see much as it was overcast and kind of foggy.  On the way back down, less crowded and the views were better.   I would try to find out the least crowded day when the sun is shining brightly to visit here.  The rest of Ba Na Hills is a whole nother issue.

This is a unique 150-metre in length, pedestrian viewing bridge. It is connected to the mountain top resort complex’s upper gondola station on Bà Nà Hills. During part of my visit, the views of lower levels were obstructed by a layer of clouds. If you are visiting Da Nang for a few days, it would be best to Bà Nà Hills on a fairly clear-sky day. Since we visited on a very cloudy day, our best views were from the 5.8-kilometre (the length of the longest of five gondola lines at Bà Nà Hills) gondola rides up and down Bà Nà Hills.

It is extremely nice, but it is also depend on the weather.  There are rain, fog, and cold since it is on the top of the mountain.  The ticket is non-refundable, and it is include in the price to ride from one mountain to another.  It is also worth it and must see while you are in Da Nang.  Beautiful place for pictures with friends and family.

I am giving this a 4 star because if the weather is bad  then you see nothing and tickets are expensive (30 USD).. The attraction is part of Sun World amusement park which is located in Ba Na Hills outside of Da Nang. The cable car system taking you up the hill is fantastic. They have built a lot of things in the hills but the hills could get surrounded by fog, mist and rain and then your money goes down the drain. So once in the parking lot look up and see if there is mist on top of the hills.

The bridge is really beautifull, although there is quite some fog. The ride with the cable car is fantastic and gives a great view .The impression of the park, before you get to the bridge, is a bit surreal. For us it looked a bit like Eurodisney in France. The food was expensive in some restaurants and the cheaper ones didn’t look so attractive. However we thought is was worth to visit.

OPEN ONLY IN 2018 .We were there recently , even IF  it was RAINING , but we were HAPPY  to be on the bridge  ! It was not A LONG WALK ! Always need to bring a light jacket because is quite high altitude and WINDY !!The best time of the year is in Spring time . Love THE CABLE  RIDE !30 USD including THE ENTRANCE , CABLE CAR UP and DOWN , everything except eating :) A lot of restaurant to choose up in Ba na HIlls . Toilets are clean !👌WE SHALL BE BACK AGAIN !👏👏👏

It was such a beautiful sight, didn't quite realise the scale of the carved hands until I actually saw it with my own hands, it was very misty, but it was still amazing to do! The grow a on the way up to the bridge was absolutely beautiful. Would recommend to anyone!!

The bridge in itself is beautiful and very original, however you have To make sure To go very early in the morning if you want to see it (and take pictures) before it gets crowded for the whole day... The park in itslef is not interesting but its location and the nature around are wonderful .
Latest attraction in Da Nang. Spectacular but over-crowded with Asian tour groups that has somewhat distracting photo taking opportunities. However, a must see while in Da Nang.
It is one of the attractions famous in Ba Na Hills. Full of tourists. Have to be patient, otherwise, u can't take the photo u like.

Well this was an amazing piece of work the trip up in the gondola was just amazing to go up 1400 Meters the view is incredible. But I'm glade we did it in early October as we were told but our guide the week before there was 25K people here in one day. With so many people it would have been very hard to get photos for us only a couple of hundred at the time we were there (lucky) the view from the bridge is great don't eat the fast food there wait till you go up the next gondola to the next level that's were all the good food is so after seeing and going onto the bridge you take the next gondola up to Sun World. (it's like universal studio in the USA) full of things to do and eat I would recommend doing this as a guided tour as we did from our hotel as they can arrange all the entrance tickets and you get a meal included with a wine or softdrink. As you get into the gondola for the 2nd trip they will give you a beer voucher for a free beer

Nice but often foggy, and very crowded. If you stay in the hotel, you got a better chance to take photo with no crown. Since the crowd from danang need 25mins to go up, and hotel guest only need 5mins to go to golden bridge. So go early at 7am when the cable car starts to run!

The bridge itself is beautiful and special!Part of a huge park that they still build. We took the cable car early in the morning straight to the bridge. Be prepared that there are many many many people there, so try to come early in the morning or late afternoon!The cable cars and bridge are a very nice half day to spend when in Hoi An/Da nang

The Golden Bridge is a recent addition to Bana Hills & is indeed very popular. I’ve never seen so many cameras but for a good reason , amazing bridge resting on giant marble hands on the side of a mountain. As it’s only a walk bridge it can get a bit crowded but if your patient you will get some amazing shots.A selfie stick is a must in order to capture the full view.The cable car ride up is also spectacular & you can go up to the next level to visit the theme park after the bridge .Pretty reasonable price for a day on the mountain which is much cooler temperature than DaNang city .

Amazing bridge with 2 hands.So beautiful..we can enjoy the scenery from the top. We take the cable car from bottom to the top around 30mins. 1 cable car can fit until 12 pax.Nice view and nice weather. Cool like in highland.So crowded because its still very new, even in weekdays and low seasonMany tourists from many countries are coming.After this we can enjoy other place such as le jardin, french village, lin ung pagoda etc

We'd seen pictures and video of the bridge, so booked a taxi to take us there. When we got there ticket prices are 700,000 VND for an adult, or child >1.3m in height and 550,000 VND for children under 1.3m. After balking at paying £100 for the 4 of us, we found out it is part of Sun World theme park. We took a punt and ended up having a great day. It all starts with an amazing 6km cable car ride. The bridge is about 2/3 of the way up the hills and the views from it are great. The theme park at the top of the hills is pretty good and everything is free - rides, shows, attractions and even arcade games. We even got a free beer. All of a sudden £100 was pretty good value, particularly as the cable car down was in a lightning storm which was pretty exciting.

We visited here on a Monday arriving at around 8.45am. I was amazed at the crowds already building at that time. We were really only coming here to look at the bridge. Wouldn’t bother for sub world unless that’s your thing. The cable car ride was long and steep. I don’t like heights so found it a difficult run. Everything here very professional. The bridge was crowded but I still thought it was worth a visit. Those hands are very big and impressive.

The bridge is part of the Ba Na hills theme park complex and set at around 1500 metres up in the hills. This is reached by a cable car trip of around 15 minutes. The bridge itself is spectacular but gets very busy. On then to the theme park which is pretty much a low quality version of Alton Towers except with fantastic views - you are literally in the clouds up there. It is worth a visit but don’t expect too much of the theme park itself.

A must visit place, you just pay a very small entrance fee and you can really enjoy an amazing experience.  I have spend here almost 4 hours. Many nice caves and views. Just one thing, it's not worth taking the lift at all! Long waiting times and the stairs to where the lift will take you are very ok.
Mit der combo Tour unbedingt buchen! Super Gaudi auf dem Lake und night market.
Мраморные горы - это комплекс из пяти холмов. На вершину можно подняться по ступенькам или на фуникулере. Там находится гроты/пещеры и множество пагод.

This was an unexpected pleasure. The trick is to skip the elevator and go in through gate 1. Skip the touristy shops below and take a photo of the map before going up. It’s beautiful views, old pagodas and amazing limestone temple caves. When we first went up we thought it was just an old temple but the paths to the caves are behind it. You could spend a couple of hours exploring all of it, or an hour to see a handful and get the feel. It was confusing down below but some helpful ladies pointed us towards the gate 1 steps. The elevator puts you too far over - the gate one steps puts you right in the middle of most of the stuff. I think the bad reviews must come from people who didn’t discover all there is to see. We loved it.

Climb the 300+ steps (or take the elevator) and be prepared for an incredible array of caves, many of which contain shrines. Some of these, spectacular on their own merit. It also affords visitors a variety of opportunities of views of the surrounding district.

Saya sangat kagum dengan keindahan pemandangan di Ngũ Hành Sơn atau Marble Mountains. Kawasannya sangat unik kerana terdapat tokong dan pagoda yang dibina di atas serta di dalam gua-gua gunung yang misteri. Suasana di sini terasa sangat suci dan tenang, terutamanya apabila melihat seni bina lama yang sebati dengan alam semula jadi. Selain mendaki, kawasan ini juga terkenal dengan perkampungan ukiran batu tradisional yang sangat mahsyur di tengah Vietnam.
